.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/04-verdicts.md:12:Verdicts are the final decision surface for the review loop. They tell downstream workflows whether the review found release blockers, required fixes, or only advisory items.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/01--entry-points-and-modes/auto-mode-deep-review-kickoff.md:53:Capture the command examples, the mode-routing block, and the `state_paths` contract together.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:24:      description: Review a single spec folder and its referenced implementation, tests, and release artifacts.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:70:      label: Blocker
.opencode/skills/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:184:  releaseReadinessStates:
.opencode/skills/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:189:    - id: release-blocking
.opencode/skills/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:201:      partialCriteria: Some claims resolve, but one or more claims remain ambiguous or blocked by missing evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:225:      partialCriteria: Runtime definitions are broadly aligned but contain non-blocking drift in examples, labels, or metadata.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:255:        - releaseReadinessState
.opencode/skills/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:480:      description: Ensure every render target contains intact begin/end markers with no overlapping generated blocks.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/02-adversarial-self-check.md:3:description: "Re-reads blocker evidence before a P0 can become a confirmed review conclusion."
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/02-adversarial-self-check.md:10:Re-reads blocker evidence before a P0 can become a confirmed review conclusion.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/02-adversarial-self-check.md:12:This control keeps the review loop from overcalling blockers. Before a P0 is treated as real enough to shape the verdict, the workflow requires a deliberate re-check of the cited evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/02-adversarial-self-check.md:16:The skill contract requires an adversarial self-check on every P0 finding. The guidance is simple but strict: re-read the cited code, actively try to disprove the blocker, and only keep the P0 label when the evidence still holds. This guard appears both in the loop rules and in the success criteria for final review completion.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/02-adversarial-self-check.md:18:The adversarial re-check is separate from the typed claim-adjudication packet. Self-check focuses on whether the blocker is genuine at all, while adjudication records the evidence trail and the final accepted severity. Together they keep FAIL verdicts from depending on inference-only or weakly supported blockers.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/02-adversarial-self-check.md:29:| `references/protocol/loop_protocol.md` | Protocol | Places blocker re-read and severity confirmation in the post-iteration evaluation flow. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/02-adversarial-self-check.md:30:| `references/convergence/convergence.md` | Protocol | Treats unresolved active P0 findings as a legal-stop blocker and verdict driver. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/02-adversarial-self-check.md:36:| `manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/adversarial-self-check-runs-on-p0-findings.md` | Manual scenario | Verifies the blocker re-check path is executed. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/02-adversarial-self-check.md:37:| `manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md` | Manual scenario | Confirms confirmed P0 findings still block stop after the self-check path. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:1:This release lets you pick which AI actually runs each review iteration. Before, every iteration dispatched through the native review agent using Claude Opus. Now you can opt into running the iteration through a CLI executor such as `codex exec` with a chosen model, reasoning effort, plus service tier. The skill's YAML workflow still owns all the loop machinery (state tracking, convergence detection, findings registry, dashboard, the reducer). The CLI is a tool inside the workflow, not a replacement for it. Ships as a symmetric pair with `deep-research` v1.8.0.0 because asymmetric shipping creates the bug class Phase 017 Cluster D was built to prevent.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:3:> Spec folder: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/017-sk-deep-cli-runtime-execution/001-executor-feature` (Level 3)
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:11:Before this release, the YAML step that runs each review iteration had a hardcoded line saying "dispatch the review agent using Opus". There was no way to pick a different executor. The iteration step now branches on a config field called `executor.kind`. The `native` branch (the default) keeps the exact same agent dispatch as before. The new `cli-codex` branch runs `codex exec` with the model and flags pulled from the config, piping the review iteration prompt through stdin. A user who does not set anything sees identical behavior to previous releases. Review dimensions, findings severity, plus verdict logic all stay exactly where they were.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:21:#### Config schema for executor selection
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:23:Adds a new `executor` block to `deep_review_config.json` that is bit-identical to the one in `deep_research_config.json`. Same six fields, same defaults, same validation. One contract, two consumers. The two configs stay aligned by design.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:29:Five new flags were added to the consolidated setup prompt in `deep-review.md`: `--executor`, `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`. Precedence is CLI flag first, then config file, then the schema default. Users get the same UX across both iterative skills, so no need to learn two different configurations.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:35:When a non-native executor runs a review iteration, the workflow appends an `executor: {kind, model, reasoningEffort, serviceTier}` block to that iteration's JSONL record. The review JSONL schema (dimensions, filesReviewed, findingsSummary, newFindingsRatio) is preserved unchanged. The audit is a new top-level field, not a replacement for any existing field.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:41:Every executor (native or CLI) must produce a markdown narrative file plus a JSONL delta record with the required review fields. The shared validator checks both after dispatch. If either is missing or malformed, a canonical `schema_mismatch` conflict event fires. Three consecutive validation failures trigger the existing stuck-recovery path.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:49:The forward-looking note at line 63 of `SKILL.md` has been replaced with a full contract section parallel to the research skill's. The section documents which kinds are shipped, which are reserved, what invariants every review executor must respect, the review-specific JSONL schema requirements (dimensions, filesReviewed, findingsSummary, newFindingsRatio), plus where the config and template live.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:67:No review-specific TypeScript was added. All four shared modules (executor-config, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, executor-audit) are consumed unchanged from the research release. The 40 tests in the research suite cover every shared behavior the review path depends on.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:80:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/assets/deep_review_config.json`       | New `executor` block (identical to deep_research_config.json's block)       |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:85:No review-specific tests added. The shared deep-loop test suite (40 tests in the research release) covers every behavior the review path consumes.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:89:`SKILL.md` gets the Executor Selection Contract section. `references/loop_protocol.md` gets the Executor Resolution subsection in section 3. `.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md` gets the five new setup flags plus the conditional executor question.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:97:No migration required. `executor.kind` defaults to `native`, which preserves pre-release behavior exactly. Same agent dispatch, same iteration file, same JSONL record with review dimensions, same findings registry semantics.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:103:  --executor=cli-codex \
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:111:This release co-ships with `deep-research` v1.8.0.0. All four shared TypeScript modules are consumed by both skills. All four YAMLs (research auto, research confirm, review auto, review confirm) use the same two-branch structure. Design rationale lives in the spec packet's `decision-record.md` ADR-005.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/assets/deep_research_config.json:14:  "executor": {
.opencode/skills/deep-research/assets/deep_research_config.json:71:    "description": "Fields managed by the offline loop optimizer (042.004). See optimizer-manifest.json for the canonical tunable/locked field registry.",
.opencode/skills/deep-research/assets/deep_research_config.json:74:    "lockedFields": ["lineage", "reducer", "fileProtection", "sessionId", "status"],
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:26:Release-readiness states are derived alongside convergence:
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:29:- `release-blocking` whenever unresolved P0 findings remain active
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:38:- Auditing the release-readiness classification logic before shipping.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:68:> **STATUS: SPEC ONLY (future implementation).** The override matrix below describes the contract that a future release should apply automatically when the review target involves security-sensitive surfaces. As of v1.9.0.0, the runtime does NOT auto-detect security-sensitivity and does NOT apply these overrides. `requiredClosedFindingReplay` and `requiredFixCompletenessGate` exist in no config / yaml / reducer surface (verified by grep). Operators running a security-sensitive review must manually tighten thresholds via `--convergence` + `--max-iterations` and manually maintain the closed-gate replay table. The contract below stands as the target spec for the next implementation pass. (Provenance for this deferred contract is recorded in this skill's changelog.)
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:82:Every terminal stop and every blocked-stop vote MUST emit the shared stop contract from REQ-001: a named `stopReason` enum plus, when STOP is vetoed, a `blocked_stop` event written to `deep-review-state.jsonl`. There is no nested `legalStop` wrapper on the persisted path. Earlier drafts of this document implied one, and that drift was the source of F009 in the 042 closing audit.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:91:| `blockedStop` | Convergence math voted stop but legal-stop gates failed. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:96:#### blocked_stop Event (canonical, persisted)
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:98:`step_emit_blocked_stop` in both `deep_start-review-loop_{auto,confirm}.yaml` appends the following record to `deep-review-state.jsonl` whenever the legal-stop decision tree vetoes STOP. The gate names and their shapes are load-bearing, the reducer reads them verbatim:
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:103:  "event": "blocked_stop",
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:106:  "blockedBy": ["dimensionCoverageGate", "p0ResolutionGate"],
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:122:  "graphBlockerDetail": [],
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:130:- `blockedBy`: array of gate names that failed (string[], never structured objects). Empty when STOP is legal, in which case no `blocked_stop` event is emitted.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:131:- `gateResults`: named sub-records keyed by `convergenceGate`, `dimensionCoverageGate`, `p0ResolutionGate`, `evidenceDensityGate`, `hotspotSaturationGate`, `claimAdjudicationGate`, `fixCompletenessReplayGate`, `candidateCoverageGate`, and `graphlessFallbackGate`. This is the full 9-gate set emitted by `step_emit_blocked_stop` in both `deep_start-review-loop_{auto,confirm}.yaml`. Each sub-record has a `pass` boolean plus gate-specific fields (score, covered/missing, activeP0, avgEvidencePerFinding, activeP0P1, securitySensitive, requiredRows, passingRows, searchDebt, mode, unavailabilityReason). The reducer reads these verbatim and does not coerce shapes. `candidateCoverageGate` and `graphlessFallbackGate` are v2-rollout gates, they pass trivially when the review-depth-v2 search path is inactive (no search debt, graph available).
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:132:- `graphBlockerDetail`: array of structured graph blockers copied from the latest graph convergence decision. Empty when graph convergence did not veto STOP.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:134:- When the graph convergence verdict is `STOP_BLOCKED`, fold the graph blocker gate name into `blockedBy` and preserve the structured blocker objects under `graphBlockerDetail`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:144:5. **Legal-stop gate bundle** -- review-specific stop gates. If a stop vote fails any gate, persist `stopReason=blockedStop` and continue.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:171:Triggers when all 4 dimensions (correctness, security, traceability, maintainability) are covered, no active P0/P1 remains, at least 1 stabilization pass has occurred, and the 5-gate legal-stop bundle passes. If gates fail, the loop records `stopReason=blockedStop` and continues despite full coverage.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:294:| 0.3-0.5 | Active churn, findings being added, merged, or reclassified frequently |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:319:   - When this sub-check fails while the existing churn signals pass, the gate records the mismatch as a diagnostic note but does not independently block STOP.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:323:The gate passes only when both the existing churn-based checks AND the semantic stability checks agree. When a semantic check fails, the persisted `blocked_stop.gateResults.findingStability` payload includes the semantic signal values.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:369:| **P0** (Blocker) | 10.0 | Correctness failures, security vulnerabilities, spec contradictions |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:406:> **GATE MODEL, two naming conventions mapped to one authoritative set.** The authoritative producer is `step_emit_blocked_stop` in both `deep_start-review-loop_{auto,confirm}.yaml`, which emits **9 gates** with the `Gate` suffix: `convergenceGate` / `dimensionCoverageGate` / `p0ResolutionGate` / `evidenceDensityGate` / `hotspotSaturationGate` / `claimAdjudicationGate` / `fixCompletenessReplayGate` / `candidateCoverageGate` / `graphlessFallbackGate`. The §Section-1 event shape (lines 98-119) mirrors this set verbatim and the reducer reads those names verbatim, so treat the event shape as authoritative when writing or reading JSONL state. The §6 table below is the high-level conceptual model and uses descriptive names WITHOUT the `Gate` suffix where one reads more naturally (for example `findingStability` maps to `convergenceGate`); each row carries its event-shape name so the mapping is explicit. `candidateCoverageGate` and `graphlessFallbackGate` are v2-rollout gates that pass trivially when the review-depth-v2 search path is inactive. The gate-model drift cluster (LG-0013, LG-0016, LG-0031, LG-0032) was reconciled in `006-gate-model-reconciliation`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:408:Deep review treats STOP as legal only when the full review-specific gate bundle passes together. Convergence math may request STOP, but the workflow must still evaluate these 9 gates and persist a blocked-stop event when any gate fails.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:412:| **findingStability** | `convergenceGate` | Rolling average, MAD noise floor, and novelty ratio must all indicate low-yield review churn | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:413:| **dimensionCoverage** | `dimensionCoverageGate` | Every configured review dimension must have been examined at least once, with required traceability coverage stabilized | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:414:| **p0Resolution** | `p0ResolutionGate` | No unresolved P0 findings may remain active at stop time | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:415:| **evidenceDensity** | `evidenceDensityGate` | Evidence density across active findings must meet the configured threshold | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:416:| **hotspotSaturation** | `hotspotSaturationGate` | Review hotspots must be revisited enough times to satisfy the saturation heuristic | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:417:| (no §6 counterpart) | `claimAdjudicationGate` | Each new P0/P1 finding must carry a typed adjudication packet, missing or failing packets veto STOP | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:418:| **fixCompletenessReplay** | `fixCompletenessReplayGate` | Security-sensitive fix reruns must replay previously closed P0/P1 gates and validate producer/consumer/matrix coverage from the remediation packet | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:419:| **candidateCoverage** (v2 rollout) | `candidateCoverageGate` | Search debt must be cleared and every required bug class must have candidate coverage before STOP, passes trivially when the v2 search path is inactive | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:420:| **graphlessFallback** (v2 rollout) | `graphlessFallbackGate` | When the code graph is unavailable, required bug classes must carry cited fallback ledger rows, passes trivially when the graph is available | Block STOP, persist `blockedStop` |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:439:      detail: "No unresolved P0 findings remain and blocker adjudication is complete."
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:463:  blockedBy = [name for name, result in gateResults.items() if not result.pass]
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:465:    pass: len(blockedBy) == 0,
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:466:    blockedBy,
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:471:When convergence math returns STOP, invoke `buildReviewLegalStop()`. If it returns `pass: false`, persist a first-class `blocked_stop` event with the failing `blockedBy` gates, the full `gateResults` bundle, a `recoveryStrategy`, and the normal run/timestamp/session lineage fields before overriding the decision to CONTINUE.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:473:### Blocked-Stop Recovery Strategy
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:479:| `p0Resolution` | Re-open the active blocker path and verify whether the P0 is real, downgraded, or still unresolved. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:498:| `guard_override` | `blockedStop` | Legacy continue override when a stop vote failed legal gates. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:499:| `quality guard failed` | `blockedStop` | Older prose for the same blocked-stop outcome. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:500:| `P0 override blocks convergence` | `blockedStop` | Legacy review wording for a stop attempt that was not legal to finalize. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:513:| **FAIL** | `activeP0 > 0` OR any legal-stop gate fails at terminal stop time | Does not meet quality standards. Blocks release. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:514:| **CONDITIONAL** | `activeP0 == 0` AND `activeP1 > 0` | Meets threshold but has required fixes before release. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:568:- `blocked_stop` event structure and gate names
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md:575:The authoritative registry of tunable vs locked fields is maintained at:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:3:scope: "deep-loop native fan-out parallel multi-executor (packet 123)"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:8:# Handover: deep-loop native fan-out parallel multi-executor (packet 123)
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:15:can run **N executor "lineages" concurrently (capped)**, each running the loop in its own isolated
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:16:sub-packet, then a cross-lineage merge — generalizing the manual pattern proven in packet 122.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:17:**Single-executor stays the default and must stay byte-identical** (hard parity gate).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:20:- **REMAINING:** Phase 3 (per-lineage spawn + `--artifact-dir-override` in 4 YAMLs), Phase 4 (salvage + coverage-graph per-sessionId), Phase 5 (consumer merges + synthesis hooks), Phase 6 (command flags + docs + final parity).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:29:Packet `123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout` is scaffolded (parent `spec.md` validates strict-clean; 6 phase children).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:31:Single-executor path is untouched (parity holds). Phases 3-6 are not started. There is one architectural
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:37:- `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` (+118 lines). Added, WITHOUT touching the existing `executorConfigSchema` / `parseExecutorConfig` / `resolveExecutorConfig`:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:38:  - `lineageExecutorSchema` = `executorConfigSchema.extend({ label (dir-safe /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/), count (int≥1, default 1), iterations (int≥1|null, default null) })`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:39:  - `fanoutConfigSchema` = `{ executors: lineageExecutorSchema[] (min 1), concurrency: int≥1 (default 2) }`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:42:- `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts` — optional `lineageId?: string` on `RunAuditedExecutorCommandInput` (the type ~L95-106) and `buildExecutorAuditRecord(executor, lineageId?)` (~L490), spread CONDITIONALLY so records are byte-identical when absent. **Defined but not yet consumed** — first consumer is Phase 3/5.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:43:- `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/tests/unit/executor-config.vitest.ts` — 36 tests (27 original + 9 fan-out).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:46:- `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` (NEW). Exports: `runCappedPool`, `settleItem`, `buildPoolSummary`, `appendStatusLedger`, `writeOrchestrationSummary`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:47:  - `runCappedPool({ items, concurrency, worker, now?, onEvent? })` — ≤concurrency in flight (clamped ≥1), never-throws per-item settlement, **ordered results**, `{ results, summary:{total,succeeded,failed,all_failed} }`. This is the concurrency-capped generalization of `lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` (which lacks a cap). **`worker` is INJECTED** — the real per-lineage worker is Phase 3.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:49:- `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/tests/unit/fanout-pool.vitest.ts` (NEW, 10 tests).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:55:### Decisions already locked (do not relitigate)
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:56:- **Scope:** full native feature; **single-executor remains the recommended default**; fan-out is opt-in; default path byte-identical (hard parity gate at Phase 1 + Phase 6).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:60:- **Backward-compat rule:** config carries EITHER `config.executor` (single) OR `config.fanout` (multi), never both. The both-present guard is deferred to Phase 6 (command surface) — no call site writes `config.fanout` yet.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:63:- **`kind` vs `type` — INCONSISTENT across the two YAMLs (grep-verified):** the dispatch step `step_dispatch_iteration` branches on **`config.executor.type`** in `deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml` (L573) but on **`config.executor.kind`** in `deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml` (L696). Both work because `normalizeExecutorConfigInput` (executor-config.ts) aliases `type`→`kind`, and the canonical schema field is `kind`. Phase 3/6 must tolerate BOTH spellings (and ideally normalize research to `kind` while there). Fan-out config entries use `kind`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:64:- **`loop-lock.ts` is PER-PACKET** (caller-supplied `lockPath`, `packetId` in the lock data). Separate sub-packets ⇒ zero lock contention. Reuse as-is; do not change it.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:65:- **Per-kind state-dir env hooks exist** for same-kind-replica isolation: `getDefaultStatePaths` reads `process.env.SPECKIT_CODEX_STATE_DIR` (and siblings per kind), used by the recursion-guard lockfile check. Two lineages of the SAME cli kind may trip the lockfile guard; scope each via its own `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR`. **Verify this in the Phase 3 dry run** (distinct kinds are safe; same-kind replicas are the risk).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:66:- **The packet-122 "prototype" is NOT a faithful per-lineage loop.** What was actually run there: `run_one.sh` + `xargs -P 2` dispatched **independent single-iteration** executor calls (`opencode run` / `codex exec` / `claude -p`) over a flat `for iter; for model` job list, with a salvage step + status ledger. There was **no convergence check, no reducer, no inter-iteration state read**. So "fan-out that reuses the existing loop verbatim" is a STRONGER claim than what was prototyped. Keep this honesty in mind when designing Phase 3 (see §4.A).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:67:- **Salvage is mandatory (proven need):** weak CLI executors (e.g. MiniMax via opencode) intermittently DON'T write their output files, and codex/headless sandboxes sometimes block in-repo writes. Phase 4 must recover the executor's stdout reply → write the missing iteration md. In 122 this recovered every otherwise-lost iteration.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:78:### Phase 3 — Per-lineage spawn + sub-packet isolation (`003-per-lineage-spawn-isolation/`)
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:80:- Add the spawn worker (and the CLI entry that wires `runCappedPool` + worker + ledger) — likely in `fanout-pool.cjs` or a sibling `scripts/fanout-run.cjs`. Use the script-entry contract from `scripts/convergence.cjs` (tsx bootstrap L33-63; argv parse; JSON-out; exit codes via `scripts/lib/cli-guards.cjs` → `classifyExitCode` 1=script/2=DB/3=input, `validateNamespaceValue`, `installSignalHandlers`).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:85:  - Override logic: if `--artifact-dir-override` / `config.fanout` sub-packet dir is present → bind `artifact_dir` to it; else run the existing inline resolver unchanged.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:86:- Reuse per-iteration libs UNCHANGED: `renderPromptPack` (prompt-pack.ts), `runAuditedExecutorCommand`/`Async` (executor-audit.ts L584/L661), `writeFirstRecordExecutor` (L509), `validateIterationOutputs` (post-dispatch-validate.ts). Pass `lineageId` to the audited command now (the field added in Phase 1).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:87:- **Test:** integration — pool spawns a stub twice into two `lineages/{label}/` trees; assert distinct trees + distinct sessionIds + no lock contention.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:89:### Phase 4 — Salvage sweep + coverage-graph per-sessionId (`004-salvage-coverage-graph/`)
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:90:- After each lineage sub-loop, sweep with `validateIterationOutputs`; on `iteration_file_missing` / `iteration_file_empty`, read `{sub-packet}/logs/iter-NNN.out`, parse the opencode `--format json` text parts (`{type:"text",part:{text}}`) else raw, write the missing `iterations/iteration-NNN.md`, append a `salvaged_from_stdout` event, re-validate; still-missing ⇒ failed marker (loop's stuck-recovery owns persistence).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:92:- **Test:** unit (missing-md+stdout ⇒ salvaged; still-missing ⇒ failed); integration (two lineages, distinct sessionIds, shared `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`, explicit no-collision assertion).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:94:### Phase 5 — Consumer merges + synthesis hooks (`005-consumer-merges-synthesis/`)
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:95:- New `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` (`--loop-type research|review`), reads every `{artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/` registry + iterations + state log.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:96:- New `step_fanout_merge` at the TOP of each `phase_synthesis`, gated on `config.fanout` present (absent ⇒ synthesis unchanged):
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:98:  - **review** (review YAML `phase_synthesis` L1124; `step_derive_verdict` L1150 → `step_build_finding_registry` L1172 → `step_compile_review_report` L1223): severity rollup with **strongest-restriction** (ANY lineage active P0 ⇒ merged FAIL) → consolidated `deep-review-findings-registry.json` → existing compiler emits `review-report.md` + verdict. NOTE the review verdict is derived in `step_derive_verdict` from `active_p0/p1/p2` counts — the merge must feed it the rolled-up counts.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:99:  - Both write `{artifact_dir}/fanout-attribution.md` (per-lineage convergence, iters, salvage events, model attribution).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:100:- **Test:** unit (research dedup/attribution; review rollup + strongest-restriction: A clean + B P0 ⇒ merged FAIL); integration (2-lineage fixture → merged registry → existing synthesis → canonical report unchanged in shape).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:103:- `commands/deep/start-research-loop.md` + `start-review-loop.md` §0: repeatable `--executor` (groups trailing `--model/--reasoning-effort/--service-tier/--executor-timeout/--iters/--label/--count`), `--executors <json>` escape hatch, `--concurrency N`; extend the Default Resolution Table + PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS; add fan-out EXAMPLES. **Default policy: 0–1 executor & no `--executors` ⇒ write `config.executor` (single, unchanged, documented as the recommended default); 2+ / `--executors` / any `count>1` ⇒ write `config.fanout`.** Add the both-present fail-fast here.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:104:- Docs: `deep-research/SKILL.md` (§4 NEVER #9 "Simulate loop dispatch" + the EXPERIMENTAL/reference-only wave section) and `deep-review/SKILL.md` (FORBIDDEN INVOCATION PATTERNS) — carve out that **command-driven fan-out is supported**; ad-hoc shell dispatch + intra-lineage wave stay forbidden/deferred. Add "Fan-Out Convergence" to both `references/convergence/convergence.md`. Add `fanout-pool.cjs`/`fanout-merge.cjs` rows to `deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md` script table + reaffirm single-DB-owner + per-sessionId isolation.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:105:- **Final parity gate (non-negotiable):** a single-executor run is byte-identical to pre-change `main` (config, state.jsonl modulo timestamps, iteration md, research.md/review-report.md). Snapshot-diff a real single-executor run.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:109:## 4. Active Blockers & Risks
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:116:There is no "run the whole loop unattended" binary to `spawn` N times. The native/`@deep-research` lineage in
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:119:- **(Option B-spawn, recommended):** add a thin **headless single-lineage loop driver** (`scripts/fanout-run.cjs` or a mode of `fanout-pool.cjs`) that, per lineage, runs the loop control-flow in JS by CALLING the existing runtime pieces as subprocesses/functions: `renderPromptPack` → `runAuditedExecutorCommand` (CLI) — note the **native/`@deep-research` path can't be driven headlessly without the agent**, so fan-out may need to require CLI executors per lineage (or treat `native` as "this agent runs it inline, others spawn"). It calls `convergence.cjs` each iteration so convergence is NOT re-implemented. This is "Option B" honestly realized; it re-implements only the loop's *control flow*, not its math.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:120:- **(Option agent-orchestrated):** the agent running the fan-out command dispatches per-lineage sub-runs itself (Task sub-agents or sequential), with `fanout-pool.cjs` providing the cap/ledger for the CLI lineages only. Closer to what was done manually, but parallelism then depends on the agent, not an unattended process.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:122:**Recommendation:** confirm with the user, then most likely Option B-spawn restricted to CLI executor lineages (native lineage, if any, runs inline in the orchestrating agent). Capture the decision in `003-…/decision-record.md` before coding. This is the single biggest risk to the plan's "native, unattended" promise.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:132:`deep-loop-runtime/tests/unit/loop-lock.vitest.ts > "allows exactly one fresh cross-process acquire to win"`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:133:is a cross-process timing test that flakes (passed 7/7 isolated once, failed 1/7 another run). `loop-lock.ts`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:134:is NOT in this packet's diff. Treat a lone `loop-lock` failure as the known flake; everything else must be green.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:8:<!-- Note: Task is for the command executor (loop management); @deep-review agent is LEAF-only (no Task). No WebFetch: review is code-only. -->
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:10:<!-- Keywords: deep-review, code-audit, iterative-review, review-loop, convergence-detection, externalized-state, fresh-context, review-agent, JSONL-state, severity-findings, P0-P1-P2, release-readiness, spec-alignment -->
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:30:- Release readiness check before shipping a feature or component
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:49:- Write a custom bash/shell dispatcher to parallelize iterations (ad-hoc shell fan-out)
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:56:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:69:- "release readiness check" / "pre-release review"
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:77:`deep review`, `code audit`, `iterative review`, `review loop`, `release readiness`, `spec folder review`, `convergence detection`, `quality audit`, `find misalignments`, `verify cross-references`, `pre-release review`, `audit spec folder`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:110:    "REVIEW_REPORT":      {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["review report", "remediation", "verdict", "release readiness", "planning packet"]},
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:114:    "REVIEW_SETUP":       {"audit code": 2.0, "review spec folder": 1.8, "release readiness": 1.5, "pre-release": 1.5},
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:149:    "ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS": ["full protocol", "all templates", "complete reference", "resume deep review", "deep-review wave", "review artifact", "release-readiness audit", "convergence-tracked", "same session lineage", "P0"],
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:331:Review mode is lineage-aware. Supported lifecycle modes are `new`, `resume`, and `restart`. Required lineage fields include `sessionId`, `parentSessionId`, `lineageMode`, `generation`, `continuedFromRun`, and `releaseReadinessState`. The reducer consumes the latest JSONL delta, the new iteration file, and prior reduced state, then emits finding registry, dashboard metrics, and strategy updates.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:335:| Severity | Criteria | Blocking |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:337:| **P0** | Correctness failure, security vulnerability, spec contradiction | Yes, blocks PASS verdict |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:371:Executor settings are owned by the YAML workflow and rendered prompt pack. Do not bypass the workflow by hand-dispatching review iterations; each iteration stays LEAF-only and produces the required markdown plus JSONL delta. The full contract -- per-iteration invariants, failure modes, JSONL audit field, config surface and precedence, executor-invariant guarantees, and the four-value code-graph readiness TrustState surface -- lives in `references/protocol/loop_protocol.md`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:423:Detailed contracts: `references/protocol/loop_protocol.md` (executor invariants, failure modes, config surface) and `references/state/state_reducer_registry.md` (two-tier content-hash dedup).
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:445:Each gate is binary: pass or block. STOP cannot be legal until every gate passes.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:447:- **Config validity**: `deep-review-config.json` parses, names every required field, and lineage block matches the current run (`sessionId`, `parentSessionId`, `lineageMode`, `generation`, `continuedFromRun`, `releaseReadinessState`).
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:458:The release-readiness handoff is valid when:
.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md:460:- The final report records stop reason, iteration count, dimension coverage, severity counts (P0/P1/P2), verdict (PASS / CONDITIONAL / FAIL), and release-readiness state (`in-progress`, `converged`, `release-blocking`).
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence_recovery.md:16:This reference defines what the deep-review loop does when convergence stalls or completes. It covers stuck-recovery strategy selection, the convergence report payload, and graph-aware signals that can support or block a final STOP.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence_recovery.md:23:- Debugging blocked STOP decisions that need recovery before synthesis can finalize.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence_recovery.md:113:Stop reason: [converged | maxIterationsReached | userPaused | blockedStop | stuckRecovery | error | manualStop]
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence_recovery.md:147:blocked_stop (only when STOP was vetoed on this run):
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence_recovery.md:148:  blockedBy: [gate names]
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence_recovery.md:182:When STOP is vetoed instead of finalized, the workflow emits the separate `blocked_stop` event from Section 3 rather than nesting a `legalStop` object inside `synthesis`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence_recovery.md:214:When graph signals are available, the convergence report (Section 3) includes an additional block:
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:45:| Shared runtime | Consumes executor and graph support | `deep-loop-runtime` owns shared libraries |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:54:| Legal-stop convergence | Blocks premature STOP until coverage and quality gates pass |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:55:| Graph-aware gates | Uses `graphEvents` as extra STOP-blocking evidence when present |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:59:The packet is lineage-aware. Every run carries `sessionId`, `parentSessionId`, `lineageMode`, `generation`, and `continuedFromRun`, so the workflow can distinguish an active resume from a restart. `fork` and `completed-continue` are reserved for a future release and are not runtime-supported today. See the Lifecycle Branches section in `references/protocol/loop_protocol.md` for the canonical one-session contract.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:63:The workflow also anchors every run to a real `spec.md`. During late INIT it follows [`references/protocol/spec_check_protocol.md`](references/protocol/spec_check_protocol.md): acquire `research/.deep-research.lock`, classify `folder_state` as `no-spec`, `spec-present`, `spec-just-created-by-this-run`, or `conflict-detected`, and then either seed a Level 1 spec or append bounded context before LOOP starts.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:65:During SYNTHESIS the same contract replaces exactly one `<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: deep-research/spec-findings -->` ... `<!-- END GENERATED: deep-research/spec-findings -->` block under the chosen host anchor while keeping `research/research.md` canonical.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:104:- Advisory lock + generated fence: The workflow holds `research/.deep-research.lock` through synthesis and replaces one `BEGIN/END GENERATED` findings block in `spec.md`.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:109:- Graph-aware legal-stop checks: When `graphEvents` exist, structural graph signals add extra STOP-blocking evidence on top of the standard convergence math.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:111:- Graph convergence guards: STOP-blocking guards. sourceDiversity (>= 0.4) and evidenceDepth (>= 1.5) must pass before convergence is accepted, preventing premature termination from single-source or shallow-evidence research.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:181:- Protocol-owned packet mutations: bounded `spec.md` seeding, context append, advisory lock lifecycle, and generated-fence replacement defined in `references/protocol/spec_check_protocol.md`.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:193:| `executor.kind` | `native` | Selects native LEAF agent or workflow-routed CLI executor |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:219:**Diagnose a blocked STOP**
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:225:Expected output: gate/blocker explanation and next focus
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:268:| `spec.md` write is blocked | Inspect `folder_state`, `research/.deep-research.lock`, and the conflict details defined in `references/protocol/spec_check_protocol.md`. |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:287:A: Only the bounded mutations in `references/protocol/spec_check_protocol.md`. seed markers or pre-init context during INIT, plus one machine-owned `BEGIN GENERATED` / `END GENERATED` findings block during SYNTHESIS.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:301:`deep-research` depends on `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/` for executor + state + coverage-graph runtime, which runs in a fully isolated, no-MCP execution mode.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:307:| `references/protocol/spec_check_protocol.md` | Bounded `spec.md` anchoring, `folder_state` rules, advisory lock lifecycle, and generated-fence write-back |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:309:| `references/state/state_jsonl.md` | Config, iteration, event, lineage, graph, and blocked-stop records |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/README.md:317:| `manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-signals.md` | Operator test case for graph stop guards and blocked-stop behavior |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/reducer-search-debt.md:16:A v2 record may validate cleanly and still hide deferred or blocked candidate-search obligations from the operator. The reducer/dashboard/report chain is where "what was searched, what is still owed" becomes durable across review iterations.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.1.0.md:1:This patch release closes the remaining 042 closing-audit issues that still affected `deep-review`. It focuses on places where the workflow promised one thing but the runtime did another, so the review loop, reducer, and docs now tell the same story.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.1.0.md:13:Every new required-severity finding (a P0 blocker or a P1 must-fix) is supposed to carry a structured "claim-adjudication packet" - a short JSON block that names the claim, cites the file-and-line evidence, lists what counter-evidence the reviewer looked for, and records the final severity plus a confidence number. The documentation said the review loop would refuse to stop until every new P0 or P1 had a valid packet. In practice the review loop never read the flag that tracked this. Reviewers could produce a packet that was missing fields or missing entirely, the workflow would flag it internally, and then stop-checking would run as if nothing was wrong. A loop that was supposed to be release-gated could finish and render a PASS verdict on top of incomplete adjudication evidence. Both review workflows (interactive and autonomous) now run a universal claim-adjudication pre-check as the first step of every stop-check cycle. If the most recent adjudication result was a failure, stop is blocked immediately with a clear recovery message asking the reviewer to rewrite the missing packets. The legal-stop decision tree also gained a dedicated gate that surfaces the adjudication status alongside the other five gates. The result is persisted to the state log as a typed event so the next iteration can read it back. Every new P0 or P1 finding must now ship with a complete packet. Anything less keeps the loop running.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.1.0.md:19:The skill keeps a small SQLite cache of "coverage graph" nodes and edges - dimensions, files, findings, evidence, and their relationships - so future iterations can reason about what has already been covered. The cache was shared across all review sessions in a spec folder. The primary key on each row was the bare logical id (`d-correctness`, `f-001`, etc.). If two reviewers ran concurrent sessions against the same spec folder and both happened to emit a node with the same logical id, the second write overwrote the first. Filtered reads still worked, but the underlying rows were corrupted. The earlier phase-008 session-isolation test only checked filtered reads over disjoint fixtures, so it never caught the collision path. The cache schema is now on version 2. Node and edge tables use a composite primary key built from spec folder, loop type, session id, and logical id. The database migrates by dropping the old tables and recreating the new ones on first open - the cache is a derived artifact, not durable state, so a drop-and-recreate is safe. The session-isolation test now has a dedicated "shared-ID collisions" block that upserts identical node and edge ids across two sessions and asserts both rows survive with their own content. A future phase that introduces real cross-session analytics on top of this cache will find it correct from day one instead of walking into a silent-overwrite trap.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.1.0.md:25:When an operator re-entered a review session, the interactive workflow asked which lifecycle mode to use: `resume` (continue where you left off), `restart` (archive the old run and start a new generation), `fork` (start a sibling run history), or `completed-continue` (reopen a finished run after snapshotting its synthesis). The first two were real. The last two were documented in several places but had no actual workflow wiring - selecting them did nothing different, and the state log silently kept the old session id. Anyone who believed they had branched a review was quietly overwriting their previous work. `fork` and `completed-continue` are now explicitly marked as deferred (reserved for a future release) everywhere the skill mentions them: `SKILL.md`, `README.md`, both loop-protocol references, both command workflows, the main agent definition, and the runtime mirrors across all three CLI runtimes. The interactive gate now offers three choices - `resume`, `restart`, or `cancel`. Both paths append a typed event to the state log that carries the full run history field set (session id, parent session id, run history mode, continued-from-run, generation, archive path, timestamp). A parity test locks the new option list so the retraction cannot silently drift back.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.1.0.md:37:The convergence reference described the shared stop expected behavior as a nested `legalStop` record - a single blob wrapping the blocker list, the per-gate results, and a replay snapshot. The shipped workflow never wrote that shape. What it actually writes on a blocked stop is a top-level `blocked_stop` event with a flat gate-results map and a recovery-strategy hint. Anyone building tooling on top of the documented shape would get zero matches. The reference also mis-labelled the third signal in the three-signal convergence vote as a "Novelty Ratio" - the actual signal is dimension coverage (every configured review dimension reached by at least one iteration, held stable for one extra pass). The shared-stop-expected behavior section now shows the real `blocked_stop` event shape verbatim, lists the six gate names the workflow actually emits, and includes a worked example with non-trivial values. The composite-convergence section corrects the third signal back to dimension coverage, renames the subsection accordingly, and clarifies that the severity-weighted new-findings ratio feeds the rolling-average and noise-floor signals rather than casting its own stop vote.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.1.0.md:43:The reducer-owned "Active Risks" section at the bottom of every review dashboard was built to surface release-blocking debt. The check it actually ran was "is there a P0?" - nothing else. If a session had active P1 findings, or a failed claim-adjudication gate, or a blocked-stop event the legal-stop tree was rejecting, the dashboard still reported "None active beyond normal review uncertainty." Operators scanning the dashboard before clicking "synthesize" had no visual signal that the session was still carrying required work. The Active Risks section now walks the state log and surfaces every kind of non-zero debt: latest iteration error, active P0 count, active P1 count, the most recent claim-adjudication failure with the list of findings that are missing packets, the most recent blocked-stop event with its blocker list and recovery hint, and (only when nothing higher-severity is present) the P2 tech-debt count. A dashboard that says "None active" now means the session is genuinely clean instead of just "has no P0."
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.1.0.md:55:The session-isolation test gained a "shared-ID collisions" block that upserts the same logical id from two sessions and asserts both rows survive. The deep-review reducer-schema parity test was updated to match the new "Preserve machine-owned markers" prose after the completed-continue snapshot wording was retracted. No tests were removed.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.1.0.md:80:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md` | Step 4a mirrors typed packet + blocked-stop emission, Lifecycle Branches table retracted |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.1.0.md:81:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence.md` | Stop expected behavior rewritten to match persisted `blocked_stop` event, Signal 3 corrected to Dimension Coverage |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.1.0.md:82:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Active Risks section widened to surface P1, claim-adjudication, and blocked-stop debt |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.1.0.md:105:| `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts` | Added "shared-ID collisions" regression block |

.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/01-severity-classification.md:12:The severity system converts raw review observations into blocker, required, or suggestion findings. Those levels control convergence math, verdict routing, and whether a finding can block release.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/01-severity-classification.md:16:The live contract uses three severity levels with fixed weights: `P0 = 10.0`, `P1 = 5.0`, `P2 = 1.0`. All severities require concrete file-line evidence. The loop uses those weights to compute `newFindingsRatio`, including half-weight refinements, and applies the P0 override so any newly discovered blocker raises the ratio to at least `0.50`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/01-severity-classification.md:28:| `SKILL.md` | Skill contract | Summarizes P0, P1, and P2 criteria and blocking behavior. |

.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:2:title: "DRV-061 -- candidateCoverageGate STOP blocker"
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:3:description: "Verify the legal-stop decision tree blocks STOP with named gate candidateCoverageGate when required bug classes remain uncovered."
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:6:# DRV-061 -- candidateCoverageGate STOP blocker
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:12:Exercise the `candidateCoverageGate` added to the legal-stop decision tree. A standard or complex v2 review with unresolved `searchDebt` (or `searchCoverage.requiredBugClasses` that are not in `covered[]`) must NOT be allowed to STOP. The blocked_stop event must name `candidateCoverageGate` and surface the remaining debt as evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:20:- Objective: Confirm legal-stop decision tree emits `blocked_stop` with `candidateCoverageGate` in `blocked_gates[]` when v2 search debt remains for non-trivial scope.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:22:- Real user request: `Run a standard-scope v2 review iteration with one required bug class uncovered and confirm STOP is blocked by candidateCoverageGate.`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:23:- Expected signals: blocked_stop event with `blocked_gates[]` containing `candidateCoverageGate`. Recovery_strategy mentions resolving deferred/blocked obligations. Reducer `searchDebt` non-empty.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:24:- Pass/fail: PASS if `blocked_gates[]` contains `candidateCoverageGate` AND `searchDebt` is non-empty. FAIL if STOP succeeds OR gate is absent from the blocker payload.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:37:2. Leave that required class out of `covered[]`, `ruledOut[]`, `deferred[]`, and `blocked[]` so `candidateCoverage` remains incomplete.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:39:4. Inspect the blocked_stop output for `blocked_gates[]` containing `candidateCoverageGate`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:40:5. Confirm the blocked output includes the remaining `searchDebt`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:45:The review cannot legally stop. The blocked_stop output names `candidateCoverageGate` in `blocked_gates[]`, and the evidence points to incomplete `candidateCoverage` plus remaining `searchDebt`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:50:- The blocked output is generic: inspect whether `candidateCoverageGate` was dropped before the final gate payload.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-candidate-coverage.md:66:- Expected verdict mode: GREEN (blocked_stop emitted)
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:1:This release hardens `deep-review` into a fully explicit review-packet expected behavior. The skill now has main `deep-review-*` naming across all surfaces, an unambiguous lifecycle and reducer model, synchronized runtime mirrors, and focused Vitest guards that keep the expected behavior from drifting. The goal is to make the workflow easier to follow and easier to trust.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:9:- Release-readiness states are now treated as first-class expected behavior terms instead of scattered prose.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:17:- The auto and confirm workflow assets now both encode migration logic, reducer refresh behavior, synthesis boundaries, and release-readiness propagation.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:33:- Added `deep-review-reducer-schema.vitest.ts` to lock reducer inputs, outputs, severity weights, and release-readiness schemas.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/graph-vocabulary.md:52:- The YAML event filter drops the event upstream: inspect `deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml` event-filter normalization block.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/graph-vocabulary.md:58:- YAML event filter: `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml` (event-normalization block).
.opencode/skills/deep-research/assets/deep_research_strategy.md:70:- Why blocked: [root cause of exhaustion]
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/07-security-sensitive-fix-overrides.md:16:This is a SPEC-ONLY contract. As of the current release, the runtime does NOT auto-detect security sensitivity and does NOT apply these overrides. `requiredClosedFindingReplay` and `requiredFixCompletenessGate` exist in no config, YAML, or reducer surface (verified by grep). Operators running a security-sensitive review must manually tighten thresholds via `--convergence` and `--max-iterations` and manually maintain the closed-gate replay table.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/07-security-sensitive-fix-overrides.md:35:| `manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/review-quality-guards-block-premature-stop.md` | Manual scenario | Covers the quality-guard layer the override contract would tighten. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:1:This release lands Phase 008 of the 042 Deep Research & Review Runtime Improvement Bundle. `deep-review` now actively utilizes the coverage graph on the live review loop, persists blocked-stop bundles through reducer-owned surfaces, fails loud (instead of silent) on malformed JSONL and missing machine-owned anchors, and splits `repeatedFindings` into semantically distinct `persistentSameSeverity` + `severityChanged` buckets for precise triage. The goal is to make the workflow easier to follow and easier to trust.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:7:- `step_emit_blocked_stop` wires a first-class `blocked_stop` JSONL event into both review workflows with review-specific gate names: `convergenceGate`, `dimensionCoverageGate`, `p0ResolutionGate`, `evidenceDensityGate`, `hotspotSaturationGate`. Each event carries the full `blockedBy` / `gateResults` / `recoveryStrategy` payload.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:9:- The reducer no longer drops non-iteration events. the blocked stop history builder scans `blocked_stop` rows (with backward-compat for the legacy nested `legalStop` payload shape) and exposes `blockedStopHistory` as a registry field.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:15:- `deep_start-review-loop_{auto,confirm}.yaml` now call `deep_loop_graph_upsert` after `step_reduce_review_state` and `deep_loop_graph_convergence` before the inline review convergence vote in `step_check_convergence`. Combined stop rule: STOP requires BOTH inline vote and graph decision to agree. `STOP_BLOCKED` routes to the blocked-stop emission path.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:16:- `buildGraphConvergenceRollup(records)` scans `graph_convergence` JSONL events and exposes `graphConvergenceScore`, `graphDecision`, `graphBlockers` as new registry fields alongside `convergenceScore`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:25:- the replace anchor section helper now throws `Error('Missing machine-owned anchor "<id>" in deep-review strategy file...')` when a required anchor is absent. Pass `--create-missing-anchors` (or `createMissingAnchors:true`) to bootstrap missing anchor blocks from scratch.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:42:- Strategy `next-focus` anchor prefers the latest blocked-stop `recoveryStrategy` when blocked-stop is the most recent loop event - operators see the blocker before choosing next-iteration direction.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:48:- New fixture: `scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/` - a 3-iteration review packet with a full legal-stop blocked bundle, including one severity upgrade (F002: P2 to P1) and two persistent same-severity findings (F001 active P0, F003 active P1). The pre-generated dashboard shows the BLOCKED STOPS section rendering iteration 3 with full gate summary.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:51:- New playbook scenarios under `manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/`: `blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md` and `023-stop with an error-reducer.md`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:58:- Reducer smoke test on blocked-stop-session fixture: `blockedStopHistory` populated with 1 entry, `severityChanged` with 1 (F002), `persistentSameSeverity` with 2 (F001 + F003), dashboard BLOCKED STOPS section renders iteration 3 with full gate summary.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:70:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md` | Modified | Legal-stop → blocked_stop emission contract |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:71:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Modified | parseJsonlDetailed + fail-closed JSONL + fail-closed anchors + blockedStopHistory + graphConvergenceScore + persistentSameSeverity + severityChanged + dashboard sections |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:72:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/*` | Created | Durable fixture with full blocked_stop bundle, severity upgrade, persistent findings |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:73:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md` | Created | Manual test for blocked-stop surfacing |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:75:| `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml` | Modified | Wired step_emit_blocked_stop + step_normalize_pause_events + step_graph_upsert + step_graph_convergence |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:89:- Missing machine-owned strategy anchors → the v1.3.0.0 reducer will throw. Pass `--create-missing-anchors` (or create the missing anchor blocks manually) to bootstrap.

.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.7.0.0.md:1:This release adds a feature catalog for `deep-research` so you can see what the loop actually does without reading source code. The catalog lists 14 features grouped into 4 areas and points to the code file for each one.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.7.0.0.md:11:Before this release, the only way to see what the research loop did was to read `SKILL.md` or the source files. No map, no per-feature breakdown, no pointer from behavior back to code.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:13:Note: `Task` is allowed for the command executor that manages the loop. The `@deep-research` agent itself is LEAF-only and does not dispatch sub-agents.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:129:    "RECOVERY": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["stuck", "recovery", "timeout", "reconstruct", "blocked stop", "blocked_stop"]},
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:131:    "SPEC_ANCHORING": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["spec.md", "generated fence", "folder_state", "lock", "spec anchoring"]},
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:281:The YAML workflow owns executor selection. Native `@deep-research` is the default path; CLI executors are routed through the workflow, not through ad hoc shell loops.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:283:Cross-CLI delegation is possible inside each executor sandbox but discouraged. Do not invoke the same CLI from within itself, and do not assume auth from a parent executor propagates to child CLIs.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:344:State files include `deep-research-config.json`, `deep-research-state.jsonl`, `deep-research-strategy.md`, `deep-research-findings-registry.json`, `deep-research-dashboard.md`, `.deep-research-pause`, `.deep-research.lock`, `resource-map.md`, `research.md`, and `iterations/iteration-NNN.md`.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:358:Late-INIT can also anchor the research run to `spec.md`: the workflow acquires the advisory lock, classifies `folder_state`, seeds or appends bounded context before LOOP, and then syncs one generated findings block back into `spec.md` during SYNTHESIS while keeping `research/research.md` canonical.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:362:- the advisory lock lives at `research/.deep-research.lock` from late INIT through save, skip-save, or cancel cleanup
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:403:9. **Simulate loop dispatch** -- Do not write custom shell loops, nested CLI loops, `/tmp` prompt dispatchers, or direct Task loops for `@deep-research`. **Command-driven fan-out via `step_fanout_spawn` in the YAML is SUPPORTED** — use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on the command; ad-hoc shell fan-out and intra-lineage wave orchestration remain forbidden.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:415:1. **Wave orchestration** -- parallel question fan-out and pruning within a single lineage remains reference-only (intra-lineage wave)
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:417:3. **Multi-lineage fan-out** -- SUPPORTED via `--executor`/`--executors` flags on the command (see §8 EXAMPLES). Each lineage is an independent full loop in `{artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/`, converging independently. This is not "wave orchestration"; it is N independent loops.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:418:4. **Alternate CLI dispatch** -- process-isolated `claude -p` or similar dispatch modes are used internally by `fanout-run.cjs`; do not write them ad-hoc from within a research session
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:455:Blocking gates: valid config/strategy/state before loop, iteration markdown plus JSONL plus reducer refresh per iteration, final `research/research.md` and convergence report after loop, and quality guards for source diversity/focus/no weak single source. Continuity save is expected but non-blocking.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md:500:Related skills: `deep-review` for iterative audit loops, `deep-loop-runtime` for shared executor/state/coverage-graph runtime, and `system-spec-kit` for command-owned state, packet anchoring, and continuity saves.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/003-per-lineage-spawn-isolation/spec.md:2:title: "Phase 003: Per-lineage spawn + sub-packet isolation"

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/003-per-lineage-spawn-isolation/spec.md:32:- Recursion-guard check: distinct kinds safe; same-kind replicas verify loop-lock/ancestry (`SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR`).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/003-per-lineage-spawn-isolation/spec.md:35:- Integration: pool spawns a stub command twice into two `lineages/{label}/` trees; distinct trees + distinct sessionIds + no lock contention.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/003-per-lineage-spawn-isolation/spec.md:38:Salvage (004); merge (005).
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/04--research-output/02-negative-knowledge.md:20:The reducer and synthesis phases keep that information alive. `reduce-state.cjs` promotes ruled-out directions into the strategy and findings registry so future iterations can avoid retrying blocked paths. Final synthesis then consolidates those eliminated paths into the required `Eliminated Alternatives` table inside `research/research.md`, which turns negative knowledge into a durable research output.

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml:511:          - blocked_by_csv: "Comma-separated graph blocker names when decision == BLOCKED"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml:514:          - gate_key_question_coverage_pass: "Boolean pass/fail derived from graph blocker coverage signals"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml:515:          - gate_evidence_density_pass: "Boolean pass/fail derived from graph blocker evidence-depth signals"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml:517:          - gate_hotspot_saturation_pass: "Research placeholder gate; true unless an explicit blocker sets otherwise"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml:520:      step_emit_blocked_stop:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml:521:        action: "Persist a first-class blocked_stop event when the legal-stop decision tree returns blocked"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml:522:        if_blocked:

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml:524:          log: "Graph convergence blocked STOP. Failed blockers: {blocked_by_csv}. Recovery strategy: {recovery_strategy}"

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml:528:            stop_blocked: false
