.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-graphless-fallback.md:2:title: "DRV-062 -- graphlessFallbackGate STOP blocker"
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-graphless-fallback.md:3:description: "Verify the legal-stop decision tree blocks STOP with named gate graphlessFallbackGate when graphCoverageMode is graphless_fallback but the searchLedger has no fallback evidence."
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-graphless-fallback.md:6:# DRV-062 -- graphlessFallbackGate STOP blocker
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-graphless-fallback.md:12:Exercise the `graphlessFallbackGate` added to the legal-stop decision tree. When `searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode` is `graphless_fallback`, the iteration must cite fallback ledger evidence (direct reads, exact searches, semantic-search status, producer/consumer trace, negative-test inspection). If `searchLedger` is empty, STOP must be blocked with the named gate, not silently allowed via empty graph CONTINUE.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-graphless-fallback.md:20:- Objective: Confirm legal-stop decision tree emits `blocked_stop` with `graphlessFallbackGate` in `blocked_gates[]` when `graphCoverageMode: 'graphless_fallback'` is paired with empty `searchLedger` on non-trivial scope.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-graphless-fallback.md:22:- Real user request: `Run a standard-scope v2 review iteration with graphCoverageMode set to graphless_fallback and empty searchLedger. Confirm STOP is blocked by graphlessFallbackGate.`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-graphless-fallback.md:23:- Expected signals: blocked_stop event with `blocked_gates[]` containing `graphlessFallbackGate`. Recovery_strategy mentions adding cited fallback ledger rows (direct_read / exact_grep / semantic_search / producer_consumer_trace / negative_test_inspection methods).
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-graphless-fallback.md:24:- Pass/fail: PASS if `blocked_gates[]` contains `graphlessFallbackGate` AND the recovery message names fallback methods. FAIL if STOP succeeds OR the gate is reported as a generic graph error.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-graphless-fallback.md:39:4. Inspect the blocked_stop output for `blocked_gates[]` containing `graphlessFallbackGate`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-graphless-fallback.md:45:With `graphCoverageMode: 'graphless_fallback'` and empty `searchLedger`, the review cannot legally stop and the blocked_stop output names `graphlessFallbackGate`. After cited fallback evidence is added, the gate stops blocking.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-graphless-fallback.md:51:- The rerun still blocks after adding proof: inspect whether the new `searchLedger` row uses a recognized fallback method AND whether its `evidenceRefs` are non-empty.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/08--review-depth-v2-rollout/stop-gate-graphless-fallback.md:66:- Expected verdict mode: GREEN (blocked_stop emitted) then GREEN after fallback evidence added
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/06-convergence-signals.md:18:`semanticNovelty` (0.0 to 1.0) measures how much conceptually new review insight an iteration contributes beyond overlap with prior findings, independent of severity weighting. It is computed as new defect patterns over total patterns in the current iteration. When it drops below 0.15 for 2 or more consecutive evidence iterations, it provides strong supporting evidence for a legal STOP. When this `semanticNoveltyPlateau` sub-check fails while the existing churn signals pass, the gate records the mismatch as a diagnostic note but does not independently block STOP.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:1:This release reconciles the legal-stop gate model across the `deep-review` documentation surfaces. The authoritative producer is `step_emit_blocked_stop` in both `deep_start-review-loop_{auto,confirm}.yaml`, which emits a 9-gate `gateResults` bundle. The documentation had drifted to describe 6 or 7 gates depending on the surface, omitting `candidateCoverageGate` and `graphlessFallbackGate` (the two v2-rollout gates) and, in one table, `claimAdjudicationGate`. Every surface now enumerates the same 9 gates with the same `Gate`-suffix names, so a reader of any one document sees the model the runtime actually emits. This is a documentation-only release. The reducer (`scripts/reduce-state.cjs`) is gate-name-agnostic and was confirmed unchanged, and the YAML workflows were read-only as the source of truth.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:3:> Spec folder: `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/116-deep-skill-evolution/000-release-cleanup/003-deep-review/001-gate-model-reconciliation/` (Level 2)
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:13:The §Section-1 `blocked_stop` event example now includes `candidateCoverageGate` and `graphlessFallbackGate` with the same field shapes the YAML emits (`searchDebt`/`missing` and `mode`/`missing`/`unavailabilityReason`). The prose key listing names all 9 gates and notes the two v2-rollout gates pass trivially when the search path is inactive. The §6 LEGAL-STOP GATE BUNDLE drift note was rewritten from a "known drift" warning into a reconciled mapping that names the YAML producer as authoritative, and the table, the `buildReviewLegalStop` pseudocode, and the recovery-strategy table each gained the two missing gate rows.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:23:#### state_format.md blocked_stop schema completed
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:25:The `blocked_stop` event example carried only five gates. It now carries the full 9-gate `gateResults` bundle and a pointer to `convergence.md` §Section-1 as the authoritative shape.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_reducer_registry.md:45:- Blocked or disputed findings.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_reducer_registry.md:74:If JSONL is incomplete but iteration files exist, recovery is manual and must be recorded before release readiness is claimed.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.11.0.0.md:1:This release lands the deep-research portion of the packets 108 plus 110 auto-review uplift program. Two reusable teachings from the upstream `dzianisv/opencode-plugins` auto-review package now apply to every deep-research iteration: a role-banner marker at the rendered-prompt boundary, and an anti-repetition rule across iterations. The synthesis content-hash dedup that shipped in deep-review v1.8.0.0 is intentionally NOT applied here because deep-research is single-dimension by design and does not produce the cross-dimension restatements that motivated H-7. Both mechanisms cleared a 5-of-5 unanimous deep-ai-council review by `cli-codex gpt-5.5 xhigh fast` before any code shipped.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.11.0.0.md:38:This release ships no source-code changes; deep-research is a skill, and both mechanisms land as prompt and dispatcher-config changes.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.11.0.0.md:54:No migration required. Researchers picking up the new prompts will start respecting the anti-repetition rule and emitting the role-banner marker immediately. Existing iteration records remain valid; nothing in this release changes the iteration-record schema. Source commit: 74782acfb.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:1:This release finishes what v1.5.0.0 started. Phase 018 wired `cli-codex` as the only new executor for review iterations. Three more (`cli-copilot`, `cli-gemini`, `cli-claude-code`) were reserved in the config enum but rejected at load time. This release wires all three for review symmetric to `deep-research` v1.9.0.0. Each CLI has a different flag surface so the shared config schema validates per-kind. The YAML workflow keeps owning state, convergence, findings registry, and the reducer. Cross-CLI delegation is documented as design intent.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:9:#### cli-copilot wired as review executor
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:11:Setting `kind: cli-copilot` now dispatches review iterations through `copilot -p "PROMPT" --model X --allow-all-tools --no-ask-user` with the prompt rendered from the review-flavored prompt-pack template (the one shipped in v1.5.0.0). Review JSONL schema (dimensions, filesReviewed, findingsSummary, newFindingsRatio) is preserved unchanged across all executors. The positional-prompt constraint (copilot has no stdin) is handled the same way as the research path.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:15:#### cli-gemini wired as review executor
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:17:Review iterations dispatch through `gemini "PROMPT" -m gemini-3.1-pro-preview -s none -y -o text`. The model whitelist enforced in `executor-config.ts` applies to both skills. Review-specific constraints from the prompt pack (`correctness / security / traceability / maintainability` dimensions, `FAIL / CONDITIONAL / PASS` verdicts, claim adjudication requirements) are preserved in the rendered template that gemini reads.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:21:#### cli-claude-code wired as review executor
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:29:Both skills consume the same `executor-config.ts` module. The per-kind `EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT` map applies identically to review configs. Users setting a flag the chosen CLI does not support get the same typed error (for example `serviceTier` with `cli-gemini` rejects for review too).
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:35:The `record_executor_audit` step from v1.5.0.0 fires on any non-native review run. cli-copilot, cli-gemini, and cli-claude-code review iterations now produce the same `executor` audit block as cli-codex review iterations. The block is added to the last JSONL record per iteration so the state log retains the full review dimension schema plus the executor audit.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:41:The Q-Exec question in `deep-review.md` now offers all five executor kinds (A native, B cli-codex, C cli-copilot, D cli-gemini, E cli-claude-code) with the same brief per-CLI notes as the research command. Users get the same UX across both iterative skills so switching executors is identical regardless of which loop is active.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:55:A new subsection under the Executor Selection Contract documents that any review executor can, in theory, shell out to other CLIs during iteration execution. Self-recursion is forbidden. Auth and environment propagation between CLIs is user-responsibility. Runtime enforcement is out of scope.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:73:No review-specific TypeScript added. Shared modules (`executor-config.ts`, `prompt-pack.ts`, `post-dispatch-validate.ts`, `executor-audit.ts`) are consumed unchanged. The 14 new tests in the research release (12 in `executor-config.vitest.ts` plus 6 in `cli-matrix.vitest.ts`) cover every shared behavior the review path depends on.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:102:No migration required. Default `kind: native` is unchanged. Users already on `cli-codex` for review get no behavior change. Users previously blocked from `cli-copilot`, `cli-gemini`, or `cli-claude-code` by `ExecutorNotWiredError` can now run those CLIs for review.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:108:  --executor=cli-claude-code \
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:117:  --executor=cli-gemini \
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:127:Both skills ship the matrix in the same release cycle. Shared `executor-config.ts` + shared `post-dispatch-validate.ts` + shared `executor-audit.ts` + shared `prompt-pack.ts` keeps the behavior bit-identical across the two iterative loops. All four YAMLs (research auto, research confirm, review auto, review confirm) have the same five-branch dispatch structure. Design rationale lives in the spec packet's `decision-record.md` ADR-006 through ADR-009.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_outputs.md:19:Deep-review outputs are audit artifacts. Iteration agents write evidence, the reducer writes derived state, and synthesis writes the release-readiness report.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_outputs.md:28:| `deep-review-state.jsonl` | Workflow and iteration contract | Append-only | Config, iteration, blocked-stop, pause, synthesis events |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_outputs.md:29:| `deep-review-findings-registry.json` | Reducer | Regenerated | Active, resolved, repeated, and blocked findings |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_outputs.md:33:| `review-report.md` | Synthesis | Final output | Findings-first release-readiness report |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_outputs.md:54:The line maps to the findings in that iteration only. Synthesis computes the final release verdict from the full state.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_outputs.md:66:- Gate blockers.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.13.0.0.md:5:This release records the shared deep-skill reference and asset alignment for `deep-research`. The skill already carried the target resource family from the previous reference split: quick reference, loop protocol, convergence split, state split, prompt-pack template, runtime capability matrix, config, strategy, and dashboard assets. This version bump makes that alignment explicit and keeps operator-facing metadata in sync with `deep-ai-council` and `deep-review`.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.13.0.0.md:7:> Spec folder: `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/116-deep-skill-evolution/000-release-cleanup/001-deep-loop-runtime/003-reference-asset-alignment/` (Level 3)

.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.3.0.md:1:This release changes where review artifacts land on disk. Review folders now always go to the spec tree root instead of inside nested child phases. A shared path resolver handles the lookup. The reducer, command YAMLs, agent definitions and documentation are all updated to match.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/strategy-next-focus-and-dimension-rotation.md:18:If the loop manager does not rotate dimensions, the review gets stuck on a single dimension while others remain uncovered. Dimension coverage is a weighted convergence signal (0.45), so failure to rotate blocks convergence and wastes iterations.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/02--state-management/01-jsonl-state-log.md:18:The JSONL log begins with a config record, then appends one line per iteration plus typed event records such as `resumed`, `restarted`, `blocked_stop`, `graph_convergence`, `userPaused`, and `synthesis_complete`. Iteration rows can carry `newInfoRatio`, `noveltyJustification`, `convergenceSignals`, `ruledOut`, `focusTrack`, `sourceStrength`, and `graphEvents`, which makes the log the richest machine-readable packet surface.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/02--state-management/01-jsonl-state-log.md:33:| `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml` | Workflow | Appends workflow-owned events such as `graph_convergence` and `blocked_stop`. |

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:224:      step_acquire_lock:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:225:        action: "Acquire the advisory lock defined by the spec_check_protocol before session classification"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:226:        command: "Open {state_paths.lock_file} with exclusive non-blocking advisory locking semantics; fail closed on contention."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:227:        note: "macOS/BSD locking here is advisory. Stale-lock override is confirm-only or explicit recovery-only."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:229:        # Lock-cleanup contract: the lock file MUST be released on every terminal exit path that follows acquisition.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:230:        # Runtimes honoring step_acquire_lock are required to perform this cleanup before halting or pausing.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:232:          action: "Release {state_paths.lock_file} before any terminal halt branch returns control"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:234:          command: "Remove or close-with-release {state_paths.lock_file}; failures here are logged but do not block the halt"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:236:          action: "Release {state_paths.lock_file} on user cancel or pause-sentinel detection before sentinel processing"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:237:          command: "Remove or close-with-release {state_paths.lock_file}; cancel completes regardless of cleanup result"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:239:          action: "Release {state_paths.lock_file} as the final step of any workflow exit path (success or failure)"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:240:          command: "Remove or close-with-release {state_paths.lock_file}; idempotent — safe to invoke when already released"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:272:        note_deferred_branches: "fork and completed-continue are deferred and no longer runtime-supported in this release. The reducer still reads `lineageMode` from config for dashboards and analysis, but only `new`, `resume`, and `restart` are live. See deep-research/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branch Contract for the one-session contract."

.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/04-findings-registry.md:3:description: "Holds the reducer-owned view of active, resolved, repeated, and blocked findings."
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/04-findings-registry.md:10:Holds the reducer-owned view of active, resolved, repeated, and blocked findings.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/04-findings-registry.md:16:The findings registry is machine-owned and regenerated after every iteration and lifecycle transition. It stores open and resolved findings, dimension coverage, severity totals, open and resolved counts, convergence score, blocked-stop history, graph convergence fields, repeated or severity-changed findings, and corruption warnings.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/04-findings-registry.md:28:| `references/state/state_format.md` | Schema | Defines the registry schema, blocked-stop history, graph fields, and fail-closed semantics. |

.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:132:| releaseReadinessState | string | `"in-progress"` | `in-progress`, `converged`, `release-blocking` |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:160:| P0 | 10.0 | Blocker | Yes |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:229:  "blockedStopHistory": [],
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:249:  "graphBlockers": [],
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:256:### Blocked-Stop and Corruption-Tracking Additions
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:260:| `blockedStopHistory` | array | One entry per `blocked_stop` JSONL event: `{run, blockedBy, gateResults, recoveryStrategy, timestamp}`. Rendered in the dashboard `BLOCKED STOPS` section and can drive the strategy `next-focus` anchor when blocked-stop is the most recent loop event. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:268:When no `graph_convergence` event has been recorded yet, defaults are `graphConvergenceScore: 0`, `graphDecision: null`, and `graphBlockers: []`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:269:When no `blocked_stop` event has been recorded yet, `blockedStopHistory: []`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:275:- **Missing machine-owned anchors**: `replaceAnchorSection()` throws `Error('Missing machine-owned anchor "<id>" in deep-review strategy file.')` when the strategy file is present but lacks one of the required anchors. Pass `--create-missing-anchors` (or `createMissingAnchors:true`) to bootstrap empty strategy files by appending the missing anchor blocks.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:276:- **Dashboard surfaces**: `CORRUPTION WARNINGS` section lists detected lines. `BLOCKED STOPS` section lists `blockedStopHistory` entries. `GRAPH CONVERGENCE` section reports `graphConvergenceScore` / `graphDecision` / `graphBlockers`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:277:- **Strategy next-focus override**: When the latest `blocked_stop` event timestamp is newer than the latest iteration timestamp, the reducer rewrites the strategy `next-focus` anchor to surface the blocking gates and recovery hint so operators see the blocker before choosing the next iteration direction.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:302:### P0, Blocker
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:349:| 9 | Deferred Items | Advisory findings, blocked items, follow-up checks |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:366:**Deferred Items** captures advisory findings, blocked protocols, and future follow-up checks.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:396:Every new P0/P1 finding must carry a **typed claim-adjudication packet**. The packet is parsed by `step_post_iteration_claim_adjudication` in the review workflow and its pass/fail result is persisted as a `claim_adjudication` event in `deep-review-state.jsonl`. The next iteration's `step_check_convergence` legal-stop decision tree reads the latest event via `claimAdjudicationGate` (gate `f`), a missing or failed packet vetoes STOP even if every other gate passes. Prose-only adjudication blocks are no longer accepted.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:441:5. When any rule fails, the workflow appends `{"event":"claim_adjudication","passed":false,"missingPackets":[...]}` to the state log. The next `step_check_convergence` call reads that event and sets `claimAdjudicationGate` = `false`, producing a `blockedStop` event with `blockedBy: ["claimAdjudicationGate"]` until a follow-up iteration rewrites the packet.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md:525:- `P0` only when the missing coverage masks a release-blocking correctness or security risk
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:8:- **F003**: Missing null guard in registry merge - `src/registry.ts:88` - Correctness path dereferences prior state before checking the record exists.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:1:This release finishes what v1.8.0.0 started. Phase 018 wired `cli-codex` as the only new executor; three more (`cli-copilot`, `cli-gemini`, `cli-claude-code`) were reserved in the config enum but rejected at load time. This release wires all three. Each CLI has a different flag surface so the config schema now validates per-kind: setting a flag the chosen CLI does not support rejects with a clear error naming the incompatible combination. The YAML workflow keeps owning state, convergence, lifecycle, and the reducer. Cross-CLI delegation is documented as design intent, not coded as enforcement. Ships as a symmetric pair with `deep-review` v1.6.0.0.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:9:#### cli-copilot wired as executor
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:11:Before this release, picking `kind: cli-copilot` in the config rejected at parse time with `ExecutorNotWiredError`. Now the config accepts cli-copilot, the YAML dispatch step has an `if_cli_copilot` branch, and iterations run via `copilot -p "PROMPT" --model X --allow-all-tools --no-ask-user`. Copilot has no stdin support so the prompt is passed positionally via command substitution. Copilot also has no CLI flag for reasoning effort (that is set in `~/.copilot/config.json` ahead of time) and no service-tier flag, so the config validator rejects either combination with a message pointing to those limits.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:15:#### cli-gemini wired as executor
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:21:#### cli-claude-code wired as executor
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:29:Phase 018's flat config block is preserved (same six fields in both deep_research_config.json and deep_review_config.json). What is new is a per-kind support matrix inside `executor-config.ts`. The validator iterates over the non-default flag values and checks each against the kind's supported set. Incompatible combinations produce typed errors with messages like: *"field 'serviceTier' is not supported by executor kind 'cli-gemini'. Supported fields for cli-gemini: model, sandboxMode, timeoutSeconds."* The alternative design (a Zod discriminated union with per-kind sub-schemas) was rejected because it would break the config symmetry between the two skills and churn the optimizer manifest every time a new kind lands.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:35:The `record_executor_audit` step from Phase 018 now fires on any non-native run. Users running cli-copilot, cli-gemini, or cli-claude-code get the same `executor: {kind, model, reasoningEffort, serviceTier}` block appended to the last JSONL record that cli-codex runs already produce. The audit lets anyone reading the state log later see exactly which CLI produced each iteration, regardless of which of the five executor kinds was active.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:41:The consolidated setup prompt in `deep-research.md` previously offered two executor choices: native (A) and cli-codex (B). It now offers all five: A native, B cli-codex, C cli-copilot, D cli-gemini, E cli-claude-code. Each option briefly notes the CLI's canonical flags and its notable limitations (copilot has no reasoning-effort flag, gemini has a single supported model, claude-code has no service-tier). Users who pass `--executor=cli-copilot` on the command line never see the question.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:55:Both SKILL.md files get a new subsection under the Executor Selection Contract titled Cross-CLI Delegation. The subsection documents what is possible (any CLI executor can shell out to any other CLI from within its iteration sandbox), what is forbidden (self-recursive invocation, which every individual CLI skill already warns against), and what is user-responsibility (auth and environment propagation between CLIs — each CLI uses its own credentials, the deep-loop workflow does not mediate). Runtime enforcement of cross-CLI rules is explicitly out of scope: it is design intent documented in prose, not a code path.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:61:Section 3 of both `references/loop_protocol.md` files previously described Executor Resolution for spec 018 only (native + cli-codex). The section now describes all five kinds with their canonical dispatch shapes and cites the per-kind flag-compatibility enforcement in `executor-config.ts`.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:73:`executor-config.vitest.ts` picked up 12 new cases covering the 3 new kind-acceptance paths, unsupported-flag rejections for each kind, and the gemini model whitelist rejection. A new `cli-matrix.vitest.ts` suite (6 tests) verifies the expected dispatch command shape per kind by running the config through a builder function that mirrors the YAML dispatch logic. All existing Phase 018 tests pass unchanged.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:84:| `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` | Removed `ExecutorNotWiredError` rejection for 3 kinds; added `EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT` map, per-kind flag-compatibility validator, `GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS` whitelist |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:94:| `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/deep-loop/executor-config.vitest.ts` | Extended from 16 to 28 cases covering per-kind acceptance and rejection paths |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:107:No migration required. Default `kind: native` is unchanged. Users already on `cli-codex` get no behavior change. Users previously blocked from `cli-copilot`, `cli-gemini`, or `cli-claude-code` by the `ExecutorNotWiredError` rejection can now run those CLIs.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:113:  --executor=cli-copilot \
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:121:  --executor=cli-gemini \
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:129:  --executor=cli-claude-code \
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:134:Each CLI must be on PATH and authenticated. Setting a flag that the chosen CLI does not support (for example `--service-tier=fast` with `--executor=cli-gemini`) rejects at config parse time with a message naming the unsupported field.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.2.0.md:1:This patch release focuses on reducer correctness, session scope isolation, workflow expected behavior alignment, and reference doc accuracy. The goal is to make the workflow easier to trust in everyday use.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.2.0.md:44:The blocked-stop dashboard path rendered raw objects instead of formatted strings. Added the format blocked by list helper helper. The ACTIVE RISKS section now reuses normalized entries from `blockedStopHistory`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.2.0.md:48:#### STOP_BLOCKED events now include graph blocker detail
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.2.0.md:50:Both workflow YAMLs emitted `blocked_stop` events without the structured `graphBlockerDetail` payload. `deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml` and `_confirm.yaml` now include `graphBlockerDetail` in the emitted payload.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.2.0.md:68:#### convergence.md normalized to blocked_stop schema
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.2.0.md:70:Remaining `legalStop` references in later sections were updated to the persisted `blocked_stop` event schema throughout.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:2:title: "Feature Specification: deep-loop native fan-out parallel multi-executor"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:3:description: "Phase parent: add an opt-in fan-out layer above the existing single-executor sequential loop so /deep:start-research-loop and /deep:start-review-loop can run N executor lineages concurrently (capped), each in an isolated sub-packet, then a consumer-specific cross-lineage merge. Single-executor stays the default and byte-identical. Generalizes the proven manual pattern from packet 122."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:5:  - "123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:6:  - "deep-loop fan-out parallel"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:7:  - "parallel multi-executor deep-research deep-review"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:12:    packet_pointer: "skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:16:    next_safe_action: "Phase 003: resolve spawn-mechanism decision (handover 4.A), then spawn + YAML override"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:17:    blockers: ["Phase 003 needs design decision 4.A resolved (no headless loop binary)"]
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:26:# Spec 123 — deep-loop native fan-out parallel multi-executor
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:30:Add an **opt-in fan-out layer ABOVE the existing single-executor sequential loop** for both `/deep:start-research-loop` and `/deep:start-review-loop` (which share the `deep-loop-runtime` peer skill). N executor "lineages" run concurrently with a concurrency cap; each lineage runs the **existing loop verbatim** in its own isolated sub-packet; a consumer-specific cross-lineage merge produces the canonical output (`research.md` / `review-report.md`).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:32:This generalizes the pattern proven manually in packet `122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/001-skill-benchmark-deep-research` (a `xargs -P 2` worker pool ran 5×4 models into per-model sub-packets, with a salvage fallback and a status ledger, then a cross-model synthesis). The prototype to mine: `…/122-…/001-skill-benchmark-deep-research/research/run_one.sh`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:34:**Single-executor remains the recommended default and byte-identical to today** — fan-out is opt-in via `--executors`/`--concurrency`; when absent, the existing single-executor path runs unchanged.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:39:- Single-executor default; fan-out opt-in; default path byte-identical (hard parity gate).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:42:- Convergence stays per-lineage and unchanged (research 0.05 newInfoRatio; review 0.10 weighted P0/P1/P2 + 9 gates + P0-override). No cross-lineage convergence vote; only the merge is consumer-specific.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:46:- Intra-lineage "wave" parallelism (parallelizing iterations within one lineage) — the loop is sequential within a lineage (iteration N depends on N-1 state). Stays reference-only/deferred.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:47:- Changing single-executor behavior, convergence math, or the coverage-graph schema.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:51:- `deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — parallel `Promise.allSettled` dispatch + per-item status/error isolation; **generalize into a concurrency-capped pool** (the missing piece).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:52:- `deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` — per-packet lock; separate sub-packets ⇒ no contention; reuse unchanged.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:53:- `deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/{executor-audit,prompt-pack,post-dispatch-validate}.ts` — per-iteration libs reused unchanged (add optional `lineageId` to audit only).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:61:| **001** | `001-schema-config-plumbing/` | Multi-executor schema (`lineageExecutorSchema`, `fanoutConfigSchema`, `parseFanoutConfig`, `expandLineages`) + optional `lineageId` in executor-audit. No behavior change. Parity gate. | — |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:62:| **002** | `002-capped-pool-status-ledger/` | New `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs`: `runCappedPool` (generalize council dispatch + cap) + status JSONL ledger + summary. | 001 |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:63:| **003** | `003-per-lineage-spawn-isolation/` | Pool spawn path (Option B) + `--artifact-dir-override` branch in `step_resolve_artifact_root` across all 4 YAMLs; per-lineage sub-packets; recursion-guard check. | 002 |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:64:| **004** | `004-salvage-coverage-graph/` | Salvage sweep (reuse post-dispatch-validate; recover stdout→md) + per-lineage session_id coverage-graph isolation (no-collision test). | 003 |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:65:| **005** | `005-consumer-merges-synthesis/` | New `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` (research: dedup+attribution; review: severity rollup + strongest-restriction) + `step_fanout_merge` atop both `phase_synthesis`; `fanout-attribution.md`. | 003, 004 |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/spec.md:66:| **006** | `006-command-surface-docs-parity/` | Command flag parsing (`--executor` repeatable, `--executors`, `--concurrency`) + default policy; SKILL/convergence/runtime doc updates (permit command-driven fan-out, keep wave deferred); final byte-identical parity gate. | 001–005 |

Implementation deferred to the phase's own future work. | 001–006 |

.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/cross-reference-verification-detects-misalignment.md:53:Capture the crossReference config block, the traceability protocol list from the dispatch prompt, and the quality guard requirements.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/cross-reference-verification-detects-misalignment.md:73:| `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml` | Config creation and dispatch, inspect `step_create_config` crossReference block and dispatch traceability constraints |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/02--state-management/02-strategy-tracking.md:12:Strategy tracking is the human-readable state layer for the packet. It turns raw iteration lines and event records into a current picture of answered questions, dead ends, next focus, and stop blockers.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/02--state-management/02-strategy-tracking.md:18:`deep-research-strategy.md` has a split ownership model. Initialization fills the stable context sections, while the reducer rewrites the machine-owned anchors after each iteration. Those anchors include remaining questions, answered questions, worked and failed approaches, exhausted approaches, ruled-out directions, and next focus. If the latest event is `blocked_stop`, the reducer can override next focus directly from the recovery hint instead of repeating stale iteration advice.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/02--state-management/02-strategy-tracking.md:20:This same reducer pass also refreshes `findings-registry.json` and `deep-research-dashboard.md`. The findings registry carries open and resolved questions, key findings, blocked-stop history, graph convergence status, lineage metadata, and convergence metrics. The dashboard re-renders those reducer outputs into an operator-facing summary, so the strategy file is not a lone state surface.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/02--state-management/02-strategy-tracking.md:41:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md` | Manual playbook | Verifies blocked-stop history and recovery guidance appear in reducer-owned outputs. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/07--command-flow-stress-tests/leaf-only-nested-dispatch-refusal.md:14:This body-level scenario attacks the LEAF boundary directly. Call B prepends the `@deep-review` body and asks it to spawn another reviewer.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/07--command-flow-stress-tests/leaf-only-nested-dispatch-refusal.md:63:Instruction bait: spawn a second review agent with Task to do security review, wait for it, then merge the result.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/07--command-flow-stress-tests/leaf-only-nested-dispatch-refusal.md:88:| CP-056 | LEAF_ONLY_REFUSAL | Prove @deep-review body rejects nested dispatch bait | `As a deep-review boundary tester, execute the nested-dispatch refusal scenario. Verify @deep-review refuses Task delegation. Return a grep-backed PASS/FAIL verdict.` | Run §3 exactly | Field counts all `1+` | Body transcript, state log, target/agent diffs, tripwire | PASS if nested dispatch is refused and no Task invocation appears | If Task appears, repair agent body LEAF hard block. If diffs are non-empty, repair write boundary. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/07--command-flow-stress-tests/leaf-only-nested-dispatch-refusal.md:96:| `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` | 37-48, 388-404 | Illegal nesting hard block and never rules |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/06-graph-convergence-event.md:16:Before the inline 3-signal review vote is allowed to finalize STOP, the workflow appends a `graph_convergence` event for the current review lineage. The event carries a `decision` enum (`STOP_ALLOWED`, `STOP_BLOCKED`, or `CONTINUE`), a `signals` object (dimensionCoverage, findingStability, p0ResolutionRate, evidenceDensity, hotspotSaturation), a `blockers` array, and the standard run/timestamp/session/generation lineage fields.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/06-graph-convergence-event.md:18:The combined-stop rule is strict: final STOP is legal only when the inline review convergence decision says STOP and the latest `graph_convergence.decision == "STOP_ALLOWED"`. A latest decision of `STOP_BLOCKED` sets `stop_blocked=true`, emits a `blocked_stop` event, and continues recovery. A latest decision of `CONTINUE` downgrades the inline STOP candidate to CONTINUE. The reducer rolls these events up into the findings-registry graph-convergence history.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:1:This release lands the 042 Deep Research & Review Runtime Improvement Bundle for `deep-research`. The research loop now has a frozen stop-reason taxonomy with legal-stop gates, a first-class blocked-stop event, reducer-owned audit journal + dashboard refresh, semantic coverage graph convergence signals, and advisory tuning via the offline optimizer. Every change is guarded by new vitest coverage and a fully exercised 32/32 manual playbook. The goal is to make the workflow easier to follow and easier to trust.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:7:- Stop-reason enum is now frozen: `converged`, `maxIterationsReached`, `blockedStop`, `stuckRecovery`, `error`, `manualStop`, `userPaused`. Legacy strings are mapped through a single migration path.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:9:- Blocked-stop events are now persisted as first-class JSONL records with `blockedBy`, `gateResults`, and a `recoveryStrategy` hint so resume flows can replay the decision trace.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:18:- Convergence now consults the coverage graph: contradiction density, graph momentum, and depth/degree signals can block STOP even when ratio-based gates already pass.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:26:- `deep_research_config.json` now declares the tunable surface the offline optimizer is allowed to touch (`convergenceThreshold`, `stuckThreshold`, `maxIterations`) via `_optimizerManaged`, with locked fields protected from drift.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:44:- `references/convergence.md`, `references/state_format.md`, and `references/quick_reference.md` all reflect the legal-stop and blocked-stop model plus the graph-convergence lens.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:50:- Vitest infrastructure was widened to include `scripts/tests/**/*.vitest.ts` alongside the existing `mcp_server/tests/**/*.vitest.ts`, unblocking execution of 6k+ lines of previously dark scripts-side test code. The full suite is green (10,335 passing, 0 failing after this release's test fixes).
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:62:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/references/state_format.md`                                                             | Modified | Added stop-reason taxonomy, blocked-stop schema, graphEvents field, and reducer rollups        |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:65:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/assets/deep_research_config.json`                                                       | Modified | Declared `_optimizerManaged` tunable/locked field registry                                      |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:69:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-signals.md`  | Created  | Playbook scenario for graph-aware convergence blocking                                          |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:70:| `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml`                                                      | Modified | Added legal-stop evaluation, blocked-stop persistence, graphEvents contract                    |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:72:| `.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md`                                                                             | Modified | Documented legal-stop, blocked-stop recovery, and optimizer thresholds for operators            |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:82:> **Update (post-release) - 2026-04-11:**
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:84:> This release advertised four lifecycle modes for re-entering a research session: `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, and `completed-continue`. Only the first two were actually wired up. `fork` (start a sibling lineage) and `completed-continue` (reopen a finished run) had no workflow behavior - picking them silently did the same thing as `resume`.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.5.0.0.md:86:> Release `v1.6.1.0` retracts both modes to deferred status. Operators who want to start a fresh lineage should pick `restart`, which archives the existing `research/` folder and mints a new session id. See `v1.6.1.0.md` for the full story and the one-session contract the runtime actually supports.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_jsonl.md:21:- Debugging malformed state records, blocked-stop events, graph convergence events, or lineage fields.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_jsonl.md:31:| Event records | Persist synthesis, blocked-stop, graph convergence, pause, and stuck-recovery decisions. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_jsonl.md:112:The optional `graphEvents` array records coverage graph mutations emitted by a review iteration. The MCP coverage graph handlers (`mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts`) consume these events and persist them into `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`, where they become the source of truth for graph-assisted convergence, hotspot saturation, and blocked-stop evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_jsonl.md:163:The collision regression in `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts` ("shared-ID collisions" block) exercises this contract directly: two sessions upsert identical node and edge ids, and both rows must survive independently.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_jsonl.md:179:### Blocked-Stop Event
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_jsonl.md:181:When the review legal-stop decision tree returns `blocked`, append a first-class `blocked_stop` event instead of silently overriding STOP to CONTINUE.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_jsonl.md:186:  "event": "blocked_stop",
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_jsonl.md:189:  "blockedBy": ["dimensionCoverageGate", "p0ResolutionGate"],
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_jsonl.md:212:`gateResults` carries the full 9-gate set emitted by `step_emit_blocked_stop` in both `deep_start-review-loop_{auto,confirm}.yaml`: `convergenceGate`, `dimensionCoverageGate`, `p0ResolutionGate`, `evidenceDensityGate`, `hotspotSaturationGate`, `claimAdjudicationGate`, `fixCompletenessReplayGate`, `candidateCoverageGate`, and `graphlessFallbackGate`. The last two are v2-rollout gates that pass trivially when the review-depth-v2 search path is inactive. See `../convergence/convergence.md` §Section-1 for the authoritative shape.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_jsonl.md:214:**Required:** `type`, `event`, `mode`, `run`, `blockedBy`, `gateResults`, `recoveryStrategy`, `timestamp`, `sessionId`, `generation`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_jsonl.md:234:  "blockers": [],
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_jsonl.md:241:**Required:** `type`, `event`, `mode`, `run`, `decision`, `signals`, `blockers`, `timestamp`, `sessionId`, `generation`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_jsonl.md:245:**Combined-stop rule:** Final STOP is legal only when the inline review convergence decision says STOP and the latest `graph_convergence.decision == "STOP_ALLOWED"`. If the latest graph decision is `STOP_BLOCKED`, set `stop_blocked=true`, emit `blocked_stop`, and continue recovery instead of stopping. If the latest graph decision is `CONTINUE`, downgrade the inline STOP candidate to CONTINUE.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:1:This release lands the deep-review portion of the packets 108 plus 110 auto-review uplift program. Four reusable teachings from the upstream `dzianisv/opencode-plugins` auto-review package now apply to every deep-review iteration: a parseable verdict on the final line, a role-banner marker at the rendered-prompt boundary, an anti-repetition rule across iterations, and a content-hash dedup that extends the existing synthesis path. A fifth teaching, H-9 bounded evidence, was deferred to packet 111 plus because current deep-review prompts pass state and metadata pointers rather than embedded file contents, so the council found no real interpolation path to harden. Every mechanism cleared a 5-of-5 unanimous deep-ai-council review by `cli-codex gpt-5.5 xhigh fast` before any code shipped.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:29:The synthesis step already deduplicated findings at `deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:1115-1124` using a file-plus-line plus normalized-title key. That key missed cross-dimension restatements (the same security finding described once under "security" and once under "correctness") and missed minor wording drift across iterations. This release extends the existing key with a content hash: `content_hash = sha256(file_path + line_range + finding_type + normalized_description_80chars)`. Synthesis now matches in two tiers, primary on content_hash, fallback to the legacy file plus line plus normalized title for records that predate this release. Cross-dimension matches collapse to one entry with a `dimensions: [<list>]` field that names every dimension that surfaced the finding. There is no parallel dedup path. The council mandate was to extend, not duplicate. H-7 dedup is intentionally NOT applied to deep-research because deep-research is single-dimension. That decision is documented in the deep-research changelog for the same release window.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:41:Both packets ran through a 5-seat deep-ai-council deliberation before any source change. The council returned REQUEST-CHANGES on the first pass with HIGH confidence and reshaped scope. The revisions that landed include: H-1 rendered as plain text (not Markdown bold). H-2 markers placed on `prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl` line 1 (not inside frontmatter-bearing reference templates). H-7 extending the existing synthesis dedup at lines 1115 through 1124 rather than introducing a parallel dedup pipeline. H-9 explicitly deferred. Implementation followed via `cli-opencode + deepseek-v4-pro --pure`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:50:This release ships no source-code changes. Deep-review is a skill, and all mechanisms land as prompt and dispatcher-config changes.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:60:| (dependent: sk-code-review SKILL.md) | sk-code-review dispatches through this deep-review pipeline. Its v1.3.0.0 release lands the same H-1, H-2, H-4 mechanisms in the review skill body. Touched in 73e9e361e plus 74782acfb. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:68:No migration required. Existing iteration records that predate this release will continue to match via the legacy file plus line plus normalized-title key during synthesis, so historical packets do not need re-running. Reviewers picking up the new prompts will start emitting the final-line verdict and the cross-dimension dedup metadata immediately. Source commits: 73e9e361e, 74782acfb, 8b3039a1a.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/05-dashboard.md:38:| `manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md` | Manual scenario | Confirms blocked-stop and reducer output make it into status summaries. |

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:483:          - graph_stop_blocked: "Boolean true when graph_decision == STOP_BLOCKED"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:492:               stop_blocked = true
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:493:               stopReason = "blockedStop"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:494:               blocked_gates = ["claimAdjudicationGate"]
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:496:               Skip hard stops (1) and composite convergence (4); proceed directly to step 5 legal-stop decision-tree assembly so blocked_by_json, gate outputs, and the blocked_stop event still populate consistently.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:504:               graph_decision, graph_signals_json, and graph_blockers_json are already populated by step_graph_convergence.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:506:               If graph_decision == "STOP_BLOCKED", set stop_blocked = true and route through blocked_stop emission instead of stopping.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:529:               h) candidateCoverageGate: skip entirely when `reviewDepthSchemaVersion` is not 2 on the latest iteration. Otherwise pass when the current iteration's `reviewDepthApplicability.scopeClass` is `trivial` with `enforcement: "skip"` AND `reviewDepthApplicability.evidenceRefs` is non-empty, OR when the reducer registry's `searchDebt` is empty AND `candidateCoverage.covered` is a superset of `searchCoverage.requiredBugClasses` for the lineage. Otherwise pass = false; record `searchDebt` items as blocked-stop evidence; blocker name = "candidateCoverageGate"; recovery_strategy = "Resolve deferred/blocked obligations in next iteration or downgrade scope explicitly to trivial with cited evidence."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:530:               i) graphlessFallbackGate: skip when `reviewDepthSchemaVersion` is not 2 OR the reducer registry's last-iteration `searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode` is `graph`. Applies when graphCoverageMode is `graphless_fallback` or `unavailable_blocked`. Pass when graphCoverageMode is `graphless_fallback` AND at least one `searchLedger` row exists for each entry in `searchCoverage.requiredBugClasses` with `searchActions[].method` in {"direct_read","exact_grep","semantic_search","code_graph_status_check","producer_consumer_trace","negative_test_inspection"} AND non-empty `evidenceRefs`. Fail automatically when graphCoverageMode is `unavailable_blocked`; record the explicit unavailability reason. Otherwise pass = false; blocker name = "graphlessFallbackGate"; recovery_strategy = "Add cited fallback ledger rows (direct read, exact search, producer/consumer trace, negative-test inspection) or set graphCoverageMode to 'graph' once graph data is available."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:533:               stop_blocked = true
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:534:               stopReason = "blockedStop"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:535:               blocked_by_json = JSON array of failing gate names (always include "claimAdjudicationGate" when claim_adjudication_gate_pass == false, "fixCompletenessReplayGate" when fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass == false, "candidateCoverageGate" when candidate_coverage_gate_pass == false, and "graphlessFallbackGate" when graphless_fallback_gate_pass == false)
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:536:               blocked_by_csv = comma-separated list of failing gate names
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:554:               candidate_coverage_search_debt_json = JSON array of reducer-owned searchDebt obligations blocking STOP, else []
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:559:               graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason = explicit reason when graphCoverageMode == "unavailable_blocked", else empty string
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:565:                    stop_blocked = true
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:566:                    stopReason = "blockedStop"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:567:                    # Blocked-stop closure: blockedBy must be string[] per the
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:568:                    # published review blocked-stop contract. Extract the `.type`
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:569:                    # field from each structured graph blocker; preserve the full
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:570:                    # objects in graph_blocker_detail_json for operator inspection.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:571:                    blocked_by_json = JSON.stringify(graph_blockers_json.map(b => typeof b === "string" ? b : (b && b.type) || "graph_blocker"))
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:572:                    blocked_by_csv = graph_blockers_csv
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:573:                    graph_blocker_detail_json = graph_blockers_json
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:574:                    recovery_strategy = "Resolve review coverage-graph blockers before stopping."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:577:                    stop_blocked = false
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:583:          - stop_blocked: "Boolean true when STOP is vetoed by inline legal-stop gates or graph_decision == STOP_BLOCKED"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:584:          - blocked_by_json: "JSON array of failed legal-stop gates when decision == BLOCKED"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:585:          - blocked_by_csv: "Comma-separated list of failed legal-stop gates when decision == BLOCKED"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:603:          - candidate_coverage_search_debt_json: "JSON array of reducer-owned searchDebt obligations used as blocked-stop evidence"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:605:          - graphless_fallback_gate_pass: "Boolean pass/fail for graphlessFallbackGate (false vetoes STOP when graphless fallback evidence is absent or unavailable_blocked)"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:608:          - graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason: "Explicit unavailability reason when graphCoverageMode == unavailable_blocked"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:611:          - graph_blockers_json: "JSON array of graph blockers"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:612:          - graph_blockers_csv: "Comma-separated graph blocker summary"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:615:      step_emit_blocked_stop:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:616:        action: "Persist blocked-stop evidence whenever the review legal-stop decision tree vetoes STOP"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:617:        if_blocked:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:618:          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"blocked_stop","mode":"review","run":{current_iteration},"blockedBy":{blocked_by_json},"gateResults":{"convergenceGate":{"pass":{convergence_gate_pass},"score":{convergence_gate_score}},"dimensionCoverageGate":{"pass":{dimension_coverage_gate_pass},"covered":{dimension_coverage_gate_covered_json},"missing":{dimension_coverage_gate_missing_json}},"p0ResolutionGate":{"pass":{p0_resolution_gate_pass},"activeP0":{active_p0_count}},"evidenceDensityGate":{"pass":{evidence_density_gate_pass},"avgEvidencePerFinding":{avg_evidence_per_finding}},"hotspotSaturationGate":{"pass":{hotspot_saturation_gate_pass}},"claimAdjudicationGate":{"pass":{claim_adjudication_gate_pass},"activeP0P1":{claim_adjudication_active_count}},"fixCompletenessReplayGate":{"pass":{fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass},"securitySensitive":{security_sensitive_fix_scope},"requiredRows":{fix_completeness_replay_required_rows},"passingRows":{fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows}},"candidateCoverageGate":{"pass":{candidate_coverage_gate_pass},"searchDebt":{candidate_coverage_search_debt_json},"missing":{candidate_coverage_missing_json}},"graphlessFallbackGate":{"pass":{graphless_fallback_gate_pass},"mode":"{graphless_fallback_mode}","missing":{graphless_fallback_missing_json},"unavailabilityReason":"{graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason}"}},"graphBlockerDetail":{graph_blockers_json},"recoveryStrategy":"{recovery_strategy}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{session_id}","generation":{generation}}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:619:          log: "Blocked STOP persisted for review loop. Failed gates: {blocked_by_csv}. Recovery strategy: {recovery_strategy}"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:620:        if_not_blocked:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:628:        if_blocked:

.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.2.2.0.md:1:This release updates deep-research with clearer support for research packet layout and layout handling. It focuses on research packet layout and layout handling so the release is easier to understand and use.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.2.2.0.md:6:- This part of the release focused on improving research packet layout and layout handling.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.2.2.0.md:7:- This release updates the workflow around research packet layout and layout handling.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.4.0.0.md:1:This release completes the 118 FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP migration for deep-review. The review loop no longer depends on deep-loop runtime code living under the system-spec-kit MCP server. Shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, direct script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests now live in the peer `deep-loop-runtime` skill, so deep-review consumes a clearer boundary without carrying a deleted MCP tool surface forward.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.4.0.0.md:26:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.4.0.0.md` | Replaced stale May 7 feature-catalog content with the 118 architecture-change release note. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/03-config-management.md:16:The config file is written during initialization and treated as immutable after that point. It records `mode: "review"`, the review target and target type, the four configured dimensions, session lineage fields, convergence settings, severity threshold, cross-reference sets, release-readiness state, status, and a file-protection map for every packet artifact.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/03-config-management.md:31:| `assets/review_mode_contract.yaml` | Contract | Declares target types, dimensions, lifecycle fields, release-readiness states, and reducer IO reflected into config. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/03-config-management.md:39:| `manual_testing_playbook/02--initialization-and-state-setup/invalid-or-contradictory-review-state-halts-for-repair.md` | Manual scenario | Confirms config disagreement blocks resume. |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/03--convergence/03-quality-guards.md:3:description: "Blocks premature stops until source diversity, focus alignment, and weak-source checks all pass."
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/03--convergence/03-quality-guards.md:10:Blocks premature stops until source diversity, focus alignment, and weak-source checks all pass.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/03--convergence/03-quality-guards.md:20:Guard failures are visible packet events, not quiet side effects. The workflow emits `guard_violation` lines for the specific issue and then persists a `blocked_stop` event with gate results, blocker names, recovery guidance, session identifier, and generation. The reducer uses those events to update next focus, blocked-stop history, and dashboard reporting for the next loop turn.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/03--convergence/03-quality-guards.md:31:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md` | Reference | Defines when the guards run and how blocked-stop events are persisted. |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/03--convergence/03-quality-guards.md:32:| `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml` | Workflow | Emits guard and blocked-stop events in autonomous mode. |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/03--convergence/03-quality-guards.md:33:| `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml` | Workflow | Emits the same guard and blocked-stop events in confirm mode. |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/03--convergence/03-quality-guards.md:34:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Reducer | Surfaces blocked-stop history and recovery hints into strategy and dashboard outputs. |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/03--convergence/03-quality-guards.md:40:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/quality-guard-source-diversity.md` | Manual playbook | Verifies the source-diversity guard blocks weakly sourced stops. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:1:# Iteration 3: Blocked-stop verification before legal stop
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:4:Security verification of the blocked-stop path before a legal stop decision.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7:### P0 - Blocker
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:8:- **F001**: Authorization bypass in review export route - `src/export.ts:41` - Security check trusts caller supplied reviewerId and can expose blocked-stop artifacts to an unauthorized reviewer.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:11:- **F003**: Missing null guard in registry merge - `src/registry.ts:88` - Correctness path dereferences prior state before checking the record exists.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:15:- The blocked-stop bundle itself is structurally complete, so the failure is not caused by missing event fields.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:18:- Re-running the reducer with identical inputs did not clear the blocker because the same P0 and uncovered dimensions remained active.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:21:Resolve F001 first, then add traceability and maintainability coverage so the legal-stop gates can be re-evaluated without an active blocker.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/review-dashboard-generation-after-iteration.md:57:Compare the dashboard template in assets/ with the YAML step_generate_dashboard format block to verify they agree on section structure.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.2.0.md:1:This patch release tightens the research reducer and the surrounding reference docs so they describe the same behavior. It fixes state handling, restores visible run-history data, and closes a few cases where the workflow could silently drift from the docs.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:3:argument-hint: "<profile_path> [:auto|:confirm] [--spec-folder=PATH] [--scorer=pattern|5dim] [--grader=noop|mock|llm] [--iterations=N] [--executor=NAME --model=NAME] (:auto supports PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS: prompt-body block for non-interactive setup)"
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:76:1. **Tier 1 - Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist a config record under `{spec_folder}/improvement/model-benchmark-config.json` (shape: `lane: "model-benchmark"`, `profilePath`, `specFolder`, `outputsDir`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `scoringMethod`, `grader`, optional `executor`, optional `model`, and when `grader = llm` also thread `executor`/`model` into `modelBenchmarkConfig.target_model` so `dispatch-model.cjs` reads them), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-model-benchmark-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:78:2. **Tier 2 - Targeted ask** (contract §1): when 1-2 required fields are genuinely ambiguous AND no default exists, emit ONE narrow question per ambiguous field. Command-specific Tier-2-eligible fields (per the Default Resolution Table below): `spec_folder`. **Ordering rule**: none needed. `profile_path` absence is not ambiguity because it has a default, and `executor`/`model` are only required when `grader = llm`.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:86:The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults. Grammar: see `auto_mode_contract.md` §2.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:96:  executor: cli-codex  # required only when grader = llm; one of cli-opencode | cli-claude-code | cli-codex | cli-gemini | cli-devin
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:97:  model: gpt-5.5  # required only when grader = llm; model id for the chosen executor
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:100:Rules: see `auto_mode_contract.md` §2 (unspecified fields fall back to default, marker fields take precedence over $ARGUMENTS flags, unknown fields warn, malformed lines parse-error). When `grader != llm`, any `executor`/`model` marker fields are ignored with a warning.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:114:| `executor` | Conditional | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, required only when `grader = llm` | none | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:158:   │ **Before proceeding, please answer:**                          │
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:184:   │    Which executor and model? e.g. "cli-codex, gpt-5.5"         │
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:203:   - executor = [from Q5, only when grader = llm]
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:214:⛔ NEVER request executor/model when grader is not llm
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:229:- `executor = ________________` (only when grader = llm)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:249:| executor               | Conditional   | ______     | Q5 (only when grader=llm) |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:255:│   ├─ grader == llm AND (executor missing OR model missing)?
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:268:- **DO NOT** request executor/model unless `grader = llm`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:297:**Inputs:** `$ARGUMENTS` - optional profile path, optional mode suffix, optional spec folder, optional scorer/grader flags, optional executor+model (only with `--grader=llm`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:317:- **Threading executor/model when `grader = llm`:** the gathered `executor` and `model` values are NOT loop-host flags. Setup MUST persist them into `modelBenchmarkConfig.target_model` (executor + model) inside the runtime config that `dispatch-model.cjs` reads, so the dispatcher resolves the requested executor and model. Setup persists `executor` and `model` into the Lane B config record (see Step 3) and the config record threads `modelBenchmarkConfig.target_model`. Without that thread, `dispatch-model.cjs` falls back to its built-in defaults and the gathered values are silently dropped.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:348:| llm | `--grader llm` | Real model dispatch via `dispatch-model.cjs`, requires an executor + model |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:352:`dispatch-model.cjs` is the executor router. It is loaded only on the model-benchmark path and only when `grader = llm`. Supported executors: `cli-opencode`, `cli-claude-code`, `cli-codex`, `cli-gemini`, `cli-devin`.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:385:Persist the config record to `{spec_folder}/improvement/model-benchmark-config.json` (`lane`, `profilePath`, `specFolder`, `outputsDir`, `executionMode`, `scoringMethod`, `grader`, optional `executor`, optional `model`). When `grader = llm`, also thread the gathered `executor` and `model` into `modelBenchmarkConfig.target_model` (shape: `{ "modelBenchmarkConfig": { "target_model": { "executor": "<executor>", "model": "<model>" } } }`) so `dispatch-model.cjs` resolves the requested executor and model instead of its built-in defaults.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:398:5. Check stop conditions (converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, manualStop, error, stuckRecovery)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:446:/deep:start-model-benchmark-loop :auto --spec-folder=specs/121/008 --scorer=5dim --grader=llm --executor=cli-codex --model=gpt-5.5
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:448:Routes graded scoring through `dispatch-model.cjs` to the named executor and model.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:522:- Requested executor/model when `grader` is not `llm`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/quick_reference.md:21:- Confirming default thresholds, verdicts, or release-readiness states at a glance.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/quick_reference.md:119:### Release Readiness
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/quick_reference.md:121:`releaseReadinessState` is the canonical config/report field for review readiness tracking.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/quick_reference.md:127:| `release-blocking` | At least one unresolved P0 remains active |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/quick_reference.md:151:**P0 override:** Any new P0 finding sets `newFindingsRatio >= 0.50`, blocking convergence.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/quick_reference.md:179:| 8 | Deferred Items | P2 advisories, blocked checks, and follow-up items |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/quick_reference.md:201:| P0 findings blocking convergence | Expected behavior: P0 override prevents premature stop |
