.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.10.1.0.md:5:This release aligns `deep-review` with the shared deep-skill reference model by adding focused convergence and state-output references. The loop behavior is unchanged: `/deep:start-review-loop` still owns state, dispatch, reducer updates, convergence checks, and synthesis.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.10.1.0.md:7:> Spec folder: `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/116-deep-skill-evolution/000-release-cleanup/001-deep-loop-runtime/003-reference-asset-alignment/` (Level 3)
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:8:> **EXECUTION POLICY**: Every scenario MUST be executed for real — not mocked, not stubbed, not classified as "unautomatable". AI agents executing these scenarios must run the actual commands, inspect real files, call real handlers, and verify real outputs. The only acceptable classifications are PASS, FAIL, or SKIP (with a specific sandbox blocker documented). "UNAUTOMATABLE" is not a valid status.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:23:- `08--fanout/`
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:70:- Release readiness depends on complete scenario coverage and no unresolved blocking contradictions.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:219:Verify that the loop honors the strategy file’s Next Focus and avoids approaches marked exhausted or blocked.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:222:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate the strategy-discipline contract for deep-research against the current deep-research docs, command entrypoint, YAML workflow, and runtime anchors. Verify iteration focus comes from Next Focus and that exhausted or blocked approaches are not retried. Return a concise user-facing verdict.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:224:Expected signals: Next Focus is read explicitly, exhausted approaches are treated as do-not-retry, and recovery mode consults deferred ideas instead of repeating blocked tactics.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:355:Verify that convergence STOP is blocked when an answered question cites <2 independent sources.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:368:Verify that convergence STOP is blocked when answered questions don't map to original key questions.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:381:Verify that convergence STOP is blocked when an answered question relies solely on a tentative source.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:430:### DR-031 | Graph convergence signals act as STOP-blocking guards
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:433:verify that when deep research convergence math votes STOP but `sourceDiversity` remains below the `0.4` guard threshold, STOP is blocked and blocked-stop evidence is recorded.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:436:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate the graph stop-blocking guard contract for deep-research against the current deep-research docs, command entrypoint, YAML workflow, and runtime anchors. Verify SOURCE_DIVERSITY_THRESHOLD = 0.4 blocks STOP when unmet, and that the research convergence reference records blocked-stop persistence with stopReason: "blockedStop" when legal-stop gates fail. Return a concise operator-facing verdict.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:438:Expected signals: `SOURCE_DIVERSITY_THRESHOLD = 0.4`; `evaluateGraphGates()` fails `sourceDiversity` when below threshold; research convergence docs map failed legal-stop gates to `stopReason: "blockedStop"` and `blocked_stop` persistence.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:443:### DR-032 | Research reducer surfaces blocked-stop history across registry, dashboard, and next-focus
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:446:verify that a research packet with at least one `blocked_stop` event surfaces that event into reducer-owned `blockedStopHistory`, the `BLOCKED STOPS` dashboard section, and the strategy `next-focus` anchor.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:449:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate blocked-stop reducer surfacing for deep-research against the current deep-research docs, command entrypoint, YAML workflow, and runtime anchors. Verify running node .opencode/skills/deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs <spec-folder> on a research packet with at least one blocked_stop event populates blockedStopHistory, renders BLOCKED STOPS in the dashboard, and rewrites the strategy next-focus anchor with the recovery strategy. Return a concise operator-facing verdict.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:451:Expected signals: `blockedStopHistory` is non-empty; each entry exposes `run`, `blockedBy`, `gateResults`, `recoveryStrategy`, and `timestamp`; `BLOCKED STOPS` renders the same blocked-stop data; the strategy `next-focus` anchor includes the recovery hint from the latest blocked-stop event.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:454:> **Feature File:** [DR-032](04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md)
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:459:verify that `graph_convergence` events become reducer-owned `graphConvergenceScore`, `graphDecision`, and `graphBlockers`, that the dashboard renders those graph signals, and that the live auto workflow invokes the graph tools before the inline 3-signal vote.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:462:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate graph-aware stop-gate integration for deep-research against the current deep-research docs, command entrypoint, YAML workflow, and runtime anchors. Verify graph_convergence reducer output populates graphConvergenceScore, graphDecision, and graphBlockers, that the dashboard renders GRAPH CONVERGENCE, and that the research auto workflow calls deep_loop_graph_upsert plus deep_loop_graph_convergence before the inline 3-signal vote. Return a concise operator-facing verdict.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:464:Expected signals: `graphConvergenceScore`, `graphDecision`, and `graphBlockers` appear in the registry; `GRAPH CONVERGENCE` appears in the dashboard; YAML includes `deep_loop_graph_upsert` and `deep_loop_graph_convergence` before the inline 3-signal vote.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:589:Validate that `/deep:start-research-loop` uses the lock, `spec_check_protocol` and generated findings fence rather than freeform `spec.md` mutation.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:630:This category covers 3 scenarios validating the opt-in fan-out dispatch layer added in the research loop: CLI lineage pool dispatch, native sequential agent dispatch, and single-executor parity.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:635:Validate that a two-CLI-lineage fan-out research config triggers `step_fanout_spawn` → `fanout-run.cjs` pool → two isolated lineages sub-packets → `step_fanout_merge` → canonical `research.md`.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:638:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate the CLI fan-out path for deep-research: confirm `step_fanout_spawn` and `step_fanout_merge` are present with correct `skip_when` guards, the `--executor` default policy is documented, and `fanout-run.vitest.ts` passes.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:640:Expected signals: YAML steps present with `skip_when`; command docs show 2+ executors → `config.fanout`; 5/5 fanout-run tests pass.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:643:> **Feature File:** [DR-052](08--fanout/fanout-cli-lineages-research.md)
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:648:Validate that native fan-out dispatches sequential `agent: deep-research` runs with per-lineage isolated artifact dirs via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir`.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:651:Prompt summary: Validate native fan-out for deep-research: confirm `step_fanout_spawn_native` uses `agent: deep-research` and passes the correct lineage artifact dir override.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:653:Expected signals: `agent: deep-research` (not `deep-review`) in native dispatch block; `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` in context; correct `skip_when`.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:656:> **Feature File:** [DR-053](08--fanout/fanout-native-sequential-research.md)
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:658:### DR-054 | Fan-out single-executor parity for research loop
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:661:Validate all three fan-out YAML steps are fully bypassed in single-executor mode, preserving byte-identical behavior.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:664:Prompt summary: Validate single-executor parity for deep-research: confirm the fan-out YAML steps have correct `skip_when` guards, the `if_absent` branch is unchanged, and vitest passes 197/197.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/01-jsonl-state-log.md:18:The same file also persists first-class lifecycle events such as `blocked_stop`, `graph_convergence`, `userPaused`, `stuckRecovery`, and `synthesis_complete`. Because the loop recomputes decisions from stored state, the JSONL contract is load-bearing for convergence replay, dashboard refreshes, and restart-safe reducer output.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/01-jsonl-state-log.md:30:| `references/convergence/convergence.md` | Protocol | Defines the shared stop-reason enum and blocked-stop persistence rules that land in JSONL. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_state_and_gates.md:114:| **Evidence** | Every active finding has concrete `file:line` evidence and is not inference-only | Block STOP, log `guard_violation`, continue loop |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_state_and_gates.md:115:| **Scope** | Findings and reviewed files stay within the declared review scope | Block STOP, log `guard_violation`, continue loop |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_state_and_gates.md:116:| **Coverage** | Configured dimensions and required traceability protocols must be covered before STOP is allowed | Block STOP, log `guard_violation`, continue loop |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:1:This release updates deep-research with clearer support for validation coverage. It focuses on validation coverage so the release is easier to understand and use.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:6:- This part of the release focused on improving validation coverage.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:7:- This release updates the workflow around validation coverage.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/03--convergence/04-graph-convergence.md:3:description: "Adds coverage-graph evidence and blockers to the deep-research legal-stop path when graph events exist."
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/03--convergence/04-graph-convergence.md:10:Adds coverage-graph evidence and blockers to the deep-research legal-stop path when graph events exist.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/03--convergence/04-graph-convergence.md:20:The reducer consumes the newest `graph_convergence` event and surfaces its verdict in the synchronized packet outputs. `findings-registry.json` stores graph score, decision, and blockers, and `deep-research-dashboard.md` renders those values for operators. If no graph events are present, the workflow omits graph checks and the reducer defaults those fields without breaking ordinary convergence.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/03--convergence/04-graph-convergence.md:33:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Reducer | Reads the latest graph event and exposes graph score, decision, and blockers. |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/03--convergence/04-graph-convergence.md:42:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-aware-stop-gate.md` | Manual playbook | Verifies graph-aware stop gates can block or allow synthesis. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:1:Initial release of deep-review as a standalone skill, split from the unified deep-research skill (v1.2.2.0). This skill owns all iterative code review and quality auditing functionality with its own command, YAML workflows, reference documentation, and manual testing playbook. The goal is to make the workflow easier to follow and easier to trust.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:3:argument-hint: "<topic> [:auto|:confirm] [--max-iterations=N] [--convergence=N] [--executor=<type> [--model=X] [--count=N] [--label=X] ...] [--executors=<json>] [--concurrency=N] (:auto supports PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS: prompt-body block for non-interactive setup)"
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:39:> Acquire `{spec_folder}/research/.deep-research.lock` before `folder_state` classification and keep `research/research.md` as the source of truth for any generated findings sync.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:60:1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist to `{artifact_dir}/deep-research-config.json` (shape: `researchTopic`, `specFolder`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `resource_map.emit`, `config.executor.*`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:70:The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults. Grammar: see `auto_mode_contract.md` §2.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:79:  executor: native  # native | cli-codex | cli-gemini | cli-claude-code | cli-opencode | cli-devin
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:80:  executor_model: ""  # optional executor-specific model id
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:81:  executor_reasoning: ""  # optional reasoning effort
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:82:  executor_service_tier: ""  # optional service tier
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:83:  executor_timeout: 900  # optional positive integer seconds
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:98:| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:99:| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:100:| `executor_reasoning` | N | flag `--reasoning-effort`, marker `executor_reasoning`, or executor default | none | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:101:| `executor_service_tier` | N | flag `--service-tier`, marker `executor_service_tier`, or executor default | none | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:102:| `executor_timeout` | N | flag `--executor-timeout`, marker `executor_timeout`, or default | `900` | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:104:| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each `--executor` group accepts `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:105:| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:107:**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-research` agent dispatches with isolated dirs. CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses concurrently in the capped pool.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:128:   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:129:   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:130:   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:131:   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:132:   |-- --executor-timeout=<seconds> -> config.executor.timeoutSeconds (positive integer, default `900`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:134:   |-- --executor=<type> [--model=X] [--reasoning-effort=Y] [--service-tier=Z] [--executor-timeout=N] [--iters=N] [--label=X] [--count=N]
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:135:   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:136:   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch for complex configs
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:137:   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:140:   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:141:   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:143:   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=10, convergenceThreshold=0.05, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:147:   - The generated `deep-research-config.json` stores executor settings under `config.executor.*`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:150:   - `--executor` -> `config.executor.type`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:151:   - `--model` -> `config.executor.model`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:152:   - `--reasoning-effort` -> `config.executor.reasoningEffort`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:153:   - `--service-tier` -> `config.executor.serviceTier`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:154:   - `--executor-timeout` -> `config.executor.timeoutSeconds`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:157:   - `parseExecutorConfig` from `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` runs at config-write time
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:158:   - Invalid combinations fail fast with clear errors, including `cli-codex` without `--model` and reserved-but-unwired executor kinds
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:168:   - Include Q-Exec only when `--executor` is NOT present and the topic text does NOT already mention executor hints such as `cli-codex`, `codex`, or `gpt-5.4`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:169:   - If Q-Exec is omitted and no executor is otherwise resolved, default to `native`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:209:   - executor config = [CLI flags, compact reply, config file, or default `native`; map compact reply fields to `config.executor.type/model/reasoningEffort/serviceTier`, and accept an optional volunteered convergence value before executor fields]
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:357:# Single-executor (default — unchanged)

.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:380:> **Note on native fan-out:** `--executor=native --count=N` dispatches N sequential `@deep-research` sub-agents with isolated artifact dirs. Not pooled. Best for count 1–3. For higher parallelism, use CLI executor kinds.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:56:| Agent | `@deep-review` (LEAF) | Single iteration executor, no sub-agents, no WebFetch |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:113:6. **Write config**: `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-config.json` with `mode: "review"`, lineage metadata (`sessionId`, `parentSessionId`, `lineageMode`, `generation`, `continuedFromRun`, `releaseReadinessState`), and review-specific fields including target, target type, dimensions, protocol plan, and release-readiness state.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:182:If convergence math or a hard-stop candidate points to STOP, the workflow must run the review legal-stop decision tree before actually stopping. That decision tree records nine review-specific gates: `convergenceGate`, `dimensionCoverageGate`, `p0ResolutionGate`, `evidenceDensityGate`, `hotspotSaturationGate`, `claimAdjudicationGate`, `fixCompletenessReplayGate`, `candidateCoverageGate`, and `graphlessFallbackGate` (see `../convergence/convergence.md` §Section-1 blocked_stop event example for the authoritative shape). The Step 4a claim-adjudication path treats `claimAdjudicationGate` as a hard STOP gate, so the count covers both the convergence decision tree and the adjudication enforcement path. `candidateCoverageGate` and `graphlessFallbackGate` are v2-rollout gates that pass trivially when the review-depth-v2 search path is inactive. If any gate fails, the loop does **not** stop. Instead it emits a first-class `blocked_stop` JSONL event with:
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:184:- `blockedBy`: array of the failed review gate names
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:188:The blocked-stop event is append-only evidence that legal-stop blocked the run. The loop then continues with the recovery or next-focus path rather than synthesizing.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:198:**P0 override**: Any new P0 finding sets `newFindingsRatio >= 0.50`, blocking convergence regardless of composite score.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:268:Before dispatching, the YAML resolves the executor via `parseExecutorConfig` from `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts`. The resolved `config.executor.kind` selects the dispatch branch:
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:280:Per-kind flag-compatibility is enforced at config parse time by `EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT` in `executor-config.ts`. Setting a flag that the chosen kind does not support throws `ExecutorConfigError` before dispatch.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:282:Cross-CLI delegation (a running executor invoking other CLIs via its shell) is documented design intent. Runtime recursion detection is out of scope. See the SKILL.md Cross-CLI Delegation subsection.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:375:**Failure semantics**: when any new P0/P1 finding is missing a packet or a required field, the workflow records `{"event":"claim_adjudication","passed":false,"missingPackets":[...]}` in `deep-review-state.jsonl`. On the next loop, `step_check_convergence` step 0 (universal pre-check) routes STOP to `BLOCKED` with `blockedBy: ["claimAdjudicationGate"]` until a follow-up iteration rewrites the packet. Downgraded findings have their `finalSeverity` updated. The original severity is preserved in the iteration file for audit trail.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:393:- Non-blocking: if generation fails, log a warning and continue the loop
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:453:5. Preserve the audit trail: note which iterations contributed to each merged finding
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:487:| 9 | Deferred Items | P2 advisories, blocked checks, and follow-up items |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:570:### Lifecycle Branches (current release)
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:572:The runtime supports three lineage modes today. `fork` and `completed-continue` were described in earlier drafts but have no workflow wiring in this release, so they MUST NOT be exposed to operators. If the long-form lineage feature is picked up later it will arrive with first-class event emission, reducer ancestry handling, and replay fixtures. Until then treat the contract below as canonical.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:724:Every executor path MUST satisfy:
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:741:Non-native executor runs append an `executor` block with model and runtime settings to the iteration's JSONL record via `executor-audit.ts`. Native runs are recoverable from YAML alone.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:745:The executor-agnostic iteration prompt lives at `assets/prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl`, rendered by `prompt-pack.ts` before dispatch and either injected as the agent's context (native) or piped to `codex exec` stdin (cli-codex). Config is in `assets/deep_review_config.json` under the `executor` key; schema in `deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts`. CLI flag precedence: `--executor/--model/--reasoning-effort/--service-tier/--executor-timeout > config file > schema default`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md:752:- The `@deep-review` LEAF agent definition is untouched: it is the native executor, not the only one.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:1:This release lands the 042 Deep Research & Review Runtime Improvement Bundle for `deep-review` and closes the long-standing architectural gap between the two deep-loop skills. `deep-review` now has a frozen stop-reason taxonomy, review-specific legal-stop gates, first-class blocked-stop events, a semantic coverage graph integration, and - new this release - the same reducer script and runtime-capability resolver that `deep-research` has always had, unifying the two packet expected behaviors at the code level. The goal is to make the workflow easier to follow and easier to trust.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:7:- Stop-reason enum is now frozen: `converged`, `maxIterationsReached`, `blockedStop`, `stuckRecovery`, `error`, `manualStop`, `userPaused`. Legacy review stop strings are mapped through a single migration path.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:9:- Blocked-stop events are now persisted as first-class JSONL records with `blockedBy` (for example `["dimensionCoverageGate", "p0ResolutionGate"]`), full `gateResults`, and a review-specific `recoveryStrategy` hint so resume flows can replay the decision.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:18:- Convergence for review now consults the coverage graph: dimension coverage gate severity was raised from warning to **blocking**, so an uncovered required dimension can no longer silently allow STOP.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:36:- `deep_review_config.json` now declares the tunable surface (`convergenceThreshold`, `stuckThreshold`, `maxIterations`) via `_optimizerManaged`, with the reducer wiring, file protection, session identity, and review dimensions all locked against optimizer drift.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:44:- Dimension coverage gate severity changed from `warning` to `blocking` for uncovered required dimensions in review mode - the earlier severity silently allowed STOP even with a required dimension untouched.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:54:- `references/convergence.md`, `references/state_format.md`, `references/quick_reference.md`, and `assets/review_mode_expected behavior.yaml` all reflect the legal-stop and blocked-stop model plus the graph-convergence lens.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:61:- Vitest infrastructure now includes `scripts/tests/**/*.vitest.ts`, unblocking execution of previously-dark scripts-side tests. Full suite green (10,335 passing).
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:79:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state_format.md`                                                              | Modified | Added stop-reason taxonomy, blocked-stop schema, graphEvents field, reducer ownership      |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:80:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence.md`                                                               | Modified | Published 3-signal weights, dimension coverage blocking severity, graph-aware guards       |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:82:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md`                                                             | Modified | Documented legal-stop + blocked-stop flow for review mode                                  |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:84:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-review.md`    | Created  | Playbook scenario for graph-aware convergence blocking in review mode                      |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:85:| `.opencode/commands/speckit/assets/speckit_deep-review_auto.yaml`                                                       | Modified | Wired reducer command, legal-stop evaluation, blocked-stop recording, graphEvents contract |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:87:| `.opencode/commands/speckit/deep-review.md`                                                                              | Modified | Documented legal-stop, blocked-stop recovery, and optimizer thresholds for review operators |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:98:> **Update (post-release) - 2026-04-11:**
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:100:> A 10-iteration closing audit of this release found two promises it did not deliver:
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:102:> - The four lifecycle modes this release advertised for re-entering a review session (`resume`, `restart`, `fork`, `completed-continue`) were only half-shipped. `fork` and `completed-continue` had no workflow wiring - picking them silently did the same thing as `resume`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:103:> - The claim-adjudication packet this release described as a hard stop gate was never actually consulted by the stop-checking logic. A reviewer could skip the packet entirely and the loop would still finish as if everything was fine.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:105:> Release `v1.3.1.0` retracts the two unwired lifecycle modes, wires the claim-adjudication gate into every stop path (including a universal pre-check that runs before the hard stops), makes the shared graph database safe for concurrent sessions, rewrites the canonical agent template so the reducer can actually parse it, and replaces the phantom stop-contract shape in the convergence reference with the real `blocked_stop` event. See `v1.3.1.0.md` for plain-English Problem/Fix pairs on each one.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.3.0.md:1:This release changes where research artifacts land on disk. Research folders now always go to the spec tree root instead of inside nested child phases. A shared path resolver handles the lookup. The reducer, command YAMLs, agent definitions and documentation are all updated to match.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/02-strategy-tracking.md:18:The reducer also depends on machine-owned anchors in this file. Missing anchors are treated as contract failures unless an explicit bootstrap path is used. That makes the strategy file more than a note-taking aid: it is a structured state surface that drives dispatch, blocked-stop overrides, and restart-safe recovery.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/02--state-management/02-strategy-tracking.md:39:| `manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md` | Manual scenario | Verifies blocked-stop state can override next focus in reducer-driven summaries. |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:3:argument-hint: "<target> [:auto|:confirm] [--max-iterations=N] [--convergence=N] [--spec-folder=PATH] [--executor=<type> --count=N --label=X ...] [--executors=<json>] [--concurrency=N] (:auto supports PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS: prompt-body block for non-interactive setup)"
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:12:> Under `:auto`, setup follows the three-tier resolution contract in §0: resolve confidently, ask only targeted ambiguity questions, then fail fast if required inputs remain unresolved. Under `:confirm`, setup keeps the consolidated interactive question block.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:58:1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist to `{spec_folder}/review/deep-review-config.json` (shape: `reviewTarget`, `reviewTargetType`, `reviewDimensions`, `specFolder`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `resource_map.emit`, `config.executor.*`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:68:The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:79:  executor: native  # one of: native | cli-codex | cli-gemini | cli-claude-code | cli-opencode | cli-devin
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:80:  executor_model: ""  # optional, executor-specific
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:81:  executor_reasoning: ""  # optional
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:82:  executor_service_tier: ""  # optional
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:83:  executor_timeout: 900  # optional
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:107:| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:108:| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:109:| `executor_reasoning` | N | flag `--reasoning-effort`, marker `executor_reasoning`, or executor default | none | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:110:| `executor_service_tier` | N | flag `--service-tier`, marker `executor_service_tier`, or executor default | none | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:111:| `executor_timeout` | N | flag `--executor-timeout`, marker `executor_timeout`, or default | `900` | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:113:| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each group accepts `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:114:| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:116:**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Review fan-out uses strongest-restriction: any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-review` sub-agents; CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses in the capped pool.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:120:Use this block only when `execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"` or when no suffix was supplied and Q2 must ask for the execution mode.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:141:   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:142:   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:143:   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:144:   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)

.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:148:   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)

.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:150:   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)

.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:237:   - executor config = [CLI flags, compact reply, config file, or default `native`; map compact reply fields to `config.executor.type/model/reasoningEffort/serviceTier`, and accept an optional volunteered convergence value before executor fields]
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:286:Run an iterative loop for code review: Initialize the review packet under `{artifact_dir}` (resolved via `resolveArtifactRoot()` — root specs use `{spec_folder}/review/`; child phases and sub-phases use `{spec_folder}/review/{packet}-pt-{NN}/`), dispatch `@deep-review` agent per iteration, evaluate convergence across review dimensions, synthesize findings into `{artifact_dir}/review-report.md`, and emit `{artifact_dir}/resource-map.md` at convergence unless `--no-resource-map` disables it. Use when auditing code, specs, skills, agents, or tracks for quality and release readiness.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:402:# Single-executor (default — unchanged)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:407:# Fan-out: two CLI lineages in parallel

.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:411:  --concurrency=2

.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:417:  --concurrency=3
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:420:> **Review fan-out verdict:** strongest-restriction applies — if any lineage has an active P0 finding, the merged verdict is FAIL regardless of other lineages' outcomes.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:429:| Review PASS, ready for release | `/create:changelog` | Generate changelog entry |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:455:- Three severity levels: P0 (Blocker), P1 (Required), P2 (Suggestion)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:458:- P0 override: new P0 findings block convergence regardless of threshold

.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:69:The stop path combines hard stops, a three-signal weighted vote, blocked-stop persistence, graph-assisted review gates, and stuck recovery. STOP is legal only when convergence math and the review gate bundle agree.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:133:A conditional synthesis section emitted only when `config.resource_map_present == true`, inserted as section 8 of the review report. It reports touched entries, untouched entries (`expected-by-scope` vs `gap`), and implementation paths absent from the map. It is descriptive and does not by itself block STOP.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:145:Resolves which executor runs each iteration and enforces per-kind flag compatibility before dispatch.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:149:`parseExecutorConfig` resolves `config.executor.kind` into one of four dispatch branches (`native`, `cli-codex`, `cli-gemini`, `cli-claude-code`). Unsupported flags throw `ExecutorConfigError` at parse time. All branches share prompt rendering, output validation, and executor audit append.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:153:See [`01--loop-lifecycle/08-executor-selection-contract.md`](01--loop-lifecycle/08-executor-selection-contract.md) for full implementation and validation file listings.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:162:`step_fanout_spawn` (CLI pool + native sequential agent dispatch), and `step_fanout_merge`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:163:with strongest-restriction verdict binding at the top of `phase_synthesis`. Single-executor
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:168:Three new YAML steps gated on `config.fanout` presence. CLI lineages run via `fanout-run.cjs`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:170:dispatches. `step_fanout_merge` produces a consolidated `deep-review-findings-registry.json`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:171:using strongest-restriction (any active P0 → `mergedVerdict=FAIL`) and binds
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:172:`p0_count/p1_count/p2_count` from the merged result for `step_derive_verdict`. This
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:174:empty single-executor state log. Command flags: `--executor` (repeatable), `--executors
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:175:<json>`, `--concurrency N`. Review verdict: strongest-restriction across all lineages.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:179:See [`01--loop-lifecycle/09-fanout-dispatch.md`](01--loop-lifecycle/09-fanout-dispatch.md) for full implementation and validation file listings.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:195:`deep-review-state.jsonl` begins with a config record, appends iteration records with severity-weighted findings and coverage data, records pause and blocked-stop and synthesis events, and can carry graph convergence and graph event payloads for review-aware coverage tracking.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:239:Holds the reducer-owned view of active, resolved, repeated, and blocked findings.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:243:`deep-review-findings-registry.json` is regenerated after each iteration and lifecycle transition, keeps severity totals and dimension coverage, stores blocked-stop and graph convergence history, and fail-closes when corruption or missing machine-owned anchors break the reducer contract.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:275:The workflow appends a `graph_convergence` event carrying a `decision` enum (`STOP_ALLOWED`, `STOP_BLOCKED`, `CONTINUE`), a signal snapshot, and blockers. Final STOP is legal only when the inline decision says STOP and the latest graph decision is `STOP_ALLOWED`. The reducer rolls these into the findings-registry graph-convergence history.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:369:These entries cover how the loop classifies findings, verifies blocker claims, adjudicates evidence, determines verdicts, and blocks premature stop attempts.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:379:The review loop uses a three-level severity contract with weights `10.0 / 5.0 / 1.0`, requires file-line evidence for every level, uses severity-weighted `newFindingsRatio`, and applies the P0 override to prevent premature convergence when a new blocker is found.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:391:Re-reads blocker evidence before a P0 can shape the verdict.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:411:Each new blocker or required finding must carry a typed adjudication packet with evidence, counterevidence search, alternative explanation, final severity, confidence, and transition history. Missing or failing packets trip `claimAdjudicationGate` and veto STOP.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:443:The legal-stop bundle combines evidence, scope, coverage, P0 resolution, evidence density, hotspot saturation, and claim adjudication checks. When a stop vote fails those checks, the loop persists a `blocked_stop` event and continues with a recovery hint instead of silently stopping.

.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:1:This release splits review-mode functionality out of deep-research into the new standalone deep-review skill. deep-research is now research-only - all review keywords, commands, and references have been removed or redirected. The goal is to make the workflow easier to follow and easier to trust.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/01--entry-points-and-modes/auto-mode-deep-research-kickoff.md:53:Capture the command examples, the mode-routing block, and the `state_paths` contract together.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/severity-classification-in-jsonl.md:18:The convergence algorithm uses `newFindingsRatio` derived from severity-weighted findings (P0: 10.0, P1: 5.0, P2: 1.0). If the JSONL records do not include accurate P0/P1/P2 counts, convergence decisions are based on incorrect data. Additionally, any new P0 finding forces `newFindingsRatio >= 0.50`, blocking premature convergence.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/severity-classification-in-jsonl.md:53:Capture Rule 11, the dispatch JSONL constraint, the severity_weights block, and the P0 override rule.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-agent-improvement-loop.md:3:argument-hint: "<agent_path> [:auto|:confirm] [--spec-folder=PATH] [--iterations=N] (:auto supports PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS: prompt-body block for non-interactive setup)"
.opencode/commands/deep/start-agent-improvement-loop.md:73:1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist to `{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-config.json` (shape: `targetPath`, `targetProfile`, `specFolder`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `scoringMode`, `maxIterations`, `agentName`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-agent-improvement-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-agent-improvement-loop.md:83:The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults. Grammar: see `auto_mode_contract.md` §2.

.opencode/commands/deep/start-agent-improvement-loop.md:412:### Step 6D: Session Boundary (current release)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-agent-improvement-loop.md:414:`/deep:start-agent-improvement-loop` is one-session-only in the current release. Every invocation starts a fresh `new`-mode session with generation 1, writes a new journal, and evaluates candidates from iteration 1 for that session.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-agent-improvement-loop.md:416:Do **not** document or attempt journal replay, iteration carry-forward, or `resume`/`restart`/`fork`/`completed-continue` behavior here. Those lineage modes were described in earlier drafts but have no shipped runtime wiring; see `.opencode/skills/deep-improvement/SKILL.md §Resume/Continuation Semantics (current release)` for the canonical retraction.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-agent-improvement-loop.md:497:- **Stop-reason enum** (post-060/005): canonical values are `converged | maxIterationsReached | blockedStop | manualStop | error | stuckRecovery`. Old `plateau`/`benchmarkPlateau` retired.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-agent-improvement-loop.md:509:- **Dispatcher**: `scripts/model-benchmark/dispatch-model.cjs` is the model-agnostic dispatcher (executor routing across cli-opencode, cli-claude-code, cli-codex, cli-gemini, cli-devin). It loads only on the model-benchmark path, never in agent-improvement mode.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-agent-improvement-loop.md:544:- Claimed a target-specific promotion carve-out that is not part of the current release contract

Single-executor path unchanged."

.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/08-executor-selection-contract.md:3:description: "Resolves which executor runs each review iteration (native agent or one of three CLIs) and enforces per-kind flag compatibility before dispatch."
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/08-executor-selection-contract.md:10:Decides whether a review iteration runs on the native `@deep-review` agent or is dispatched to an external CLI, and guarantees the chosen executor only receives flags it actually supports.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/08-executor-selection-contract.md:16:Before each dispatch, the workflow resolves the executor via `parseExecutorConfig` from `deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts`. The resolved `config.executor.kind` selects one of four dispatch branches:
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/08-executor-selection-contract.md:25:All branches share three steps: pre-dispatch prompt rendering (`renderPromptPack`), post-dispatch validation (`validateIterationOutputs` asserting iteration file plus JSONL delta plus required fields), and an executor audit append (`appendExecutorAuditToLastRecord`, skipped when kind is `native`).
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/08-executor-selection-contract.md:27:Per-kind flag compatibility is enforced at config parse time by `EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT`. Setting a flag the chosen kind does not support throws `ExecutorConfigError` before any dispatch. Cross-CLI delegation (a running executor invoking other CLIs through its shell) is documented design intent, runtime recursion detection is out of scope.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/08-executor-selection-contract.md:37:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` | Runtime | `parseExecutorConfig`, `EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT`, `ExecutorConfigError`. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/08-executor-selection-contract.md:45:| `manual_testing_playbook/01--entry-points-and-modes/parameterized-invocation-max-iterations-convergence.md` | Manual scenario | Exercises parameterized invocation paths that feed executor config. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/08-executor-selection-contract.md:46:| `manual_testing_playbook/07--command-flow-stress-tests/write-boundary-reducer-owned-files.md` | Manual scenario | Confirms executor write boundaries against reducer-owned files. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/08-executor-selection-contract.md:54:- Feature file path: `01--loop-lifecycle/08-executor-selection-contract.md`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/08-executor-selection-contract.md:55:- Primary sources: `references/protocol/loop_protocol.md`, `SKILL.md`, `deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts`
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:1:This release hardens the shipped cli executor matrix against the seven P1 findings surfaced by the 30-iteration deep-research pass on Phase 017 refinements. Executor identity is now written on the first JSONL record of every non-native iteration. A new typed `dispatch_failure` event preserves executor attribution when a crash or timeout leaves no valid iteration record. Copilot large-prompt handling gains an `@path` fallback in every dispatch branch so oversized prompts no longer risk shell argument-length limits. `graph-metadata.json` records a `save_lineage` tag so freshness claims stop depending on timestamp subtraction alone. The readiness contract docs narrow to the four trust-state values the seven code-graph handlers actually emit. The evidence-marker parser now handles indented and nested fences correctly. Retry-budget telemetry emits structured `retry_attempt` records without changing the `N=3` heuristic. Caller-context coverage extends to `setImmediate`, `queueMicrotask` and `timers/promises` boundaries.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:3:> Spec folder: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/018-cli-executor-remediation` (Level 3)
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:9:#### First-write executor provenance
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:11:Before this release, the `executor` audit block was appended AFTER validation as the final merge on the last JSONL record. If the CLI executor crashed or produced no output, the failure was anonymous. `writeFirstRecordExecutor` now runs before dispatch for every non-native kind and stamps executor identity on the first iteration record. Post-dispatch validation now REQUIRES the executor field for non-native runs, so missing provenance fails fast with a typed `executor_missing` reason. Native runs are unchanged.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:17:A new typed `{type:'event', event:'dispatch_failure', executor, reason, iteration, timestamp}` record lets the workflow preserve executor identity when no valid iteration record exists. Crashes, timeouts and missing-output cases now emit this event with the executor block attached. The reducer tolerates the event without emitting a duplicate `schema_mismatch`. Three consecutive dispatch_failure events trigger the existing stuck-recovery path.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:23:Phase 019 documented an `@path` fallback for oversized Copilot prompts. This release actually implements it. The `if_cli_copilot` branch in all four YAMLs (research auto + confirm, review auto + confirm) now runs a size check on the rendered prompt file. If the prompt is at or below 16 KB, the dispatch uses the existing positional form. Above 16 KB the dispatch uses a wrapper prompt: `copilot -p "Read the instructions in @<path> and follow them exactly. Do not deviate." --model X --allow-all-tools --no-ask-user`. The `cli-matrix.vitest.ts` suite gained two cases covering both sides of the threshold.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:35:A new `lib/continuity/timestamp-normalize.ts` module exposes `isLowPrecision(raw)`, `normalizeTimestampPrecision(raw)` and `comparePrecisionAware(a, b, thresholdMs)`. Date-only values and midnight-UTC values are treated as low-precision and bypass strict staleness thresholds so they stop producing false-stale warnings. The 10-minute continuity threshold is unchanged. The helper is additive for future validator wiring. Existing validator callers are untouched this release.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:47:Three new committed vitest cases cover `setImmediate`, `queueMicrotask` and `timers/promises` async boundaries for the AsyncLocalStorage caller-context path. The 30-iteration research verified these boundaries with local probes. This release promotes the proofs into permanent regression coverage so future consumers can trust the contract holds.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:55:Indented code blocks (four-space-leading triple-backtick fences) no longer produce false-positive evidence-marker warnings. Nested line-start triple-backtick fences inside an already-open fence are tracked as content rather than fence closers. Four-backtick fences in isolation remain safe. Mismatched or unclosed fences stay separate false-negative cases with their own regression fixtures documenting expected behavior.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:59:#### description.json merge-preserving repair
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:61:Phase 017's `generate-description.js` collapsed parse failures and schema violations into the same `null` branch, which then rebuilt a minimal replacement that dropped authored rich content. The 30-iteration research quantified the blast radius: 29 of 86 packet-level `description.json` files in the `026` tree were one invalid edit away from lossy repair. The loader now returns a discriminated result `{ok, reason: 'file_missing' | 'parse_error' | 'schema_error', partial?}`. The new `mergePreserveRepair` helper preserves authored narrative plus extension keys when the file is parseable but schema-invalid. Canonical derived fields always win on conflict. A new specimen regression suite uses real rich 017 packet files. The `SPECKIT_DESCRIPTION_REPAIR_MERGE_SAFE` feature flag defaults to on. Setting it to `false` reverts to Phase 017 behavior for rollback.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:73:#### Copilot concurrency and reasoning-effort clarification
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:75:`cli-copilot/SKILL.md` now explicitly states the repo-safe concurrency cap is 3 (per the Phase 018 `feedback_copilot_concurrency_override` memory rule) while the upstream API cap is 5. Reasoning effort is clarified as config-driven (`~/.copilot/config.json`) rather than available as a CLI flag.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:99:62 new tests across 8 new or extended suites: executor-audit extended, post-dispatch-validate extended, new dispatch-failure suite, new description-repair suite, new description-repair-specimens suite, cli-matrix extended, new graph-metadata-lineage suite, new timestamp-normalize suite, extended evidence-marker-audit suite, extended caller-context suite, new retry-budget-telemetry suite. All existing tests pass unchanged.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:110:| `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts`       | Added `writeFirstRecordExecutor` + `emitDispatchFailure`. Existing `appendExecutorAuditToLastRecord` retained for idempotent success-path merge.          |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:111:| `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/post-dispatch-validate.ts` | Require executor on non-native first record. Tolerate `dispatch_failure` records. New reason codes: `executor_missing`, `dispatch_failure_logged`.       |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:112:| `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/description/repair.ts`             | New module: `mergePreserveRepair` + `MergePreserveInput` + `MergePreserveResult`.                                                                         |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:113:| `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec-folder/generate-description.ts`      | Loader returns discriminated result. Schema-error routes to mergePreserveRepair. Parse-error keeps minimal replacement. Feature flag wired.               |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:131:| `tests/deep-loop/executor-audit.vitest.ts`                                      | Extended with first-write + failure-emit cases.                                                |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:134:| `tests/description/repair.vitest.ts`                                            | New suite covering mergePreserveRepair logic, canonical wins, preserved keys.                  |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:145:`deep-research/SKILL.md` readiness contract narrowed. `deep-review/SKILL.md` same. `cli-copilot/SKILL.md` concurrency-cap clarification. `system-spec-kit/SKILL.md` retry-budget heuristic language. Continuity threshold docs update in multiple references. Feature-catalog + manual-testing-playbook entries for post-insert retry budget.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:153:No migration required. Existing native-path users see identical behavior. Existing `cli-codex` users gain first-write executor provenance automatically. Users running Copilot with large prompts now get the `@path` fallback instead of silent ARG_MAX failure. The `save_lineage` field is optional during this release. A follow-up packet will tighten it to required once the backfill script runs across production metadata.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/001-schema-config-plumbing/spec.md:36:- **Parity gate:** existing single-executor `parseExecutorConfig` tests byte-identical; full vitest green.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/001-schema-config-plumbing/spec.md:39:Pool, spawn, merge, YAML, docs (later phases).
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/03--review-dimensions/02-security.md:18:Because security findings can escalate to P0, this dimension sits near the front of the loop and participates in both the core coverage model and the claim-adjudication path. A confirmed security blocker can keep the loop in `release-blocking` state and prevent PASS even when the rest of the review is clean.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/03--review-dimensions/02-security.md:38:| `manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/adversarial-self-check-runs-on-p0-findings.md` | Manual scenario | Exercises blocker validation on high-severity findings, including security blockers. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/03--review-dimensions/02-security.md:39:| `manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md` | Manual scenario | Confirms critical findings, often security-driven, block early stop. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/07-resource-map-coverage-gate.md:18:When no resource map was present at init, the section is omitted entirely and the review report carries its 9 core sections. The gate is descriptive: it does not by itself block STOP or force a verdict, but an `expected-by-scope` untouched entry is a strong signal that the review scope was not fully exercised.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/review-quality-guards-block-premature-stop.md:2:title: "DRV-018 -- Review quality guards block premature stop"
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/review-quality-guards-block-premature-stop.md:6:# DRV-018 -- Review quality guards block premature stop
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/review-quality-guards-block-premature-stop.md:14:This scenario validates review quality guards block premature stop for `DRV-018`. The objective is to verify that 3 binary gates (evidence, scope, coverage) must all pass before the review loop can transition to synthesis.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/review-quality-guards-block-premature-stop.md:20:These 3 contract-level binary gates (evidence, scope, coverage) declared in `review_mode_contract.yaml` are the coarse layer. The legal-stop decision tree expands them into the 9 named event-shape gates emitted by `step_emit_blocked_stop`: `convergenceGate`, `dimensionCoverageGate`, `p0ResolutionGate`, `evidenceDensityGate`, `hotspotSaturationGate`, `claimAdjudicationGate`, `fixCompletenessReplayGate`, `candidateCoverageGate`, and `graphlessFallbackGate`. This scenario validates the 3 binary gates, the 9-gate bundle is the authoritative stop check (see `references/convergence/convergence.md` §6).
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/review-quality-guards-block-premature-stop.md:89:- Feature file path: `04--convergence-and-recovery/review-quality-guards-block-premature-stop.md`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/adversarial-self-check-runs-on-p0-findings.md:18:P0 findings block convergence and trigger FAIL verdicts. A false-positive P0 wastes remediation effort and erodes trust. The adversarial self-check is the quality gate that prevents phantom P0 findings from reaching the final report.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md:12:This phase evaluates the review history after every iteration. It mixes hard stops, a weighted stop vote, legal-stop gates, graph-aware blockers, pause handling, and stuck recovery so the loop only exits when the review is actually saturated.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md:16:The live convergence model evaluates outcomes in priority order: max iterations, all dimensions covered with stabilization, stuck detection, composite convergence, legal-stop gate bundle, then default continue. The weighted vote uses rolling average, MAD noise floor, and dimension coverage as its three core signals. A new P0 finding forces `newFindingsRatio` up to at least `0.50`, which blocks premature stop.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md:18:Even when the weighted vote requests STOP, the loop still has to pass the review gate bundle. Failed gates persist a first-class `blocked_stop` event with `blockedBy`, `gateResults`, and `recoveryStrategy`, then continue the loop. The same stop path can be vetoed by graph convergence or by a failed claim adjudication packet, and stuck recovery pivots toward the least-covered dimension instead of repeating the same exhausted pass.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md:29:| `references/protocol/loop_protocol.md` | Protocol | Wires convergence, pause sentinel checks, blocked-stop persistence, and loop-decision behavior into the lifecycle. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md:30:| `references/state/state_format.md` | Schema | Defines blocked-stop, graph-convergence, pause, recovery, and synthesis event shapes in JSONL. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md:31:| `assets/review_mode_contract.yaml` | Contract | Declares convergence defaults, severity math, quality gates, verdicts, and release-readiness states. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md:40:| `manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-review.md` | Manual scenario | Verifies graph-assisted convergence and blockers. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md:41:| `manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md` | Manual scenario | Confirms blocked-stop events surface into reducer-owned state. |

Single-executor path unchanged."

.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:1:This release lands Phase 008 of the 042 Deep Research & Review Runtime Improvement Bundle - closing the 12 P0/P1/P2 recommendations surfaced by the 20-iteration deep-research audit. `deep-research` now actively utilizes the coverage graph on the live loop path: `deep_loop_graph_upsert` and `deep_loop_graph_convergence` are called from the auto/confirm YAML workflows, reducer-owned surfaces expose `graphConvergenceScore` / `graphDecision` / `graphBlockers`, and blocked-stop JSONL events surface in the findings registry, dashboard, and strategy `next-focus` anchor. The goal is to make the workflow easier to follow and easier to trust.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:7:- `step_emit_blocked_stop` wires a first-class `blocked_stop` JSONL event into both research workflows. Each event carries `blockedBy`, `gateResults`, `recoveryStrategy`, and full session run history so downstream reducers can surface the exact blocker.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:9:- The reducer no longer filters non-iteration JSONL rows. `blocked_stop`, `userPaused`, and `stuckRecovery` events are preserved in the `events` stream and consumed by the blocked stop history builder / the graph convergence rollup builder.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:16:- Both `deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml` and `deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml` now call `deep_loop_graph_upsert` after `step_reduce_state` (persisting iteration `graphEvents` to the SQLite store) and `deep_loop_graph_convergence` before the inline 3-signal vote in `step_check_convergence`. The combined stop rule: STOP requires BOTH the inline vote and graph decision to agree; `STOP_BLOCKED` routes to the `blocked_stop` emission path.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:17:- The reducer's `buildGraphConvergenceRollup(events)` consumes `graph_convergence` JSONL events and exposes `graphConvergenceScore`, `graphDecision`, `graphBlockers` as new registry fields alongside `convergenceScore`.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:24:- the update strategy content helper overrides the `next-focus` anchor with the latest blocked-stop `recoveryStrategy` when blocked-stop is the most recent loop event, so operators see the blocker and recovery hint before choosing the next iteration direction.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:25:- `state_format.md` §5 documents all new registry fields, the `blocked_stop` and `graph_convergence` event schemas, and the combined-stop rule.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:46:- New playbook scenarios under `manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/`: `blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md` and `graph-aware-stop-gate.md`.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:53:- Reducer smoke tests on the new fixtures: `iterationsCompleted: 2` recovery, `blockedStopHistory` surfaces correctly, `graphConvergenceScore` defaults to 0 when no events exist.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:63:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/references/state_format.md`                                                  | Modified | Documented `blocked_stop`, `userPaused`, `stuckRecovery`, `graph_convergence` event schemas |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:64:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md`                                                 | Modified | Legal-stop → blocked_stop emission contract                                              |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:65:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs`                                                    | Modified | Preserves non-iteration events; adds `blockedStopHistory`, `graphConvergenceScore`, dashboard sections |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:67:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md` | Created  | Manual test for blocked-stop reducer surfacing                                           |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:69:| `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml`                                           | Modified | Wired step_emit_blocked_stop + step_normalize_pause_events + graph upsert + graph convergence |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:88:No migration is required for existing research packets. New registry fields (`blockedStopHistory`, `graphConvergenceScore`, `graphDecision`, `graphBlockers`) are additive only. Existing v1.5.0.0 packets resume cleanly under the new reducer logic. Raw JSONL events like `paused` and `stuck_recovery` are rewritten to their normalized enum values at emission time - packets created under v1.5.0.0 remain readable, but new emissions will use the normalized forms.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:3:description: "Iterative code-review skill that runs multiple review cycles with fresh context per iteration, file-based state continuity, severity-weighted convergence detection, and a binary release-readiness verdict."
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:8:  - "release readiness"
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:17:> Single-pass review misses cross-reference drift and severity misclassifications. deep-review runs multi-iteration audit cycles with fresh context per pass, severity-weighted convergence, and a PASS / CONDITIONAL / FAIL release-readiness verdict.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:62:| Verdict | PASS, CONDITIONAL, FAIL (binary release gate) | Findings only |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:63:| Best for | Release gates, audits where misses are expensive | Quick feedback during active development |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:70:- **Semantic coverage graph** persists per-iteration `graphEvents` so structural blind spots block premature STOP.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:118:grep -E "^Release Readiness Verdict|^## " {spec_folder}/review/review-report.md
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:145:| Convergence check | Three-signal weighted vote plus blocked-stop persistence, graph-assisted review gates, and stuck recovery, STOP requires convergence math + gate bundle agreement | [`feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md`](./feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md) |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:154:| JSONL state log | Append-only execution history, opens with config record, carries iteration + pause + blocked-stop + synthesis events, can include graph convergence + graphEvents payloads | [`feature_catalog/02--state-management/01-jsonl-state-log.md`](./feature_catalog/02--state-management/01-jsonl-state-log.md) |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:157:| Findings registry | Reducer-owned view of active / resolved / repeated / blocked findings, severity totals, dimension coverage, blocked-stop + graph history | [`feature_catalog/02--state-management/04-findings-registry.md`](./feature_catalog/02--state-management/04-findings-registry.md) |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:177:| Quality gates | Legal-stop bundle: evidence + scope + coverage + P0 resolution + evidence density + hotspot saturation + claim adjudication, failed votes persist a `blocked_stop` event and continue with a recovery hint | [`feature_catalog/04--severity-system/05-quality-gates.md`](./feature_catalog/04--severity-system/05-quality-gates.md) |

.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:321:User request: Audit the deep-research skill before the release cleanup ships.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:355:| P0 findings keep blocking convergence | Real P0s surviving adversarial check, or misclassified P1s elevated to P0 | Open the relevant `iterations/iteration-NNN.md`, read the Hunter / Skeptic / Referee output. If misclassified, downgrade severity in the JSONL entry and re-run convergence evaluation. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:369:A: `sk-code-review` is a single-pass review. `deep-review` runs multiple iterations until convergence, tracks dimension coverage, runs adversarial self-checks on P0 findings, and produces a structured nine-section `review-report.md` with a three-way verdict. Use `sk-code-review` for quick feedback during active development. Use `deep-review` for release gates and audits where missed findings are expensive.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:385:A: Release is blocked. Run `/speckit:plan` to create a remediation plan from the findings in `{spec_folder}/review/review-report.md`, implement the fixes, then re-run `deep-review` to confirm resolution. The re-run uses auto-resume and continues from prior state.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:409:`deep-review` depends on [`.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/`](../deep-loop-runtime/) for the executor, state schema, and coverage-graph runtime (post-118 FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP split from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`).

.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:451:| [`deep-loop-runtime`](../deep-loop-runtime/) | Owns the executor, state schema, and coverage-graph runtime that `deep-review` consumes |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:453:| [`sk-code-review`](../sk-code-review/) | Single-pass baseline, use for quick feedback, use `deep-review` for release gates |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:476:| Release readiness | `in-progress`, `converged`, `release-blocking` |

.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md:3:description: "Applies the statistical stop vote, legal-stop gates, and blocked-stop handling before research can end."
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md:10:Applies the statistical stop vote, legal-stop gates, and blocked-stop handling before research can end.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md:20:That legal-stop bundle is workflow-visible, not only prose guidance. The YAML workflows record graph convergence results when available, evaluate the gate bundle, and emit a first-class `blocked_stop` event if the stop is not yet legal. That event carries gate results, blockers, recovery guidance, session identifier, and generation so the reducer and dashboard can show why the loop had to continue.
