.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md:33:| `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml` | Workflow | Mirrors the same convergence and blocked-stop flow with confirm-mode approvals. |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/01--loop-lifecycle/03-convergence-check.md:41:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md` | Manual playbook | Verifies blocked-stop events surface through reducer-owned packet outputs. |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.1.0.md:1:This patch release closes the remaining 042 closing-audit fallout for `deep-research`. It retracts two lifecycle modes that never actually worked, documents the graph-event format the runtime already depends on, and fixes session-collision risk in the shared graph cache.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.1.0.md:13:When a research packet already existed, the workflow asked operators to pick how to continue: resume the same run, start a fresh run, `fork` into a sibling run history, or `completed-continue` (reopen a finished run after snapshotting its synthesis). The first two were real. The last two were described in several references but had no workflow wiring - picking them did the same thing as `resume` and the packet quietly kept the old session id. Operators who thought they had branched a research run history ended up overwriting the original. `fork` and `completed-continue` are now explicitly marked as deferred (reserved for a future release) everywhere the skill mentions them - `SKILL.md`, `README.md`, the two loop-protocol references, both command workflows, and the runtime agent definitions across all three CLI mirrors (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini). The visible lifecycle choices are now `new`, `resume`, and `restart`. `resume` keeps the current session id and appends a typed `resumed` event to the state log. `restart` archives the existing `research/` folder under a timestamped `research_archive/` directory, mints a fresh session id, and appends a typed `restarted` event with the archive path. Both events carry the full run history field set the reducer reads.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.1.0.md:37:No new tests were added for the deep-research side of this release - the closing-audit remediation work added a shared-ID collision regression to `session-isolation.vitest.ts`, which covers the graph database change above.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.1.0.md:89:| `.opencode/changelog/12--deep-research/v1.5.0.0.md` | Append-only "Update (post-release)" block pointing at this release |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.6.1.0.md:97:No migration is required. Existing research packets continue to resume exactly as before. Operators who were relying on the `fork` or `completed-continue` lifecycle choices should archive the prior `research/` folder manually or pick `restart`, which does both in one step. The two deferred modes remain reserved for a future release and will return only when they ship with first-class event emission and replay coverage.

.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:33:Sets up a research packet and locks the lineage contract before the first iteration runs.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:69:The loop first applies hard stops and the three-signal `shouldContinue()` vote, then runs the legal-stop gate bundle and optional graph convergence check before accepting STOP. When the legal-stop bundle blocks a stop, the workflow records a first-class `blocked_stop` event and keeps the loop running with an explicit recovery hint.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:114:`step_fanout_spawn` (CLI pool + native sequential agent dispatch), and `step_fanout_merge`
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:115:at the top of `phase_synthesis`. Single-executor path is byte-identical to pre-change
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:120:Three new YAML steps gated on `config.fanout` presence. CLI lineages run via `fanout-run.cjs`
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:122:dispatches. `step_fanout_merge` produces a consolidated `deep-research-findings-registry.json`
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:123:before `step_compile_research` runs. Command flags: `--executor` (repeatable), `--executors
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:124:<json>`, `--concurrency N`. Default policy: 0–1 executor → `config.executor`; 2+ →
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:125:`config.fanout`.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:129:See [`01--loop-lifecycle/07-fanout-dispatch.md`](01--loop-lifecycle/07-fanout-dispatch.md) for full implementation and validation file listings.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:145:`deep-research-state.jsonl` stores the config record, iteration records, and typed events such as `resumed`, `restarted`, `blocked_stop`, `graph_convergence`, and `synthesis_complete`. Iteration lines can carry `ruledOut`, `noveltyJustification`, `convergenceSignals`, `focusTrack`, `sourceStrength`, and `graphEvents`, and the reader contract explicitly tolerates malformed lines by skipping them with defaults.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:225:Blocks weak or incomplete STOP decisions even after convergence math votes to stop.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:229:The legal-stop bundle checks coverage, evidence density, and research quality before STOP becomes final. Current guard behavior requires source diversity, focus alignment with the original key-question set, and protection against single weak-source answers. Failures emit `guard_violation` and `blocked_stop` events and force the loop back to CONTINUE.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:245:When `graphEvents` are present, the workflow calls the graph convergence tool, appends a `graph_convergence` event, and folds graph score and blockers into the legal-stop gate path. The reducer surfaces the latest graph decision and blockers in `findings-registry.json` and `deep-research-dashboard.md`, while the system degrades cleanly when no graph data is available.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/README.md:2:title: Blocked Stop Fixture
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/README.md:3:description: End-to-end fixture for the deep-review blocked_stop reducer path.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/README.md:6:# Blocked Stop Fixture
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/README.md:8:This fixture demonstrates the full `blocked_stop -> registry -> dashboard` reducer path for a review packet.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/README.md:13:- A complete `blocked_stop` bundle with all required `gateResults`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/README.md:16:- Three iteration files that let the reducer populate `blockedStopHistory`, `persistentSameSeverity`, and `severityChanged`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/README.md:19:- `REQ-014` blocked-stop surfacing through the reducer and dashboard
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/README.md:20:- `REQ-021` reducer handling for blocked-stop history plus severity-transition tracking
.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session/README.md:26:  .opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/tests/fixtures/blocked-stop-session
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:1:This release lets you pick which AI actually runs each research iteration. Before, every iteration dispatched through the native research agent using Claude Opus. Now you can opt into running the iteration through a CLI executor such as `codex exec` with a chosen model, reasoning effort, plus service tier. The skill's YAML workflow still owns all the loop machinery (state tracking, convergence detection, lifecycle events, the reducer). The CLI is a tool inside the workflow, not a replacement for it. Ships as a symmetric pair with `deep-review` v1.5.0.0.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:3:> Spec folder: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/017-sk-deep-cli-runtime-execution/001-executor-feature` (Level 3)
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:11:Before this release, the YAML step that runs each iteration had a hardcoded line saying "dispatch the research agent using Opus". There was no way to pick a different executor without rewriting the YAML. The iteration step now branches on a config field called `executor.kind`. The `native` branch (the default) keeps the exact same agent dispatch as before. The new `cli-codex` branch runs `codex exec` with the model and flags pulled from the config, piping the iteration prompt through stdin. A user who does not set anything sees identical behavior to previous releases, so nothing breaks for existing sessions.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:17:Before, the iteration context (state summary, focus area, constraints, file paths) lived inline inside the YAML file. That worked for the native agent because the YAML runtime could pass the inline string directly into the agent's context. It did not work for a CLI executor which needs a file it can read. The YAML block has been extracted into a template file at `assets/prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl`. Both dispatch paths render the same template and see the same content. No implicit context gap between the native agent path and the CLI path.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:21:#### Config schema for executor selection
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:23:Adds a new `executor` block to `deep_research_config.json` with six fields: `kind`, `model`, `reasoningEffort`, `serviceTier`, `sandboxMode`, `timeoutSeconds`. Default is `kind: "native"` with every other field null. That means any existing config file keeps working without changes. Invalid combinations (picking `cli-codex` without setting a model, or picking an executor that is reserved but not yet wired) are caught at config load time with typed errors.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:29:Five new flags were added to the consolidated setup prompt in `deep-research.md`: `--executor`, `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`. Precedence is CLI flag first, then config file, then the schema default. If none of these are specified and the research topic does not mention an executor, the setup prompt adds a single optional question so the user can pick native or cli-codex. Existing invocations that use none of the new flags keep the native default.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:35:When a non-native executor runs an iteration, the workflow appends an `executor: {kind, model, reasoningEffort, serviceTier}` block to that iteration's JSONL record. Anyone reading the state log later can see exactly which executor produced each iteration. Native runs skip the audit because they are recoverable from the YAML itself.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:41:Every executor (native or CLI) must produce two things per iteration: a markdown narrative file plus a JSONL delta record with required fields. A new validator checks both after dispatch. If the iteration file is missing or empty, or if the JSONL delta was not appended, or if it is missing required fields, the validator emits a canonical `schema_mismatch` conflict event. Three consecutive validation failures trigger the existing stuck-recovery path.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:49:The forward-looking note at line 61 of `SKILL.md` that said "if the user specifies an executor CLI, that is the how" has been replaced with a full contract section. The section documents which kinds are shipped, which are reserved, what invariants every executor must respect, how failure is detected and recovered, plus where the config and template live. Anyone reading SKILL.md now sees the real behavior, not a forward-looking promise.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:67:Four new unit test suites shipped under `tests/deep-loop/`. `executor-config.vitest.ts` covers 16 schema and validation cases. `prompt-pack.vitest.ts` covers 11 template rendering cases. `post-dispatch-validate.vitest.ts` covers 8 invariant check cases. `executor-audit.vitest.ts` covers 5 JSONL merge cases. Total 40 tests, all green. Existing iteration tests pass unchanged with the native default.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:78:| `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml`                  | Two-branch `step_dispatch_iteration` with executor resolution, shared prompt pack render, post-dispatch validation, audit recording  |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:80:| `.opencode/skills/deep-research/assets/deep_research_config.json`                   | New `executor` block with six fields (defaults preserve native)                                                                      |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:82:| `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts`         | Shared Zod schema, parseExecutorConfig, resolveExecutorConfig, ExecutorNotWiredError, ExecutorConfigError (new file)                 |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:85:| `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts`          | Shared buildExecutorAuditRecord, appendExecutorAuditToLastRecord (new file)                                                          |
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:93:`SKILL.md` gets the new Executor Selection Contract section. `references/loop_protocol.md` gets the new Executor Resolution subsection in section 3. `.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md` gets the five new setup flags plus the conditional executor question.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:101:No migration required. `executor.kind` defaults to `native`, which preserves pre-release behavior exactly. Same agent dispatch, same iteration file, same JSONL record.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-review.md:3:description: "Verify that graph-backed dimension coverage can block premature STOP in deep review."
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-review.md:14:This scenario validates graph-backed legal-stop behavior for `DRV-032`. The objective is to verify that when review stability signals nominate STOP but graph-backed dimension coverage remains below threshold, the legal-stop gates block premature STOP.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-review.md:29:- Then: Graph convergence gates block premature STOP.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-review.md:31:- Prompt: `Validate the graph-backed legal-stop gate and report whether blocked-stop state persists when coverage fails.`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-review.md:32:- Expected execution process: Inspect the deep-review convergence reference for legal-stop gate behavior first, then the coverage-graph convergence handler for review `dimensionCoverage` thresholds, then fixture evidence for persisted `blocked_stop`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-review.md:34:- Expected signals: review convergence docs describe `blockedStop` when legal-stop gates fail. Graph convergence handler enforces review `dimensionCoverage`. Fixture evidence shows `blocked_stop` with `blockedBy: ["dimensionCoverage", ...]`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-review.md:35:- Pass/fail posture: PASS if graph-backed review convergence, legal-stop failure, and blocked-stop persistence line up across the review convergence reference, graph convergence handler, and fixture evidence. FAIL if stable findings can terminate the loop despite dimension-coverage failure.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-review.md:48:Validate the graph-backed legal-stop gate and report whether blocked-stop state persists when coverage fails.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-review.md:52:3. `bash: rg -n 'blocked_stop|blockedStop|dimensionCoverage' .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/fixtures/deep-loop-optimizer/sample-040-corpus.jsonl`

.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-review.md:56:Capture the review convergence legal-stop wording, the handler threshold/check for review `dimensionCoverage`, and the sample blocked-stop JSONL record naming `dimensionCoverage` in `blockedBy`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/graph-convergence-review.md:58:PASS if the review docs, graph convergence handler, and blocked-stop fixture all agree that dimension-coverage failure prevents STOP even when other signals are favorable. .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:382:Appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard; repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append; fsyncs writes; exposes round-state readers for resume.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/fail-closed-reducer.md:3:description: "Verify that malformed JSONL exits 2 unless --lenient is passed, and missing machine-owned anchors block reducer writes unless --create-missing-anchors is used."
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/fail-closed-reducer.md:14:This scenario validates fail-closed reducer behavior for `DRV-034`. The objective is to verify that malformed JSONL blocks the reducer unless `--lenient` is passed, that missing machine-owned anchors block strategy rewrites unless `--create-missing-anchors` is passed, and that `corruptionWarnings` remains visible in reducer-owned state even when lenient recovery is used.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/fail-closed-reducer.md:18:Fail-closed behavior protects review state from quietly drifting into partial writes or ambiguous recovery. Operators need a predictable contract: corruption must stop the reducer by default, missing anchors must block unsafe rewrites by default, and both escape hatches must be explicit so recovery stays intentional.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/fail-closed-reducer.md:45:3. Capture exit codes, stderr, registry warnings, and the anchor block so another operator can replay both fail-closed paths and both explicit recovery paths.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:2:title: "DRV-017 -- P0 override blocks convergence"
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:3:description: "Verify that a new P0 finding sets newFindingsRatio >= 0.50, blocking convergence regardless of other signals."
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:6:# DRV-017 -- P0 override blocks convergence
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:14:This scenario validates P0 override blocks convergence for `DRV-017`. The objective is to verify that any new P0 finding sets `newFindingsRatio >= 0.50`, which is high enough to block the composite convergence score from reaching the 0.60 stop threshold.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:26:- Objective: Verify new P0 finding sets newFindingsRatio >= 0.50, blocking convergence.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:28:- Prompt: `Validate that new P0 findings block deep-review convergence by forcing a high newFindingsRatio.`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:31:- Expected signals: P0 finding sets `newFindingsRatio >= 0.50`, this blocks the rolling average signal from contributing to convergence, the composite score cannot reach 0.60, and review continues.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:32:- Pass/fail posture: PASS if the P0 override is enforced and documented as blocking convergence. FAIL if convergence can trigger despite an active new P0 finding.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:45:Validate that new P0 findings block deep-review convergence by forcing a high newFindingsRatio.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:47:1. `bash: rg -n 'P0.*override|P0.*block|P0.*convergence|newFindingsRatio.*0\.50|severity.*override|P0.*ratio' .opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:48:2. `bash: rg -n 'P0|severity_override|newFindingsRatio|p0_override|p0_block' .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:49:3. `bash: rg -n 'P0 override|P0.*block.*convergence|newFindingsRatio.*0\.50|severity.*override' .opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/quick_reference.md .opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md .opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:51:P0 finding sets `newFindingsRatio >= 0.50`, this blocks the rolling average signal, the composite score cannot reach 0.60, and the review continues.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:55:PASS if the P0 override is enforced and documented as blocking convergence. FAIL if convergence can trigger despite an active new P0 finding.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:77:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md` | Severity classification and P0 blocking rules, use `ANCHOR:how-it-works` and `ANCHOR:rules` |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md:87:- Feature file path: `04--convergence-and-recovery/p0-override-blocks-convergence.md`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:2:title: "DRV-033 -- Review reducer surfaces blocked-stop history across registry, dashboard, and next-focus"
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:3:description: "Verify that blocked_stop events preserve the review gate bundle in blockedStopHistory, render in the dashboard, and rewrite the strategy next-focus anchor with recovery guidance."
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:6:# DRV-033 -- Review reducer surfaces blocked-stop history across registry, dashboard, and next-focus
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:14:This scenario validates blocked-stop reducer surfacing for `DRV-033`. The objective is to verify that a review packet with at least one `blocked_stop` event preserves the review-specific legal-stop bundle in `blockedStopHistory`, renders that blocked-stop evidence in the dashboard, and rewrites the strategy `next-focus` anchor with the recovery strategy.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:18:Review blocked-stop events are the operator-facing explanation for why convergence could not legally stop. If the reducer does not surface `convergenceGate`, `dimensionCoverageGate`, `p0ResolutionGate`, `evidenceDensityGate`, and `hotspotSaturationGate` across the registry, dashboard, and strategy anchor, the operator loses both the veto reason and the next action.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:26:- Title: Review reducer surfaces blocked-stop history across registry, dashboard, and next-focus.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:27:- Given: A review packet with at least one `blocked_stop` event whose `blockedBy` and `gateResults` use the review-specific legal-stop names `convergenceGate`, `dimensionCoverageGate`, `p0ResolutionGate`, `evidenceDensityGate`, and `hotspotSaturationGate`.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:29:- Then: `deep-review-findings-registry.json` exposes `blockedStopHistory` entries that preserve the review gate names, `deep-review-dashboard.md` renders `BLOCKED STOPS`, and the `ANCHOR:next-focus` block in `deep-review-strategy.md` contains the blocked-stop recovery strategy.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:30:- Real user request: If review convergence gets blocked, where do I see which review gate failed and what next-focus guidance the reducer surfaced for recovery?
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:31:- Prompt: `Validate blocked-stop reducer surfacing in deep-review dashboard and strategy recovery output.`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:33:- Desired user-facing outcome: The user can point to the exact review gate that blocked STOP and explain which recovery instruction the reducer surfaced next.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:34:- Expected signals: `blockedStopHistory` is non-empty. Review entries preserve `convergenceGate`, `dimensionCoverageGate`, `p0ResolutionGate`, `evidenceDensityGate`, and `hotspotSaturationGate`. `BLOCKED STOPS` renders the same blocked-stop data. The strategy `next-focus` anchor contains the blocked-stop recovery strategy.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:35:- Pass/fail posture: PASS if all three reducer-owned review surfaces show the blocked-stop data and the review-specific gate names remain intact. FAIL if any surface is missing, stale, or drops the review gate bundle.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:49:Validate blocked-stop reducer surfacing in deep-review dashboard and strategy recovery output.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:53:2. `bash: cat {spec_folder}/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json | jq '.blockedStopHistory'`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:58:`blockedStopHistory` contains reducer-promoted blocked-stop entries with the review gate names. `BLOCKED STOPS` renders each blocked-stop event. `ANCHOR:next-focus` includes the recovery strategy from the latest blocked-stop record.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:61:Capture the populated `blockedStopHistory` array, the dashboard `BLOCKED STOPS` excerpt, and the strategy `next-focus` anchor showing the recovery guidance.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:64:PASS if all three review surfaces show the same blocked-stop data and preserve the review gate names. FAIL if any surface is missing blocked-stop data or the gate bundle is incomplete after the reducer run.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:83:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Canonical reducer implementation, promotes `blocked_stop` into `blockedStopHistory`, renders `BLOCKED STOPS`, and rewrites `ANCHOR:next-focus` |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:84:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md` | Review state contract, defines `blockedStopHistory`, dashboard `BLOCKED STOPS`, strategy next-focus override, and review-specific gate payload shape |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:85:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md` | Review legal-stop workflow contract, defines the review-specific gate names used in `blocked_stop` events |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:94:- Feature file path: `04--convergence-and-recovery/blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md`

.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:1:This release updates deep-research with clearer support for request routing. It focuses on request routing so the release is easier to understand and use.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:6:- This part of the release focused on improving request routing.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:7:- This release updates the workflow around request routing.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.4.0.0.md:1:This release gives `deep-research` a fully explicit, executable packet expected behavior. The research loop now has main run history and lifecycle rules, reducer-owned state surfaces, runtime-parity guidance across all mirrors, and focused expected behavior tests that lock the behavior in place. The goal is to make the workflow easier to follow and easier to trust.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.12.0.0.md:1:This release rebinds deep-research onto the new `deep-loop-runtime` peer skill (arc 118, FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP). The 13 lib files plus the 4 graph operations (convergence, upsert, query, status) previously hosted inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/` now live in `deep-loop-runtime/`. Deep-research workflow YAMLs invoke `.cjs` scripts directly instead of the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools.

skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/fallback-router.ts\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/prompt-pack.ts\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/bayesian-scorer.ts\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/session-state-hierarchy.cjs\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/round-state-jsonl.cjs\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/cost-guards.cjs\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/tests/.gitkeep\n\n(Results .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:10875:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:9354:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2806:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/review/hs/logs/iter-009.out:43:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780205290663,"sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_function_4vnhn0n98zpe_1","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: ---\n2: name: deep-loop-runtime\n3: version: 1.4.0\n4: description: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\n5: allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n6: ---\n7: \n8: # Deep Loop Runtime\n9: \n10: Shared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n11: \n12: ---\n13: \n14: ## 1. WHEN TO USE\n15: \n16: This skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n17: \n18: Use it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n19: \n20: - executor configuration and audit metadata\n21: - prompt-pack rendering\n22: - post-dispatch validation\n23: - atomic state files and JSONL repair\n24: - loop locking and permissions gates\n25: - Bayesian scoring and fallback routing\n26: - coverage-graph schema, query, and signal interpretation\n27: \n28: ### Activation Triggers\n29: \n30: This skill is activated when:\n31: \n32: - A deep-review or deep-research workflow YAML invokes `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/<name>.cjs`\n33: - A test file imports `from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/...'`\n34: - An operator queries / mutates the coverage graph via the runtime scripts\n35: - A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing\n36: \n37: ### When NOT to Use\n38: \n39: Do not invoke this skill when:\n40: \n41: - A user requests an interactive command (use `/deep:start-review-loop` or `/deep:start-research-loop` instead — those orchestrate this runtime)\n42: - A package outside deep-review / deep-research needs deep-loop semantics (out of supported scope; open a packet to extend ownership)\n43: - An MCP tool surface is needed (this skill explicitly has none — the isolation ADR removed the deep-loop MCP tools)\n44: \n45: ---\n46: \n47: ## 2. SMART ROUTING\n48: \n49: This skill has no direct LLM-facing keyword triggers — it is invoked by workflow YAMLs (via `bash:` script calls) and by test imports. The router below documents which sub-domain to load for a given consumer query.\n50: \n51: ### Primary Detection Signal\n52: \n53: ```bash\n54: request_text=\"$(printf '%s' \"$USER_REQUEST\" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')\"\n55: case \"$request_text\" in\n56:   *\"deep-loop runtime\"*|*\"deep-loop-runtime\"*|*\"executor config\"*|*\"prompt pack\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n57:   *\"coverage graph\"*|*\"deep_loop_graph\"*|*\"bayesian scorer\"*|*\"loop lock\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n58:   *\"fallback router\"*|*\"post-dispatch validate\"*|*\"jsonl repair\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n59:   *) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=0 ;;\n60: esac\n61: ```\n62: \n63: ### Phase Detection\n64: \n65: ```text\n66: TASK CONTEXT\n67:     |\n68:     +- STEP 0: Detect runtime-call intent vs. consumer-orchestration intent\n69:     +- STEP 1: If consumer (workflow YAML), invoke the relevant scripts/*.cjs\n70:     +- STEP 2: If contributor (modifying lib code), load matching lib/ subtree\n71:     +- STEP 3: If test author, load tests/ + lib/ pair\n72: ```\n73: \n74: ### Resource Domains\n75: \n76: ```text\n77: lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)\n78: lib/coverage-graph/*.ts       # 3 schema + query + signals modules\n79: scripts/*.cjs                 # 4 entry points (convergence, upsert, query, status)\n80: database/*.sqlite              # runtime-owned SQLite (deep-loop-graph.sqlite)\n81: tests/{unit,integration,lifecycle}/  # 21+ vitest files split by responsibility\n82: ```\n83: \n84: ### Resource Loading Levels\n85: \n86: | Tier | When loaded | Purpose |\n87: |------|-------------|---------|\n88: | ALWAYS | SKILL.md frontmatter | description, allowed-tools, version |\n89: | CONDITIONAL | When a runtime call dispatches | `lib/` modules required by the called function |\n90: | ON_DEMAND | When a test fails or a contributor extends | `tests/` + matching `lib/` sub-tree |\n91: \n92: ### Smart Router Pseudocode\n93: \n94: ```text\n95: IF request mentions a specific lib name (e.g. \"bayesian-scorer\", \"prompt-pack\"):\n96:   → Load lib/deep-loop/<name>.ts + matching tests/unit/<name>.vitest.ts\n97: ELIF request mentions coverage-graph / convergence / signals:\n98:   → Load lib/coverage-graph/*.ts + tests/integration/review-depth-*.vitest.ts\n99: ELIF request mentions a script (convergence.cjs, status.cjs, etc.):\n100:   → Load scripts/<name>.cjs + tests/integration/<name>-script.vitest.ts + lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts\n101: ELSE:\n102:   → Defer to consumer skill (deep-review / deep-research)\n103: ```\n104: \n105: ---\n106: \n107: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS\n108: \n109: ### Runtime Architecture\n110: \n111: The runtime exposes deep-loop primitives to consumer workflows through two paths: **direct `.cjs` script invocation** (replacing the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools) and **TypeScript library imports** (from workflow scripts and tests).\n112: \n113: ```text\n114: [deep-review/deep-research workflow YAML]\n115:             │\n116:             ├─ bash: node .../scripts/<name>.cjs   (coverage-graph mutations)\n117:             │       └─→ lib/coverage-graph/*.ts → database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite\n118:             │\n119:             └─ ts-import: lib/deep-loop/<lib>.ts   (runtime primitives)\n120:                     └─→ atomic-state, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, etc.\n121: ```\n122: \n123: ### Library Structure\n124: \n125: **`lib/deep-loop/` (10 modules):**\n126: - `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings\n127: - `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)\n128: - `prompt-pack.ts` — renders the iteration prompt template\n129: - `post-dispatch-validate.ts` — validates iteration outputs (markdown + JSONL + delta)\n130: - `atomic-state.ts` — atomic state-log writes\n131: - `jsonl-repair.ts` — recovers corrupt JSONL state lines\n132: - `loop-lock.ts` — single-writer locking\n133: - `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks\n134: - `bayesian-scorer.ts` — convergence scoring\n135: - `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix\n136: \n137: **`lib/coverage-graph/` (3 modules):**\n138: - `coverage-graph-db.ts` — SQLite schema + node-kind allow-list + connection lifecycle\n139: - `coverage-graph-query.ts` — query builders\n140: - `coverage-graph-signals.ts` — convergence signal extraction\n141: \n142: ### Council Primitives\n143: \n144: The Runtime Boundary Decision (ADR-001) extends `deep-loop-runtime/lib/` with council-compatible infrastructure primitives while keeping operator-facing and domain workflow semantics in `deep-ai-council`. These primitives are consumed by downstream deep-ai-council orchestration (per-topic multi-round, session-findings registry, command and skill wiring, parity and cost docs) for per-topic orchestration, multi-topic session state, command wiring, parity tests and docs.\n145: \n146: **`lib/council/` (5 modules):**\n147: - `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.\n148: - `round-state-jsonl.cjs` — appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard, repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append, fsyncs writes, and exposes round-state readers for resume.\n149: - `adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs` — scores Round-N -> Round-N+1 adjudicator verdict deltas using ADR-003 weights for option change, confidence delta, material-risk Jaccard delta, axis flip rate, and blocking-disagreement delta.\n150: - `cost-guards.cjs` — normalizes and enforces ADR-004 defaults for `max_rounds_per_topic`, `max_topics_per_session`, `saturation_threshold`, and `seats_per_round`, including upper-bound seat-output calculation.\n151: - `session-state-hierarchy.cjs` — creates and validates the ADR-002 session -> topic -> round state shape, including stable `topic-NNN-slug` and `round-NNN` ids.\n152: \n153: The existing `lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts`, and `lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` remain the sibling runtime contracts. Council modules mirror those durability semantics in a council-scoped CJS surface so downstream `deep-ai-council` phases can consume them without modifying deep-review or deep-research behavior.\n154: \n155: ### Script Entry Points\n156: \n157: Four `.cjs` scripts replace the 4 deleted MCP tools, each honoring the same JSON-in / JSON-out contract documented in ADR-001 (the script interface contract):\n158: \n159: | Script | Replaces | Purpose | Exit codes |\n160: |--------|----------|---------|------------|\n161: | `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes typed CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED decisions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n162: | `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores nodes + edges from iteration `graphEvents` | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n163: | `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims, contradictions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n164: | `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Session-scoped health report | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n165: | `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |\n166: | `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |\n167: | `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |\n168: | `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |\n169: \n170: **Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.\n171: \n172: Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.\n173: \n174: ### Test Organization\n175: \n176: ```text\n177: tests/\n178: ├── unit/         # 13 files — per-module unit tests (atomic-state, executor-config, etc.)\n179: ├── integration/  # 7 files — script invocations + review-depth fixtures\n180: ├── lifecycle/    # 1 file — db-open-close pattern verification\n181: └── _helpers/     # 1 file — spawn-cjs helper for direct-invocation tests\n182: ```\n183: \n184: The system-spec-kit `mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` so a single vitest run discovers both surfaces.\n185: \n186: ---\n187: \n188: ## 4. RULES\n189: \n190: ### ALWAYS\n191: \n192: - **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)\n193: - **ALWAYS** emit JSON-only output to stdout from `scripts/*.cjs` (workflow YAML parses stdout for output bindings)\n194: - **ALWAYS** include `'use strict';` on line 2 of every `.cjs` script (per sk-code OPENCODE JS style)\n195: - **ALWAYS** include a `MODULE: <name>` header comment in every `lib/**/*.ts` file (per sk-code OPENCODE TS-MODULE-HEADER convention)\n196: - **ALWAYS** preserve atomic-state semantics when mutating JSONL state — never partial-write\n197: - **ALWAYS** acquire `loop-lock` before any state-log mutation\n198: \n199: ### NEVER\n200: \n201: - **NEVER** register MCP tools from this skill — the isolation ADR explicitly removed the MCP surface; reintroducing tools defeats the FULL_ISOLATE direction\n202: - **NEVER** open the SQLite DB outside `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` — single owner of the connection\n203: - **NEVER** import from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/` or `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/` — those locations are empty after the runtime moved here; import from this skill instead\n204: - **NEVER** write to `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` without holding the `loop-lock`\n205: - **NEVER** swallow exceptions in script entry points — propagate to the exit-code matrix\n206: \n207: ### ESCALATE IF\n208: \n209: - **ESCALATE** to the user if a runtime call requires a new MCP tool — the architectural direction prohibits MCP additions; the user must decide on policy override\n210: - **ESCALATE** to a new ownership ADR if a new consumer skill (beyond deep-review and deep-research) needs deep-loop runtime — extension requires a new ownership ADR\n211: - **ESCALATE** if the SQLite schema requires a backward-incompatible change — coordinate with all consumer workflows + add migration to `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n212: - **ESCALATE** if a script's stdout JSON contract changes — every workflow YAML output binding depends on the existing shape\n213: \n214: ---\n215: \n216: ## 5. SCOPE\n217: \n218: In scope:\n219: \n220: - runtime libraries under `lib/deep-loop/`\n221: - coverage-graph runtime libraries under `lib/coverage-graph/`\n222: - `.cjs` script entry points under `scripts/`\n223: - runtime-owned SQLite storage under `database/`\n224: - runtime tests under `tests/`\n225: \n226: Out of scope:\n227: \n228: - MCP tool registration\n229: - MCP handler ownership\n230: - DB lifecycle inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`\n231: - direct user command routing\n232: \n233: ---\n234: \n235: ## 6. ARCHITECTURE\n236: \n237: - `lib/deep-loop/` holds shared deep-loop runtime libraries.\n238: - `lib/coverage-graph/` holds coverage-graph schema, query, and signal helpers.\n239: - `scripts/` holds `.cjs` entry points used by workflow YAML and collateral commands.\n240: - `database/` owns relocated runtime SQLite artifacts.\n241: - `tests/` holds runtime-owned tests split from the MCP server surface.\n242: \n243: Shipped layout:\n244: \n245: | Path | Populated By | Purpose |\n246: |------|--------------|---------|\n247: | `lib/deep-loop/` | 10 files | Shared executor config, audit, prompt-pack, validation, state, repair, locking, permissions, scoring, and fallback routing. |\n248: | `lib/coverage-graph/` | 3 files | Coverage-graph schema owner, query helpers, and signal interpretation. |\n249: | `scripts/` | 4 files | Direct `status`, `query`, `upsert`, and `convergence` entry points replacing the removed deep-loop MCP tools. |\n250: | `database/` | SQLite | Runtime-owned `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |\n251: | `tests/` | Unit, integration, lifecycle | Runtime-owned tests discovered by the system-spec-kit MCP server Vitest config. |\n252: \n253: ---\n254: \n255: ## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS\n256: \n257: - **ADR-001 (AI Council SPLIT ruling)**: original AI Council SPLIT ruling. Superseded by user directive.\n258: - **Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP)**: Moves deep-loop runtime, coverage-graph schema/query/signals, script entry points, storage, and tests into this peer skill while removing the deep-loop MCP tool surface.\n259: - **Deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML** call this runtime through script entry points after the runtime cutover.\n260: - **system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts** includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` for cross-package test discovery.\n261: - **deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs** depends on the moved coverage-graph runtime via lib imports.\n262: \n263: ---\n264: \n265: ## 8. SUCCESS CRITERIA\n266: \n267: The runtime is healthy when:\n268: \n269: - Every `scripts/` entry point (`status`, `query`, `upsert`, `convergence`) returns valid JSON on its documented stdout contract\n270: - Consumer workflows resolve their `lib/` imports without path errors\n271: - The runtime-owned SQLite under `database/` is present and its coverage-graph schema matches `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n272: - Runtime tests under `tests/` pass through the system-spec-kit Vitest discovery glob\n273: \n274: ---\n275: \n276: ## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES\n277: \n278: - ADR-001 (script interface contract): documents the script-shim and DB-relocation contract.\n279: - Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP): documents the MCP tool surface removal rationale.\n280: - Decision rationale lives in this skill's changelog and originating decision records.\n281: \n282: Related resources:\n283: \n284: - Consumer skills: the `deep-review` and `deep-research` workflows call this runtime through the script entry points\n285: - `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` discovers this skill's `tests/`\n286: - Per-release detail and rationale: this skill's `changelog/` directory\n\n(End of file - total 286 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"---\nname: deep-loop-runtime\nversion: 1.4.0\ndescription: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\nallowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n---\n\n# Deep Loop Runtime\n\nShared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n\n---\n\n## 1. WHEN TO USE\n\nThis skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n\nUse it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n\n- executor configuration and audit metadata","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md","time":{"start":1780205290639,"end":1780205290662}},"id":"prt_e7c81029a0018WgctXtXLcmFh5","sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","messageID":"msg_e7c80eee8001ZiIO6I4pjCr85X"}}

depth (CALLS structurally regex-only) + RQ-03 depth (5-state trust-state, INV-F5 concurrency exposures) + RQ-04 groundwork (two-cycle magnitude-blind, 3/12 gates typed boolean)","graphEvents":[{"type":"node","id":"code-graph/lib/tree-sitter-parser.ts","label":"AST walker — has NO call_expression
