accumulator"},{"type":"edge","id":"E8","relation":"structural_gap","source":"code-graph/lib/tree-sitter-parser.ts","target":"code-graph/lib/structural-indexer.ts::CALLS-regex"},{"type":"edge","id":"E9","relation":"overrides_provenance","source":"code-graph/lib/structural-indexer.ts::CALLS","target":"code-graph/lib/tree-sitter-parser.ts::detectorProvenance"},{"type":"edge","id":"E10","relation":"priority_branch","source":"skill-advisor/lib/freshness/trust-state.ts::createTrustState","target":"unavailable>absent>stale>live"},{"type":"edge","id":"E11","relation":"captures_but_ignores","source":"skill-advisor/lib/promotion/shadow-cycle.ts::deltaVsLive","target":"skill-advisor/lib/promotion/two-cycle-requirement.ts::consecutivePasses"},{"type":"edge","id":"E12","relation":"typed_boolean","source":"skill-advisor/lib/promotion/gate-bundle.ts::paritySlices","target":"ambiguityStable/derivedLaneAttributionComplete/stuffingRejected"}]}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:10878:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:9357:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3284:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/003-embedder-testing-and-architecture/001-local-embeddings-foundation/016-llama-cpp-retrieval-quality-probe/scratch/probe-corpus.json:455:    "content": "---\ntitle: \"...it/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-runtime-executor-hardening/001-foundational-runtime/implementation-summary]\"\ndescription: \"Phase 017 complete: 25 commits shipped to main closing all 10 original review P1 findings + 9 new segment-2 P1 findings + 10 P2 refactors. Verdict: CONDITIONAL → PASS. Headline: H-56-1 canonical save metadata no-op eliminated. Two new clusters remediated: D (code-graph 6:1 sibling asymmetry) and E (Copilot runtime observability gap).\"\ntrigger_phrases:\n  - \"017 implementation summary\"\n  - \"phase 017 summary\"\n  - \"review findings remediation summary\"\n  - \"h-56-1 fix\"\n  - \"canonical save metadata\"\nimportance_tier: \"critical\"\ncontextType: \"implementation\"\ntemplate_source_hint: \"<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: impl-summary-core | v2.2 -->\"\n_memory:\n  continuity:\n    packet_pointer: \"system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-runtime-executor-hardening/001-foundational-runtime\"\n    last_updated_at: \"2026-04-17T18:00:00Z\"\n    last_updated_by: \"claude-opus-4.7\"\n    recent_action: \"Phase 017 implementation complete: 25 commits landed on main across 4 waves (A=5, B=10, C=4, D=3) + 3 support commits. All 27 tasks closed. Verdict CONDITIONAL → PASS with hasAdvisories=true for 8+3 parking-lot P2 items deferred to Phase 019.\"\n    next_safe_action: \"Draft v3.4.0.2 release + optional /deep:start-review-loop :auto on Phase 017 scope for final ship gate (if desired beyond smoke-test validation already performed). Phase 018 autonomous Copilot iteration unblocked.\"\n    blockers: []\n    completion_pct: 100\n    key_files:\n      - \"spec.md\"\n      - \"plan.md\"\n      - \"tasks.md\"\n      - \"checklist.md\"\n      - \"implementation-summary.md\"\n    session_dedup:\n      fingerprint: \"sha256:phase-017-remediation-complete-25-commits\"\n      session_id: \"017-implementation-2026-04-17\"\n      parent_session_id: \"016-foundational-runtime/001-initial-research\"\n    open_questions: []\n    answered_questions:\n      - \"H-56-1 canonical save metadata no-op\"\n      - \"Copilot compact-cache parity requirement\"\n      - \"MCP transport-layer caller identity mechanism\"\n      - \"trustState vocabulary alignment\"\n      - \"EVIDENCE marker false-positive detection strategy\"\n---\n# Implementation Summary: Phase 017 Review-Findings Remediation\n\n<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 2 -->\n<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: impl-summary-core | v2.2 -->\n<!-- HVR_REFERENCE: .opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/hvr_rules.md -->\n\n> **Status: COMPLETE.** Phase 017 shipped 25 commits to `main` on 2026-04-17, closing all 27 remediation tasks from the consolidated backlog (10 original review P1 + 9 new segment-2 P1 + 10 P2 refactors). Headline: **H-56-1 canonical save metadata no-op eliminated** via 2-line fix in `workflow.ts` + 38-folder lastUpdated cascade. Verdict transitions from **CONDITIONAL → PASS** with `hasAdvisories=true` for 8+3 parking-lot P2 items deferred to Phase 019.\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:metadata -->\n## Metadata\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Spec Folder** | `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-runtime-executor-hardening/001-foundational-runtime/` |\n| **Parent Packet** | `026-graph-and-context-optimization` |\n| **Started** | 2026-04-17 14:04 (spec folder scaffold) |\n| **Completed** | 2026-04-17 18:00 (Wave D Cluster D3 merged) |\n| **Level** | 2 |\n| **Stage** | COMPLETE — all 4 waves (A/B/C/D) shipped |\n| **Review Source** | `../review/016-foundational-runtime-pt-01/review-report.md` (7-iteration deep-review, verdict CONDITIONAL, 10 P1 + 18 P2) |\n| **Segment-2 Source** | `../research/016-foundational-runtime-pt-01/segment-2-synthesis.md` (7-iteration Opus meta-research, 14 new findings, 3 compound hypotheses) |\n| **Tasks Closed** | 27 (10 P1 review + 9 P1 segment-2 + 8 P2 Wave D) |\n| **Commits to Main** | 25 (Wave A=5, Wave B=10, Wave C=4, Wave D=3, Support=3) |\n| **Architectural Primitives Added** | 4 (AsyncLocalStorage caller-context, readiness-contract module, shared-provenance module, retry-budget) |\n| **New Test Files** | 16 vitest suites (~280 test cases total) |\n| **Effort Budget (planned)** | ~105h (3-engineer parallelized) / ~60h critical-path |\n| **Effort Actual (shipped)** | ~6 wall-clock hours via parallel cli-copilot gpt-5.4 high (3 concurrent max per `feedback_copilot_concurrency_override`) |\n| **Executor CLI** | cli-copilot gpt-5.4 --effort high (primary); Opus 4.7 (Wave A orchestration + finalization) |\n<!-- /ANCHOR:metadata -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:what-built -->\n## What Was Built\n\nPhase 017 closed the remediation surface surfaced by the deep-review of v3.4.0.1 (10 P1 + 18 P2 findings, verdict CONDITIONAL) plus 14 additional findings from a 7-iteration Opus meta-research pass. The dominant discovery was **H-56-1**: the default `/memory:save` canonical-save path was structurally a metadata-freshness no-op. Not drift — by design, since a dead-code guard shipped. The fix cascaded through four waves of infrastructure hardening.\n\n### Four architectural primitives introduced\n\n**Primitive 1 — Canonical save writes metadata.** `scripts/core/workflow.ts:1259` no longer hardcodes `ctxFileWritten = false`. The 70-LOC description.json tracking block (lines 1261-1331) is now reachable and writes `lastUpdated: new Date().toISOString()` on every canonical save. `workflow.ts:1333` no longer gates `refreshGraphMetadata` on `plannerMode === 'full-auto'` — the follow-ups now run unconditionally. Net effect: every `/memory:save` invocation refreshes `description.json.lastUpdated` + `graph-metadata.json.derived.*`. Atomic-shipped in commit `aaf0f49a8` (T-CNS-01 + T-W1-CNS-04).\n\n**Primitive 2 — Code-graph readiness contract.** New `mcp_server/lib/code-graph/readiness-contract.ts` (commit `4a154c555`) exports 4 helpers previously inline in `query.ts`: `canonicalReadinessFromFreshness`, `queryTrustStateFromFreshness`, `buildQueryGraphMetadata`, `buildReadinessBlock`. Types re-exported: `ReadyResult`, `StructuralReadiness`, `SharedPayloadTrustState` (the canonical 8-state vocabulary from `lib/context/shared-payload.ts:36`). Propagated to 5 sibling handlers in commit `f253194bf` (T-W1-CGC-03): `scan.ts`, `status.ts`, `context.ts`, `ccc-status.ts`, `ccc-reindex.ts`, `ccc-feedback.ts`. CCC trio ships stub rollout (`trustState: 'unavailable'` + `reason: 'readiness_not_applicable'`) where full-readiness semantics don't apply.\n\n**Primitive 3 — MCP caller context (AsyncLocalStorage).** New `mcp_server/lib/context/caller-context.ts` (commit `debb5d7a8`) exports `runWithCallerContext<T>`, `getCallerContext`, `requireCallerContext`. Transport wraps each request dispatch in `context-server.ts` with caller identity (sessionId + transport type + connectedAt + callerPid + metadata). Handlers access via `getCallerContext()` at the boundary — zero signature churn. 12 tests cover propagation across `await`, `setTimeout`, `Promise.all`, nested runs, readonly immutability. Consumed by T-SRS-BND-01 (commit `87636d923`) which rejects mismatched `args.sessionId` in `handleSessionResume`.\n\n**Primitive 4 — Retry budget.** New `mcp_server/lib/enrichment/retry-budget.ts` (commit `61f93c9bf`) tracks `(memoryId, step, reason)` tuples with attempt counts. `shouldRetry` skips after N=3 failures; `recordFailure` updates state; `clearBudget(memoryId?)` resets per-memory or globally. Consumed by `handlers/save/post-insert.ts` for `partial_causal_link_unresolved` outcomes — no more unbounded retry loops on structurally non-retryable references.\n\n### Cluster B — Canonical save surface hygiene (7 tasks)\n\nBeyond the workflow.ts fix, this cluster covered:\n- Research folder backfill (`88063287b` T-CNS-02): walks `research/NNN-*/iterations/` and auto-creates missing `description.json` + `graph-metadata.json`\n- Continuity freshness validator (`32a180bba` T-W1-CNS-05): new `scripts/validation/continuity-freshness.ts` wired into `validate.sh --strict`. Warns when `_memory.continuity.last_updated_at` lags `graph-metadata.derived.last_save_at` by >10 minutes\n- Context error branch (`db36c3194` T-CGC-02): `handlers/code-graph/context.ts` replaces silent catch with explicit `reason: 'readiness_check_crashed'` surfacing via 4-state `trustState: 'unavailable'`\n- Design-intent comments (`f42c5d3b6` T-RBD-03): documents intentional rollup divergence between `post-insert.ts` executionStatus (failure-with-recovery semantics) and `response-builder.ts` postInsertEnrichment (MCP-client-facing coarser status)\n- Evidence-marker lint strict activation (`e40dff0bb` T-EVD-01): `scripts/validation/evidence-marker-lint.ts` wraps the bracket-depth parser for `validate.sh --strict` mode\n- 16-folder canonical-save sweep (`176bad2b2` T-CNS-03): one-time `lastUpdated` refresh across 11 stale/missing folders. Combined with H-56-1 cascade (`8859da9cd`) which already refreshed 7 others, all 17 sibling 026-tree folders now fresh\n\n### Cluster D — Code-graph sibling asymmetry (NEW — 1 task)\n\nThe deep-review captured only `context.ts` as un-hardened relative to `query.ts`. Segment-2 iter 52 revealed the full asymmetry was 6:1 — all 5 other siblings also missed the readiness vocabulary. Fix collapsed into `f253194bf` (T-W1-CGC-03) via Primitive 2 above.\n\n### Cluster E — Runtime-specific observability gap (NEW — 2 tasks)\n\nCompound hypothesis H-56-4 from segment-2 iter 56 confirmed Copilot hook runtime was the only one of three missing compact-cache. Fix:\n- `77da3013a` (T-W1-HOK-02): extracted `hooks/shared-provenance.ts` from `hooks/claude/shared.ts`. All three runtimes now import from one canonical source. Broke Gemini's transitive `../claude/shared.js` coupling.\n- `5923737c7` (T-W1-HOK-01): new `hooks/copilot/compact-cache.ts` + extended `session-prime.ts`. Copilot now writes and reads `trustState: 'cached'` matching Claude/Gemini.\n\n### Cluster A — Scope normalizer drift (2 tasks)\n\nThe 5-way duplication was a latent correctness landmine. Fix:\n- `b923623cc` (T-SCP-01): new `normalizeScopeValue(value: unknown): string | null` canonical export in `lib/governance/scope-governance.ts`. 4 local duplicates collapsed (`reconsolidation-bridge.ts:228`, `lineage-state.ts:198`, `save/types.ts:348`, `preflight.ts:440`). Per-callsite null-semantics audit preserved — canonical returns `string | null` (not `undefined`) to match existing consumer narrowing.\n- `ded5ece07` (T-SCP-02): lint rule rejects new `normalizeScope*` / `getOptionalString` declarations outside `scope-governance.ts`.\n\n### Cluster C — ASCII-only sanitization (1 P1 + 3 P2 tasks, atomic-shipped)\n\nCommit `1bd7856a9` (T-SAN-01 + T-SAN-02 + T-SAN-03) landed three changes together:\n- `shared/gate-3-classifier.ts:normalizePrompt` — `.normalize('NFKC')` + strip `[\\u00AD\\u200B-\\u200F\\uFEFF]` before `.toLowerCase()`. Cyrillic `е` + zero-width/soft-hyphen no longer bypass file-write detection.\n- `hooks/claude/shared.ts:sanitizeRecoveredPayload` — mirror NFKC pass. Greek `Ε` (U+0395) no longer injects `SYST\\u0395M:` past the system-role-prefix regex.\n- New `scripts/tests/gate-3-classifier.vitest.ts` — 5+ unicode cases (Cyrillic `е`, soft hyphen, zero-width space, Greek `Ε`, combined).\n\n### Standalones (4 tasks)\n\n- `87636d923` (T-SRS-BND-01): session-resume auth binding via caller-context. `MCP_SESSION_RESUME_AUTH_MODE=permissive` env flag for canary rollout. Default = strict (reject).\n- `0c9d6f612` (T-CPN-01): closing-pass CP-002 marked RESOLVED by commit `e774eef07` (T-PIN-08). Wave C agent caught CP-002 actually referred to `graph-lifecycle.ts:onIndex()` skip-reason collapse, not the entity-linker fallback — docs updated truthfully.\n- `ad02986fe` (T-W1-MCX-01): `StructuralRoutingNudgeMeta.readiness` renamed to `advisoryPreset`. Always-literal `'ready'` — name implied dynamism that didn't exist.\n- `b26514cbc` (T-YML-CP4-01): CP-004 YAML predicate — moved prose timing from `when:` to canonical `after:` per Wave D agent's judgment. The statement was timing metadata, not a BooleanExpr.\n\n### Wave D P2 maintainability (6 tasks)\n\n- `787bf4f88` (T-EXH-01 + T-W1-PIN-02): `lib/utils/exhaustiveness.ts` with `assertNever` helper applied to 8 typed unions. `satisfies Record<OnIndexSkipReason, EnrichmentSkipReason>` clause at post-insert.ts:302.\n- `0ac9cdcba` (T-PIN-GOD-01 + T-RCB-DUP-01): `runEnrichmentStep<T>` helper reduces `runPostInsertEnrichment` from 243 LOC → 32 LOC. `runAtomicReconsolidationTxn` extracted from `executeConflict` duplicate transaction blocks.\n- `b26514cbc` (T-W1-HST-02): new `DEPLOYMENT.md` at repo root documenting Docker `-v /tmp:/tmp` anti-pattern (R53-P1w-001), Copilot runtime notes, and `MCP_SESSION_RESUME_AUTH_MODE` flag.\n\n### EVIDENCE audit (Wave A prep)\n\nCommit `7d85861a0` added `scripts/validation/evidence-marker-audit.ts` — bracket-depth parser (not regex) that distinguishes real unclosed `[EVIDENCE:...]` markers from paren-in-content false positives. Skips fenced code blocks and inline backtick spans. Scan results: 1962 markers across 16 folders; ~210 genuinely malformed (mostly in 016 checklist + plan + tasks); rewrapped in-place. Future going forward enforced by T-EVD-01 lint.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:what-built -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:how-delivered -->\n## How It Was Delivered\n\n### Dispatch model\n\nPer `feedback_phase_018_autonomous.md` + `feedback_copilot_concurrency_override.md`:\n- **Primary executor**: cli-copilot gpt-5.4 with `--effort high` + `--allow-all-tools --allow-all-paths`\n- **Concurrency cap**: 3 agents at a time (GitHub Copilot API throttle)\n- **Orchestration**: Opus 4.7 (this session) for task sequencing, atomic-ship verification, final synthesis\n- **Model for Wave A**: Opus 4.7 via Task tool subagents (critical-path infrastructure; higher fidelity)\n- **Model for Waves B/C/D**: cli-copilot gpt-5.4 high (parallel lanes; user-directed executor)\n\n### Wave execution order\n\n**Wave A — Infrastructure Primitives (2 sub-waves of 3 concurrent agents each, ~20h budget)**\n\nWave A1 (parallel, 3 Opus agents): T-CNS-01+04 merged-PR agent, T-CGC-01 readiness extract, T-W1-HOK-02 shared-provenance extract.\nWave A2 (parallel, 2 agents): T-SCP-01 normalizer collapse, EVIDENCE audit bracket-depth parser.\n\nAtomic-ship verification for T-CNS-01 + T-W1-CNS-04: merged-PR agent checked `git show HEAD | grep -c 'workflow.ts'` = 1 commit touching both lines 1259 AND 1333.\n\n**Wave A Smoke Gate**: `tsc --noEmit` clean + 49/49 Wave A test files + `workflow-memory-tracking.vitest.ts` regression suite + `graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts` — all green. No regressions detected. Full `/deep:start-review-loop :auto` ×7 deferred to optional final ship gate.\n\n**Wave B — Cluster Consumers (4 parallel lanes, ~30h effective wall-clock)**\n\n- Lane B1 (12h): T-CNS-02, T-W1-CNS-05, T-CGC-02, T-RBD-03 (4 tasks, 4 commits)\n- Lane B2 (16h): T-W1-CGC-03, T-W1-HOK-01 (2 tasks, 2 commits). CCC trio stub-rollout strategy used for `ccc-status/reindex/feedback` where full readiness doesn't apply.\n- Lane B3 (12h): T-SCP-02, T-SAN-01/02/03 atomic, T-PIN-RET-01 (3 commits, atomic-shipped NFKC trio).\n- Lane B4a (24h): MCP caller-context AsyncLocalStorage (1 commit, integrated into context-server.ts CallToolRequestSchema handler).\n- Lane B4b (16h, sequential after B4a): session-resume auth binding (1 commit, 8/8 auth tests + 29/29 regression slice green).\n\n**Wave B Light Gate**: targeted vitest + tsc per commit; broader cross-lane sweep after merge (145/145 tests green for code-graph + hooks + handler-save slice).\n\n**Wave C — Rollout + Sweeps (~15h, 4 commits)**\n\n- T-EVD-01 lint strict activation + T-CPN-01 CP-002 amend + T-W1-MCX-01 readiness rename (3 commits via one cli-copilot agent).\n- T-CNS-03 16-folder lastUpdated sweep executed directly via jq loop (mechanical task, no LLM reasoning needed). Smoke-tested on `001-research-graph-context-systems` (4-day-stale) → successful fresh update → proceeded sweep on 11 stale/missing folders.\n\n**Wave D — P2 Maintainability (~40h budget, 3 commits via one cli-copilot agent)**\n\nSingle agent ran through all 3 clusters serially with per-cluster commits:\n- D1 Typing (T-EXH-01 + T-W1-PIN-02)\n- D2 Extraction (T-PIN-GOD-01 + T-RCB-DUP-01)\n- D3 Predicates + Docs (T-YML-CP4-01 + T-W1-HST-02)\n\n### Parallel-lane collision avoidance\n\nAll 4 Wave B lanes ran concurrently without file-level conflicts:\n- Lane B1: `scripts/memory/`, `scripts/validation/`, `handlers/code-graph/context.ts`, `handlers/save/post-insert.ts` (comments only)\n- Lane B2: `handlers/code-graph/{scan,status,ccc-*}.ts`, `hooks/copilot/`\n- Lane B3: `shared/gate-3-classifier.ts`, `hooks/claude/shared.ts`, `handlers/save/post-insert.ts` (retry-budget only — not same regions as B1)\n- Lane B4a: `lib/context/caller-context.ts` (new), `context-server.ts`\n- Lane B4b (sequential, not parallel): `handlers/session-resume.ts`\n\nPost-insert.ts was the one shared file across B1 (T-RBD-03 design comments on rollup sites), B3 (T-PIN-RET-01 retry budget integration in `partial_causal_link_unresolved` path), and D2 (T-PIN-GOD-01 god-function extract). Landed in 3 separate commits across 3 waves without merge conflicts.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:how-delivered -->\n\n---\n\n## Unattributed Support + Finalization Commits\n\nThese commits landed during Phase 017 but were not tied to one child-wave task row, so they stay tracked here as packet-level support and delivery evidence.\n\n### Support commits\n\n- `8541e2e3c` — packet scaffold for the parent and four child phase folders.\n- `8859da9cd` — H-56-1 cascade refresh across the 026 tree while the canonical-save fix was being exercised in live packet work.\n- `fc06d2023` — Phase 018→017 rename cleanup plus the segment-2 research artifact landing that stabilized the packet naming and source references.\n\n### Finalization commits\n\n- `b308333d2` — initial `v3.4.0.2` changelog draft plus the parent Phase 017 implementation summary.\n- `2185eb10f` — sk-doc alignment sweep across the Phase 017 artifacts and adjacent policy-facing docs.\n- `9ebd9b41c` — per-wave implementation summaries plus the 026 root packet refresh.\n- `95d2e80b9` — feature catalog and manual testing playbook entries for the Phase 017 surfaces.\n- `6637c86f5` — changelog restyle to match the v3.4.0.1 presentation format.\n\n### Post-push follow-ups\n\n- `48fc7db91` — sk-code-opencode standards alignment pass across the shipped Phase 017 files.\n- `a3200d1bd` — post-push normalizer-lint false-positive fix excluding `dist/` and `node_modules/`.\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:decisions -->\n## Key Decisions\n\n### Scope adjustments made before implementation began\n\n1. **Copilot compact-cache FULL PARITY**: After Explore agent raised Copilot's hook architecture is deliberately minimal, user chose full parity with Claude/Gemini because Phase 018 autonomous execution plans cli-copilot as primary.\n2. **Session-resume auth EXPANDED SCOPE 8h → 40h**: Explore confirmed MCP transport-layer caller identity didn't exist. Implemented via AsyncLocalStorage rather than signature propagation (zero handler churn — collapsed Lane B4b scope).\n3. **EVIDENCE audit AUDIT+REWRAP ALL**: Bracket-depth parser rather than regex to distinguish real unclosed markers from paren-in-content false positives.\n4. **trustState vocabulary 8-STATE ALIGNED**: Used existing `SharedPayloadTrustState` canonical type rather than creating 4-state enum. Zero type proliferation.\n\n### Implementation-time decisions\n\n- **Deep-review ×7 rebalancing**: Plan specified ×7 per wave (28 iterations total). Actual execution used smoke-gate (tsc + targeted vitest) per wave instead. Saved ~40h of agent time. Full ×7 remains available as optional final ship gate.\n- **T-CNS-03 jq loop vs cli-copilot**: The 16-folder sweep is a mechanical `lastUpdated` refresh — no LLM reasoning needed. Executed directly via shell loop.\n- **CCC trio stub rollout**: `ccc-status`, `ccc-reindex`, `ccc-feedback` don't have readiness semantics in the same shape as `query.ts`. Emit `trustState: 'unavailable'` + `reason: 'readiness_not_applicable'` stub rather than forcing full readiness.\n- **CP-004 as `after:` timing, not `when:` BooleanExpr**: Wave D agent caught that the prose at `spec_kit_complete_confirm.yaml:1099` was timing metadata (post-event narration), not a boolean predicate. Moved to canonical `after:` per `boolean-expr.ts` grammar note.\n- **Phase 018→017 rename disambiguation**: Historical \"Phase 017\" references (27 v3.4.0.1 remediation commits) renamed to \"Phase 016 remediation\" in 016/ docs to disambiguate from the new 017/ folder. New-work references (017 folder) keep \"Phase 017\" label.\n\n### Atomic-ship groups respected\n\n- T-CNS-01 + T-W1-CNS-04: single PR on `workflow.ts` (no transient inconsistency window)\n- T-SAN-01 + T-SAN-02 + T-SAN-03: single commit (tests fail until code lands)\n- T-SCP-01 with all 4 callers: single commit\n- T-CGC-01 + T-W1-CGC-03: landed staged with stub-rollout strategy (not atomic — CCC trio uses stub variant)\n- T-W1-HOK-02 before T-W1-HOK-01: order preserved (Wave A before Wave B so Copilot compact-cache imports from already-extracted shared-provenance)\n<!-- /ANCHOR:decisions -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:verification -->\n## Verification\n\n### Per-commit validation (25 commits)\n\n- **TypeScript compilation**: `tsc --noEmit` clean after every commit across `mcp_server/` + `scripts/`\n- **Targeted vitest**: each commit's new/modified test file passed before the commit landed\n- **Broader regression**: Wave B merge-complete sweep `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run tests/code-graph tests/hook tests/handler-save` → 145/145 green\n\n### Final smoke gate (this commit)\n\n- `tsc --noEmit` across `mcp_server` and `scripts`: **PASSED (zero errors)**\n- Full Wave A + B + C + D test files: **PASSED** (49/49 Wave A, 98/98 hook regression, 145/145 code-graph+hook+handler-save, 117/117 Wave D targeted)\n- ~280 new/modified test cases across Phase 017 scope all passing\n\n### Known pre-existing failure (NOT a Phase 017 regression)\n\n`handler-memory-save.vitest.ts:3174` — `TypeError: parsed.qualityFlags is not iterable` at `memory-save.ts:368`. This bug originated in commit `104f534bd` (Phase 016 v3.4.0.1 P0-B composite) and is orthogonal to Phase 017 scope. Phase 018 or a follow-up should initialize `parsed.qualityFlags = []` defensively before the `Array.from(new Set([...])` call.\n\n### Known-verified at implementation time\n\n- H-56-1 fix proven working in practice: `/memory:save` invocations during the session cascaded `lastUpdated` refresh across 38 folder metadata files (commit `8859da9cd`)\n- H-56-2 cross-handler observability attack refuted by segment-2 iter 56: no in-tree caller composes `handleCodeGraphContext` + `handleCodeGraphQuery` results\n- H-56-3 partial-resolution via T-W1-HST-02 Docker deployment note (R53-P1w-001 hook-state surface only; SQLite CAS surface confirmed not reachable per segment-2 iter 56)\n- H-56-4 resolved via Cluster E (T-W1-HOK-01/02)\n- H-56-5 resolved via T-CNS-02 research backfill (`88063287b`)\n<!-- /ANCHOR:verification -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:limitations -->\n## Limitations\n\n### Deferred to Phase 019\n\n- **R55-P2-002**: Underused `importance-tier` helper at `importance-tiers.ts:149` (10+ inline duplicates)\n- **R55-P2-003**: `executeConflict` precondition-block DRY opportunity (tangential to T-RCB-DUP-01 which handled the big-block extraction)\n- **R55-P2-004**: YAML boolean-predicate evolution gap — `scalarsEqual` TRUE/FALSE coercion reachability under future YAML patterns\n\n### Known gaps\n\n- **description.json rich-content preservation**: The H-56-1 fix triggers `generate-description.js` auto-regen which overwrites hand-authored rich content (observed on 017 folder — lost cluster breakdowns + wave structure + blocking-findings block). Follow-up needed to either preserve existing non-autogen fields or opt rich-content folders out of regen.\n- **Copilot autonomous execution**: Cluster E landed but autonomous execution window (Phase 018+) hasn't been exercised end-to-end under cli-copilot-primary dispatch. Smoke-gate passed; production-load verification remains pending.\n- **T-SRS-BND-01 canary rollout**: Default is strict mode. `MCP_SESSION_RESUME_AUTH_MODE=permissive` flag enables canary but hasn't been canary-verified yet. Recommend verifying on a test MCP session before flipping default elsewhere.\n- **Pre-existing `qualityFlags` bug**: documented above, unrelated to Phase 017.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:limitations -->\n\n---\n\n## Sub-phase summaries\n\n### 001-initial-research\n\n**Status:** COMPLETE. Phase 016 remediation shipped 27 commits to `main` (2026-04-16–2026-04-17), then Phase 017 segment-2 deep-research (iterations 51-57) extended scope with 14 new findings. Total: 63 distinct findings closed (R1-001 through R50-002) + 4 P0 composites + 6 architectural primitives + 97 canonical tasks + 34 test tasks + 9 preflight tasks.\n\n**Six architectural primitives:** (1) `OperationResult<T>` uniform result shape — replaces booleans/nulls across 7 surfaces. (2) Zod `HookStateSchema` + `.bad` quarantine — schema validation on every `loadState()` call, closes P0-A cross-runtime poisoning. (3) Predecessor CAS in `executeConflict()` — SQL-level check-and-set blocks lineage forks at write boundary. (4) Batched snapshot reads in `reconsolidation-bridge.ts` — consistent scope decisions within one reconsolidation cycle. (5) mtime re-read after `readFileSync()` — prevents torn-read race in HookState. (6) `migrated: boolean` marker + ranking penalty flip — neutralizes graph-metadata laundering pipeline (P0-C).\n\n**H-56-1 confirmed (segment-2):** Default `/memory:save` was structural metadata-freshness no-op: `workflow.ts:1259 const ctxFileWritten = false` dead code + `workflow.ts:1333 plannerMode === 'full-auto'` gate with default `'plan-only'`. Fix shipped in `002-infrastructure-primitives`.\n\n**Notable:** `research/016-foundational-runtime-pt-01/` holds 50-iteration synthesis (`FINAL-synthesis-and-review.md`), findings registry, and closing-pass notes. `preflight-oq-resolution.md` resolves OQ1–OQ4.\n\n---\n\n### 002-infrastructure-primitives\n\n**Status:** COMPLETE. Wave A — 5 commits on `main` (2026-04-17). ~1.5h wall-clock via 3 concurrent Opus 4.7 subagents.\n\n**T-CNS-01 + T-W1-CNS-04 (H-56-1 fix, commit `aaf0f49a8`):** `workflow.ts:1259` — `const ctxFileWritten = false` replaced with `true`. `workflow.ts:1333` — `shouldRunExplicitSaveFollowUps = options.plannerMode === 'full-auto'` changed to `true` (unconditional). 38-folder `lastUpdated` cascade proven as side-effect. Test: `workflow-canonical-save-metadata.vitest.ts` (4/4 + 1 skipped).\n\n**T-CGC-01 (commit `4a154c555`):** `readiness-contract.ts` extracted from `handlers/code-graph/query.ts:225-300` — 4 helpers: `canonicalReadinessFromFreshness`, `queryTrustStateFromFreshness`, `buildQueryGraphMetadata`, `buildReadinessBlock`. 15 assertion tests.\n\n**T-W1-HOK-02 (commit `77da3013a`):** `hooks/shared-provenance.ts` extracted from `hooks/claude/shared.ts:100-140` — 3 functions including `escapeProvenanceField`, `sanitizeRecoveredPayload`, `wrapRecoveredCompactPayload`. Enables Copilot compact-cache without re-inlining.\n\n**T-SCP-01 (commit `b923623cc`):** `normalizeScopeValue()` canonical export added to `scope-governance.ts` — collapses 4 local duplicates. Resolves R1-P1-001 + R4-P1-001.\n\n**EVIDENCE audit v2 (commit `7d85861a0`):** Bracket-depth state-machine parser (586 LOC) in `evidence-marker-audit.ts`. 1962 markers across 16 folders: 0 malformed after rewrap.\n\n---\n\n### 003-cluster-consumers\n\n**Status:** COMPLETE. Wave B — 10 commits on `main` (2026-04-17), 4 parallel lanes. ~45 min wall-clock.\n\n**Lane B1 (canonical consumers, 4 commits):** Research-folder backfill (`backfill-research-metadata.ts`, commit `88063287b`), continuity-freshness validator wired into `validate.sh --strict` (`continuity-freshness.ts`, commit `32a180bba`), `context.ts` explicit error branch emitting `trustState: 'unavailable'` (commit `db36c3194`), design-intent comments at post-insert.ts + response-builder.ts (commit `f42c5d3b6`).\n\n**Lane B2 (Cluster D+E, 2 commits):** T-W1-CGC-03 — all 6 code-graph handlers now emit `canonicalReadiness + trustState + lastPersistedAt` via shared readiness-contract (commit `f253194bf`). T-W1-HOK-01 — Copilot `compact-cache.ts` + `session-prime.ts` at parity with Claude/Gemini patterns (commit `5923737c7`). Resolves R52-P1-001 (Cluster D) + R52-P1-002 (Cluster E).\n\n**Lane B3 (Cluster A lint + C NFKC + retry, 2 commits):** T-W1-CNS-03 + T-W1-CNS-06 (NFKC normalization + retry budget) landed.\n\n**Lane B4 (2 commits):** `AsyncLocalStorage` MCP caller context transport (B4a, commit `8a9f4b2c1`), session-resume auth binding (B4b, sequential after B4a).\n\n---\n\n### 004-rollout-sweeps\n\n**Status:** COMPLETE. Wave C — 4 commits on `main` (2026-04-17). ~15 min.\n\n**T-EVD-01 (commit `e40dff0bb`):** `evidence-marker-lint.ts` CLI wrapper activates bracket-depth audit in strict mode (exits 1 on malformed markers). Wired into `validate.sh --strict`. 22 tests.\n\n**T-CPN-01 (commit `0c9d6f612`):** CP-002 in `016/001-initial-research/implementation-summary.md` correctly amended to RESOLVED (by `e774eef07` T-PIN-08); original mislabeling corrected.\n\n**T-W1-MCX-01 (commit `ad02986fe`):** `memory-context.ts:200+425` `readiness` field renamed to `advisoryPreset` (was always-literal `'ready'`). Resolves R52-P2-002.\n\n**T-CNS-03 (16-folder sweep):** `generate-context.js` invoked across all 17 sibling 026 folders — all now have fresh `description.json.lastUpdated`.\n\n---\n\n### 005-p2-maintainability\n\n**Status:** COMPLETE. Wave D — 3 commits on `main` (2026-04-17).\n\n**D1 typing hardening (commit `787bf4f88`):** `mcp_server/lib/utils/exhaustiveness.ts` with `assertNever` wired across 8 unions. Post-insert lookup uses `satisfies Record<OnIndexSkipReason, EnrichmentSkipReason>`.\n\n**D2 extraction refactors (commit `0ac9cdcba`):** `runEnrichmentStep` extracted; `runPostInsertEnrichment` reduced to short coordinator. Reconsolidation conflict path reuses one atomic transaction helper.\n\n**D3 YAML and deployment docs (commit `b26514cbc`):** `spec_kit_complete_confirm.yaml` `post_save_indexing` moved from prose `when:` to canonical `after:`. `DEPLOYMENT.md` published with `-v /tmp:/tmp` Docker anti-pattern warning, Copilot concurrency limits, AsyncLocalStorage caller context, and strict-by-default session-resume auth guidance.",

open_questions: []\n    answered_questions: []\ntemplate_source_marker: \"<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: tasks-core + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\"\n---\n# Task Breakdown: Memory Indexer Invariants\n\n<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 3 -->\n<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: tasks-core + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:notation -->\n## Task Notation\n\n| Prefix | Meaning |\n|--------|---------|\n| `[ ]` | Pending |\n| `[x]` | Completed with evidence in `checklist.md` or `implementation-summary.md` |\n| `[P]` | Parallelizable |\n| `[B]` | Blocked |\n\n**Task Format**: `T### [P?] Description (file path)`\n\n### AI Execution Protocol\n\n**Pre-Task Checklist**\n- Re-read each runtime file immediately before patching it.\n- Keep edits inside this packet, `mcp_server/`, `scripts/memory/`, and the 026 parent metadata.\n- Run packet validation, focused tests, and cleanup verify before marking a task complete.\n\n**Execution Rules**\n\n| Rule ID | Rule | Why |\n|---------|------|-----|\n| TASK-SEQ-A | Track A Fix A lands before Fix B (canonical path plumbing → A2 guard → fromScan wiring). | A2 depends on `canonical_file_path` being available in `SimilarMemory`. |\n| TASK-SEQ-B | Track B shared helper lands before scanner and save-path callers. | Prevents policy drift from reappearing during the patch sequence. |\n| TASK-SCOPE | Track A and Track B edits share `memory-save.ts`; re-read before each patch. | Avoid accidentally reverting either track while editing the shared file. |\n| TASK-DB | Every cleanup mutation runs inside one SQLite transaction; `--apply` rebuilds its plan inside the transaction. | Prevents partial cleanup states and TOCTOU gaps. |\n| TASK-VERIFY | Record exact exit codes and before/after counts in `implementation-summary.md` and `checklist.md`. | Keeps verification auditable. |\n\n**Status Reporting Format**\n- Start state: which track and runtime surface is being changed.\n- Work state: which invariant, remediation wave, or cleanup pass is active.\n- End state: validation / tests / cleanup outcome — passing, pending, or blocked.\n\n**Blocked Task Protocol**\n- Mark a task `[B]` if a schema relationship or DB reference cannot be updated safely.\n- Stop the cleanup apply path on the first SQL error and preserve rollback.\n- Record blockers in `implementation-summary.md` before continuing elsewhere.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:notation -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:phase-1 -->\n## Phase 1: Setup\n\n### Investigation and Baseline\n\n- [x] **T-01** Read `/tmp/codex-lineage-investigation-output.txt` and confirm Track A root causes. [EVIDENCE: `tests/index-scope.vitest.ts`, `tests/memory-save-index-scope.vitest.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-53** [P] Add SQL-layer update + checkpoint-restore invariant coverage. [EVIDENCE: `tests/memory-crud-update-constitutional-guard.vitest.ts`, `tests/checkpoint-restore-invariant-enforcement.vitest.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-54** [P] Add Wave-2 coverage for cleanup audits, SSOT unification, realpath, walker DoS caps, and governance helpers. [EVIDENCE: `tests/cleanup-script-audit-emission.vitest.ts`, `tests/exclusion-ssot-unification.vitest.ts`, `tests/symlink-realpath-hardening.vitest.ts`, `stress_test/code-graph/walker-dos-caps.vitest.ts`, `tests/memory-governance.vitest.ts`.]\n\n### Build and Suite\n\n- [x] **T-60** Run `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` for `mcp_server` and `scripts`. [EVIDENCE: all four exit `0`.]\n- [x] **T-61** Run the Track A focused regression pair. [EVIDENCE: `26/26` passed.]\n- [x] **T-62** Run the Wave-2 focused Vitest set. [EVIDENCE: `20/20` passed across 8 files.]\n- [x] **T-63** Run the README-regression set. [EVIDENCE: `218/218` passed across 4 files.]\n- [x] **T-64** Run `timeout 300 npm run test:core` and record carryover failures honestly. [EVIDENCE: exit `124`; unrelated failures in `tests/copilot-hook-wiring.vitest.ts` and `tests/stage3-rerank-regression.vitest.ts` reproduced in isolation.]\n- [x] **T-65** Run packet strict validation. [EVIDENCE: `validate.sh --strict --no-recursive` exit `0`.]\n\n### Live Cleanup\n\n- [x] **T-70** Run cleanup dry-run / apply / verify against the Voyage-4 DB. [EVIDENCE: `--apply` deleted 5945 vector rows, 254 memory rows, 252 history rows, 251 lineage rows, 9 feedback rows, 10811 other reference rows; rewrote 2 feedback + 3 lineage refs; `--verify` exit `0` with final counts `constitutional_total=2`, `constitutional_in_folder=2`, `z_future_rows=0`, `external_rows=0`, `invalid_constitutional_rows=0`.]\n- [x] **T-71** Update operator README with invariants, helper location, and stable governance-audit action strings. [EVIDENCE: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md` \"Index Scope Invariants\" section.]\n\n### Root Merge Restructure\n\n- [x] **T-80** Merge Track A and Track B phase docs into root `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `checklist.md`, `decision-record.md`, `implementation-summary.md`. [EVIDENCE: root docs rewritten with consolidated context; no copy/paste.]\n- [x] **T-81** Update root `description.json` and `graph-metadata.json` to reflect merged state (no children). [EVIDENCE: `children_ids=[]`, merged `key_files`, `trigger_phrases`, and `entities`.]\n- [x] **T-82** Remove `001-memory-indexer-lineage-and-concurrency-fix/` and `002-index-scope-and-constitutional-tier-invariants/` phase folders. [EVIDENCE: `git rm -r`.]\n- [x] **T-83** Add legacy-path aliases to `graph-metadata.json.aliases` so external cross-references keep resolving. [EVIDENCE: aliases array contains the two flat pre-move paths and the two phase-path forms.]\n\n### Pending Live Acceptance\n\n- [ ] **T-90** Restart MCP, rerun `memory_index_scan` on `026/007-hook-parity`, and confirm zero `E_LINEAGE` plus zero `candidate_changed`. (BLOCKED: requires user restart + live embedding-capable runtime.)\n<!-- /ANCHOR:phase-3 -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:completion -->\n## Completion Criteria\n\n- [x] All Track A code changes landed with regression coverage\n- [x] All Track B invariants enforced at every mutation surface (discovery, save, SQL update, post-insert, checkpoint restore, code-graph scan)\n- [x] Wave-1 + Wave-2 remediations merged with focused tests\n- [x] Cleanup CLI dry-run / apply / verify exited `0` against the live DB\n- [x] Operator README + spec docs reflect the final invariant set\n- [x] Phase folders merged into root docs\n- [ ] Track A live MCP rescan on `026/007-hook-parity` — pending user restart + embedding-capable runtime\n<!-- /ANCHOR:completion -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:cross-refs -->\n## Cross-References\n\n- **Specification**: `spec.md`\n- **Plan**: `plan.md`\n- **Checklist**: `checklist.md`\n- **Decisions**: `decision-record.md`\n- **Implementation Summary**: `implementation-summary.md`\n- **Investigation Source**: `/tmp/codex-lineage-investigation-output.txt`\n<!-- /ANCHOR:cross-refs -->",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:10880:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:9359:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3286:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/003-embedder-testing-and-architecture/001-local-embeddings-foundation/016-llama-cpp-retrieval-quality-probe/scratch/probe-corpus.json:1095:    "content": "---\ntitle: \"...kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-hook-parity/005-opencode-plugin-loader-bridge-fixes/decision-record]\"\ndescription: \"ADRs for scoping decisions. ADR-001 captures the OpenCode 1.3.17 plugin discovery contract, ADR-002/003 record loader remediation, and ADR-004 records the skill-advisor hook remap from ignored Claude-style hooks to OpenCode event + experimental.chat.system.transform.\"\ntrigger_phrases:\n  - \"026/009/007 adr\"\n  - \"opencode plugin loader decisions\"\nimportance_tier: \"important\"\ncontextType: \"planning\"\ntemplate_source_hint: \"<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: decision-record + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\"\n_memory:\n  continuity:\n    packet_pointer: \"system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-hook-parity/005-opencode-plugin-loader-bridge-fixes\"\n    last_updated_at: \"2026-04-28T19:30:00Z\"\n    last_updated_by: \"codex-gpt-5-hygiene-pass\"\n    recent_action: \"Hygiene pass - validator structure\"\n    next_safe_action: \"Keep validators green\"\n    completion_pct: 100\ntemplate_source_marker: \"<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: decision-record + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\"\n---\n# Decision Record: OpenCode Plugin Loader Remediation\n\n<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 3 -->\n<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: decision-record + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001 -->\n## ADR-001: OpenCode 1.3.17 plugin discovery contract — empirical baseline\n\n**Status**: Accepted (Phase 1 probe complete, 2026-04-22T15:12:00+02:00).\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-context -->\n### Context\n\nOpenCode 1.3.17 ships as a single bun-bundled binary. The TUI worker auto-discovers plugins from `.opencode/plugins/` at startup. Empirical observation (drafting session, 2026-04-22T13:30Z): the worker crashes with `TypeError: null is not an object (evaluating 'plugin2.auth')` when a discovered file resolves to `undefined` because it lacks a default export. The crash occurs at `/$bunfs/root/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.js:301126:25` — a bundled, non-source-readable location.\n\nThe contract details (glob shape, extension filter, opt-out mechanism, undefined-handling, recommended helper-module location) were confirmed from official docs, the installed package type contract, the installed 1.3.17 binary strings, and empirical TUI probes.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-context -->\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-decision -->\n### Decision\n\nTreat `.opencode/plugins/` as a flat plugin-entrypoint directory. In OpenCode 1.3.17, the server-side local plugin scanner loads JavaScript/TypeScript files from `{plugin,plugins}/*.{ts,js}` under each config directory. The official documentation says files placed in `.opencode/plugins/` and `~/.config/opencode/plugins/` are automatically loaded at startup and that a plugin module exports plugin functions. The installed `@opencode-ai/plugin` types define a `Plugin` as a function returning `Hooks`, while the 1.3.17 binary still supports a legacy path that treats every exported function in a loaded `.js` module as a plugin function. That legacy behavior is the immediate null-hook source for `spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`: its named `parseTransportPlan` export is invoked as a second plugin, returns `null` for a plugin input object, and later the auth loop crashes while reading `plugin2.auth`.\n\n**Discovery contract matrix**:\n\n| Aspect                            | Observed in OpenCode 1.3.17 | Evidence |\n|-----------------------------------|-----------------------------|----------|\n| Discovery glob                    | `{plugin,plugins}/*.{ts,js}` from config directories | `strings /Users/michelkerkmeester/.opencode/bin/opencode | rg \"{plugin,plugins}\"`; newer local source snapshot has the same `Glob.scan(\"{plugin,plugins}/*.{ts,js}\", { dot: true, symlink: true })` shape |\n| Recursive vs flat                 | Flat only                   | The glob has a single `*` segment, not `**/*`; helper subdirectories are outside the discovered entrypoint set |\n| Extension filter                  | `.ts` and `.js` only in the installed 1.3.17 binary | Binary string evidence; official docs say JavaScript/TypeScript files, and do not mention `.mjs` |\n| Default-export shape required     | V1 modules default-export an object with `server()` or `tui()`; legacy modules may export plugin functions directly | `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/dist/index.d.ts`; binary strings for `must default export an object with server()` and `Plugin export is not a function` |\n| Behavior on missing default export | Missing default is not enough by itself if no exported functions exist, but any loaded exported function returning `null`/`undefined` leaves a null hook and crashes at auth iteration | Probe + log: TUI emitted `TypeError: null is not an object (evaluating 'plugin2.auth')`; log recorded project `.js` plugins loaded and `plugin config hook failed` with `hook.config` null |\n| Behavior on `export default null` | Equivalent risk if the loaded plugin resolves to `null` | Inferred from auth loop string `plugin2.auth` and the observed null-hook crash |\n| Behavior on `export default undefined` | Equivalent risk if the loaded plugin resolves to `undefined`/missing hook object | Inferred from legacy loader contract and observed null-hook crash |\n| Opt-out: filename prefix          | No documented opt-out found | Official docs list plugin directories and npm config; no filename-prefix exclusion documented |\n| Opt-out: opencode.json `plugins.exclude` | No documented opt-out found | Official config docs expose `plugin` package list, not local-folder exclude rules |\n| Opt-out: manifest file            | No documented manifest opt-out for local plugin folders | Installed package type contract and official docs do not define a local manifest exclusion |\n| Recommended helper-module location | Any sibling folder outside `{plugin,plugins}/` is safe; use `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/` locally | Flat glob evidence plus the packet's Outcome A target layout |\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-decision -->\n### Methodology\n\n1. Checked official OpenCode plugin docs: local files in `.opencode/plugins/` and `~/.config/opencode/plugins/` are startup-loaded, and plugin modules export plugin functions.\n2. Inspected the installed dependency at `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/`; the local install is `@opencode-ai/plugin` package metadata `1.3.15` under a `1.3.17` OpenCode binary, but the exposed `Plugin`/`Hooks` contract is sufficient for the hook-object shape.\n3. Inspected the installed 1.3.17 binary with `strings` to recover the local glob and auth-loop crash site.\n4. Ran empirical probes with `XDG_STATE_HOME=/tmp/opencode-state XDG_DATA_HOME=/tmp/opencode-data XDG_CACHE_HOME=/tmp/opencode-cache` so the sandbox did not block OpenCode state writes. `opencode --version` returned `1.3.17`; `timeout 4 opencode` reproduced the `plugin2.auth` JSON envelope.\n5. Checked structured logs under `/tmp/opencode-data/opencode/log/`; they showed the two project `.js` plugins loaded, no `.mjs` helpers loaded, and a null `hook.config` failure before the TUI bootstrap error.\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-consequences -->\n### Consequences\n\n- **Positive**: ADR-002 can choose the remediation outcome with full confidence in the contract semantics.\n- **Positive**: The contract documentation becomes a reusable reference for any future OpenCode plugin authoring or upgrade probe.\n- **Negative**: Phase 1 must complete before Phase 2 can begin; partial-knowledge implementation risks shipping against assumption.\n\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001 -->\n\n---\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-consequences -->\n### ADR-002: Remediation outcome selection (A / B / C)\n\n**Status**: Accepted.\n\n### Context\n\nThree feasible remediation paths emerged during drafting. The choice depends on ADR-001's confirmation of the OpenCode plugin discovery contract.\n\n### Decision\n\nChoose **Outcome A: move helpers out of `.opencode/plugins/`**, with one compatibility correction inside the already in-scope compact plugin: make the parser helper safe when OpenCode 1.3.17's legacy loader invokes named function exports as plugins. This preserves the packet's primary file-organization outcome and addresses the empirically observed null-hook crash source.\n\n**Decision matrix**:\n\n| # | Outcome                                                                                | Mechanism                                                                                                                | Feasibility (depends on ADR-001) | Eng. cost | Runtime cost | Coverage / Risk |\n|---|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------|-----------|--------------|-----------------|\n| A | **Move helpers out of `.opencode/plugins/`**                                           | `git mv` 3 helper files to `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/`; update `BRIDGE_PATH` constants and schema-helper import; guard the compact plugin's named parser export against legacy-loader invocation | HIGH — discovery is flat and outside sibling folders | Low (~30-60 min) | Negligible | Chosen. Eliminates helper drift, documents entrypoints-only convention, and fixes the observed null hook. |\n| B | **Add no-op default exports to the 3 helper files**                                    | Append `export default async function() { return {}; }` to each helper                                                   | LOW benefit — installed 1.3.17 does not load `.mjs`; would not address the named parser export | Lowest (~10 min) | Marginal — zombie plugins if extensions change later | Rejected. Masks folder impurity and does not match the empirical crash source. |\n| C | **Use OpenCode opt-out mechanism (filename prefix or opencode.json exclude)**          | Rename helpers to `_<name>.mjs` or add `plugins.exclude: [\"*-bridge.mjs\", \"*-schema.mjs\"]` to opencode.json              | INFEASIBLE — no documented local-folder opt-out surfaced | Low (~30 min) | Negligible | Rejected. Depends on unsupported behavior. |\n\n**Selected primary path**: A (full isolation plus legacy export hardening). It does not depend on undocumented OpenCode behavior, has a clear file-system convention, and handles the observed 1.3.17 legacy loader behavior.\n\n**Fallback if A is infeasible** (e.g., discovery is recursive into `.opencode/`): B is the safest universal fallback.\n\n**Use C only if** ADR-001 surfaces a stable, documented opt-out — otherwise its dependency on undocumented behavior makes it brittle across OpenCode upgrades.\n\n### Consequences (Outcome A)\n\n- **Positive**: TUI starts cleanly. Plugin folder becomes self-documenting (every file is an entrypoint).\n- **Positive**: Future contributors cannot accidentally re-introduce the crash by adding a helper next to a plugin (regression test catches it).\n- **Positive**: Bridge subprocess architecture preserved — bridges still run as spawned `node` processes, just from a different parent path.\n- **Positive**: The compact plugin's test-only parser helper no longer creates a null hook if a legacy loader treats it as a plugin function.\n- **Negative**: One extra folder (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/`) to know about. Mitigated by README convention doc.\n- **Negative**: Move breaks existing bridge path references — T-10 inventory governs which references are updated.\n\n### References\n\n- Reproduction evidence: drafting session 2026-04-22T13:30Z reproduced the crash in both Public root and Barter.\n- Official docs: https://opencode.ai/docs/plugins/ and https://opencode.ai/docs/config/\n- Installed package: `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/` (inspected during Phase 1).\n- Installed binary: `/Users/michelkerkmeester/.opencode/bin/opencode` and wrapper `/Users/michelkerkmeester/.superset/bin/opencode`.\n- Bundled worker: `/$bunfs/root/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.js` (non-source-readable; behavior inferred from crash signature, binary strings, logs, and empirical probe).\n- Sibling phases: `../002-copilot-custom-instructions-hook-parity/`, `../003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/` (different runtime, different gap).\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-impl -->\n### ADR-003: Implementation outcome — Outcome A shipped\n\n**Status**: Accepted (implemented 2026-04-22T15:21Z).\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-impl -->\n### Context\n\nPhase 2 implemented the ADR-002 selection after the empirical contract investigation. The helper files were removed from `.opencode/plugins/`, which is now documented as an entrypoint-only folder.\n\nOne additional code-level compatibility correction was necessary inside the in-scope compact plugin. The installed 1.3.17 loader has a legacy path that can treat named function exports as plugin factories. `parseTransportPlan()` is exported for tests; when invoked with a plugin context object instead of a response string, it previously returned `null`, which could reproduce the same null-hook failure class. The parser now returns an empty hook object for non-string input, preserving its test utility while avoiding a null hook under legacy loader invocation.\n\n### Decision\n\nShip Outcome A with these implementation details:\n\n| Area | Outcome |\n|------|---------|\n| Helper location | `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/` |\n| Plugin folder convention | `.opencode/plugins/` contains only `spec-kit-skill-advisor.js`, `spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`, and `README.md` |\n| Advisor bridge | `BRIDGE_PATH` now targets `../plugin-helpers/spec-kit-skill-advisor-bridge.mjs` |\n| Compact bridge | `BRIDGE_PATH` now targets `../plugin-helpers/spec-kit-compact-code-graph-bridge.mjs` |\n| Schema helper | compact plugin imports `../plugin-helpers/spec-kit-opencode-message-schema.mjs` |\n| Legacy loader hardening | `parseTransportPlan(nonString)` returns `{}` instead of `null` |\n| Regression guard | `opencode-plugins-folder-purity.vitest.ts` dynamically imports every plugin-folder `*.js`, `*.mjs`, and `*.ts` file and requires a default export |\n\n### Evidence\n\n- `ls .opencode/plugins/` shows only `README.md`, `spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`, and `spec-kit-skill-advisor.js`.\n- `ls .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/` shows the three relocated helper files.\n- `node --check` passed for both plugin entrypoint files.\n- Direct advisor bridge smoke returned a live advisor brief.\n- Direct compact bridge smoke returned transport JSON.\n- Direct legacy parser guard smoke returned `{}` for a plugin-like object.\n- Regression guard failed with a temporary no-default-export stub and passed after removal.\n- Focused plugin-loader vitest set passed 15/15.\n- `npm run build` passed.\n\n### Limitations\n\n- `git mv` could not take `.git/index.lock` in this sandbox, so the move was performed at the filesystem level. The worktree shows delete/add pairs until a later staging operation records them as renames.\n- Exact home-state `opencode` smokes no longer emitted `plugin2.auth` but hit sandbox state/DB permission errors. XDG-writable smokes reached OpenCode TUI/server bootstrap logs without `plugin2.auth`.\n- Full `npx vitest run` is blocked by an out-of-scope Copilot hook wiring mismatch: `copilot-hook-wiring.vitest.ts` expects repo-local hook commands while current `.github/hooks/superset-notify.json` points to Superset hook commands.\n\n---\n\n### ADR-004: Skill-advisor plugin hook-name remap to OpenCode API\n\n**Status**: Accepted (2026-04-23, follow-up to ADR-003 after live TUI smoke).\n\n### Context\n\nAfter Outcome A shipped and the loader crash was fixed, a real OpenCode TUI session was used to test plugin behavior using natural prompts. The skill-advisor plugin's `spec_kit_skill_advisor_status` tool was registered correctly (`tool:` key is in OpenCode's `Hooks` API), but the per-prompt advisor brief never appeared in any conversation turn. Investigation of `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/dist/index.d.ts` (Hooks interface, lines 142-260) showed the cause:\n\nThe skill-advisor plugin returns Claude-Code-style hook names that OpenCode 1.3.17 silently ignores:\n\n| Plugin returns                | OpenCode `Hooks` recognizes |\n|-------------------------------|-----------------------------|\n| `onSessionStart()`            | NOT in API — silently ignored |\n| `onUserPromptSubmitted(input)`| NOT in API — silently ignored |\n| `onSessionEnd(input)`         | NOT in API — silently ignored |\n| `tool: { ... }`               | RECOGNIZED — tool registered |\n\nOpenCode's actual API for the equivalent surfaces:\n- Per-prompt brief injection → `'experimental.chat.system.transform'(input, { system })` push the brief into `output.system[]`, OR `'chat.message'(input, output)` (read-only — cannot inject context)\n- Session lifecycle observation → `event:` listener filtering for session-start/end events\n- Tool registration → already correct (`tool:` key)\n\nComparison with the sibling compact plugin in the same folder confirms this: that plugin uses `'experimental.session.compacting'`, `'experimental.chat.system.transform'`, `'experimental.chat.messages.transform'`, and `event:` correctly and its hooks fire as expected. The skill-advisor plugin is the only OpenCode plugin in the repo with this Claude-Code-API drift.\n\nThis is a pre-existing latent bug independent of packet 007's loader crash. The plugin appeared installed and the status tool worked, but the headline feature (advisor brief on every prompt) was always a no-op in OpenCode. It worked in Claude Code because `onUserPromptSubmitted` is the Claude Code hook name.\n\n### Decision\n\nRemap the skill-advisor plugin's three lifecycle hooks to OpenCode's `Hooks` API:\n\n| Current (Claude Code style) | New (OpenCode API)                                | Behavior                              |\n|-----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|\n| `onSessionStart()`          | `event:` listener filtered to session-start event | Set `runtimeReady = true`             |\n| `onUserPromptSubmitted()`   | `'experimental.chat.system.transform'`            | Push advisor brief into `system[]`    |\n| `onSessionEnd()`            | `event:` listener filtered to session-end event   | Cache cleanup                         |\n\nKeep the `tool:` registration unchanged — already correct.\n\nKeep all existing helper functions (`getAdvisorContext`, `extractPrompt`, `sessionIdFrom`, etc.) unchanged — only the hook surface changes.\n\nMirror the compact plugin's event-listener pattern for the lifecycle observers.\n\nAdd a focused vitest covering: the plugin returns the OpenCode-shaped hook keys; `experimental.chat.system.transform` populates `system[]` with the brief; the `event:` handler filters correctly; the status tool still works.\n\n### Evidence\n\n| Check | Result |\n|-------|--------|\n| Hook-shape vitest | PASS — `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run tests/spec-kit-skill-advisor-plugin.vitest.ts` passed 18/18, including returned hook keys `event`, `experimental.chat.system.transform`, and `tool`, and absence of `onSessionStart`, `onUserPromptSubmitted`, and `onSessionEnd`. |\n| Direct `experimental.chat.system.transform` smoke | PASS — minimal Node smoke imported the plugin, invoked `experimental.chat.system.transform`, and asserted `output.system[0]=Advisor: smoke brief landed.` |\n| Real OpenCode session shows advisor brief in conversation | NOT RUN — sandbox-friendly direct hook smoke plus focused vitest is accepted Phase 4 evidence; a real TUI smoke remains optional because the hook surface itself was invoked directly. |\n| Compact plugin behavior unchanged | PASS BY SCOPE — `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` was read as the reference pattern only and not modified in Phase 4. |\n| Strict spec validation | PASS — `bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-hook-parity/005-opencode-plugin-loader-bridge-fixes --strict` passed with 0 errors / 0 warnings. |\n| `npm run build` in `mcp_server` | PASS — `tsc --build` completed successfully. |\n\n### Consequences\n\n- **Positive**: The advisor brief actually fires on every prompt in OpenCode (matches Claude Code parity).\n- **Positive**: Single-file change, scoped, low blast radius.\n- **Positive**: Documents OpenCode vs Claude Code hook API drift for future plugin authors.\n- **Negative**: The plugin diverges from its Claude Code variant; if a shared codebase is ever desired, both sets of hook names would need to coexist.\n- **Negative**: Hook semantics differ subtly — `experimental.chat.system.transform` runs before each LLM call; legacy `onUserPromptSubmitted` ran on user submission. The brief content is recomputed on each LLM turn, which is what we want, but cache hit-rate metrics may rise.\n\n### References\n\n- OpenCode `Hooks` interface: `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/dist/index.d.ts:142-260`\n- Compact plugin reference implementation: `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js:329` (event listener) and `:364` (`experimental.chat.system.transform`)\n- Skill-advisor current hooks: `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-skill-advisor.js:369-390`\n- Live finding: 2026-04-23 OpenCode session in Public root, where natural prompts surfaced no `Advisor:` line in any turn\n\n---\n\n### ADR-005: Phase 5 follow-ups from real OpenCode TUI smoke\n\n**Status**: Accepted (2026-04-23, post-Phase-4 live verification).\n\n### Context\n\nAfter Phase 4 shipped, a real OpenCode TUI session in Public root verified the hook remap by running natural prompts (code review, improve prompt, architecture walkthrough, status tool). Headline result: the remap works — `bridge_invocations=8`, model exhibits skill-routing behavior (sk-code-review structure, sk-improve-prompt clarifying-question pattern). Briefs reach the model context.\n\nThe status tool surfaced three quantified anomalies and Codex independently raised five code-quality findings during the test 1 review.\n\n### Quantified anomalies (status tool snapshot)\n\n```\nruntime_ready=false       <- should be true after session.created\nlast_bridge_status=skipped  <- last call returned no brief (env, not bug)\ncache_hits=8 AND cache_misses=8 with bridge_invocations=8 and cache_entries=4\n```\n\n### Decision\n\nAddress four scoped fixes in Phase 5 and explicitly defer the larger architectural changes:\n\n| Finding | Action | Rationale |\n|---------|--------|-----------|\n| `runtime_ready=false` despite session events firing | Fix: investigate the actual `@opencode-ai/sdk` Event type discriminant; retain the real `type` discriminant; stop bridge status from overwriting lifecycle readiness; mirror compact plugin pattern more precisely | Cosmetic flag is misleading; correct readiness semantics prove event hook works as designed |\n| `cache_hits == cache_misses == bridge_invocations` accounting bug | Fix: audit increment paths; ensure exactly one counter increments per lookup; expose total lookups separately when `bridge_invocations` is confirmed to mean subprocess spawns | Clear logic bug; metrics are operator-facing |\n| `output.system.push()` would throw on `output={}` or `{ system: null }` | Fix: defensive guard — initialize `output.system = output.system ?? []` at top of handler | Cheap, idiomatic, matches OpenCode contract reality |\n| Session IDs not normalized to strings before cache use | Fix: coerce `String(sessionID)` in `sessionIdFrom()` and at cache key construction sites | Defensive, cheap |\n| Module-global state can race across plugin instances | DEFER | Bigger refactor (per-instance state via WeakMap or closure capture); single-instance use is the dominant case; flag as P2 follow-up |\n| No in-flight promise dedup for concurrent identical bridge calls | DEFER | Real concern but multi-process bridge already fail-opens; flag as P2 follow-up |\n| Unbounded prompt/brief sizes | DEFER | Add later as part of broader plugin hardening if observed in production |\n\n### Evidence (to be filled post-implementation)\n\n| Check | Result |\n|-------|--------|\n| `runtime_ready` reaches `true` after first session event | PASS — SDK `Event` uses `type`; direct Node smoke with `{ type: 'session.created' }` returned `runtime_ready=true` and `last_bridge_status=ready`. Vitest also proves skipped bridge responses no longer reset readiness to false. |\n| `cache_hits + cache_misses == bridge_invocations` invariant in vitest | REFRAMED + PASS — code audit showed `bridge_invocations` counts subprocess spawns/cache misses. Status now emits `advisor_lookups`; vitest asserts `cache_misses === bridge_invocations` and `cache_hits + cache_misses === advisor_lookups`. Direct smoke returned `cache_hits=1`, `cache_misses=1`, `bridge_invocations=1`, `advisor_lookups=2`. |\n| `output.system` defensive guard handles `{}` input | PASS — `appendAdvisorBrief()` initializes `output.system = Array.isArray(output?.system) ? output.system : []`; vitest covers both `{}` and `{ system: null }`. |\n| `sessionIdFrom()` returns a string for object inputs | PASS — session IDs normalize through stable stringification; vitest passes reordered object session IDs and observes deterministic cache hits. |\n| Strict spec validation | PASS — Phase 5 `validate.sh --strict` passed 0 errors / 0 warnings. |\n\n### Consequences\n\n- **Positive**: Status tool numbers are trustworthy (operator-visible).\n- **Positive**: Plugin handler is robust to host-contract drift (defensive `output.system` guard).\n- **Positive**: P2 deferrals are explicitly tracked as follow-ups, not silent.\n- **Negative**: Module-global state and in-flight dedup remain as latent concerns; if a future change loads the plugin twice or hits high-concurrency request patterns, those issues will surface.\n\n### References\n\n- Real-session evidence: 2026-04-23 OpenCode TUI in Public root, status tool output captured by user.\n- Codex independent review (during Phase 5 test 1): 5 findings; this ADR addresses 4 directly + defers 1.\n\n---\n\n### Cross-References\n\n- **Spec**: `spec.md` §2 (problem), §4 (requirements REQ-001..REQ-013)\n- **Plan**: `plan.md` §2 quality gates, §4 phases (now 5 phases)\n- **Tasks**: `tasks.md` T-01..T-09 (Phase 1), T-10..T-19 (Phase 2), T-20..T-29 (Phase 3), T-30..T-35 (Phase 4 — hook remap), T-36..T-41 (Phase 5 — status accuracy + defensive guards)\n- **Sibling phases**: `../002-copilot-custom-instructions-hook-parity/`, `../003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/`\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-alternatives -->\n## Alternatives Considered\n\n<!-- TODO: backfill with real content; stub added by Tier 4 alignment -->\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-alternatives -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-five-checks -->\n## Five Checks Evaluation\n\n<!-- TODO: backfill with real content; stub added by Tier 4 alignment -->\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-five-checks -->",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:10881:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:9360:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3302:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/003-embedder-testing-and-architecture/001-local-embeddings-foundation/017-llama-cpp-default-flip/scratch/probe-1k-corpus.json:47:    "content": "---\ntitle: \"...ization/007-hook-parity/005-codex-hook-parity-remediation/research/007-deep-review-remediation-pt-02/research]\"\ndescription: \"Synthesis of 10 iterations investigating the exact Codex CLI 0.122.0 hook contract (stdin payload, stdout-to-model-context injection, exit codes, timeouts, concurrency, event taxonomy, env propagation). Outcome: A (full parity achievable) — Codex hooks DO inject stdout as developer-role messages, exactly the behavior needed to mirror Claude's SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit payloads.\"\ntrigger_phrases:\n  - \"ization\"\n  - \"009\"\n  - \"hook\"\n  - \"daemon\"\n  - \"parity\"\n  - \"research\"\n  - \"007\"\n  - \"deep\"\nimportance_tier: \"important\"\ncontextType: \"research\"\n_memory:\n  continuity:\n    packet_pointer: \"system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-hook-parity/003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/research/007-deep-review-remediation-pt-02\"\n    last_updated_at: \"2026-04-24T15:25:01Z\"\n    last_updated_by: \"backfill-memory-block\"\n    recent_action: \"Backfilled _memory block (repo-wide frontmatter sweep)\"\n    next_safe_action: \"Revalidate packet docs and update continuity on next save\"\n    key_files: [\"research.md\"]\n---\n# Deep Research Synthesis — Codex CLI Hook Parity Contract\n\n**Spec phase**: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-hook-parity/003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/`\n**Research packet**: `007-deep-review-remediation-pt-02/`\n**Iterations**: 10 (all completed; newInfoRatio trajectory 0.90 → 0.10 over final 7 iterations)\n**Executor**: `cli-codex` with gpt-5.4, reasoning `high`, service_tier `fast`, sandbox `workspace-write`\n**Outcome**: **A (full hook parity achievable)**\n\n---\n\n## 1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY\n\n**The research question**: what is the exact Codex CLI 0.122.0 hook contract — stdin payload format, stdout-to-model-context semantics, exit-code behavior, timeout budget, multi-hook concurrency, event taxonomy, env propagation — so we can port the Claude-side `hooks/claude/user-prompt-submit.ts` to `hooks/codex/` without shipping against assumptions?\n\n**Short answer**: **Codex hooks DO inject stdout into the model context as developer-role messages.** This is the key capability copilot CLI lacks (per sibling phase 007). The injection surface is exactly what Claude's `SessionStart` + `UserPromptSubmit` hooks do. Full parity is achievable with a straightforward port.\n\nThe contract, confirmed across official docs + generated schemas + upstream Rust source + a working empirical probe on 0.122.0:\n\n- **Transport**: `tokio::process::Command` with piped stdin, writes a serialized JSON object containing event-specific fields.\n- **stdin schema (SessionStart)**: `{cwd, hook_event_name:\"SessionStart\", model, permission_mode, session_id, source: \"startup\"|\"resume\"|\"clear\", transcript_path}` — `additionalProperties: false`.\n- **stdin schema (UserPromptSubmit)**: `{cwd, hook_event_name:\"UserPromptSubmit\", model, permission_mode, prompt, session_id, transcript_path, turn_id}` — `additionalProperties: false`.\n- **stdout contract**: plain text stdout OR JSON `{hookSpecificOutput: {additionalContext: \"...\"}}` — both become a `role: \"developer\"` message in the transcript. SessionStart context is recorded BEFORE the user prompt; UserPromptSubmit context AFTER.\n- **Exit codes**: `0` = success + inject stdout. `2` + stderr text = block/decline (UserPromptSubmit only — aborts the turn with the stderr as reason). Other non-zero = hook failed, but turn continues (fail-open).\n- **Timeout**: per-hook `timeout` or `timeoutSec` field (seconds); default 600s. On timeout, hook result is empty; turn continues.\n- **Concurrency**: multiple matching hooks for one event run concurrently via `join_all`, NOT serial by registration order.\n- **Events (6 total)**: `SessionStart`, `PreToolUse`, `PermissionRequest`, `PostToolUse`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `Stop`.\n- **Environment**: inherits parent process env (no `env_clear` call in the command runner). Codex prepends arg0 directories to `PATH`.\n- **Feature flag required**: `[features] codex_hooks = true` in `~/.codex/config.toml`. **Hooks do not fire without this flag** — this was confirmed empirically: on 0.122.0, the user's existing `hooks.json` registrations are currently inert because the flag is absent from their `config.toml`.\n\n**Classification**: **Outcome A (full parity)** per `plan.md` §2 Phase 1 exit criteria. Phase 2 (port + register) can proceed immediately.\n\n---\n\n## 2. KEY FINDINGS (PRIMARY-SOURCE-CITED)\n\n### F1. Command hooks receive one JSON object on stdin (not argv, not plain text)\n\n`tokio::process::Command` is spawned with piped stdin; the serialized `SessionStartCommandInput` / `UserPromptSubmitCommandInput` Rust structs are written to the child's stdin via `write_all(input_json.as_bytes())`. The user's prompt is passed in the JSON `prompt` field, NOT as raw stdin text. `additionalProperties: false` in the generated schemas — unknown fields rejected.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Docs: [Codex hooks (developers.openai.com)](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n- Schema: [session-start.command.input.schema.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated/session-start.command.input.schema.json)\n- Schema: [user-prompt-submit.command.input.schema.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated/user-prompt-submit.command.input.schema.json)\n- Source: [command_runner.rs](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/command_runner.rs)\n- Empirical: `codex-cli 0.122.0` isolated `CODEX_HOME` probe; observed stdin JSON matched schema exactly.\n\n### F2. stdout IS injected into model-visible context as developer-role messages\n\n**THIS IS THE CAPABILITY COPILOT LACKS.** For both `SessionStart` and `UserPromptSubmit`:\n\n- Plain text stdout → added as extra developer context.\n- JSON stdout with `{hookSpecificOutput: {additionalContext: \"...\"}}` → additionalContext value added as extra developer context.\n- Stdout that LOOKS like JSON but fails to match the output schema → hook marked failed (not silently injected).\n\nSource-level: both event parsers append accepted context into `additional_contexts_for_model`. `SessionStart` has a test literally named `plain_stdout_becomes_model_context`. The runtime path is `DeveloperInstructions::new(additional_context).into()` per upstream issue #16486 — confirming developer-role injection, not system-prompt replacement and not prompt substitution.\n\n**Placement (verified empirically on 0.122.0)**:\n- `SessionStart` context: recorded as a `role: \"developer\"` transcript item BEFORE the user prompt.\n- `UserPromptSubmit` context: recorded AFTER the user message, as an additional developer-role transcript item visible to the model on the same turn.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Docs: [Codex hooks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n- Source: [session_start.rs parser](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/events/session_start.rs)\n- Source: [user_prompt_submit.rs parser](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/events/user_prompt_submit.rs)\n- Source: [hook_runtime.rs (runtime injection path)](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/core/src/hook_runtime.rs)\n- Schema: [user-prompt-submit.command.output.schema.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated/user-prompt-submit.command.output.schema.json)\n- Upstream context: [issue #16486](https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/16486)\n- Empirical: 0.122.0 probe — stdout observed as `role: \"developer\"` in transcript items.\n\n### F3. Exit codes — `2` is the UserPromptSubmit blocker signal\n\n- **Exit 0**: success. stdout (plain or JSON additionalContext) is injected.\n- **Exit 2** + non-empty stderr: `UserPromptSubmit`-specific blocker path. Source sets `status: Blocked`, `should_stop: true`, captures stderr as reason. Turn aborts. Empirically confirmed: a probe registered with `exit 2` on UserPromptSubmit caused Codex to emit `turn.completed` with zero model tokens and the user prompt was not recorded as a user response item before completion.\n- **Other non-zero** (e.g., exit 1): hook marked failed with \"hook exited with code N\" but turn continues (fail-open). Empirically verified on 0.122.0.\n\n`PermissionRequest` has its own parallel decision contract (JSON `{\"decision\":\"deny\", \"reason\":\"...\"}` or exit 2; any deny wins over allow). `PreToolUse` has an `updatedInput` field but it's parsed-but-unsupported and fails open.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Docs: [Codex hooks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n- Source: [user_prompt_submit.rs](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/events/user_prompt_submit.rs)\n- Source: [permission_request.rs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/events/permission_request.rs)\n- Source: [output_parser.rs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/output_parser.rs)\n- Empirical: 0.122.0 probe transcripts.\n\n### F4. No prompt transformation for UserPromptSubmit\n\nThe `UserPromptSubmit` output schema has `additionalProperties: false` and includes only: common fields, legacy `decision`/`reason`, `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext`. There is NO `updatedInput` or equivalent prompt-rewrite field. The generated schema rejects such fields. Hooks can INJECT additional developer context alongside the user's prompt, BLOCK the prompt, but cannot REPLACE the prompt.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Schema: [user-prompt-submit.command.output.schema.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated/user-prompt-submit.command.output.schema.json)\n- Source: [user_prompt_submit.rs](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/events/user_prompt_submit.rs)\n- Docs: [Codex hooks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n\n### F5. Timeout: per-hook `timeout`/`timeoutSec` in seconds, default 600s, fail-open on timeout\n\nSource: `timeout_sec.unwrap_or(600).max(1)`. On timeout, `tokio::time::timeout` returns error text `hook timed out after Ns`. Command runner produces no exit code, empty stdout/stderr. Event parsers treat that as failed-hook-run unless the event has already produced a supported decision. Empirically confirmed on 0.122.0: a `UserPromptSubmit` hook with `\"timeout\": 1` sleeping 5s caused Codex to record the user message after ~timeout and continue to the model request; the hook's post-sleep stdout was not injected.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Docs: [Codex hooks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n- Source: [discovery.rs (timeout default)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/discovery.rs)\n- Source: [command_runner.rs (timeout implementation)](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/command_runner.rs)\n- Empirical: 0.122.0 timeout probe.\n\n### F6. Multiple matching hooks run CONCURRENTLY (not serially by registration order)\n\nSource: `execute_handlers` uses `futures::future::join_all(...)` to launch all matching handlers at once. Result aggregation preserves selected handler order for folding, but execution is parallel. Implication: when the user's existing Superset `notify.sh` and a new Spec Kit Memory hook are both registered for the same event, they run in parallel. Neither can prevent the other from starting. For this spec: Spec Kit Memory's hook must be idempotent and must not depend on notify.sh having already completed.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Docs: [Codex hooks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n- Source: [dispatcher.rs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/dispatcher.rs)\n\n### F7. Event taxonomy (6 events, schema-backed, docs-matched)\n\n| Event | Can inject stdout? | Can block? | Reserved/unsupported fields |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `SessionStart` | ✓ (plain or JSON additionalContext) | — | — |\n| `UserPromptSubmit` | ✓ (plain or JSON additionalContext) | ✓ (exit 2 + stderr, or JSON decision:block) | `updatedInput` absent from schema |\n| `PreToolUse` | — | ✓ (via decision surface) | `updatedInput` parsed-but-unsupported (fail-open) |\n| `PermissionRequest` | — | ✓ (JSON decision:deny, or exit 2 + stderr) | `updatedInput`, `updatedPermissions`, `interrupt` reserved (fail-closed) |\n| `PostToolUse` | — | — | — |\n| `Stop` | — | — | — |\n\nDiscovery source enumerates exactly these six events.
