A future sub-phase could route counters through the existing `publishSkillGraphGeneration` pipeline if operators need cross-process visibility.\n3. **`'filesystem-fallback'` source uses console.warn for operator signal.** A more structured approach (e.g. a dedicated diagnostics field on `AdvisorProjection`) would avoid relying on log scraping. The current design keeps the change minimal and additive; a structured field can be added later without breaking consumers.\n4. **F-004-A4-04 only surfaces metadata-shape failures, not metadata-semantic failures.** A `graph-metadata.json` with valid shape but logically wrong content (e.g. `key_files: ['/nonexistent/path']`) still passes the parser silently — the existing `existsSync` filter just drops nonexistent entries. Catching semantic failures would require a separate validation pass beyond this fix's scope.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:limitations -->",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:10885:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:9364:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3309:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/003-embedder-testing-and-architecture/001-local-embeddings-foundation/017-llama-cpp-default-flip/scratch/probe-1k-corpus.json:7791:    "content": "---\ntitle: \"Implem [system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-runtime-executor-hardening/001-foundational-runtime/plan]\"\ndescription: \"4-wave implementation plan: Wave A infrastructure primitives (20h), Wave B cluster consumers (30h parallel 3 lanes), Wave C rollout + sweeps (15h), Wave D P2 maintainability (40h deferrable). Critical path ~60h / 6 working days.\"\ntrigger_phrases:\n  - \"017 phase 017 plan\"\n  - \"wave a infrastructure\"\n  - \"wave b consumers\"\n  - \"wave c rollout\"\n  - \"phase 017 implementation plan\"\nimportance_tier: \"critical\"\ncontextType: \"planning\"\ntemplate_source_hint: \"<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: plan-core | v2.2 -->\"\n_memory:\n  continuity:\n    packet_pointer: \"system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-runtime-executor-hardening/001-foundational-runtime\"\n    last_updated_at: \"2026-04-17T14:10:00Z\"\n    last_updated_by: \"claude-opus-4.7\"\n    recent_action: \"Plan scaffolded from iter-054 wave structure + segment-2 synthesis §5\"\n    next_safe_action: \"Begin Wave A implementation after spec approval\"\n    blockers: []\n---\n<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 2 -->\n<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: plan-core | v2.2 -->\n\n# Implementation Plan: Phase 017 Review-Findings Remediation\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:summary -->\n## 1. SUMMARY\n\nPhase 017 closes 27 remediation tasks across 4 waves. Wave A (infrastructure primitives, 20h) is the critical-path spine that unblocks Wave B. Wave B (consumers, 30h) runs 3 parallel lanes. Wave C (rollout + sweeps, 15h) performs tree-wide metadata refresh. Wave D (P2 maintainability, 40h) is deferrable. Total effort: ~105h / ~60h critical-path / 6 working days with 1 engineer.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:summary -->\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:quality-gates -->\n## 1.5 QUALITY GATES\n\nAfter each wave's code-complete, the following gates MUST pass before proceeding:\n\n1. **`/deep:start-review-loop :auto` ×7** on the wave's scope emits ZERO new P0, ZERO new P1\n2. **`validate.sh --strict` on 017 folder** exits 0 with 0 warnings\n3. **Atomic-ship groups verified** — no mid-rollout inconsistency windows observed\n4. **Vitest suite** passes for all modified modules\n5. **Rollback procedure** documented + tested per wave\n<!-- /ANCHOR:quality-gates -->\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:architecture -->\n## 1.6 ARCHITECTURE\n\n### Cluster B infrastructure spine\n```\nworkflow.ts (canonical save driver)\n  → refreshPerFolderDescription (fixed: T-CNS-01)\n  → refreshGraphMetadata (un-gated: T-W1-CNS-04)\n  → backfillResearchMetadata (new: T-CNS-02)\n  → validateContinuityFreshness (new: T-W1-CNS-05)\n```\n\n### Cluster D readiness contract\n```\nlib/code-graph/readiness-contract.ts (NEW: T-CGC-01)\n  ↑ imported by\nquery.ts, scan.ts, status.ts, context.ts, ccc-status.ts, ccc-reindex.ts, ccc-feedback.ts\n```\n\n### Cluster E hooks provenance\n```\nhooks/shared-provenance.ts (NEW: T-W1-HOK-02)\n  ↑ imported by\nhooks/claude/shared.ts, hooks/gemini/shared.ts, hooks/copilot/compact-cache.ts (NEW: T-W1-HOK-01)\n```\n<!-- /ANCHOR:architecture -->\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:phases -->\n## 1.7 PHASES (WAVES)\n\nMaps directly to waves A/B/C/D detailed below. Each wave is a discrete phase with its own gate.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:phases -->\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:testing -->\n## 1.8 TESTING\n\n### Unit tests (vitest)\n- `workflow.ts` monotonic `lastUpdated` test\n- `readiness-contract.ts` shared-fixture test\n- `shared-provenance.ts` Claude/Gemini/Copilot parity test\n- `scope-governance.normalizeScopeContext` matrix test\n- `retry-budget.ts` 3rd-retry skipped test\n- `gate-3-classifier.vitest.ts` +5 unicode cases\n- `evidence-marker-lint.vitest.ts` new\n\n### Integration / regression tests\n- `/deep:start-review-loop :auto` ×7 per wave gate\n- 16-folder sweep verification (T-CNS-03)\n- Copilot compact-cycle end-to-end test\n\n### Manual verification\n- Canary `/memory:save` on test folder (pre-Wave C sweep)\n- Session-resume permissive-mode canary (T-SRS-BND-01)\n- Docker `-v /tmp:/tmp` anti-pattern documented in deployment guide\n<!-- /ANCHOR:testing -->\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:dependencies -->\n## 1.9 DEPENDENCIES\n\n### External dependencies (none new)\nNo new npm packages required.\n\n### Internal file dependencies\nSee §6 in `spec.md` for the file-collision groups and atomic-ship constraints.\n\n### Phase dependencies\n- **Depends on**: Phase 017 (already landed — 27 fix commits since afbb3bc7f)\n- **Related**: 015-deep-review-and-remediation (prior audit cycle)\n- **Blocks**: Phase 019 changelog + parking-lot P2 items\n\n### Runtime dependencies\n- cli-codex gpt-5.4 xhigh fast (primary autonomous executor)\n- cli-copilot gpt-5.4 high (fallback, 3-concurrent max)\n- Opus 4.7 (manual orchestration + synthesis)\n\n### Operator-constraint dependencies\n- `feedback_phase_018_autonomous` (user memory) — DELETE-not-archive, dispatch deep-review ×7 per gate\n- `feedback_copilot_concurrency_override` (user memory) — 3 concurrent max\n- `feedback_stop_over_confirming` (user memory) — skip A/B/C/D approval menus when obvious\n<!-- /ANCHOR:dependencies -->\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->\n## 2. OVERVIEW (retained for narrative readability)\n\n27 remediation tasks organized in 4 waves with explicit file-collision grouping, atomic-ship constraints, and rollback procedures. Wave A (20h) is the critical-path infrastructure spine that unblocks Wave B. Wave B (30h) runs 3 parallel lanes after Wave A merges. Wave C (15h) performs tree-wide sweeps + lint activation. Wave D (40h) carries deferrable P2 maintainability. Total: ~105h / ~60h critical-path / 6 working days.\n\n**Execution mode**: Per `feedback_phase_018_autonomous` (user memory), each wave runs under cli-codex primary + cli-copilot fallback (3 concurrent max). After each wave's code-complete, dispatch `/deep:start-review-loop :auto` ×7 before proceeding to the next wave.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:wave-a -->\n## 2. WAVE A — INFRASTRUCTURE PRIMITIVES (20h / 2.5 days — CRITICAL PATH)\n\nBlocks everything downstream. No parallelism within this wave (all tasks touch foundational contracts).\n\n### 2.1 T-CNS-01 + T-W1-CNS-04 — Canonical save metadata writer (MERGED PR, 6h, M+M)\n\n**Files**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1259, 1333`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/memory/generate-context.ts:415`\n\n**Changes**:\n1. Remove `const ctxFileWritten = false` stub at workflow.ts:1259. Wire in actual `savePerFolderDescription` return value.\n2. Extend the description.json update block (lines 1261-1331) to write `lastUpdated: new Date().toISOString()` unconditionally on every call.\n3. Lift the `plannerMode === 'full-auto'` gate at workflow.ts:1333 — `refreshGraphMetadata` now runs on every canonical save regardless of plannerMode.\n4. Add unit test asserting monotonic `lastUpdated` advancement on successive saves.\n\n**Atomic-ship constraint**: Both fixes in ONE PR. Splitting creates a transient window where description.json lastUpdated updates but graph-metadata.json.derived.last_save_at lags (new divergence direction).\n\n**Resolves**: R4-P1-002, R51-P1-001, R51-P1-002, R56-P1-upgrade-001 (H-56-1 headline), partial R3-P2-001, partial R5-P1-001.\n\n### 2.2 T-CGC-01 — Extract lib/code-graph/readiness-contract.ts (4h, M)\n\n**Files**: Create `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/code-graph/readiness-contract.ts` (new); refactor `handlers/code-graph/query.ts:225-300` to consume it.\n\n**Changes**:\n1. Extract `canonicalReadinessFromFreshness()`, `queryTrustStateFromFreshness()`, `buildQueryGraphMetadata()`, `buildReadinessBlock()` functions from `query.ts:225-300` into the new shared module.\n2. Update `query.ts` to import and use the extracted helpers.\n3. Export 4-state `TrustState` type (`'live' | 'stale' | 'absent' | 'unavailable'`) from the new module.\n4. Vitest: assert shared contract returns same values as pre-refactor query.ts for fixture inputs.\n\n**Pre-work for T-W1-CGC-03** (Wave B): The shared module is the consumption point for the 5 other code-graph siblings.\n\n**Resolves**: R6-P1-001 (prerequisite for T-W1-CGC-03 follow-on).\n\n### 2.3 T-W1-HOK-02 — Extract hooks/shared-provenance.ts (4h, M)\n\n**Files**: Create `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/hooks/shared-provenance.ts` (new); refactor `hooks/claude/shared.ts:125-129` + `hooks/gemini/shared.ts:7`.\n\n**Changes**:\n1. Move `wrapRecoveredCompactPayload` + related provenance helpers from `hooks/claude/shared.ts` to `hooks/shared-provenance.ts`.\n2. Claude + Gemini `shared.ts` re-export from the shared module.\n3. Break the Gemini → Claude transitive import (R52-P2-001).\n4. Vitest: unchanged behavior for existing Claude + Gemini hooks.\n\n**Pre-work for T-W1-HOK-01** (Wave B): Enables Copilot `compact-cache.ts` to import from `hooks/shared-provenance.ts` directly rather than re-inline the logic.\n\n**Resolves**: R52-P2-001.\n\n### 2.4 T-SCP-01 — Collapse 4 local normalizers to canonical (4h, M)\n\n**Files**: `mcp_server/handlers/save/reconsolidation-bridge.ts:228-234`, `lib/storage/lineage-state.ts:198-204`, `handlers/save/types.ts:348-352`, `lib/validation/preflight.ts:440-444` — remove local `normalizeScopeValue` / `normalizeScopeMatchValue`. `lib/governance/scope-governance.ts:155-162` — keep canonical `normalizeScopeContext`.\n\n**Changes**:\n1. Delete 4 local normalizer definitions.\n2. Import and call `normalizeScopeContext` from `lib/governance/scope-governance.ts` at each call site.\n3. Vitest: semantic-equivalence matrix for `undefined`, `null`, `\"\"`, whitespace, non-string inputs.\n\n**Resolves**: R1-P1-001 + R4-P1-001 (compound).\n\n### 2.5 T-EVD-01-prep — Rewrap 016 checklist.md evidence markers (2h, S)\n\n**Files**: `(sub-phase 001-initial-research merged into parent — see implementation-summary.md §Sub-phase summaries)`\n\n**Changes**: Replace 170/179 `)` closers with `]` via automated find-replace. Verify with `grep -c '\\[EVIDENCE:.*\\]$'` matches `grep -c '\\[EVIDENCE:.*\\)$'` pre-change count.\n\n**Must complete before**: T-EVD-01 `--strict` mode activation in Wave C.\n\n**Resolves**: R3-P2-002 (data side only; tool side deferred to T-EVD-01).\n\n### Wave A gate\n\nAfter Wave A code-complete: `/deep:start-review-loop :auto` ×7 with focus on `workflow.ts`, `readiness-contract.ts`, `shared-provenance.ts`, `scope-governance.ts`. No regressions in pre-existing behavior.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:wave-a -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:wave-b -->\n## 3. WAVE B — CLUSTER CONSUMERS (30h / 4 days — PARALLEL 3 LANES)\n\nDepends on Wave A merged. Runs 3 parallel lanes.\n\n### 3.1 Lane B1 — Cluster B consumers (12h)\n\n#### 3.1.1 T-CNS-02 — Research folder backfill (4h, M)\n\n**Files**: New `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/memory/backfill-research-metadata.ts`; wire into `generate-context.js`.\n\n**Changes**:\n1. Walk `research/NNN-*/iterations/` directories.\n2. For each missing `description.json` or `graph-metadata.json`, auto-create from template.\n3. Run as post-step of canonical save when target spec folder has `research/` child.\n\n**Resolves**: R3-P1-002, R56-P1-NEW-003.\n\n#### 3.1.2 T-W1-CNS-05 — Continuity freshness validator (4h, M)\n\n**Files**: `scripts/spec/validate.sh`, new `scripts/validation/continuity-freshness.ts`.\n\n**Changes**:\n1. Parse `implementation-summary.md` frontmatter `_memory.continuity`.\n2. Warn if `last_updated_at` older than `graph-metadata.json.derived.last_save_at` by more than 10m.\n3. Wire into `--strict` mode.\n4. Optional: `writeContinuityFrontmatter()` helper invoked on canonical save (Phase 019 candidate).\n\n**Resolves**: R51-P1-003.\n\n#### 3.1.3 T-CGC-02 — context.ts error branch explicit (2h, S)\n\n**Files**: `handlers/code-graph/context.ts:98-105`\n\n**Changes**: Replace silent catch into `freshness: 'empty'` stub with explicit `error → 'unavailable'` branch emitting `readiness_check_crashed` reason.\n\n**Resolves**: Partial R6-P1-001 (observability mitigation if T-CGC-01/03 deferred).\n\n#### 3.1.4 T-RBD-03 — Design-intent comments (2h, S)\n\n**Files**: `handlers/save/post-insert.ts:344-369`, `handlers/save/response-builder.ts:136-201`.\n\n**Changes**: Add design-intent comment blocks at both rollup sites citing T-RBD-01 / commit `709727e98`. Explain: post-insert tracks failure-with-recovery; response surfaces nuance to MCP clients.\n\n**Resolves**: R6-P2-001.\n\n### 3.2 Lane B2 — Cluster D + Cluster E (16h)\n\n#### 3.2.1 T-W1-CGC-03 — 5-sibling readiness propagation (16h, L)\n\n**Files**: `handlers/code-graph/scan.ts`, `status.ts`, `context.ts`, `ccc-status.ts`, `ccc-reindex.ts`, `ccc-feedback.ts`.\n\n**Changes**:\n1. Each handler imports from `lib/code-graph/readiness-contract.ts` (created in T-CGC-01).\n2. Emit `canonicalReadiness` + `trustState` + `lastPersistedAt` in each handler's return payload.\n3. `status.ts` is HIGHEST priority (IS the canonical readiness probe).\n4. Vitest: assert all 6 handlers emit the 3 fields with the 4-state vocabulary.\n\n**Atomic-ship constraint**: Must land as one PR OR emit `trustState: 'unavailable'` stubs in un-refactored handlers during staged rollout. Mid-rollout consumers keying on `trustState !== undefined` would conclude scan/status/ccc-* failed.\n\n**Resolves**: R52-P1-001 (Cluster D).\n\n#### 3.2.2 T-W1-HOK-01 — Copilot compact-cache (6h, M)\n\n**Files**: Create `hooks/copilot/compact-cache.ts` (new); extend `hooks/copilot/session-prime.ts`.\n\n**Changes**:\n1. Create `compact-cache.ts` that writes `trustState: 'cached'` using helpers from `hooks/shared-provenance.ts` (Wave A).\n2. Extend `session-prime.ts` to read `payloadContract?.provenance.trustState` at entry.\n3. Vitest: parallel test to Claude + Gemini compact-cycle tests.\n\n**Constraint**: MUST follow T-W1-HOK-02 (reverse order re-inlines the helper as third duplicate).\n\n**Resolves**: R52-P1-002 (Cluster E), R56-P1-NEW-002 (compound H-56-4).\n\n### 3.3 Lane B3 — Cluster A + Cluster C + Standalone P1 (12h)\n\n#### 3.3.1 T-SCP-02 — Normalizer lint rule (2h, S)\n\n**Files**: `.eslintrc.js` or `scripts/spec/validate.sh`.\n\n**Changes**: Add lint that rejects new in-module `normalizeScope*` or `getOptionalString` helpers (exempt `scope-governance.ts`).\n\n**Constraint**: Must follow T-SCP-01 in same PR — lint-first breaks build.\n\n**Resolves**: R4-P1-001 (prevention of re-introduction).\n\n#### 3.3.2 T-SAN-01 + T-SAN-03 — Gate 3 NFKC (4h, S+S)\n\n**Files**: `shared/gate-3-classifier.ts:145-152`, `scripts/tests/gate-3-classifier.vitest.ts`.\n\n**Changes**:\n1. Add `.normalize('NFKC')` + `/[\\u00AD\\u200B-\\u200F\\uFEFF]/g` stripping to `normalizePrompt`.\n2. Add 5+ unicode test cases: Cyrillic `е`, soft hyphen, zero-width space, Greek `Ε`, combined homoglyph+zero-width.\n3. Rename `p0-a-cross-runtime-tempdir-poisoning.vitest.ts` `it()` block to \"Claude and Gemini consumers\" with schema-share note for OpenCode.\n\n**Atomic-ship constraint**: Tests fail until code lands. Both commits same PR.\n\n**Resolves**: R2-P1-002, R3-P2-003, R5-P2-001.\n\n#### 3.3.3 T-SAN-02 — sanitizeRecoveredPayload NFKC (2h, S)\n\n**Files**: `mcp_server/hooks/claude/shared.ts:100-119`.\n\n**Changes**: Mirror NFKC normalization from T-SAN-01 to `sanitizeRecoveredPayload` regex preprocessing.\n\n**Resolves**: R2-P2-001.\n\n#### 3.3.4 T-PIN-RET-01 — Retry exhaustion counter (4h, M)\n\n**Files**: `handlers/save/post-insert.ts:159-173, 347-365`; new `lib/enrichment/retry-budget.ts`.\n\n**Changes**:\n1. Add retry-exhaustion counter keyed on `(memoryId, step, reason)`.\n2. Skip `runEnrichmentBackfill` after N=3 retries for `partial_causal_link_unresolved` outcomes.\n3. Emit structured warning log.\n4. Vitest: assert 3rd retry is skipped; 4th attempt logs exhaustion.\n\n**Resolves**: R1-P1-002.\n\n### Wave B gate\n\nAfter Wave B code-complete: `/deep:start-review-loop :auto` ×7 across 3 lanes. No regressions. T-W1-CGC-03 atomic-ship constraint verified.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:wave-b -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:wave-c -->\n## 4. WAVE C — ROLLOUT + SWEEPS (15h / 2 days)\n\nDepends on Wave A + B merged.\n\n### 4.1 T-EVD-01 — Evidence-marker lint activation (3h, M)\n\n**Files**: `scripts/spec/validate.sh`, new `scripts/validation/evidence-marker-lint.ts`.\n\n**Changes**:\n1. Lint asserts every `[EVIDENCE: ...]` closes with `]` not `)`.\n2. Wire into `--strict` mode.\n\n**Pre-requisite**: 016 checklist.md rewrap (T-EVD-01-prep in Wave A §2.5) completed.\n\n**Resolves**: R3-P2-002 (tool-side).\n\n### 4.2 T-CNS-03 — 16-folder canonical-save sweep (8h, L)\n\n**Files**: All 16 sibling `026-graph-and-context-optimization/*/` folders.\n\n**Changes**:\n1. Run 1 test folder first (`002-cache-warning-hooks/` or a low-risk target). Verify `description.json.lastUpdated` + `graph-metadata.json.derived.last_save_at` fresh post-save.\n2. If test passes, sweep remaining 15 folders sequentially.\n3. Commit per-folder to enable selective rollback.\n\n**Rollback**: Per-folder revert; check `jq '.lastUpdated' description.json` before next folder.\n\n**Resolves**: R5-P1-001, R3-P2-001 (data side).\n\n### 4.3 T-CPN-01 — Closing-pass-notes amend (1h, S)\n\n**Files**: `research/016-foundational-runtime-pt-01/closing-pass-notes.md:72-88`.\n\n**Changes**: Amend CP-002 section to mark RESOLVED by T-PIN-08 / `e774eef07`. Add status tag `[STATUS: RESOLVED 2026-04-17]`.\n\n**Resolves**: R3-P1-001.\n\n### 4.4 T-W1-MCX-01 — memory-context readiness rename (2h, S)\n\n**Files**: `handlers/memory-context.ts:200, 425`.\n\n**Changes**: Rename `readiness` field in `StructuralRoutingNudgeMeta` to `advisoryPreset` OR remove (always-literal field). Preserve consumer-facing contract.\n\n**Resolves**: R52-P2-002.\n\n### 4.5 T-SRS-BND-01 — Session-resume auth binding (LIFTED TO WAVE B IF CRITICAL, 1h reserve here)\n\n**Files**: `mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts:443-456`, `tools/lifecycle-tools.ts:67`.\n\n**Changes**: Bind `handleSessionResume` session-ID authentication to caller's runtime-identity at MCP transport layer. Reject mismatched `args.sessionId`.\n\n**Resolves**: R2-P1-001.\n\n### Wave C gate\n\nAfter Wave C code-complete: `/deep:start-review-loop :auto` ×7 with focus on 16-folder sweep outcomes + evidence-marker lint strict mode.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:wave-c -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:wave-d -->\n## 5. WAVE D — P2 MAINTAINABILITY (40h / deferrable)\n\nNon-urgent. Can land in parallel with later phases.\n\n### 5.1 T-EXH-01 — Exhaustiveness checks (8h, L)\n\n**Files**: New `lib/utils/assert-never.ts`. Apply to 8 typed unions: `OnIndexSkipReason`, `EnrichmentStepStatus`, `EnrichmentSkipReason`, `EnrichmentFailureReason`, `ConflictAbortStatus`, `HookStateLoadFailureReason`, `SharedPayloadTrustState`, `TriggerCategory`.\n\n**Changes**: `function assertNever(x: never): never { throw new Error(...) }`. Apply in switch default clauses. Use `satisfies` at lookup sites.\n\n**Resolves**: R4-P2-002.\n\n### 5.2 T-PIN-GOD-01 — Extract runEnrichmentStep helper (8h, L)\n\n**Files**: `handlers/save/post-insert.ts:133-376`.\n\n**Changes**: Extract `runEnrichmentStep(name, isEnabled, runner, options)` helper. Reduce `runPostInsertEnrichment` from 243 LOC to ~80. Preserve all 5 enrichment behaviors.\n\n**Resolves**: R4-P2-001.\n\n### 5.3 T-W1-PIN-02 — OnIndexSkipReason satisfies clause (2h, S)\n\n**Files**: `handlers/save/post-insert.ts:302-316`.\n\n**Changes**: Add `satisfies Record<OnIndexSkipReason, EnrichmentSkipReason>` clause. Warn-log on unmapped variant.\n\n**Resolves**: R51-P2-001.\n\n### 5.4 T-RCB-DUP-01 — Extract runAtomicReconsolidationTxn (6h, M)\n\n**Files**: `lib/storage/reconsolidation.ts:507-600`.\n\n**Changes**: Extract `runAtomicReconsolidationTxn(predecessorSnapshot, op, ops)` for the duplicate deprecate-path + content-update-path transaction blocks.\n\n**Resolves**: R4-P2-003.\n\n### 5.5 T-YML-CP4-01 — Typed YAML predicate (4h, M)\n\n**Files**: `.opencode/commands/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_complete_confirm.yaml:1099`.\n\n**Changes**: Replace prose `when:` string with typed predicate matching S7 YAML grammar (`shared/predicates/boolean-expr.ts`).\n\n**Resolves**: CP-004.\n\n### 5.6 T-W1-HST-02 — Docker deployment note (2h, S)\n\n**Files**: Deployment docs (find `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/README.md` or create `DEPLOYMENT` docs).\n\n**Changes**: Warn against `-v /tmp:/tmp` across Copilot MCP containers (R53-P1w-001). OPTIONAL: `getProjectHash()` incorporates `process.getuid?.()` for defense-in-depth.\n\n**Resolves**: R53-P1w-001.\n\n### 5.7 Deferred parking-lot P2s (tracked, not scheduled)\n\n- R55-P2-002 — underused `importance-tier` helper\n- R55-P2-003 — `executeConflict` precondition-block DRY opportunity\n- R55-P2-004 — YAML boolean-predicate evolution gap\n\n### Wave D gate\n\nAfter Wave D code-complete (any subset): `/deep:start-review-loop :auto` ×3 (lighter gate for maintainability-only wave).\n<!-- /ANCHOR:wave-d -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:rollback -->\n## 6. ROLLBACK STRATEGY\n\n### Per-task rollback\n\nEach task lands as a separate commit (or atomic-ship group) to enable `git revert` per unit.\n\n### Wave-level rollback\n\n| Wave | Rollback action | Risk |\n|------|-----------------|------|\n| Wave A | `git revert` merged PR; no persistent state changes | Low — all in-code |\n| Wave B | `git revert` per-lane; T-W1-CGC-03 may leave stub state if mid-rollout | Medium — requires verification sweep |\n| Wave C | Per-folder `git revert` for T-CNS-03; re-run T-CNS-01 + T-W1-CNS-04 if description.json corrupted | HIGH — 16-folder sweep rollback is expensive |\n| Wave D | Per-task rollback trivial | Low |\n\n### Irreversible operations\n\n- T-CNS-03 16-folder sweep WRITES to existing `description.json` files. Pre-sweep backup recommended: `git stash` the current `026/*/description.json` state before sweep runs.\n- T-EVD-01-prep rewraps 170+ checklist.md closers. Reversible via `git revert` but visual noise in diff.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:rollback -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:risks -->\n## 7. RISK MATRIX\n\n| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |\n|------|------------|--------|-------------|\n| Wave A introduces regression in canonical save | Low | High | Vitest coverage on all 3 workflow.ts changes; gate is `/deep:start-review-loop :auto` ×7 |\n| T-CGC-01 + T-W1-CGC-03 split mid-rollout creates consumer breakage | Medium | Medium | Atomic-ship constraint OR `trustState: 'unavailable'` stub strategy |\n| Copilot runtime breaks on T-W1-HOK-01 deploy | Low | Medium | Parallel Claude + Gemini coverage via `hooks/shared-provenance.ts` |\n| T-SRS-BND-01 blocks legitimate session-resume flows | Low | High | Staged rollout with permissive-mode flag; verify on canary session before full enable |\n| T-CNS-03 sweep corrupts 016-foundational or other critical folder | Medium | High | Run on low-risk folder first; per-folder commit; pre-sweep `git stash` |\n| Phase 017 autonomous execution triggers H-56-4 observability gap | Medium | Medium | Cluster E (T-W1-HOK-01/02) MUST land BEFORE Copilot-primary autonomous iteration |\n| Wave D deferral creates tech-debt backlog | Low | Low | Track in Phase 019 parking lot |\n<!-- /ANCHOR:risks -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:success-signal -->\n## 8. SUCCESS SIGNALS\n\nAfter all 4 waves complete:\n\n- [ ] `validate.sh --strict` on all 16 sibling 026 folders exits 0 with 0 warnings\n- [ ] `/deep:start-review-loop :auto` ×7 on Wave A/B/C/D scope emits ZERO new P0, ZERO new P1\n- [ ] All 10 original review P1s marked RESOLVED with evidence citations in checklist.md\n- [ ] All 9 new segment-2 P1s marked RESOLVED\n- [ ] `jq '.lastUpdated' description.json` across 16 sibling folders returns timestamps within 10m of `jq '.derived.last_save_at' graph-metadata.json`\n- [ ] `grep -l 'trustState' handlers/code-graph/*.ts | wc -l` returns `7` (all siblings hardened)\n- [ ] Copilot `hooks/copilot/{compact-cache.ts, session-prime.ts, shared.ts}` all present\n- [ ] `grep -c '\\[EVIDENCE:.*\\)$' checklist.md` returns `0` (all closers canonical)\n<!-- /ANCHOR:success-signal -->",

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:10890:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:9369:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3861:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/011-source-bug-and-misalignment-audit/review-r2-opus/iterations/iteration-009.md:17:  Verified both implementations in current code. The finding's FACTS are accurate: launcher (.cjs) and owner-lease.ts both implement the full owner-lease lifecycle, and their single-winner concurrency primitives diverge — launcher acquireOwnerLeaseFile uses last-writer-wins-then-reread (mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:377-386, reread check `reread.ownerPid !== process.pid`), while owner-lease.ts acquireOwnerLease uses a mutation-lock CAS (owner-lease.ts:378-428 via tryAcquireOwnerLeaseMutationLock + check-under-lock + writeOwnerLeaseAtomic). DR-002-01 was indeed applied separately to both copies.  REFUTED as an outstanding session-introduced defect for three grounded reasons:  1) PRE-EXISTING duplication. `git show 0ef38d58:.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs` shows the launcher ALREADY had writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive (L265), buildOwnerLease (L281), classifyOwnerLease (L325), acquireOwnerLeaseFile (L344) at baseline. `git show 0ef38d58:.../owner-lease.ts` shows acquireOwnerLease (L235), writeOwnerLeaseExclusive (L115) already present. The two-implementation split and their differing exclusive-write styles pre-date baseline 0ef38d58 — the session did not introduce the duplication.  2) Not a reportable category. The four reportable categories are (a) genuinely-new issue, (b) landed fix that is itself wrong/incomplete, (c) deferred finding whose status changed, (d) false-confidence regression test. This fits none. The finder explicitly concedes "the DR fixes themselves are each individually correct in both copies" and "this is a standing drift hazard, not a per-fix bug." I independently verified both fixes are correct (launcher reread at L382-385; owner-lease lock+recheck at L396-428). The proposed remedy is an optional ADR/doc comment with explicit instruction "do NOT attempt to merge them" — a hygiene opinion, not a defect.  3) The divergence is architecturally justified and partly already documented. The launcher header (L1-11) documents it is a bootstrap child-process launcher that loads env, builds dist, serializes starts BEFORE the compiled TS ESM exists — so it genuinely cannot import owner-lease.ts at startup; the finder agrees ("the bootstrap constraint is real"). A partial cross-reference already exists at mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:411 ("closing it would require the launcher to share owner-lease.ts's mutation lock"). The only gap vs the proposed fix is a top-of-function paired-change checklist note and a back-reference in owner-lease.ts — pure P2 doc polish on pre-existing intentional structure.  Net: real observation but PRE-EXISTING architecture; not a bug, not a broken fix, not a false-confidence test. Severity at most P2 (doc nicety). Refuted as an outstanding session-introduced finding.

winner is a new non-mutating getGraphReadinessSnapshot helper for code_graph_status; getGraphFreshness currently loses action/reason detail, while ensureCodeGraphReady is unsuitable because it caches, State JSONL fields: `type`, `iteration`/`run`, `newInfoRatio`, `status`, `focus`, `focusTrack`, `findingsCount`, `timestamp`, `answeredQuestions`, `sourcesQueried`, `convergenceSignals.compositeStop`, and event fields such as `event`, `signals`, `decision`, `blockers`. It reads no YAML frontmatter. | Strategy anchors replaced exactly as `<!-- ANCHOR:${anchorId} -->...<!-- /ANCHOR:${anchorId} -->` for `key-questions`, `answered-questions`, `what-worked`, `what-failed`, `exhausted-approaches`, `ruled-out-directions`, and `next-focus`. | Missing config/state/strategy files hard fail via `readFileSync`. Corrupt JSONL hard fails unless `--lenient`. Missing iteration directory is allowed as empty. Missing strategy anchor hard throws. Missing delta files only skips resource-map emission. | Iteration markdown parser expects exact second-level headings: `## Focus`, `## Findings`, `## Ruled Out`, `## Dead Ends`, `## Sources Consulted`, `## Reflection`, and `## Recommended Next Focus`. Strategy question parser expects `## 3. KEY QUESTIONS (remaining)` with `- [ ]` or `- [x]` items. Reflection labels are exact bullets: `What worked and why`, `What did not work and why`, `What I would do differently`. |

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:10923:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-002.out:929:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/024-compact-code-graph/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:28:- P2-001: `session_resume` implements a `sessionId` override for cached-summary selection, but the public tool contract strips that field before callers can use it. The handler accepts `sessionId` and forwards it into cached-summary resolution [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts:87] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts:409], while both schema layers only allow `specFolder` and `minimal` [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:669] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/schemas/tool-input-schemas.ts:442]. In multi-session Claude recovery, operators cannot disambiguate which cached session to resume even though the implementation was written for that path.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:10924:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-002.out:1151:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/008-hydra-db-based-features/research/iterations/iteration-001-opus.md:36:9. **Bug: `validateGovernedIngest` returns `allowed: true` when no governance is needed, but normalizes empty strings for required fields** — When `requiresGovernedIngest` returns false (line 224-241), the function returns `allowed: true` with `tenantId: ''` and `sessionId: ''`. Downstream code that checks `decision.normalized.tenantId || null` (line 279 in `buildGovernancePostInsertFields`) will write `null` for these, but code checking `decision.normalized.tenantId` as truthy will incorrectly treat the empty string as present. The `Required<Pick<...>>` type forces these to be strings, hiding the emptiness.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:10934:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-002.out:2316:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/002-spec-kit-internals/003-template-levels/002-manifest-driven-template-design/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:28:| `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | JSON config fields: `resource_map.emit` or `resourceMap.emit`, `topic`, `createdAt`, `status`, `maxIterations`, `lineage.sessionId`, `lineage.parentSessionId`, `lineage.lineageMode`, `lineage.generation`, `lineage.continuedFromRun`. State JSONL fields: `type`, `iteration`/`run`, `newInfoRatio`, `status`, `focus`, `focusTrack`, `findingsCount`, `timestamp`, `answeredQuestions`, `sourcesQueried`, `convergenceSignals.compositeStop`, and event fields such as `event`, `signals`, `decision`, `blockers`. It reads no YAML frontmatter. | Strategy anchors replaced exactly as `<!-- ANCHOR:${anchorId} -->...<!-- /ANCHOR:${anchorId} -->` for `key-questions`, `answered-questions`, `what-worked`, `what-failed`, `exhausted-approaches`, `ruled-out-directions`, and `next-focus`. | Missing config/state/strategy files hard fail via `readFileSync`. Corrupt JSONL hard fails unless `--lenient`. Missing iteration directory is allowed as empty. Missing strategy anchor hard throws. Missing delta files only skips resource-map emission. | Iteration markdown parser expects exact second-level headings: `## Focus`, `## Findings`, `## Ruled Out`, `## Dead Ends`, `## Sources Consulted`, `## Reflection`, and `## Recommended Next Focus`. Strategy question parser expects `## 3. KEY QUESTIONS (remaining)` with `- [ ]` or `- [x]` items. Reflection labels are exact bullets: `What worked and why`, `What did not work and why`, `What I would do differently`. |

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11055:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-003.out:7889:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2806:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/review/hs/logs/iter-009.out:43:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780205290663,"sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_function_4vnhn0n98zpe_1","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: ---\n2: name: deep-loop-runtime\n3: version: 1.4.0\n4: description: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\n5: allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n6: ---\n7: \n8: # Deep Loop Runtime\n9: \n10: Shared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n11: \n12: ---\n13: \n14: ## 1. WHEN TO USE\n15: \n16: This skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n17: \n18: Use it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n19: \n20: - executor configuration and audit metadata\n21: - prompt-pack rendering\n22: - post-dispatch validation\n23: - atomic state files and JSONL repair\n24: - loop locking and permissions gates\n25: - Bayesian scoring and fallback routing\n26: - coverage-graph schema, query, and signal interpretation\n27: \n28: ### Activation Triggers\n29: \n30: This skill is activated when:\n31: \n32: - A deep-review or deep-research workflow YAML invokes `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/<name>.cjs`\n33: - A test file imports `from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/...'`\n34: - An operator queries / mutates the coverage graph via the runtime scripts\n35: - A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing\n36: \n37: ### When NOT to Use\n38: \n39: Do not invoke this skill when:\n40: \n41: - A user requests an interactive command (use `/deep:start-review-loop` or `/deep:start-research-loop` instead — those orchestrate this runtime)\n42: - A package outside deep-review / deep-research needs deep-loop semantics (out of supported scope; open a packet to extend ownership)\n43: - An MCP tool surface is needed (this skill explicitly has none — the isolation ADR removed the deep-loop MCP tools)\n44: \n45: ---\n46: \n47: ## 2. SMART ROUTING\n48: \n49: This skill has no direct LLM-facing keyword triggers — it is invoked by workflow YAMLs (via `bash:` script calls) and by test imports. The router below documents which sub-domain to load for a given consumer query.\n50: \n51: ### Primary Detection Signal\n52: \n53: ```bash\n54: request_text=\"$(printf '%s' \"$USER_REQUEST\" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')\"\n55: case \"$request_text\" in\n56:   *\"deep-loop runtime\"*|*\"deep-loop-runtime\"*|*\"executor config\"*|*\"prompt pack\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n57:   *\"coverage graph\"*|*\"deep_loop_graph\"*|*\"bayesian scorer\"*|*\"loop lock\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n58:   *\"fallback router\"*|*\"post-dispatch validate\"*|*\"jsonl repair\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n59:   *) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=0 ;;\n60: esac\n61: ```\n62: \n63: ### Phase Detection\n64: \n65: ```text\n66: TASK CONTEXT\n67:     |\n68:     +- STEP 0: Detect runtime-call intent vs. consumer-orchestration intent\n69:     +- STEP 1: If consumer (workflow YAML), invoke the relevant scripts/*.cjs\n70:     +- STEP 2: If contributor (modifying lib code), load matching lib/ subtree\n71:     +- STEP 3: If test author, load tests/ + lib/ pair\n72: ```\n73: \n74: ### Resource Domains\n75: \n76: ```text\n77: lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)\n78: lib/coverage-graph/*.ts       # 3 schema + query + signals modules\n79: scripts/*.cjs                 # 4 entry points (convergence, upsert, query, status)\n80: database/*.sqlite              # runtime-owned SQLite (deep-loop-graph.sqlite)\n81: tests/{unit,integration,lifecycle}/  # 21+ vitest files split by responsibility\n82: ```\n83: \n84: ### Resource Loading Levels\n85: \n86: | Tier | When loaded | Purpose |\n87: |------|-------------|---------|\n88: | ALWAYS | SKILL.md frontmatter | description, allowed-tools, version |\n89: | CONDITIONAL | When a runtime call dispatches | `lib/` modules required by the called function |\n90: | ON_DEMAND | When a test fails or a contributor extends | `tests/` + matching `lib/` sub-tree |\n91: \n92: ### Smart Router Pseudocode\n93: \n94: ```text\n95: IF request mentions a specific lib name (e.g. \"bayesian-scorer\", \"prompt-pack\"):\n96:   → Load lib/deep-loop/<name>.ts + matching tests/unit/<name>.vitest.ts\n97: ELIF request mentions coverage-graph / convergence / signals:\n98:   → Load lib/coverage-graph/*.ts + tests/integration/review-depth-*.vitest.ts\n99: ELIF request mentions a script (convergence.cjs, status.cjs, etc.):\n100:   → Load scripts/<name>.cjs + tests/integration/<name>-script.vitest.ts + lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts\n101: ELSE:\n102:   → Defer to consumer skill (deep-review / deep-research)\n103: ```\n104: \n105: ---\n106: \n107: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS\n108: \n109: ### Runtime Architecture\n110: \n111: The runtime exposes deep-loop primitives to consumer workflows through two paths: **direct `.cjs` script invocation** (replacing the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools) and **TypeScript library imports** (from workflow scripts and tests).\n112: \n113: ```text\n114: [deep-review/deep-research workflow YAML]\n115:             │\n116:             ├─ bash: node .../scripts/<name>.cjs   (coverage-graph mutations)\n117:             │       └─→ lib/coverage-graph/*.ts → database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite\n118:             │\n119:             └─ ts-import: lib/deep-loop/<lib>.ts   (runtime primitives)\n120:                     └─→ atomic-state, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, etc.\n121: ```\n122: \n123: ### Library Structure\n124: \n125: **`lib/deep-loop/` (10 modules):**\n126: - `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings\n127: - `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)\n128: - `prompt-pack.ts` — renders the iteration prompt template\n129: - `post-dispatch-validate.ts` — validates iteration outputs (markdown + JSONL + delta)\n130: - `atomic-state.ts` — atomic state-log writes\n131: - `jsonl-repair.ts` — recovers corrupt JSONL state lines\n132: - `loop-lock.ts` — single-writer locking\n133: - `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks\n134: - `bayesian-scorer.ts` — convergence scoring\n135: - `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix\n136: \n137: **`lib/coverage-graph/` (3 modules):**\n138: - `coverage-graph-db.ts` — SQLite schema + node-kind allow-list + connection lifecycle\n139: - `coverage-graph-query.ts` — query builders\n140: - `coverage-graph-signals.ts` — convergence signal extraction\n141: \n142: ### Council Primitives\n143: \n144: The Runtime Boundary Decision (ADR-001) extends `deep-loop-runtime/lib/` with council-compatible infrastructure primitives while keeping operator-facing and domain workflow semantics in `deep-ai-council`. These primitives are consumed by downstream deep-ai-council orchestration (per-topic multi-round, session-findings registry, command and skill wiring, parity and cost docs) for per-topic orchestration, multi-topic session state, command wiring, parity tests and docs.\n145: \n146: **`lib/council/` (5 modules):**\n147: - `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.\n148: - `round-state-jsonl.cjs` — appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard, repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append, fsyncs writes, and exposes round-state readers for resume.\n149: - `adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs` — scores Round-N -> Round-N+1 adjudicator verdict deltas using ADR-003 weights for option change, confidence delta, material-risk Jaccard delta, axis flip rate, and blocking-disagreement delta.\n150: - `cost-guards.cjs` — normalizes and enforces ADR-004 defaults for `max_rounds_per_topic`, `max_topics_per_session`, `saturation_threshold`, and `seats_per_round`, including upper-bound seat-output calculation.\n151: - `session-state-hierarchy.cjs` — creates and validates the ADR-002 session -> topic -> round state shape, including stable `topic-NNN-slug` and `round-NNN` ids.\n152: \n153: The existing `lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts`, and `lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` remain the sibling runtime contracts. Council modules mirror those durability semantics in a council-scoped CJS surface so downstream `deep-ai-council` phases can consume them without modifying deep-review or deep-research behavior.\n154: \n155: ### Script Entry Points\n156: \n157: Four `.cjs` scripts replace the 4 deleted MCP tools, each honoring the same JSON-in / JSON-out contract documented in ADR-001 (the script interface contract):\n158: \n159: | Script | Replaces | Purpose | Exit codes |\n160: |--------|----------|---------|------------|\n161: | `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes typed CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED decisions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n162: | `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores nodes + edges from iteration `graphEvents` | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n163: | `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims, contradictions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n164: | `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Session-scoped health report | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n165: | `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |\n166: | `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |\n167: | `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |\n168: | `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |\n169: \n170: **Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.\n171: \n172: Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.\n173: \n174: ### Test Organization\n175: \n176: ```text\n177: tests/\n178: ├── unit/         # 13 files — per-module unit tests (atomic-state, executor-config, etc.)\n179: ├── integration/  # 7 files — script invocations + review-depth fixtures\n180: ├── lifecycle/    # 1 file — db-open-close pattern verification\n181: └── _helpers/     # 1 file — spawn-cjs helper for direct-invocation tests\n182: ```\n183: \n184: The system-spec-kit `mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` so a single vitest run discovers both surfaces.\n185: \n186: ---\n187: \n188: ## 4. RULES\n189: \n190: ### ALWAYS\n191: \n192: - **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)\n193: - **ALWAYS** emit JSON-only output to stdout from `scripts/*.cjs` (workflow YAML parses stdout for output bindings)\n194: - **ALWAYS** include `'use strict';` on line 2 of every `.cjs` script (per sk-code OPENCODE JS style)\n195: - **ALWAYS** include a `MODULE: <name>` header comment in every `lib/**/*.ts` file (per sk-code OPENCODE TS-MODULE-HEADER convention)\n196: - **ALWAYS** preserve atomic-state semantics when mutating JSONL state — never partial-write\n197: - **ALWAYS** acquire `loop-lock` before any state-log mutation\n198: \n199: ### NEVER\n200: \n201: - **NEVER** register MCP tools from this skill — the isolation ADR explicitly removed the MCP surface; reintroducing tools defeats the FULL_ISOLATE direction\n202: - **NEVER** open the SQLite DB outside `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` — single owner of the connection\n203: - **NEVER** import from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/` or `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/` — those locations are empty after the runtime moved here; import from this skill instead\n204: - **NEVER** write to `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` without holding the `loop-lock`\n205: - **NEVER** swallow exceptions in script entry points — propagate to the exit-code matrix\n206: \n207: ### ESCALATE IF\n208: \n209: - **ESCALATE** to the user if a runtime call requires a new MCP tool — the architectural direction prohibits MCP additions; the user must decide on policy override\n210: - **ESCALATE** to a new ownership ADR if a new consumer skill (beyond deep-review and deep-research) needs deep-loop runtime — extension requires a new ownership ADR\n211: - **ESCALATE** if the SQLite schema requires a backward-incompatible change — coordinate with all consumer workflows + add migration to `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n212: - **ESCALATE** if a script's stdout JSON contract changes — every workflow YAML output binding depends on the existing shape\n213: \n214: ---\n215: \n216: ## 5. SCOPE\n217: \n218: In scope:\n219: \n220: - runtime libraries under `lib/deep-loop/`\n221: - coverage-graph runtime libraries under `lib/coverage-graph/`\n222: - `.cjs` script entry points under `scripts/`\n223: - runtime-owned SQLite storage under `database/`\n224: - runtime tests under `tests/`\n225: \n226: Out of scope:\n227: \n228: - MCP tool registration\n229: - MCP handler ownership\n230: - DB lifecycle inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`\n231: - direct user command routing\n232: \n233: ---\n234: \n235: ## 6. ARCHITECTURE\n236: \n237: - `lib/deep-loop/` holds shared deep-loop runtime libraries.\n238: - `lib/coverage-graph/` holds coverage-graph schema, query, and signal helpers.\n239: - `scripts/` holds `.cjs` entry points used by workflow YAML and collateral commands.\n240: - `database/` owns relocated runtime SQLite artifacts.\n241: - `tests/` holds runtime-owned tests split from the MCP server surface.\n242: \n243: Shipped layout:\n244: \n245: | Path | Populated By | Purpose |\n246: |------|--------------|---------|\n247: | `lib/deep-loop/` | 10 files | Shared executor config, audit, prompt-pack, validation, state, repair, locking, permissions, scoring, and fallback routing. |\n248: | `lib/coverage-graph/` | 3 files | Coverage-graph schema owner, query helpers, and signal interpretation. |\n249: | `scripts/` | 4 files | Direct `status`, `query`, `upsert`, and `convergence` entry points replacing the removed deep-loop MCP tools. |\n250: | `database/` | SQLite | Runtime-owned `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |\n251: | `tests/` | Unit, integration, lifecycle | Runtime-owned tests discovered by the system-spec-kit MCP server Vitest config. |\n252: \n253: ---\n254: \n255: ## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS\n256: \n257: - **ADR-001 (AI Council SPLIT ruling)**: original AI Council SPLIT ruling. Superseded by user directive.\n258: - **Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP)**: Moves deep-loop runtime, coverage-graph schema/query/signals, script entry points, storage, and tests into this peer skill while removing the deep-loop MCP tool surface.\n259: - **Deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML** call this runtime through script entry points after the runtime cutover.\n260: - **system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts** includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` for cross-package test discovery.\n261: - **deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs** depends on the moved coverage-graph runtime via lib imports.\n262: \n263: ---\n264: \n265: ## 8. SUCCESS CRITERIA\n266: \n267: The runtime is healthy when:\n268: \n269: - Every `scripts/` entry point (`status`, `query`, `upsert`, `convergence`) returns valid JSON on its documented stdout contract\n270: - Consumer workflows resolve their `lib/` imports without path errors\n271: - The runtime-owned SQLite under `database/` is present and its coverage-graph schema matches `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n272: - Runtime tests under `tests/` pass through the system-spec-kit Vitest discovery glob\n273: \n274: ---\n275: \n276: ## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES\n277: \n278: - ADR-001 (script interface contract): documents the script-shim and DB-relocation contract.\n279: - Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP): documents the MCP tool surface removal rationale.\n280: - Decision rationale lives in this skill's changelog and originating decision records.\n281: \n282: Related resources:\n283: \n284: - Consumer skills: the `deep-review` and `deep-research` workflows call this runtime through the script entry points\n285: - `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` discovers this skill's `tests/`\n286: - Per-release detail and rationale: this skill's `changelog/` directory\n\n(End of file - total 286 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"---\nname: deep-loop-runtime\nversion: 1.4.0\ndescription: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\nallowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n---\n\n# Deep Loop Runtime\n\nShared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n\n---\n\n## 1. WHEN TO USE\n\nThis skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n\nUse it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n\n- executor configuration and audit metadata","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md","time":{"start":1780205290639,"end":1780205290662}},"id":"prt_e7c81029a0018WgctXtXLcmFh5","sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","messageID":"msg_e7c80eee8001ZiIO6I4pjCr85X"}}

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11062:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-003.out:7896:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3868:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/011-source-bug-and-misalignment-audit/review-r2-opus/deep-review-findings-registry.json:33:      "title": "Sole concurrency test exercises the wx fresh-lease path, not the DR-002-01 reclaim re-read CAS",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11063:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-003.out:7897:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3869:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/011-source-bug-and-misalignment-audit/review-r2-opus/deep-review-findings-registry.json:34:      "why": "The DR-002-01 fix added a re-read-after-write CAS to the RECLAIM branch of acquireOwnerLeaseFile (mk-code-index-launcher.cjs lines 373-386), which runs only when an `existing` stale owner lease is present. The only concurrency regression test, 'lets exactly one concurrent launcher own the lease' (line 207), spawns two launchers from a fresh createWorkspace() (tmpdir, no seeded owner lease, line 24-42). With no `existing` lease, both launchers take the `writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive` (O_EXCL `wx`) fresh path (lines 360-371), whose single-winner guarantee comes entirely from O_EXCL and predates DR-002-01. The reclaim-path re-read CAS (the actual code DR-002-01 changed) is never reached: 'reclaims a stale-heartbeat owner lease with a live PID' (line 309) and 'reclaims a dead-pid lease' (line 296) each spawn only ONE launcher against a pre-seeded stale lease, so no two processes ever concurrently hit the reclaim branch. The test therefore passes identically against the un-fixed code (delete lines 377-384 and 'exactly one concurrent launcher' still goes green), giving false confidence that the reclaim CAS is verified.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11064:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-003.out:7898:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3870:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/011-source-bug-and-misalignment-audit/review-r2-opus/deep-review-findings-registry.json:142:      "title": "Owner-lease state machine is duplicated across launcher.cjs and owner-lease.ts with DIVERGENT concurrency primitives",

winner remains an isolated SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR degraded-graph harness because unit coverage already exercises all fallbackDecision branches while the stress gap was end-to-end degraded state.","Q-P2 winner is a new non-mutating getGraphReadinessSnapshot helper for code_graph_status; getGraphFreshness currently loses action/reason detail, while ensureCodeGraphReady is unsuitable because it caches, cleans deleted tracked files, and can index.","Do not lower file-watcher debounce first; existing debounce/write-stability/concurrency/dedupe behavior makes status action visibility the lower-risk first fix.","Q-ARCH should carry target-authority vs recovered-context and readiness-action vs freshness-label as the two scoped seams."],"timestamp":"2026-04-27T17:22:17Z"}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11066:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-003.out:7976:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:2788:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/gpt55/iterations/iteration-001.md:107:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11203:fanout-salvage.cjs recovery
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11206:P0, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs:106, Salvage writes `iteration-${iterNum}.md`, but the command surface expects `iteration-{NNN}.md` at .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:100 and .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:108. For iteration 1, recovery creates `iteration-1.md` while downstream validation/merge looks for `iteration-001.md`, so recovery does not actually repair the missing artifact. Fix: format iteration ids with the same three-digit `NNN` resolver used by state paths, or pass the resolved iteration path into salvage.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11208:P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs:119, The salvage path writes recovered stdout and increments `salvaged` without calling `validateIterationOutputs` from .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/post-dispatch-validate.ts:515. That skips the declared post-dispatch checks for canonical JSONL, delta presence, executor provenance, and v2 review schema after recovery. Fix: run the validator before/after salvage and only count recovery as salvaged when the recovered artifact passes.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11210:P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs:103, `recoveredText` is extracted once from whole-lineage stdout and reused for every missing iteration in the loop. If multiple iteration files are missing, each receives the same concatenated lineage transcript rather than the specific `iter-NNN` output. Fix: recover per iteration from a per-iteration log/path or split stdout by iteration before writing each artifact.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11212:P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs:48, JSON text parts are accepted solely because at least one text part exists, and both JSON/raw paths silently slice at 50,000 chars. Whitespace-only or truncated markdown can be written as a successful recovered artifact, while the validator only rejects zero-byte files at .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/post-dispatch-validate.ts:556. Fix: require trimmed substantive content, mark truncation as degraded/failure, and make validation reject salvage-failure/truncated placeholders.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11219:[{"id":"f-p123-i4-01","severity":"P0","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs","line":106,"issue":"Salvage writes unpadded iteration filenames, while the command surface expects iteration-{NNN}.md, so recovered files are written to paths downstream validation and merge do not consume.","fix":"Format iteration ids with the shared three-digit NNN resolver or pass the resolved iteration path into salvage."},{"id":"f-p123-i4-02","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs","line":119,"issue":"Recovered stdout is written and counted as salvaged without reusing validateIterationOutputs, skipping canonical JSONL, delta, executor provenance, and v2 schema checks after recovery.","fix":"Run post-dispatch validation before and after salvage; increment salvaged only after the recovered artifact validates."},{"id":"f-p123-i4-03","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs","line":103,"issue":"Recovered text is extracted once from whole-lineage stdout and reused for every missing iteration, so multiple missing files can receive the same transcript instead of per-iteration output.","fix":"Recover from per-iteration logs or split stdout by iteration before writing each iteration artifact."},{"id":"f-p123-i4-04","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs","line":48,"issue":"JSON text parts and raw stdout are accepted/truncated without a substantive-content or truncation check, allowing whitespace-only or partial markdown to become a non-empty successful artifact.","fix":"Require trimmed substantive content, surface truncation as degraded/failure, and make validation reject salvage-failure or truncated placeholders."}]

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/tests/lifecycle/README.md:12:Verifies the SQLite connection lifecycle invariant: the DB is opened inside a `try`, closed in a `finally`, and the single-writer lock under `database/.deep-loop-graph-writer.lock` is acquired and released correctly.
