    21	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    22	// 1. HELPERS
    23	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    24	
    25	function inputError(message) {
    26	  const err = new Error(message);
    27	  err.code = 'INPUT_VALIDATION';
    28	  return err;
    29	}
    30	
    31	function jsonOut(payload) {
    32	  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n`);
    33	}
    34	
    35	function parseArgs(argv = process.argv.slice(2)) {
    36	  const args = {};
    37	  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i += 1) {
    38	    const token = argv[i];
    39	    if (!token.startsWith('--')) {
    40	      throw inputError(`Unexpected positional argument: ${token}`);
    41	    }
    42	    const key = token.slice(2).replace(/-([a-z])/g, (_, c) => c.toUpperCase());
    43	    const next = argv[i + 1];
    44	    if (next === undefined || next.startsWith('--')) {
    45	      args[key] = true;
    46	    } else {
    47	      args[key] = next;
    48	      i += 1;
    49	    }
    50	  }
    51	  return args;
    52	}
    53	
    54	function ensureString(args, key) {
    55	  if (!args[key] || typeof args[key] !== 'string') {
    56	    throw inputError(`${key} is required`);
    57	  }
    58	  return args[key];
    59	}
    60	
    61	function tryReadJson(filePath) {
    62	  if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) return null;
    63	  try {
    64	    return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));
    65	  } catch {
    66	    return null;
    67	  }
    68	}
    69	
    70	function readStateLog(stateLogPath) {
    71	  if (!fs.existsSync(stateLogPath)) return [];
    72	  const lines = fs.readFileSync(stateLogPath, 'utf8').trim().split('\n').filter(Boolean);
    73	  return lines.flatMap((line) => {
    74	    try {
    75	      return [JSON.parse(line)];
    76	    } catch {
    77	      return [];
    78	    }
    79	  });
    80	}
    81	
    82	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    83	// 2. RESEARCH MERGE
    84	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    85	
    86	/**
    87	 * Merge research findings registries from all lineages.
    88	 * Deduplicates by findingId; cross-model attribution via lineage labels.
    89	 * Returns the merged registry object.
    90	 */
    91	function mergeResearchRegistries(lineageData) {
    92	  const findingById = new Map();
    93	
    94	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
    95	    if (!registry || !Array.isArray(registry.keyFindings)) continue;
    96	    for (const finding of registry.keyFindings) {
    97	      const id = finding.id || finding.title;
    98	      if (!id) continue;
    99	      if (findingById.has(id)) {
   100	        const existing = findingById.get(id);
   101	        // Add this lineage as a contributing source
   102	        if (!existing._lineages) existing._lineages = [];
   103	        if (!existing._lineages.includes(label)) existing._lineages.push(label);
   104	      } else {
   105	        findingById.set(id, { ...finding, _lineages: [label] });
   106	      }
   107	    }
   108	  }
   109	
   110	  const mergedFindings = [...findingById.values()];
   111	  const openQuestionsById = new Map();
   112	  const ruledOutById = new Map();
   113	
   114	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
   115	    if (!registry) continue;
   116	    for (const q of registry.openQuestions ?? []) {
   117	      const id = q.id || q.question;
   118	      if (!id) continue;
   119	      if (!openQuestionsById.has(id)) openQuestionsById.set(id, { ...q, _lineages: [label] });
   120	      else openQuestionsById.get(id)._lineages.push(label);
   121	    }
   122	    for (const d of registry.ruledOutDirections ?? []) {
   123	      const id = d.id || d.direction;
   124	      if (!id) continue;
   125	      if (!ruledOutById.has(id)) ruledOutById.set(id, { ...d, _lineages: [label] });
   126	    }
   127	  }
   128	
   129	  const totalIters = lineageData.reduce((sum, { registry }) => {
   130	    return sum + (registry?.metrics?.iterationsCompleted ?? 0);
   131	  }, 0);
   132	
   133	  const avgConvergence =
   134	    lineageData.length > 0
   135	      ? lineageData.reduce((sum, { registry }) => sum + (registry?.metrics?.convergenceScore ?? 0), 0) /
   136	        lineageData.length
   137	      : 0;
   138	
   139	  return {
   140	    mergedFrom: lineageData.map(({ label }) => label),
   141	    openQuestions: [...openQuestionsById.values()],
   142	    resolvedQuestions: [],
   143	    keyFindings: mergedFindings,
   144	    ruledOutDirections: [...ruledOutById.values()],
   145	    metrics: {
   146	      iterationsCompleted: totalIters,
   147	      openQuestions: openQuestionsById.size,
   148	      resolvedQuestions: 0,
   149	      keyFindings: mergedFindings.length,
   150	      convergenceScore: Math.round(avgConvergence * 1000) / 1000,
   151	      coverageBySources: {},
   152	    },
   153	  };
   154	}
   155	
   156	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   157	// 3. REVIEW MERGE  (strongest-restriction)
   158	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   159	
   160	const SEVERITY_RANK = { P0: 3, P1: 2, P2: 1 };
   161	
   162	/**
   163	 * Merge review findings registries with strongest-restriction severity rollup.
   164	 * Any lineage with an active P0 finding causes the merged result to be FAIL.
   165	 * Deduplication is by findingId; cross-lineage P0 wins if any lineage reports it.
   166	 */
   167	function mergeReviewRegistries(lineageData) {
   168	  const findingById = new Map();
   169	
   170	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
   171	    if (!registry || !Array.isArray(registry.openFindings)) continue;
   172	    for (const finding of registry.openFindings) {
   173	      if (finding.status !== 'active') continue;
   174	      const id = finding.findingId || finding.title;
   175	      if (!id) continue;
   176	      if (findingById.has(id)) {
   177	        const existing = findingById.get(id);
   178	        // Strongest-restriction: escalate to highest severity seen across lineages
   179	        if ((SEVERITY_RANK[finding.severity] ?? 0) > (SEVERITY_RANK[existing.severity] ?? 0)) {
   180	          findingById.set(id, { ...finding, _lineages: [...(existing._lineages || []), label] });
   181	        } else {
   182	          if (!existing._lineages.includes(label)) existing._lineages.push(label);
   183	        }
   184	      } else {
   185	        findingById.set(id, { ...finding, _lineages: [label] });
   186	      }
   187	    }
   188	  }
   189	
   190	  const mergedFindings = [...findingById.values()];
   191	  const activeP0 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P0' && f.status === 'active').length;
   192	  const activeP1 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P1' && f.status === 'active').length;
   193	  const activeP2 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P2' && f.status === 'active').length;
   194	
   195	  // Strongest-restriction verdict
   196	  let mergedVerdict;
   197	  if (activeP0 > 0) {
   198	    mergedVerdict = 'FAIL';
   199	  } else if (activeP1 > 0) {
   200	    mergedVerdict = 'CONDITIONAL';
   201	  } else {
   202	    mergedVerdict = 'PASS';
   203	  }
   204	
   205	  return {
   206	    mergedFrom: lineageData.map(({ label }) => label),
   207	    mergedVerdict,
   208	    openFindings: mergedFindings,
   209	    resolvedFindings: [],
   210	    findingsBySeverity: { P0: activeP0, P1: activeP1, P2: activeP2 },
   211	    openFindingsCount: mergedFindings.length,
   212	    resolvedFindingsCount: 0,
   213	    activeP0,
   214	    activeP1,
   215	    activeP2,
   216	  };
   217	}
   218	
   219	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   220	// 4. ATTRIBUTION
   221	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   222	
   223	/**
   224	 * Build fanout-attribution.md summarizing per-lineage convergence, iters, salvage, model.
   225	 */
   226	function buildAttributionMd(lineageData, loopType) {
   227	  const lines = [
   228	    `# Fan-Out Attribution`,
   229	    ``,
   230	    `Loop type: **${loopType}**  |  Lineage count: **${lineageData.length}**`,
   231	    ``,
   232	    `| Label | Kind | Model | Iterations | Convergence | Salvaged | Verdict |`,
   233	    `|-------|------|-------|-----------|-------------|----------|---------|`,
   234	  ];
   235	
   236	  for (const { label, registry, stateRecords, kind, model } of lineageData) {
   237	    const iters = stateRecords.filter((r) => r.type === 'iteration').length;
   238	    const salvage = stateRecords.filter((r) => r.type === 'event' && r.event === 'salvaged_from_stdout').length;
   239	    const convergenceScore = registry?.metrics?.convergenceScore ?? registry?.convergenceScore ?? 'n/a';
   240	    const verdict =
   241	      loopType === 'review'
   242	        ? registry?.findingsBySeverity?.P0 > 0
   243	          ? 'FAIL'
   244	          : registry?.findingsBySeverity?.P1 > 0
   245	          ? 'CONDITIONAL'
   246	          : 'PASS'
   247	        : 'n/a';
   248	    lines.push(`| ${label} | ${kind ?? 'unknown'} | ${model ?? 'default'} | ${iters} | ${convergenceScore} | ${salvage} | ${verdict} |`);
   249	  }
   250	
   251	  lines.push('');
   252	  return lines.join('\n');
   253	}
   254	
   255	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   256	// 5. MAIN
   257	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   258	
   259	function main() {
   260	  const args = parseArgs();
   261	  const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');
   262	  if (loopType !== 'research' && loopType !== 'review') {
   263	    throw inputError('loopType must be "research" or "review"');
   264	  }
   265	  const artifactDir = ensureString(args, 'artifactDir');
   266	  const lineagesDir = path.join(artifactDir, 'lineages');
   267	
   268	  if (!fs.existsSync(lineagesDir)) {
   269	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no lineages directory — nothing to merge', merged: 0 });
   270	    return;
   271	  }
   272	
   273	  const labelDirs = fs.readdirSync(lineagesDir).filter((entry) =>
   274	    fs.statSync(path.join(lineagesDir, entry)).isDirectory(),
   275	  );
   276	
   277	  if (labelDirs.length === 0) {
   278	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no lineage subdirs found', merged: 0 });
   279	    return;
   280	  }
   281	
   282	  // Load per-lineage data
   283	  const registryName =
   284	    loopType === 'review' ? 'deep-review-findings-registry.json' : 'deep-research-findings-registry.json';
   285	  const stateLogName = loopType === 'review' ? 'deep-review-state.jsonl' : 'deep-research-state.jsonl';
   286	  const summaryPath = path.join(artifactDir, 'orchestration-summary.json');
   287	  const orchestrationSummary = tryReadJson(summaryPath) ?? {};
   288	
   289	  const lineageData = labelDirs.map((label) => {
   290	    const lineageDir = path.join(lineagesDir, label);
   291	    const registry = tryReadJson(path.join(lineageDir, registryName));
   292	    const stateRecords = readStateLog(path.join(lineageDir, stateLogName));
   293	    // Infer kind/model from state log executor records
   294	    const executorRecord = stateRecords.find((r) => r.type === 'event' && r.event === 'executor_start');
   295	    return {
   296	      label,
   297	      lineageDir,
   298	      registry,
   299	      stateRecords,
   300	      kind: executorRecord?.kind ?? orchestrationSummary?.[label]?.kind ?? 'unknown',
   301	      model: executorRecord?.model ?? orchestrationSummary?.[label]?.model ?? 'unknown',
   302	    };
   303	  });
   304	
   305	  const lineagesWithRegistry = lineageData.filter((d) => d.registry !== null);
   306	
   307	  let mergedRegistry;
   308	  if (loopType === 'review') {
   309	    mergedRegistry = mergeReviewRegistries(lineagesWithRegistry);
   310	  } else {
   311	    mergedRegistry = mergeResearchRegistries(lineagesWithRegistry);
   312	  }
   313	
   314	  // Write merged registry to base artifact dir (replacing single-executor path)
   315	  const mergedRegistryPath = path.join(artifactDir, registryName);
   316	  fs.writeFileSync(mergedRegistryPath, JSON.stringify(mergedRegistry, null, 2), 'utf8');
   317	
   318	  // Write attribution markdown
   319	  const attributionPath = path.join(artifactDir, 'fanout-attribution.md');
   320	  fs.writeFileSync(attributionPath, buildAttributionMd(lineageData, loopType), 'utf8');
   321	
   322	  jsonOut({
   323	    status: 'ok',
   324	    loop_type: loopType,
   325	    merged_lineages: lineagesWithRegistry.length,
   326	    skipped_no_registry: lineageData.length - lineagesWithRegistry.length,
   327	    merged_registry_path: mergedRegistryPath,
   328	    attribution_path: attributionPath,
   329	    ...(loopType === 'review'
   330	      ? { merged_verdict: mergedRegistry.mergedVerdict, active_p0: mergedRegistry.activeP0, active_p1: mergedRegistry.activeP1 }
   331	      : { key_findings: mergedRegistry.keyFindings?.length ?? 0 }),
   332	  });
   333	}
   334	
   335	// Exports for unit testing
   336	module.exports = { mergeResearchRegistries, mergeReviewRegistries, buildAttributionMd };
   337	
   338	if (require.main === module) {
   339	  try {
   340	    main();
   341	  } catch (err) {
   342	    const code = err && err.code === 'INPUT_VALIDATION' ? 3 : 1;
   343	    jsonOut({
   344	      status: 'error',
   345	      error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
   346	      code: err && err.code ? err.code : 'SCRIPT_ERROR',
   347	    });
   348	    if (code === 1) {
   349	      process.stderr.write(
   350	        JSON.stringify({ error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err), stack: err && err.stack }) + '\n',
   351	      );
   352	    }
   353	    process.exit(code);
   354	  }
   355	}

codex
One concrete shape is already visible: `fanout-run.cjs` spawns real CLI lineages directly, while a separate audit helper has process-group timeout handling. I’m checking whether the runner actually uses that helper or duplicates a weaker path.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/lib/cli-guards.cjs | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Deep-Loop Script CLI Guards                                              ║
     3	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
     4	'use strict';
     5	
     6	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     7	// 1. IMPORTS
     8	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     9	
    10	const fs = require('node:fs');
    11	const path = require('node:path');
    12	
    13	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    14	// 2. CONSTANTS
    15	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    16	
    17	let signalHandlersInstalled = false;
    18	const DEFAULT_WRITER_LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS = 1_000;
    19	const DEFAULT_WRITER_LOCK_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS = 25;
    20	
    21	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    22	// 3. HELPERS
    23	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    24	
    25	function findRepoRoot(startDir) {
    26	  let current = path.resolve(startDir);
    27	  let opencodeMatch = null;
    28	  while (current !== path.dirname(current)) {
    29	    // `.git` is the unambiguous repo-root marker; prefer it so nested `.opencode`
    30	    // sandbox/residue dirs under skills/** cannot short-circuit detection.
    31	    if (fs.existsSync(path.join(current, '.git'))) {
    32	      return current;
    33	    }
    34	    if (opencodeMatch === null && fs.existsSync(path.join(current, '.opencode'))) {
    35	      opencodeMatch = current;
    36	    }
    37	    current = path.dirname(current);
    38	  }
    39	  return opencodeMatch || path.resolve(startDir, '..', '..', '..', '..', '..');
    40	}
    41	
    42	const REPO_ROOT = findRepoRoot(__dirname);
    43	
    44	function hasTraversalSegment(value) {
    45	  return value.split(/[\\/]+/).some((segment) => segment === '..');
    46	}
    47	
    48	function isInsideRepo(resolvedPath) {
    49	  const relative = path.relative(REPO_ROOT, resolvedPath);
    50	  return relative === '' || (!relative.startsWith('..') && !path.isAbsolute(relative));
    51	}
    52	
    53	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    54	// 4. CORE LOGIC
    55	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    56	
    57	/**
    58	 * Validate a namespace-scoped value (specFolder, sessionId) for safety.
    59	 * Rejects null bytes, `..` path traversal segments, and absolute paths
    60	 * that escape the repository root.
    61	 *
    62	 * @param {string} value - The value to validate.
    63	 * @param {string} key - Label for error messages (e.g. 'specFolder').
    64	 * @param {Function} makeInputError - Factory that creates an InputValidation
    65	 *   error from a message string.
    66	 * @returns {string} The validated value, unchanged when valid.
    67	 * @throws {Error} With code INPUT_VALIDATION on failure.
    68	 */
    69	function validateNamespaceValue(value, key, makeInputError) {
    70	  if (value.includes('\0')) {
    71	    throw makeInputError(`${key} must not contain null bytes`);
    72	  }
    73	  if (hasTraversalSegment(value)) {
    74	    throw makeInputError(`${key} must not contain '..' path segments`);
    75	  }
    76	  if (path.isAbsolute(value) && !isInsideRepo(path.resolve(value))) {
    77	    throw makeInputError(`${key} absolute path must stay inside the repository root`);
    78	  }
    79	  return value;
    80	}
    81	
    82	/**
    83	 * Install one-shot signal handlers (SIGINT, SIGTERM) that run a cleanup
    84	 * callback before exiting. Idempotent — subsequent calls are no-ops.
    85	 *
    86	 * @param {Function} cleanup - Cleanup callback invoked before process exit.
    87	 */
    88	function installSignalHandlers(cleanup) {
    89	  if (signalHandlersInstalled) return;
    90	  signalHandlersInstalled = true;
    91	
    92	  for (const signal of ['SIGINT', 'SIGTERM']) {
    93	    process.once(signal, () => {
    94	      try {
    95	        cleanup();
    96	      } finally {
    97	        process.exit(signal === 'SIGINT' ? 130 : 143);
    98	      }
    99	    });
   100	  }
   101	}
   102	
   103	/**
   104	 * Map an error to a structured exit code for CLI scripts.
   105	 *
   106	 * @param {Error|Object} err - An error object, optionally carrying a `code`
   107	 *   property.
   108	 * @returns {number} Exit code: 1=script error, 2=DB/permissions error,
   109	 *   3=input validation error.
   110	 */
   111	function classifyExitCode(err) {
   112	  const code = err && typeof err === 'object' && 'code' in err ? err.code : undefined;
   113	  if (code === 'INPUT_VALIDATION') return 3;
   114	  if (
   115	    code === 'DB_ERROR'
   116	    || code === 'SQLITE_ERROR'
   117	    || code === 'SQLITE_BUSY'
   118	    || code === 'SQLITE_CANTOPEN'
   119	    || code === 'EACCES'
   120	    || code === 'EPERM'
   121	    || code === 'ENOTDIR'
   122	  ) {
   123	    return 2;
   124	  }
   125	  return 1;
   126	}
   127	
   128	/**
   129	 * Throw a synthetic fault when the DEEP_LOOP_TEST_FAULT environment
   130	 * variable is set. Used by contract tests to verify that scripts
   131	 * handle injected DB and script-level errors correctly.
   132	 *
   133	 * @throws {Error} With code DB_ERROR or generic script error.
   134	 */
   135	function maybeThrowTestFault() {
   136	  if (process.env.DEEP_LOOP_TEST_FAULT === 'db') {
   137	    const err = new Error('Injected DB fault for deep-loop script contract tests');
   138	    err.code = 'DB_ERROR';
   139	    throw err;
   140	  }
   141	  if (process.env.DEEP_LOOP_TEST_FAULT === 'script') {
   142	    throw new Error('Injected script fault for deep-loop script contract tests');
   143	  }
   144	}
   145	
   146	/**
   147	 * Synchronous sleep using Atomics.wait on a shared buffer.
   148	 *
   149	 * @param {number} ms - Milliseconds to sleep. Non-positive values are
   150	 *   ignored.
   151	 */
   152	function sleepSync(ms) {
   153	  if (!Number.isFinite(ms) || ms <= 0) return;
   154	  const buffer = new SharedArrayBuffer(4);
   155	  const view = new Int32Array(buffer);
   156	  Atomics.wait(view, 0, 0, ms);
   157	}
   158	
   159	function readNonNegativeEnvNumber(name, fallback) {
   160	  const raw = process.env[name];
   161	  if (raw === undefined || raw === '') return fallback;
   162	  const value = Number(raw);
   163	  return Number.isFinite(value) && value >= 0 ? value : fallback;
   164	}
   165	
   166	/**
   167	 * Acquire an exclusive writer lock via `wx` file open on the given path.
   168	 * Returns a release function that closes the file descriptor and removes
   169	 * the lock file. Retries briefly on contention so parallel test files and
   170	 * near-simultaneous runtime callers do not fail on transient writer overlap.
   171	 *
   172	 * @param {string} lockPath - Path to the lock file to create.
   173	 * @returns {Function} Release function (call to unlock and clean up).
   174	 * @throws {Error} With code DB_ERROR if the lock remains held after retry.
   175	 */
   176	function acquireWriterLock(lockPath) {
   177	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(lockPath), { recursive: true });
   178	  const maxWaitMs = readNonNegativeEnvNumber('DEEP_LOOP_WRITER_LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS', DEFAULT_WRITER_LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS);
   179	  const retryIntervalMs = readNonNegativeEnvNumber('DEEP_LOOP_WRITER_LOCK_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS', DEFAULT_WRITER_LOCK_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS);
   180	  const deadline = Date.now() + maxWaitMs;
   181	  let fd;
   182	
   183	  while (true) {
   184	    try {
   185	      fd = fs.openSync(lockPath, 'wx');
   186	      break;
   187	    } catch (err) {
   188	      const code = err && typeof err === 'object' && 'code' in err ? err.code : undefined;
   189	      if (code !== 'EEXIST') throw err;
   190	
   191	      const remainingMs = deadline - Date.now();
   192	      if (maxWaitMs <= 0 || remainingMs <= 0) {
   193	        const lockErr = new Error('deep-loop graph writer lock is held by another process');
   194	        lockErr.code = 'DB_ERROR';
   195	        throw lockErr;
   196	      }
   197	      sleepSync(Math.min(retryIntervalMs, remainingMs));
   198	    }
   199	  }
   200	
   201	  return () => {
   202	    if (typeof fd === 'number') {
   203	      try {
   204	        fs.closeSync(fd);
   205	      } catch {
   206	        // Best-effort cleanup during process teardown.
   207	      }
   208	      fd = undefined;
   209	    }
   210	    fs.rmSync(lockPath, { force: true });
   211	  };
   212	}
   213	
   214	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   215	// 5. EXPORTS
   216	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   217	
   218	module.exports = {
   219	  acquireWriterLock,
   220	  classifyExitCode,
   221	  installSignalHandlers,
   222	  maybeThrowTestFault,
   223	  sleepSync,
   224	  validateNamespaceValue,
   225	};

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	import { existsSync, mkdtempSync, readFileSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
     2	import { tmpdir } from 'node:os';

     5	import { afterEach, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
     6	
     7	import { runtimeRoot, spawnCjs } from '../helpers/spawn-cjs';
     8	
     9	const tempDirs: string[] = [];
    10	
    11	function makeTempDir(prefix: string): string {
    12	  const dir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), prefix));
    13	  tempDirs.push(dir);
    14	  return dir;
    15	}
    16	
    17	/**
    18	 * Write a stub binary at `binDir/{name}` that exits 0.
    19	 * Used to simulate headless CLI executors without real API keys.
    20	 */
    21	function writeStubBinary(binDir: string, name: string): string {
    22	  const stubPath = join(binDir, name);
    23	  writeFileSync(stubPath, '#!/bin/sh\necho "stub-done"\nexit 0\n', { mode: 0o755 });
    24	  return stubPath;
    25	}
    26	
    27	afterEach(() => {

    31	  }
    32	});
    33	
    34	const fanoutRunScript = resolve(runtimeRoot, 'scripts', 'fanout-run.cjs');
    35	
    36	describe('fanout-run.cjs — module basics', () => {
    37	  it('exits 0 and emits ok JSON when there are no CLI lineages (native-only config)', async () => {
    38	    const baseDir = makeTempDir('fanout-run-native-');
    39	    const nativeOnlyConfig = JSON.stringify({
    40	      executors: [{ label: 'native-a', kind: 'native', count: 1 }],
    41	      concurrency: 2,
    42	    });
    43	
    44	    const result = await spawnCjs(fanoutRunScript, [
    45	      '--spec-folder',
    46	      'specs/test-fanout-run-native',
    47	      '--loop-type',
    48	      'research',
    49	      '--fanout-config-json',
    50	      nativeOnlyConfig,
    51	      '--base-artifact-dir',
    52	      baseDir,
    53	    ]);
    54	
    55	    expect(result.exitCode).toBe(0);
    56	    const json = JSON.parse(result.stdout.split('\n').filter(Boolean).at(-1) ?? '{}') as Record<string, unknown>;
    57	    expect(json.status).toBe('ok');
    58	    expect(json.results).toEqual([]);

     1	import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';

     3	import { mkdtempSync, readFileSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';

     7	import { runAuditedExecutorCommandAsync } from '../../lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.js';
     8	import type { ExecutorConfig } from '../../lib/deep-loop/executor-config.js';
     9	
    10	const posixIt = process.platform === 'win32' ? it.skip : it;
    11	
    12	/**
    13	 * Returns a default cli-codex ExecutorConfig for use in tests.
    14	 */
    15	function cliCodexExecutor(): ExecutorConfig {
    16	  return {
    17	    kind: 'cli-codex',
    18	    model: 'gpt-5.4',
    19	    reasoningEffort: 'high',
    20	    serviceTier: 'priority',
    21	    sandboxMode: null,
    22	    timeoutSeconds: 900,
    23	  };
    24	}
    25	
    26	/**
    27	 * Checks whether a process is alive using a zero-signal kill probe.
    28	 */
    29	function processIsAlive(pid: number): boolean {
    30	  try {
    31	    process.kill(pid, 0);
    32	    return true;
    33	  } catch {
    34	    return false;
    35	  }
    36	}
    37	
    38	/**
    39	 * Polls until a process is no longer alive, with a limited number of attempts.
    40	 */
    41	async function waitUntilDead(pid: number): Promise<void> {
    42	  for (let attempt = 0; attempt < 20; attempt += 1) {
    43	    if (!processIsAlive(pid)) {
    44	      return;
    45	    }
    46	    await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 50));
    47	  }
    48	}
    49	
    50	describe('executor-audit process-group supervisor', () => {
    51	  posixIt('kills a SIGTERM-ignoring child and grandchild process group after timeout escalation', async () => {
    52	    const tempDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'executor-process-group-'));
    53	    const stateLogPath = join(tempDir, 'state.jsonl');
    54	    const childScriptPath = join(tempDir, 'ignore-sigterm-child.cjs');
    55	    const pidsPath = join(tempDir, 'pids.json');
    56	
    57	    try {
    58	      writeFileSync(stateLogPath, '{"type":"event","event":"start"}\n', 'utf8');
    59	      writeFileSync(
    60	        childScriptPath,
    61	        [
    62	          "const { spawn } = require('node:child_process');",
    63	          "const { writeFileSync } = require('node:fs');",
    64	          "const grandchild = spawn(process.execPath, ['-e', \"process.on('SIGTERM', () => {}); setInterval(() => {}, 1000);\"], { stdio: 'ignore' });",
    65	          "writeFileSync(process.argv[2], JSON.stringify({ child: process.pid, grandchild: grandchild.pid }));",
    66	          "process.on('SIGTERM', () => {});",
    67	          'setInterval(() => {}, 1000);',
    68	          '',
    69	        ].join('\n'),
    70	        'utf8',
    71	      );
    72	
    73	      const exitCode = await runAuditedExecutorCommandAsync({
    74	        command: process.execPath,
    75	        args: [childScriptPath, pidsPath],
    76	        cwd: tempDir,
    77	        timeoutSeconds: 0.15,
    78	        timeoutGraceMs: 75,
    79	        stateLogPath,
    80	        executor: cliCodexExecutor(),
    81	        iteration: 4,
    82	        guardContext: {
    83	          env: {},
    84	          ancestryCmdlines: [],
    85	          statePaths: [],
    86	        },
    87	      });
    88	
    89	      const pids = JSON.parse(readFileSync(pidsPath, 'utf8')) as { child: number; grandchild: number };
    90	      await waitUntilDead(pids.child);
    91	      await waitUntilDead(pids.grandchild);
    92	
    93	      const records = readFileSync(stateLogPath, 'utf8').trimEnd().split('\n').map((line) => JSON.parse(line));
    94	      expect(exitCode).toBe(0);
    95	      expect(processIsAlive(pids.child)).toBe(false);
    96	      expect(processIsAlive(pids.grandchild)).toBe(false);
    97	      expect(records.at(-1)).toMatchObject({
    98	        type: 'event',
    99	        event: 'dispatch_failure',
   100	        iteration: 4,
   101	        reason: 'timeout',
   102	        executor: { kind: 'cli-codex' },
   103	      });
   104	    } finally {
   105	      rmSync(tempDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
   106	    }
   107	  });
   108	});

     1	// MODULE: Deep-Loop Lock
     2	
     3	import { closeSync, existsSync, fsyncSync, openSync, readFileSync, renameSync, rmSync, unlinkSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
     4	import { dirname } from 'node:path';
     5	
     6	// ───── TYPE DEFINITIONS ─────
     7	
     8	import type { ExecutorKind } from './executor-config.js';
     9	
    10	export interface LoopLockData {
    11	  ownerPid: number;
    12	  startedAtIso: string;
    13	  ttlMs: number;
    14	  lastHeartbeatIso: string;
    15	  packetId: string;
    16	  runtimeKind: ExecutorKind | 'main';
    17	}
    18	
    19	export type LoopLockAcquireResult =
    20	  | { acquired: true; lock: LoopLockData; reclaimed?: LoopLockData }
    21	  | { acquired: false; holder: LoopLockData };
    22	
    23	// ───── DOMAIN ERRORS ─────
    24	
    25	export class LoopLockHeldError extends Error {
    26	  holder: LoopLockData;
    27	
    28	  constructor(holder: LoopLockData) {
    29	    super(`Deep-loop lock is held by pid ${holder.ownerPid} for packet ${holder.packetId}`);
    30	    this.name = 'LoopLockHeldError';
    31	    this.holder = holder;
    32	  }
    33	}
    34	
    35	// ───── HELPERS ─────
    36	
    37	type SerializedLoopLockData = {
    38	  owner_pid: number;
    39	  started_at_iso: string;
    40	  ttl_ms: number;
    41	  last_heartbeat_iso: string;
    42	  packet_id: string;
    43	  runtime_kind: ExecutorKind | 'main';
    44	};
    45	
    46	function fsyncPath(path: string): void {
    47	  let fd: number | undefined;
    48	  try {
    49	    fd = openSync(path, 'r');
    50	    fsyncSync(fd);
    51	  } finally {
    52	    if (typeof fd === 'number') {
    53	      closeSync(fd);
    54	    }
    55	  }
    56	}
    57	
    58	function makeTempPath(targetPath: string): string {
    59	  return `${targetPath}.tmp.${process.pid}.${Date.now()}.${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;
    60	}
    61	
    62	function serializeLock(data: LoopLockData): SerializedLoopLockData {
    63	  return {
    64	    owner_pid: data.ownerPid,
    65	    started_at_iso: data.startedAtIso,
    66	    ttl_ms: data.ttlMs,
    67	    last_heartbeat_iso: data.lastHeartbeatIso,
    68	    packet_id: data.packetId,
    69	    runtime_kind: data.runtimeKind,
    70	  };
    71	}
    72	
    73	function deserializeLock(raw: unknown): LoopLockData | null {
    74	  if (raw === null || typeof raw !== 'object' || Array.isArray(raw)) {
    75	    return null;
    76	  }
    77	
    78	  const candidate = raw as Partial<SerializedLoopLockData>;
    79	  if (
    80	    !Number.isInteger(candidate.owner_pid) ||
    81	    typeof candidate.started_at_iso !== 'string' ||
    82	    !Number.isInteger(candidate.ttl_ms) ||
    83	    typeof candidate.last_heartbeat_iso !== 'string' ||
    84	    typeof candidate.packet_id !== 'string' ||
    85	    typeof candidate.runtime_kind !== 'string'
    86	  ) {
    87	    return null;
    88	  }
    89	
    90	  const ownerPid = candidate.owner_pid as number;
    91	  const ttlMs = candidate.ttl_ms as number;
    92	
    93	  return {
    94	    ownerPid,
    95	    startedAtIso: candidate.started_at_iso,
    96	    ttlMs,
    97	    lastHeartbeatIso: candidate.last_heartbeat_iso,
    98	    packetId: candidate.packet_id,
    99	    runtimeKind: candidate.runtime_kind as ExecutorKind | 'main',
   100	  };
   101	}
   102	
   103	function readLoopLock(lockPath: string): LoopLockData | null {
   104	  try {
   105	    return deserializeLock(JSON.parse(readFileSync(lockPath, 'utf8')));
   106	  } catch {
   107	    return null;
   108	  }
   109	}
   110	
   111	function writeLoopLockAtomic(lockPath: string, data: LoopLockData): void {
   112	  const tempPath = makeTempPath(lockPath);
   113	  try {
   114	    writeFileSync(tempPath, `${JSON.stringify(serializeLock(data), null, 2)}\n`, { encoding: 'utf8', flag: 'wx' });
   115	    fsyncPath(tempPath);
   116	    renameSync(tempPath, lockPath);
   117	    try {
   118	      fsyncPath(dirname(lockPath));
   119	    } catch {
   120	    }
   121	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   122	    rmSync(tempPath, { force: true });
   123	    throw error;
   124	  }
   125	}
   126	
   127	function writeLoopLockExclusive(lockPath: string, data: LoopLockData): boolean {
   128	  let fd: number | undefined;
   129	  try {
   130	    fd = openSync(lockPath, 'wx');
   131	    writeFileSync(fd, `${JSON.stringify(serializeLock(data), null, 2)}\n`, { encoding: 'utf8' });
   132	    fsyncSync(fd);
   133	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   134	    const code = error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error ? (error as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code : undefined;
   135	    if (code === 'EEXIST') {
   136	      return false;
   137	    }
   138	    throw error;
   139	  } finally {
   140	    if (typeof fd === 'number') {
   141	      closeSync(fd);
   142	    }
   143	  }
   144	
   145	  try {
   146	    fsyncPath(dirname(lockPath));
   147	  } catch {
   148	  }
   149	  return true;
   150	}
   151	
   152	// ───── EXPORTS ─────
   153	
   154	/**
   155	 * Check whether a process is still alive.
   156	 *
   157	 * Uses kill(pid, 0) as a POSIX signal check. Returns true for
   158	 * processes the current user has permission to signal.
   159	 *
   160	 * @param pid - Process ID to check.
   161	 * @returns True if the process exists and is reachable.
   162	 */
   163	export function processAlive(pid: number): boolean {
   164	  if (!Number.isInteger(pid) || pid <= 0) {
   165	    return false;
   166	  }
   167	
   168	  try {
   169	    process.kill(pid, 0);
   170	    return true;
   171	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   172	    if (error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error) {
   173	      if (error.code === 'ESRCH') return false;
   174	      if (error.code === 'EPERM') return true;
   175	    }
   176	    return true;
   177	  }
   178	}
   179	
   180	/**
   181	 * Determine whether a loop lock has expired or the owner process is dead.
   182	 *
   183	 * A lock is stale if the owner PID no longer exists or the heartbeat
   184	 * has exceeded twice the TTL.
   185	 *
   186	 * @param data - The loop lock data to check.
   187	 * @param now - Reference timestamp (defaults to now).
   188	 * @returns True if the lock is stale and can be reclaimed.
   189	 */
   190	export function isStaleLoopLock(data: LoopLockData, now: Date = new Date()): boolean {
   191	  const heartbeatMs = Date.parse(data.lastHeartbeatIso);
   192	  const ttlMs = Number.isFinite(data.ttlMs) ? data.ttlMs : 0;
   193	  const expired = !Number.isFinite(heartbeatMs) || now.getTime() - heartbeatMs > ttlMs * 2;
   194	
   195	  return expired || !processAlive(data.ownerPid);
   196	}
   197	
   198	/**
   199	 * Acquire the deep-loop lock, reclaiming stale locks automatically.
   200	 *
   201	 * @param lockPath - File path for the lock file.
   202	 * @param data - Lock metadata for the acquiring process.
   203	 * @returns Result indicating whether the lock was acquired and by whom.
   204	 */
   205	export function acquireLoopLock(lockPath: string, data: LoopLockData): LoopLockAcquireResult {
   206	  const holder = existsSync(lockPath) ? readLoopLock(lockPath) : null;
   207	  if (holder && !isStaleLoopLock(holder)) {
   208	    return { acquired: false, holder };
   209	  }
   210	
   211	  if (!holder) {
   212	    if (writeLoopLockExclusive(lockPath, data)) {
   213	      return { acquired: true, lock: data };
   214	    }
   215	    const current = readLoopLock(lockPath);
   216	    return { acquired: false, holder: current ?? data };
   217	  }
   218	
   219	  writeLoopLockAtomic(lockPath, data);
   220	  return holder ? { acquired: true, lock: data, reclaimed: holder } : { acquired: true, lock: data };
   221	}
   222	
   223	/**
   224	 * Refresh the loop lock heartbeat for the owning process.
   225	 *
   226	 * @param lockPath - File path for the lock file.
   227	 * @param ownerPid - PID of the owning process (must match current owner).
   228	 * @param now - Reference timestamp (defaults to now).
   229	 * @returns True if the heartbeat was successfully refreshed.
   230	 */
   231	export function refreshLoopLock(lockPath: string, ownerPid: number, now: Date = new Date()): boolean {
   232	  const holder = existsSync(lockPath) ? readLoopLock(lockPath) : null;
   233	  if (!holder || holder.ownerPid !== ownerPid) {
   234	    return false;
   235	  }
   236	
   237	  writeLoopLockAtomic(lockPath, {
   238	    ...holder,
   239	    lastHeartbeatIso: now.toISOString(),
   240	  });
   241	  return true;
   242	}
   243	
   244	/**
   245	 * Release the loop lock for the owning process.
   246	 *
   247	 * @param lockPath - File path for the lock file.
   248	 * @param ownerPid - PID of the owning process (must match current owner).
   249	 * @returns True if the lock was successfully released.
   250	 */
   251	export function releaseLoopLock(lockPath: string, ownerPid: number): boolean {
   252	  const holder = existsSync(lockPath) ? readLoopLock(lockPath) : null;
   253	  if (!holder || holder.ownerPid !== ownerPid) {
   254	    return false;
   255	  }
   256	
   257	  unlinkSync(lockPath);
   258	  return true;
   259	}

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:119:- **(Option B-spawn, recommended):** add a thin **headless single-lineage loop driver** (`scripts/fanout-run.cjs` or a mode of `fanout-pool.cjs`) that, per lineage, runs the loop control-flow in JS by CALLING the existing runtime pieces as subprocesses/functions: `renderPromptPack` → `runAuditedExecutorCommand` (CLI) — note the **native/`@deep-research` path can't be driven headlessly without the agent**, so fan-out may need to require CLI executors per lineage (or treat `native` as "this agent runs it inline, others spawn"). It calls `convergence.cjs` each iteration so convergence is NOT re-implemented. This is "Option B" honestly realized; it re-implements only the loop's *control flow*, not its math.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:36:- **`scripts/fanout-run.cjs`** — CLI lineage pool driver. TSX-bootstrapped; uses `parseFanoutConfig` / `expandLineages` from `executor-config.ts` and `runCappedPool` from `fanout-pool.cjs`. Filters to CLI lineages only (`kind !== 'native'`); native lineages are dispatched by the YAML. For each CLI lineage: creates `{base}/lineages/{label}/` + `{label}/.executor-state/` dirs; builds a "run the full loop" prompt; constructs the per-kind CLI command (`codex exec`, `claude -p`, `opencode run`, `gemini`, `devin --print`); sets `SPECKIT_FANOUT_LINEAGE_ID={label}` + `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` per-lineage to prevent same-kind replica lockfile collisions; runs via `spawnSync` with a generous timeout derived from `lineage.iterations * lineage.timeoutSeconds * 2` (capped at 4 hours); saves stdout to `{lineageDir}/logs/fanout-lineage.out`; calls `runSalvageSweep` after each subprocess exits. Exit codes: 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:42:- **`step_fanout_spawn` added to all 4 YAMLs** — New top-level workflow step with `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"`. When config.fanout is present: spawns CLI lineages via `fanout-run.cjs` (`step_fanout_spawn_cli`); dispatches native lineages as sequential `agent: deep-research`/`deep-review` runs (`step_fanout_spawn_native`); after all lineages converge, jumps to `phase_synthesis` skipping `phase_init` and `phase_main_loop`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:44:- **`tests/unit/fanout-run.vitest.ts`** — 5 integration tests: native-only config returns early with ok (no pool spawn); bad JSON config exits 3; missing required args exits 3; 2 cli-codex lineages create distinct `lineages/{label}/` dirs + `orchestration-summary.json` + `orchestration-status.log`; same-kind replicas get distinct `.executor-state` dirs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:50:- **`fanout-run.cjs` worker updated** — After `spawnSync` returns: saves `result.stdout` to `{lineageDir}/logs/fanout-lineage.out`; calls `runSalvageSweep(lineageDir, loopType, savedStdout)`; includes salvage stats in the returned worker result.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:56:- **`scripts/fanout-merge.cjs`** — Cross-lineage merge script. Reads every `{artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/` sub-packet registry + state log; produces a consolidated registry at the base artifact dir. Research merge: deduplicates `keyFindings` by `id` field, keeping the first-seen entry and appending a `_lineages` attribution array; aggregates total iteration count and averages convergence score; merges `openQuestions` and `ruledOutDirections` similarly. Review merge: applies **strongest-restriction** — iterates active findings across all lineages, escalates to highest severity for duplicate `findingId`, counts active P0/P1/P2, derives `mergedVerdict` (FAIL if activeP0 > 0, CONDITIONAL if activeP1 > 0, PASS otherwise). Both write `{artifact_dir}/fanout-attribution.md` with a per-lineage table (label, kind, model, iterations, convergence score, salvaged count, verdict). Main exits 0=ok, 3=input validation. Exports `mergeResearchRegistries`, `mergeReviewRegistries`, `buildAttributionMd` for unit testing.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:58:- **`step_fanout_merge` added to top of `phase_synthesis` in all 4 YAMLs** — `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"`. Calls `fanout-merge.cjs` with the base `artifact_dir`. Review variant adds `bind_from_output: {p0_count: "active_p0", p1_count: "active_p1", p2_count: "active_p2"}` so `step_derive_verdict` uses merged counts rather than an empty single-executor state log. Research variant feeds the merged `deep-research-findings-registry.json` into the existing `step_compile_research` unchanged.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:60:- **`tests/unit/fanout-merge.vitest.ts`** — 10 tests: `mergeResearchRegistries` (3: dedup by id + cross-lineage tracking, total iter aggregation, null registry handled); `mergeReviewRegistries` strongest-restriction (5: clean+P0→FAIL, all clean→PASS, P1-only→CONDITIONAL, duplicate findingId escalates severity, non-active findings ignored); `fanout-merge.cjs` script e2e (2: no lineages dir → ok, 2-lineage review fixture → merged FAIL registry + attribution.md).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:68:- **`deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md`** — Script table extended with 4 new rows (`fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, `fanout-merge.cjs`). Fan-out isolation invariant documented: per-lineage `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override, per-lineage `session_id` for coverage-graph DB writes (no schema change), per-kind `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` for lockfile isolation.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:89:- CLI kinds: `fanout-run.cjs` spawns N headless CLI subprocesses, each running the full loop end-to-end (reading the skill, following the YAML, converging, writing outputs) with `--artifact-dir-override` isolating its sub-packet. "Native codex sub-agents" = N `codex exec` processes; "native claude sub-agents" = N `claude -p` processes.

Salvage operates on this combined output. Per-iteration stdout files (`logs/iter-NNN.out`) as planned in the original handover are not produced by the full-loop subprocess model — the salvage mechanism works at the lineage level instead.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:36:- Schema/flag design: an `iterationPlan` of ordered bands (from-to or count; executor kind and model; optional focus/dimensions; optional dependsOn/order) plus the `iteration-plan` (a.k.a. `model-schedule`) flag desugaring one-to-one into the existing `executors` lineage array, integrated through `step_fanout_spawn`, `step_fanout_spawn_cli`, `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, and `executor-config.ts` (reuse-first; no parallel dispatcher).

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-010.md:102:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.

Consider using a more specific path or pattern.)","metadata":{"count":100,"truncated":true},"title":"","time":{"start":1780205284558,"end":1780205284637}},"id":"prt_e7c80ecc5001h06kd3Z43ILnLb","sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","messageID":"msg_e7c80bc17001cYehspZgESjQPT"}}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-010.md:2409:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:9342:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2806:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/review/hs/logs/iter-009.out:43:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780205290663,"sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_function_4vnhn0n98zpe_1","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: ---\n2: name: deep-loop-runtime\n3: version: 1.4.0\n4: description: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\n5: allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n6: ---\n7: \n8: # Deep Loop Runtime\n9: \n10: Shared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n11: \n12: ---\n13: \n14: ## 1. WHEN TO USE\n15: \n16: This skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n17: \n18: Use it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n19: \n20: - executor configuration and audit metadata\n21: - prompt-pack rendering\n22: - post-dispatch validation\n23: - atomic state files and JSONL repair\n24: - loop locking and permissions gates\n25: - Bayesian scoring and fallback routing\n26: - coverage-graph schema, query, and signal interpretation\n27: \n28: ### Activation Triggers\n29: \n30: This skill is activated when:\n31: \n32: - A deep-review or deep-research workflow YAML invokes `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/<name>.cjs`\n33: - A test file imports `from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/...'`\n34: - An operator queries / mutates the coverage graph via the runtime scripts\n35: - A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing\n36: \n37: ### When NOT to Use\n38: \n39: Do not invoke this skill when:\n40: \n41: - A user requests an interactive command (use `/deep:start-review-loop` or `/deep:start-research-loop` instead — those orchestrate this runtime)\n42: - A package outside deep-review / deep-research needs deep-loop semantics (out of supported scope; open a packet to extend ownership)\n43: - An MCP tool surface is needed (this skill explicitly has none — the isolation ADR removed the deep-loop MCP tools)\n44: \n45: ---\n46: \n47: ## 2. SMART ROUTING\n48: \n49: This skill has no direct LLM-facing keyword triggers — it is invoked by workflow YAMLs (via `bash:` script calls) and by test imports. The router below documents which sub-domain to load for a given consumer query.\n50: \n51: ### Primary Detection Signal\n52: \n53: ```bash\n54: request_text=\"$(printf '%s' \"$USER_REQUEST\" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')\"\n55: case \"$request_text\" in\n56:   *\"deep-loop runtime\"*|*\"deep-loop-runtime\"*|*\"executor config\"*|*\"prompt pack\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n57:   *\"coverage graph\"*|*\"deep_loop_graph\"*|*\"bayesian scorer\"*|*\"loop lock\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n58:   *\"fallback router\"*|*\"post-dispatch validate\"*|*\"jsonl repair\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n59:   *) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=0 ;;\n60: esac\n61: ```\n62: \n63: ### Phase Detection\n64: \n65: ```text\n66: TASK CONTEXT\n67:     |\n68:     +- STEP 0: Detect runtime-call intent vs. consumer-orchestration intent\n69:     +- STEP 1: If consumer (workflow YAML), invoke the relevant scripts/*.cjs\n70:     +- STEP 2: If contributor (modifying lib code), load matching lib/ subtree\n71:     +- STEP 3: If test author, load tests/ + lib/ pair\n72: ```\n73: \n74: ### Resource Domains\n75: \n76: ```text\n77: lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)\n78: lib/coverage-graph/*.ts       # 3 schema + query + signals modules\n79: scripts/*.cjs                 # 4 entry points (convergence, upsert, query, status)\n80: database/*.sqlite              # runtime-owned SQLite (deep-loop-graph.sqlite)\n81: tests/{unit,integration,lifecycle}/  # 21+ vitest files split by responsibility\n82: ```\n83: \n84: ### Resource Loading Levels\n85: \n86: | Tier | When loaded | Purpose |\n87: |------|-------------|---------|\n88: | ALWAYS | SKILL.md frontmatter | description, allowed-tools, version |\n89: | CONDITIONAL | When a runtime call dispatches | `lib/` modules required by the called function |\n90: | ON_DEMAND | When a test fails or a contributor extends | `tests/` + matching `lib/` sub-tree |\n91: \n92: ### Smart Router Pseudocode\n93: \n94: ```text\n95: IF request mentions a specific lib name (e.g. \"bayesian-scorer\", \"prompt-pack\"):\n96:   → Load lib/deep-loop/<name>.ts + matching tests/unit/<name>.vitest.ts\n97: ELIF request mentions coverage-graph / convergence / signals:\n98:   → Load lib/coverage-graph/*.ts + tests/integration/review-depth-*.vitest.ts\n99: ELIF request mentions a script (convergence.cjs, status.cjs, etc.):\n100:   → Load scripts/<name>.cjs + tests/integration/<name>-script.vitest.ts + lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts\n101: ELSE:\n102:   → Defer to consumer skill (deep-review / deep-research)\n103: ```\n104: \n105: ---\n106: \n107: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS\n108: \n109: ### Runtime Architecture\n110: \n111: The runtime exposes deep-loop primitives to consumer workflows through two paths: **direct `.cjs` script invocation** (replacing the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools) and **TypeScript library imports** (from workflow scripts and tests).\n112: \n113: ```text\n114: [deep-review/deep-research workflow YAML]\n115:             │\n116:             ├─ bash: node .../scripts/<name>.cjs   (coverage-graph mutations)\n117:             │       └─→ lib/coverage-graph/*.ts → database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite\n118:             │\n119:             └─ ts-import: lib/deep-loop/<lib>.ts   (runtime primitives)\n120:                     └─→ atomic-state, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, etc.\n121: ```\n122: \n123: ### Library Structure\n124: \n125: **`lib/deep-loop/` (10 modules):**\n126: - `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings\n127: - `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)\n128: - `prompt-pack.ts` — renders the iteration prompt template\n129: - `post-dispatch-validate.ts` — validates iteration outputs (markdown + JSONL + delta)\n130: - `atomic-state.ts` — atomic state-log writes\n131: - `jsonl-repair.ts` — recovers corrupt JSONL state lines\n132: - `loop-lock.ts` — single-writer locking\n133: - `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks\n134: - `bayesian-scorer.ts` — convergence scoring\n135: - `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix\n136: \n137: **`lib/coverage-graph/` (3 modules):**\n138: - `coverage-graph-db.ts` — SQLite schema + node-kind allow-list + connection lifecycle\n139: - `coverage-graph-query.ts` — query builders\n140: - `coverage-graph-signals.ts` — convergence signal extraction\n141: \n142: ### Council Primitives\n143: \n144: The Runtime Boundary Decision (ADR-001) extends `deep-loop-runtime/lib/` with council-compatible infrastructure primitives while keeping operator-facing and domain workflow semantics in `deep-ai-council`. These primitives are consumed by downstream deep-ai-council orchestration (per-topic multi-round, session-findings registry, command and skill wiring, parity and cost docs) for per-topic orchestration, multi-topic session state, command wiring, parity tests and docs.\n145: \n146: **`lib/council/` (5 modules):**\n147: - `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.\n148: - `round-state-jsonl.cjs` — appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard, repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append, fsyncs writes, and exposes round-state readers for resume.\n149: - `adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs` — scores Round-N -> Round-N+1 adjudicator verdict deltas using ADR-003 weights for option change, confidence delta, material-risk Jaccard delta, axis flip rate, and blocking-disagreement delta.\n150: - `cost-guards.cjs` — normalizes and enforces ADR-004 defaults for `max_rounds_per_topic`, `max_topics_per_session`, `saturation_threshold`, and `seats_per_round`, including upper-bound seat-output calculation.\n151: - `session-state-hierarchy.cjs` — creates and validates the ADR-002 session -> topic -> round state shape, including stable `topic-NNN-slug` and `round-NNN` ids.\n152: \n153: The existing `lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts`, and `lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` remain the sibling runtime contracts. Council modules mirror those durability semantics in a council-scoped CJS surface so downstream `deep-ai-council` phases can consume them without modifying deep-review or deep-research behavior.\n154: \n155: ### Script Entry Points\n156: \n157: Four `.cjs` scripts replace the 4 deleted MCP tools, each honoring the same JSON-in / JSON-out contract documented in ADR-001 (the script interface contract):\n158: \n159: | Script | Replaces | Purpose | Exit codes |\n160: |--------|----------|---------|------------|\n161: | `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes typed CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED decisions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n162: | `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores nodes + edges from iteration `graphEvents` | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n163: | `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims, contradictions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n164: | `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Session-scoped health report | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n165: | `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |\n166: | `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |\n167: | `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |\n168: | `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |\n169: \n170: **Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.\n171: \n172: Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.\n173: \n174: ### Test Organization\n175: \n176: ```text\n177: tests/\n178: ├── unit/         # 13 files — per-module unit tests (atomic-state, executor-config, etc.)\n179: ├── integration/  # 7 files — script invocations + review-depth fixtures\n180: ├── lifecycle/    # 1 file — db-open-close pattern verification\n181: └── _helpers/     # 1 file — spawn-cjs helper for direct-invocation tests\n182: ```\n183: \n184: The system-spec-kit `mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` so a single vitest run discovers both surfaces.\n185: \n186: ---\n187: \n188: ## 4. RULES\n189: \n190: ### ALWAYS\n191: \n192: - **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)\n193: - **ALWAYS** emit JSON-only output to stdout from `scripts/*.cjs` (workflow YAML parses stdout for output bindings)\n194: - **ALWAYS** include `'use strict';` on line 2 of every `.cjs` script (per sk-code OPENCODE JS style)\n195: - **ALWAYS** include a `MODULE: <name>` header comment in every `lib/**/*.ts` file (per sk-code OPENCODE TS-MODULE-HEADER convention)\n196: - **ALWAYS** preserve atomic-state semantics when mutating JSONL state — never partial-write\n197: - **ALWAYS** acquire `loop-lock` before any state-log mutation\n198: \n199: ### NEVER\n200: \n201: - **NEVER** register MCP tools from this skill — the isolation ADR explicitly removed the MCP surface; reintroducing tools defeats the FULL_ISOLATE direction\n202: - **NEVER** open the SQLite DB outside `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` — single owner of the connection\n203: - **NEVER** import from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/` or `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/` — those locations are empty after the runtime moved here; import from this skill instead\n204: - **NEVER** write to `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` without holding the `loop-lock`\n205: - **NEVER** swallow exceptions in script entry points — propagate to the exit-code matrix\n206: \n207: ### ESCALATE IF\n208: \n209: - **ESCALATE** to the user if a runtime call requires a new MCP tool — the architectural direction prohibits MCP additions; the user must decide on policy override\n210: - **ESCALATE** to a new ownership ADR if a new consumer skill (beyond deep-review and deep-research) needs deep-loop runtime — extension requires a new ownership ADR\n211: - **ESCALATE** if the SQLite schema requires a backward-incompatible change — coordinate with all consumer workflows + add migration to `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n212: - **ESCALATE** if a script's stdout JSON contract changes — every workflow YAML output binding depends on the existing shape\n213: \n214: ---\n215: \n216: ## 5. SCOPE\n217: \n218: In scope:\n219: \n220: - runtime libraries under `lib/deep-loop/`\n221: - coverage-graph runtime libraries under `lib/coverage-graph/`\n222: - `.cjs` script entry points under `scripts/`\n223: - runtime-owned SQLite storage under `database/`\n224: - runtime tests under `tests/`\n225: \n226: Out of scope:\n227: \n228: - MCP tool registration\n229: - MCP handler ownership\n230: - DB lifecycle inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`\n231: - direct user command routing\n232: \n233: ---\n234: \n235: ## 6. ARCHITECTURE\n236: \n237: - `lib/deep-loop/` holds shared deep-loop runtime libraries.\n238: - `lib/coverage-graph/` holds coverage-graph schema, query, and signal helpers.\n239: - `scripts/` holds `.cjs` entry points used by workflow YAML and collateral commands.\n240: - `database/` owns relocated runtime SQLite artifacts.\n241: - `tests/` holds runtime-owned tests split from the MCP server surface.\n242: \n243: Shipped layout:\n244: \n245: | Path | Populated By | Purpose |\n246: |------|--------------|---------|\n247: | `lib/deep-loop/` | 10 files | Shared executor config, audit, prompt-pack, validation, state, repair, locking, permissions, scoring, and fallback routing. |\n248: | `lib/coverage-graph/` | 3 files | Coverage-graph schema owner, query helpers, and signal interpretation. |\n249: | `scripts/` | 4 files | Direct `status`, `query`, `upsert`, and `convergence` entry points replacing the removed deep-loop MCP tools. |\n250: | `database/` | SQLite | Runtime-owned `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |\n251: | `tests/` | Unit, integration, lifecycle | Runtime-owned tests discovered by the system-spec-kit MCP server Vitest config. |\n252: \n253: ---\n254: \n255: ## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS\n256: \n257: - **ADR-001 (AI Council SPLIT ruling)**: original AI Council SPLIT ruling. Superseded by user directive.\n258: - **Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP)**: Moves deep-loop runtime, coverage-graph schema/query/signals, script entry points, storage, and tests into this peer skill while removing the deep-loop MCP tool surface.\n259: - **Deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML** call this runtime through script entry points after the runtime cutover.\n260: - **system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts** includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` for cross-package test discovery.\n261: - **deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs** depends on the moved coverage-graph runtime via lib imports.\n262: \n263: ---\n264: \n265: ## 8. SUCCESS CRITERIA\n266: \n267: The runtime is healthy when:\n268: \n269: - Every `scripts/` entry point (`status`, `query`, `upsert`, `convergence`) returns valid JSON on its documented stdout contract\n270: - Consumer workflows resolve their `lib/` imports without path errors\n271: - The runtime-owned SQLite under `database/` is present and its coverage-graph schema matches `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n272: - Runtime tests under `tests/` pass through the system-spec-kit Vitest discovery glob\n273: \n274: ---\n275: \n276: ## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES\n277: \n278: - ADR-001 (script interface contract): documents the script-shim and DB-relocation contract.\n279: - Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP): documents the MCP tool surface removal rationale.\n280: - Decision rationale lives in this skill's changelog and originating decision records.\n281: \n282: Related resources:\n283: \n284: - Consumer skills: the `deep-review` and `deep-research` workflows call this runtime through the script entry points\n285: - `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` discovers this skill's `tests/`\n286: - Per-release detail and rationale: this skill's `changelog/` directory\n\n(End of file - total 286 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"---\nname: deep-loop-runtime\nversion: 1.4.0\ndescription: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\nallowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n---\n\n# Deep Loop Runtime\n\nShared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n\n---\n\n## 1. WHEN TO USE\n\nThis skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n\nUse it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n\n- executor configuration and audit metadata","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md","time":{"start":1780205290639,"end":1780205290662}},"id":"prt_e7c81029a0018WgctXtXLcmFh5","sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","messageID":"msg_e7c80eee8001ZiIO6I4pjCr85X"}}

The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.
