.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-010.md:2507:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:10613:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7877:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2806:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/review/hs/logs/iter-009.out:43:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780205290663,"sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_function_4vnhn0n98zpe_1","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: ---\n2: name: deep-loop-runtime\n3: version: 1.4.0\n4: description: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\n5: allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n6: ---\n7: \n8: # Deep Loop Runtime\n9: \n10: Shared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n11: \n12: ---\n13: \n14: ## 1. WHEN TO USE\n15: \n16: This skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n17: \n18: Use it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n19: \n20: - executor configuration and audit metadata\n21: - prompt-pack rendering\n22: - post-dispatch validation\n23: - atomic state files and JSONL repair\n24: - loop locking and permissions gates\n25: - Bayesian scoring and fallback routing\n26: - coverage-graph schema, query, and signal interpretation\n27: \n28: ### Activation Triggers\n29: \n30: This skill is activated when:\n31: \n32: - A deep-review or deep-research workflow YAML invokes `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/<name>.cjs`\n33: - A test file imports `from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/...'`\n34: - An operator queries / mutates the coverage graph via the runtime scripts\n35: - A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing\n36: \n37: ### When NOT to Use\n38: \n39: Do not invoke this skill when:\n40: \n41: - A user requests an interactive command (use `/deep:start-review-loop` or `/deep:start-research-loop` instead — those orchestrate this runtime)\n42: - A package outside deep-review / deep-research needs deep-loop semantics (out of supported scope; open a packet to extend ownership)\n43: - An MCP tool surface is needed (this skill explicitly has none — the isolation ADR removed the deep-loop MCP tools)\n44: \n45: ---\n46: \n47: ## 2. SMART ROUTING\n48: \n49: This skill has no direct LLM-facing keyword triggers — it is invoked by workflow YAMLs (via `bash:` script calls) and by test imports. The router below documents which sub-domain to load for a given consumer query.\n50: \n51: ### Primary Detection Signal\n52: \n53: ```bash\n54: request_text=\"$(printf '%s' \"$USER_REQUEST\" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')\"\n55: case \"$request_text\" in\n56:   *\"deep-loop runtime\"*|*\"deep-loop-runtime\"*|*\"executor config\"*|*\"prompt pack\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n57:   *\"coverage graph\"*|*\"deep_loop_graph\"*|*\"bayesian scorer\"*|*\"loop lock\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n58:   *\"fallback router\"*|*\"post-dispatch validate\"*|*\"jsonl repair\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n59:   *) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=0 ;;\n60: esac\n61: ```\n62: \n63: ### Phase Detection\n64: \n65: ```text\n66: TASK CONTEXT\n67:     |\n68:     +- STEP 0: Detect runtime-call intent vs. consumer-orchestration intent\n69:     +- STEP 1: If consumer (workflow YAML), invoke the relevant scripts/*.cjs\n70:     +- STEP 2: If contributor (modifying lib code), load matching lib/ subtree\n71:     +- STEP 3: If test author, load tests/ + lib/ pair\n72: ```\n73: \n74: ### Resource Domains\n75: \n76: ```text\n77: lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)\n78: lib/coverage-graph/*.ts       # 3 schema + query + signals modules\n79: scripts/*.cjs                 # 4 entry points (convergence, upsert, query, status)\n80: database/*.sqlite              # runtime-owned SQLite (deep-loop-graph.sqlite)\n81: tests/{unit,integration,lifecycle}/  # 21+ vitest files split by responsibility\n82: ```\n83: \n84: ### Resource Loading Levels\n85: \n86: | Tier | When loaded | Purpose |\n87: |------|-------------|---------|\n88: | ALWAYS | SKILL.md frontmatter | description, allowed-tools, version |\n89: | CONDITIONAL | When a runtime call dispatches | `lib/` modules required by the called function |\n90: | ON_DEMAND | When a test fails or a contributor extends | `tests/` + matching `lib/` sub-tree |\n91: \n92: ### Smart Router Pseudocode\n93: \n94: ```text\n95: IF request mentions a specific lib name (e.g. \"bayesian-scorer\", \"prompt-pack\"):\n96:   → Load lib/deep-loop/<name>.ts + matching tests/unit/<name>.vitest.ts\n97: ELIF request mentions coverage-graph / convergence / signals:\n98:   → Load lib/coverage-graph/*.ts + tests/integration/review-depth-*.vitest.ts\n99: ELIF request mentions a script (convergence.cjs, status.cjs, etc.):\n100:   → Load scripts/<name>.cjs + tests/integration/<name>-script.vitest.ts + lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts\n101: ELSE:\n102:   → Defer to consumer skill (deep-review / deep-research)\n103: ```\n104: \n105: ---\n106: \n107: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS\n108: \n109: ### Runtime Architecture\n110: \n111: The runtime exposes deep-loop primitives to consumer workflows through two paths: **direct `.cjs` script invocation** (replacing the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools) and **TypeScript library imports** (from workflow scripts and tests).\n112: \n113: ```text\n114: [deep-review/deep-research workflow YAML]\n115:             │\n116:             ├─ bash: node .../scripts/<name>.cjs   (coverage-graph mutations)\n117:             │       └─→ lib/coverage-graph/*.ts → database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite\n118:             │\n119:             └─ ts-import: lib/deep-loop/<lib>.ts   (runtime primitives)\n120:                     └─→ atomic-state, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, etc.\n121: ```\n122: \n123: ### Library Structure\n124: \n125: **`lib/deep-loop/` (10 modules):**\n126: - `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings\n127: - `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)\n128: - `prompt-pack.ts` — renders the iteration prompt template\n129: - `post-dispatch-validate.ts` — validates iteration outputs (markdown + JSONL + delta)\n130: - `atomic-state.ts` — atomic state-log writes\n131: - `jsonl-repair.ts` — recovers corrupt JSONL state lines\n132: - `loop-lock.ts` — single-writer locking\n133: - `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks\n134: - `bayesian-scorer.ts` — convergence scoring\n135: - `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix\n136: \n137: **`lib/coverage-graph/` (3 modules):**\n138: - `coverage-graph-db.ts` — SQLite schema + node-kind allow-list + connection lifecycle\n139: - `coverage-graph-query.ts` — query builders\n140: - `coverage-graph-signals.ts` — convergence signal extraction\n141: \n142: ### Council Primitives\n143: \n144: The Runtime Boundary Decision (ADR-001) extends `deep-loop-runtime/lib/` with council-compatible infrastructure primitives while keeping operator-facing and domain workflow semantics in `deep-ai-council`. These primitives are consumed by downstream deep-ai-council orchestration (per-topic multi-round, session-findings registry, command and skill wiring, parity and cost docs) for per-topic orchestration, multi-topic session state, command wiring, parity tests and docs.\n145: \n146: **`lib/council/` (5 modules):**\n147: - `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.\n148: - `round-state-jsonl.cjs` — appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard, repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append, fsyncs writes, and exposes round-state readers for resume.\n149: - `adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs` — scores Round-N -> Round-N+1 adjudicator verdict deltas using ADR-003 weights for option change, confidence delta, material-risk Jaccard delta, axis flip rate, and blocking-disagreement delta.\n150: - `cost-guards.cjs` — normalizes and enforces ADR-004 defaults for `max_rounds_per_topic`, `max_topics_per_session`, `saturation_threshold`, and `seats_per_round`, including upper-bound seat-output calculation.\n151: - `session-state-hierarchy.cjs` — creates and validates the ADR-002 session -> topic -> round state shape, including stable `topic-NNN-slug` and `round-NNN` ids.\n152: \n153: The existing `lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts`, and `lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` remain the sibling runtime contracts. Council modules mirror those durability semantics in a council-scoped CJS surface so downstream `deep-ai-council` phases can consume them without modifying deep-review or deep-research behavior.\n154: \n155: ### Script Entry Points\n156: \n157: Four `.cjs` scripts replace the 4 deleted MCP tools, each honoring the same JSON-in / JSON-out contract documented in ADR-001 (the script interface contract):\n158: \n159: | Script | Replaces | Purpose | Exit codes |\n160: |--------|----------|---------|------------|\n161: | `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes typed CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED decisions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n162: | `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores nodes + edges from iteration `graphEvents` | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n163: | `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims, contradictions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n164: | `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Session-scoped health report | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n165: | `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |\n166: | `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |\n167: | `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |\n168: | `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |\n169: \n170: **Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.\n171: \n172: Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.\n173: \n174: ### Test Organization\n175: \n176: ```text\n177: tests/\n178: ├── unit/         # 13 files — per-module unit tests (atomic-state, executor-config, etc.)\n179: ├── integration/  # 7 files — script invocations + review-depth fixtures\n180: ├── lifecycle/    # 1 file — db-open-close pattern verification\n181: └── _helpers/     # 1 file — spawn-cjs helper for direct-invocation tests\n182: ```\n183: \n184: The system-spec-kit `mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` so a single vitest run discovers both surfaces.\n185: \n186: ---\n187: \n188: ## 4. RULES\n189: \n190: ### ALWAYS\n191: \n192: - **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)\n193: - **ALWAYS** emit JSON-only output to stdout from `scripts/*.cjs` (workflow YAML parses stdout for output bindings)\n194: - **ALWAYS** include `'use strict';` on line 2 of every `.cjs` script (per sk-code OPENCODE JS style)\n195: - **ALWAYS** include a `MODULE: <name>` header comment in every `lib/**/*.ts` file (per sk-code OPENCODE TS-MODULE-HEADER convention)\n196: - **ALWAYS** preserve atomic-state semantics when mutating JSONL state — never partial-write\n197: - **ALWAYS** acquire `loop-lock` before any state-log mutation\n198: \n199: ### NEVER\n200: \n201: - **NEVER** register MCP tools from this skill — the isolation ADR explicitly removed the MCP surface; reintroducing tools defeats the FULL_ISOLATE direction\n202: - **NEVER** open the SQLite DB outside `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` — single owner of the connection\n203: - **NEVER** import from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/` or `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/` — those locations are empty after the runtime moved here; import from this skill instead\n204: - **NEVER** write to `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` without holding the `loop-lock`\n205: - **NEVER** swallow exceptions in script entry points — propagate to the exit-code matrix\n206: \n207: ### ESCALATE IF\n208: \n209: - **ESCALATE** to the user if a runtime call requires a new MCP tool — the architectural direction prohibits MCP additions; the user must decide on policy override\n210: - **ESCALATE** to a new ownership ADR if a new consumer skill (beyond deep-review and deep-research) needs deep-loop runtime — extension requires a new ownership ADR\n211: - **ESCALATE** if the SQLite schema requires a backward-incompatible change — coordinate with all consumer workflows + add migration to `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n212: - **ESCALATE** if a script's stdout JSON contract changes — every workflow YAML output binding depends on the existing shape\n213: \n214: ---\n215: \n216: ## 5. SCOPE\n217: \n218: In scope:\n219: \n220: - runtime libraries under `lib/deep-loop/`\n221: - coverage-graph runtime libraries under `lib/coverage-graph/`\n222: - `.cjs` script entry points under `scripts/`\n223: - runtime-owned SQLite storage under `database/`\n224: - runtime tests under `tests/`\n225: \n226: Out of scope:\n227: \n228: - MCP tool registration\n229: - MCP handler ownership\n230: - DB lifecycle inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`\n231: - direct user command routing\n232: \n233: ---\n234: \n235: ## 6. ARCHITECTURE\n236: \n237: - `lib/deep-loop/` holds shared deep-loop runtime libraries.\n238: - `lib/coverage-graph/` holds coverage-graph schema, query, and signal helpers.\n239: - `scripts/` holds `.cjs` entry points used by workflow YAML and collateral commands.\n240: - `database/` owns relocated runtime SQLite artifacts.\n241: - `tests/` holds runtime-owned tests split from the MCP server surface.\n242: \n243: Shipped layout:\n244: \n245: | Path | Populated By | Purpose |\n246: |------|--------------|---------|\n247: | `lib/deep-loop/` | 10 files | Shared executor config, audit, prompt-pack, validation, state, repair, locking, permissions, scoring, and fallback routing. |\n248: | `lib/coverage-graph/` | 3 files | Coverage-graph schema owner, query helpers, and signal interpretation. |\n249: | `scripts/` | 4 files | Direct `status`, `query`, `upsert`, and `convergence` entry points replacing the removed deep-loop MCP tools. |\n250: | `database/` | SQLite | Runtime-owned `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |\n251: | `tests/` | Unit, integration, lifecycle | Runtime-owned tests discovered by the system-spec-kit MCP server Vitest config. |\n252: \n253: ---\n254: \n255: ## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS\n256: \n257: - **ADR-001 (AI Council SPLIT ruling)**: original AI Council SPLIT ruling. Superseded by user directive.\n258: - **Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP)**: Moves deep-loop runtime, coverage-graph schema/query/signals, script entry points, storage, and tests into this peer skill while removing the deep-loop MCP tool surface.\n259: - **Deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML** call this runtime through script entry points after the runtime cutover.\n260: - **system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts** includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` for cross-package test discovery.\n261: - **deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs** depends on the moved coverage-graph runtime via lib imports.\n262: \n263: ---\n264: \n265: ## 8. SUCCESS CRITERIA\n266: \n267: The runtime is healthy when:\n268: \n269: - Every `scripts/` entry point (`status`, `query`, `upsert`, `convergence`) returns valid JSON on its documented stdout contract\n270: - Consumer workflows resolve their `lib/` imports without path errors\n271: - The runtime-owned SQLite under `database/` is present and its coverage-graph schema matches `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n272: - Runtime tests under `tests/` pass through the system-spec-kit Vitest discovery glob\n273: \n274: ---\n275: \n276: ## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES\n277: \n278: - ADR-001 (script interface contract): documents the script-shim and DB-relocation contract.\n279: - Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP): documents the MCP tool surface removal rationale.\n280: - Decision rationale lives in this skill's changelog and originating decision records.\n281: \n282: Related resources:\n283: \n284: - Consumer skills: the `deep-review` and `deep-research` workflows call this runtime through the script entry points\n285: - `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` discovers this skill's `tests/`\n286: - Per-release detail and rationale: this skill's `changelog/` directory\n\n(End of file - total 286 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"---\nname: deep-loop-runtime\nversion: 1.4.0\ndescription: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\nallowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n---\n\n# Deep Loop Runtime\n\nShared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n\n---\n\n## 1. WHEN TO USE\n\nThis skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n\nUse it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n\n- executor configuration and audit metadata","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md","time":{"start":1780205290639,"end":1780205290662}},"id":"prt_e7c81029a0018WgctXtXLcmFh5","sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","messageID":"msg_e7c80eee8001ZiIO6I4pjCr85X"}}

The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.

The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-010.md:2519:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:10875:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:9354:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2806:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/review/hs/logs/iter-009.out:43:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780205290663,"sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_function_4vnhn0n98zpe_1","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: ---\n2: name: deep-loop-runtime\n3: version: 1.4.0\n4: description: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\n5: allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n6: ---\n7: \n8: # Deep Loop Runtime\n9: \n10: Shared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n11: \n12: ---\n13: \n14: ## 1. WHEN TO USE\n15: \n16: This skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n17: \n18: Use it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n19: \n20: - executor configuration and audit metadata\n21: - prompt-pack rendering\n22: - post-dispatch validation\n23: - atomic state files and JSONL repair\n24: - loop locking and permissions gates\n25: - Bayesian scoring and fallback routing\n26: - coverage-graph schema, query, and signal interpretation\n27: \n28: ### Activation Triggers\n29: \n30: This skill is activated when:\n31: \n32: - A deep-review or deep-research workflow YAML invokes `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/<name>.cjs`\n33: - A test file imports `from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/...'`\n34: - An operator queries / mutates the coverage graph via the runtime scripts\n35: - A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing\n36: \n37: ### When NOT to Use\n38: \n39: Do not invoke this skill when:\n40: \n41: - A user requests an interactive command (use `/deep:start-review-loop` or `/deep:start-research-loop` instead — those orchestrate this runtime)\n42: - A package outside deep-review / deep-research needs deep-loop semantics (out of supported scope; open a packet to extend ownership)\n43: - An MCP tool surface is needed (this skill explicitly has none — the isolation ADR removed the deep-loop MCP tools)\n44: \n45: ---\n46: \n47: ## 2. SMART ROUTING\n48: \n49: This skill has no direct LLM-facing keyword triggers — it is invoked by workflow YAMLs (via `bash:` script calls) and by test imports. The router below documents which sub-domain to load for a given consumer query.\n50: \n51: ### Primary Detection Signal\n52: \n53: ```bash\n54: request_text=\"$(printf '%s' \"$USER_REQUEST\" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')\"\n55: case \"$request_text\" in\n56:   *\"deep-loop runtime\"*|*\"deep-loop-runtime\"*|*\"executor config\"*|*\"prompt pack\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n57:   *\"coverage graph\"*|*\"deep_loop_graph\"*|*\"bayesian scorer\"*|*\"loop lock\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n58:   *\"fallback router\"*|*\"post-dispatch validate\"*|*\"jsonl repair\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n59:   *) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=0 ;;\n60: esac\n61: ```\n62: \n63: ### Phase Detection\n64: \n65: ```text\n66: TASK CONTEXT\n67:     |\n68:     +- STEP 0: Detect runtime-call intent vs. consumer-orchestration intent\n69:     +- STEP 1: If consumer (workflow YAML), invoke the relevant scripts/*.cjs\n70:     +- STEP 2: If contributor (modifying lib code), load matching lib/ subtree\n71:     +- STEP 3: If test author, load tests/ + lib/ pair\n72: ```\n73: \n74: ### Resource Domains\n75: \n76: ```text\n77: lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)\n78: lib/coverage-graph/*.ts       # 3 schema + query + signals modules\n79: scripts/*.cjs                 # 4 entry points (convergence, upsert, query, status)\n80: database/*.sqlite              # runtime-owned SQLite (deep-loop-graph.sqlite)\n81: tests/{unit,integration,lifecycle}/  # 21+ vitest files split by responsibility\n82: ```\n83: \n84: ### Resource Loading Levels\n85: \n86: | Tier | When loaded | Purpose |\n87: |------|-------------|---------|\n88: | ALWAYS | SKILL.md frontmatter | description, allowed-tools, version |\n89: | CONDITIONAL | When a runtime call dispatches | `lib/` modules required by the called function |\n90: | ON_DEMAND | When a test fails or a contributor extends | `tests/` + matching `lib/` sub-tree |\n91: \n92: ### Smart Router Pseudocode\n93: \n94: ```text\n95: IF request mentions a specific lib name (e.g. \"bayesian-scorer\", \"prompt-pack\"):\n96:   → Load lib/deep-loop/<name>.ts + matching tests/unit/<name>.vitest.ts\n97: ELIF request mentions coverage-graph / convergence / signals:\n98:   → Load lib/coverage-graph/*.ts + tests/integration/review-depth-*.vitest.ts\n99: ELIF request mentions a script (convergence.cjs, status.cjs, etc.):\n100:   → Load scripts/<name>.cjs + tests/integration/<name>-script.vitest.ts + lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts\n101: ELSE:\n102:   → Defer to consumer skill (deep-review / deep-research)\n103: ```\n104: \n105: ---\n106: \n107: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS\n108: \n109: ### Runtime Architecture\n110: \n111: The runtime exposes deep-loop primitives to consumer workflows through two paths: **direct `.cjs` script invocation** (replacing the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools) and **TypeScript library imports** (from workflow scripts and tests).\n112: \n113: ```text\n114: [deep-review/deep-research workflow YAML]\n115:             │\n116:             ├─ bash: node .../scripts/<name>.cjs   (coverage-graph mutations)\n117:             │       └─→ lib/coverage-graph/*.ts → database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite\n118:             │\n119:             └─ ts-import: lib/deep-loop/<lib>.ts   (runtime primitives)\n120:                     └─→ atomic-state, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, etc.\n121: ```\n122: \n123: ### Library Structure\n124: \n125: **`lib/deep-loop/` (10 modules):**\n126: - `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings\n127: - `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)\n128: - `prompt-pack.ts` — renders the iteration prompt template\n129: - `post-dispatch-validate.ts` — validates iteration outputs (markdown + JSONL + delta)\n130: - `atomic-state.ts` — atomic state-log writes\n131: - `jsonl-repair.ts` — recovers corrupt JSONL state lines\n132: - `loop-lock.ts` — single-writer locking\n133: - `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks\n134: - `bayesian-scorer.ts` — convergence scoring\n135: - `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix\n136: \n137: **`lib/coverage-graph/` (3 modules):**\n138: - `coverage-graph-db.ts` — SQLite schema + node-kind allow-list + connection lifecycle\n139: - `coverage-graph-query.ts` — query builders\n140: - `coverage-graph-signals.ts` — convergence signal extraction\n141: \n142: ### Council Primitives\n143: \n144: The Runtime Boundary Decision (ADR-001) extends `deep-loop-runtime/lib/` with council-compatible infrastructure primitives while keeping operator-facing and domain workflow semantics in `deep-ai-council`. These primitives are consumed by downstream deep-ai-council orchestration (per-topic multi-round, session-findings registry, command and skill wiring, parity and cost docs) for per-topic orchestration, multi-topic session state, command wiring, parity tests and docs.\n145: \n146: **`lib/council/` (5 modules):**\n147: - `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.\n148: - `round-state-jsonl.cjs` — appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard, repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append, fsyncs writes, and exposes round-state readers for resume.\n149: - `adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs` — scores Round-N -> Round-N+1 adjudicator verdict deltas using ADR-003 weights for option change, confidence delta, material-risk Jaccard delta, axis flip rate, and blocking-disagreement delta.\n150: - `cost-guards.cjs` — normalizes and enforces ADR-004 defaults for `max_rounds_per_topic`, `max_topics_per_session`, `saturation_threshold`, and `seats_per_round`, including upper-bound seat-output calculation.\n151: - `session-state-hierarchy.cjs` — creates and validates the ADR-002 session -> topic -> round state shape, including stable `topic-NNN-slug` and `round-NNN` ids.\n152: \n153: The existing `lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts`, and `lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` remain the sibling runtime contracts. Council modules mirror those durability semantics in a council-scoped CJS surface so downstream `deep-ai-council` phases can consume them without modifying deep-review or deep-research behavior.\n154: \n155: ### Script Entry Points\n156: \n157: Four `.cjs` scripts replace the 4 deleted MCP tools, each honoring the same JSON-in / JSON-out contract documented in ADR-001 (the script interface contract):\n158: \n159: | Script | Replaces | Purpose | Exit codes |\n160: |--------|----------|---------|------------|\n161: | `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes typed CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED decisions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n162: | `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores nodes + edges from iteration `graphEvents` | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n163: | `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims, contradictions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n164: | `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Session-scoped health report | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n165: | `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |\n166: | `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |\n167: | `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |\n168: | `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |\n169: \n170: **Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.\n171: \n172: Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.\n173: \n174: ### Test Organization\n175: \n176: ```text\n177: tests/\n178: ├── unit/         # 13 files — per-module unit tests (atomic-state, executor-config, etc.)\n179: ├── integration/  # 7 files — script invocations + review-depth fixtures\n180: ├── lifecycle/    # 1 file — db-open-close pattern verification\n181: └── _helpers/     # 1 file — spawn-cjs helper for direct-invocation tests\n182: ```\n183: \n184: The system-spec-kit `mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` so a single vitest run discovers both surfaces.\n185: \n186: ---\n187: \n188: ## 4. RULES\n189: \n190: ### ALWAYS\n191: \n192: - **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)\n193: - **ALWAYS** emit JSON-only output to stdout from `scripts/*.cjs` (workflow YAML parses stdout for output bindings)\n194: - **ALWAYS** include `'use strict';` on line 2 of every `.cjs` script (per sk-code OPENCODE JS style)\n195: - **ALWAYS** include a `MODULE: <name>` header comment in every `lib/**/*.ts` file (per sk-code OPENCODE TS-MODULE-HEADER convention)\n196: - **ALWAYS** preserve atomic-state semantics when mutating JSONL state — never partial-write\n197: - **ALWAYS** acquire `loop-lock` before any state-log mutation\n198: \n199: ### NEVER\n200: \n201: - **NEVER** register MCP tools from this skill — the isolation ADR explicitly removed the MCP surface; reintroducing tools defeats the FULL_ISOLATE direction\n202: - **NEVER** open the SQLite DB outside `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` — single owner of the connection\n203: - **NEVER** import from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/` or `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/` — those locations are empty after the runtime moved here; import from this skill instead\n204: - **NEVER** write to `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` without holding the `loop-lock`\n205: - **NEVER** swallow exceptions in script entry points — propagate to the exit-code matrix\n206: \n207: ### ESCALATE IF\n208: \n209: - **ESCALATE** to the user if a runtime call requires a new MCP tool — the architectural direction prohibits MCP additions; the user must decide on policy override\n210: - **ESCALATE** to a new ownership ADR if a new consumer skill (beyond deep-review and deep-research) needs deep-loop runtime — extension requires a new ownership ADR\n211: - **ESCALATE** if the SQLite schema requires a backward-incompatible change — coordinate with all consumer workflows + add migration to `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n212: - **ESCALATE** if a script's stdout JSON contract changes — every workflow YAML output binding depends on the existing shape\n213: \n214: ---\n215: \n216: ## 5. SCOPE\n217: \n218: In scope:\n219: \n220: - runtime libraries under `lib/deep-loop/`\n221: - coverage-graph runtime libraries under `lib/coverage-graph/`\n222: - `.cjs` script entry points under `scripts/`\n223: - runtime-owned SQLite storage under `database/`\n224: - runtime tests under `tests/`\n225: \n226: Out of scope:\n227: \n228: - MCP tool registration\n229: - MCP handler ownership\n230: - DB lifecycle inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`\n231: - direct user command routing\n232: \n233: ---\n234: \n235: ## 6. ARCHITECTURE\n236: \n237: - `lib/deep-loop/` holds shared deep-loop runtime libraries.\n238: - `lib/coverage-graph/` holds coverage-graph schema, query, and signal helpers.\n239: - `scripts/` holds `.cjs` entry points used by workflow YAML and collateral commands.\n240: - `database/` owns relocated runtime SQLite artifacts.\n241: - `tests/` holds runtime-owned tests split from the MCP server surface.\n242: \n243: Shipped layout:\n244: \n245: | Path | Populated By | Purpose |\n246: |------|--------------|---------|\n247: | `lib/deep-loop/` | 10 files | Shared executor config, audit, prompt-pack, validation, state, repair, locking, permissions, scoring, and fallback routing. |\n248: | `lib/coverage-graph/` | 3 files | Coverage-graph schema owner, query helpers, and signal interpretation. |\n249: | `scripts/` | 4 files | Direct `status`, `query`, `upsert`, and `convergence` entry points replacing the removed deep-loop MCP tools. |\n250: | `database/` | SQLite | Runtime-owned `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |\n251: | `tests/` | Unit, integration, lifecycle | Runtime-owned tests discovered by the system-spec-kit MCP server Vitest config. |\n252: \n253: ---\n254: \n255: ## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS\n256: \n257: - **ADR-001 (AI Council SPLIT ruling)**: original AI Council SPLIT ruling. Superseded by user directive.\n258: - **Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP)**: Moves deep-loop runtime, coverage-graph schema/query/signals, script entry points, storage, and tests into this peer skill while removing the deep-loop MCP tool surface.\n259: - **Deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML** call this runtime through script entry points after the runtime cutover.\n260: - **system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts** includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` for cross-package test discovery.\n261: - **deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs** depends on the moved coverage-graph runtime via lib imports.\n262: \n263: ---\n264: \n265: ## 8. SUCCESS CRITERIA\n266: \n267: The runtime is healthy when:\n268: \n269: - Every `scripts/` entry point (`status`, `query`, `upsert`, `convergence`) returns valid JSON on its documented stdout contract\n270: - Consumer workflows resolve their `lib/` imports without path errors\n271: - The runtime-owned SQLite under `database/` is present and its coverage-graph schema matches `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n272: - Runtime tests under `tests/` pass through the system-spec-kit Vitest discovery glob\n273: \n274: ---\n275: \n276: ## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES\n277: \n278: - ADR-001 (script interface contract): documents the script-shim and DB-relocation contract.\n279: - Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP): documents the MCP tool surface removal rationale.\n280: - Decision rationale lives in this skill's changelog and originating decision records.\n281: \n282: Related resources:\n283: \n284: - Consumer skills: the `deep-review` and `deep-research` workflows call this runtime through the script entry points\n285: - `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` discovers this skill's `tests/`\n286: - Per-release detail and rationale: this skill's `changelog/` directory\n\n(End of file - total 286 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"---\nname: deep-loop-runtime\nversion: 1.4.0\ndescription: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\nallowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n---\n\n# Deep Loop Runtime\n\nShared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n\n---\n\n## 1. WHEN TO USE\n\nThis skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n\nUse it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n\n- executor configuration and audit metadata","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md","time":{"start":1780205290639,"end":1780205290662}},"id":"prt_e7c81029a0018WgctXtXLcmFh5","sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","messageID":"msg_e7c80eee8001ZiIO6I4pjCr85X"}}

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-010.md:2563:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11055:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-003.out:7889:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2806:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/review/hs/logs/iter-009.out:43:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780205290663,"sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_function_4vnhn0n98zpe_1","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: ---\n2: name: deep-loop-runtime\n3: version: 1.4.0\n4: description: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\n5: allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n6: ---\n7: \n8: # Deep Loop Runtime\n9: \n10: Shared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n11: \n12: ---\n13: \n14: ## 1. WHEN TO USE\n15: \n16: This skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n17: \n18: Use it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n19: \n20: - executor configuration and audit metadata\n21: - prompt-pack rendering\n22: - post-dispatch validation\n23: - atomic state files and JSONL repair\n24: - loop locking and permissions gates\n25: - Bayesian scoring and fallback routing\n26: - coverage-graph schema, query, and signal interpretation\n27: \n28: ### Activation Triggers\n29: \n30: This skill is activated when:\n31: \n32: - A deep-review or deep-research workflow YAML invokes `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/<name>.cjs`\n33: - A test file imports `from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/...'`\n34: - An operator queries / mutates the coverage graph via the runtime scripts\n35: - A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing\n36: \n37: ### When NOT to Use\n38: \n39: Do not invoke this skill when:\n40: \n41: - A user requests an interactive command (use `/deep:start-review-loop` or `/deep:start-research-loop` instead — those orchestrate this runtime)\n42: - A package outside deep-review / deep-research needs deep-loop semantics (out of supported scope; open a packet to extend ownership)\n43: - An MCP tool surface is needed (this skill explicitly has none — the isolation ADR removed the deep-loop MCP tools)\n44: \n45: ---\n46: \n47: ## 2. SMART ROUTING\n48: \n49: This skill has no direct LLM-facing keyword triggers — it is invoked by workflow YAMLs (via `bash:` script calls) and by test imports. The router below documents which sub-domain to load for a given consumer query.\n50: \n51: ### Primary Detection Signal\n52: \n53: ```bash\n54: request_text=\"$(printf '%s' \"$USER_REQUEST\" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')\"\n55: case \"$request_text\" in\n56:   *\"deep-loop runtime\"*|*\"deep-loop-runtime\"*|*\"executor config\"*|*\"prompt pack\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n57:   *\"coverage graph\"*|*\"deep_loop_graph\"*|*\"bayesian scorer\"*|*\"loop lock\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n58:   *\"fallback router\"*|*\"post-dispatch validate\"*|*\"jsonl repair\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n59:   *) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=0 ;;\n60: esac\n61: ```\n62: \n63: ### Phase Detection\n64: \n65: ```text\n66: TASK CONTEXT\n67:     |\n68:     +- STEP 0: Detect runtime-call intent vs. consumer-orchestration intent\n69:     +- STEP 1: If consumer (workflow YAML), invoke the relevant scripts/*.cjs\n70:     +- STEP 2: If contributor (modifying lib code), load matching lib/ subtree\n71:     +- STEP 3: If test author, load tests/ + lib/ pair\n72: ```\n73: \n74: ### Resource Domains\n75: \n76: ```text\n77: lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)\n78: lib/coverage-graph/*.ts       # 3 schema + query + signals modules\n79: scripts/*.cjs                 # 4 entry points (convergence, upsert, query, status)\n80: database/*.sqlite              # runtime-owned SQLite (deep-loop-graph.sqlite)\n81: tests/{unit,integration,lifecycle}/  # 21+ vitest files split by responsibility\n82: ```\n83: \n84: ### Resource Loading Levels\n85: \n86: | Tier | When loaded | Purpose |\n87: |------|-------------|---------|\n88: | ALWAYS | SKILL.md frontmatter | description, allowed-tools, version |\n89: | CONDITIONAL | When a runtime call dispatches | `lib/` modules required by the called function |\n90: | ON_DEMAND | When a test fails or a contributor extends | `tests/` + matching `lib/` sub-tree |\n91: \n92: ### Smart Router Pseudocode\n93: \n94: ```text\n95: IF request mentions a specific lib name (e.g. \"bayesian-scorer\", \"prompt-pack\"):\n96:   → Load lib/deep-loop/<name>.ts + matching tests/unit/<name>.vitest.ts\n97: ELIF request mentions coverage-graph / convergence / signals:\n98:   → Load lib/coverage-graph/*.ts + tests/integration/review-depth-*.vitest.ts\n99: ELIF request mentions a script (convergence.cjs, status.cjs, etc.):\n100:   → Load scripts/<name>.cjs + tests/integration/<name>-script.vitest.ts + lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts\n101: ELSE:\n102:   → Defer to consumer skill (deep-review / deep-research)\n103: ```\n104: \n105: ---\n106: \n107: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS\n108: \n109: ### Runtime Architecture\n110: \n111: The runtime exposes deep-loop primitives to consumer workflows through two paths: **direct `.cjs` script invocation** (replacing the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools) and **TypeScript library imports** (from workflow scripts and tests).\n112: \n113: ```text\n114: [deep-review/deep-research workflow YAML]\n115:             │\n116:             ├─ bash: node .../scripts/<name>.cjs   (coverage-graph mutations)\n117:             │       └─→ lib/coverage-graph/*.ts → database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite\n118:             │\n119:             └─ ts-import: lib/deep-loop/<lib>.ts   (runtime primitives)\n120:                     └─→ atomic-state, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, etc.\n121: ```\n122: \n123: ### Library Structure\n124: \n125: **`lib/deep-loop/` (10 modules):**\n126: - `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings\n127: - `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)\n128: - `prompt-pack.ts` — renders the iteration prompt template\n129: - `post-dispatch-validate.ts` — validates iteration outputs (markdown + JSONL + delta)\n130: - `atomic-state.ts` — atomic state-log writes\n131: - `jsonl-repair.ts` — recovers corrupt JSONL state lines\n132: - `loop-lock.ts` — single-writer locking\n133: - `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks\n134: - `bayesian-scorer.ts` — convergence scoring\n135: - `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix\n136: \n137: **`lib/coverage-graph/` (3 modules):**\n138: - `coverage-graph-db.ts` — SQLite schema + node-kind allow-list + connection lifecycle\n139: - `coverage-graph-query.ts` — query builders\n140: - `coverage-graph-signals.ts` — convergence signal extraction\n141: \n142: ### Council Primitives\n143: \n144: The Runtime Boundary Decision (ADR-001) extends `deep-loop-runtime/lib/` with council-compatible infrastructure primitives while keeping operator-facing and domain workflow semantics in `deep-ai-council`. These primitives are consumed by downstream deep-ai-council orchestration (per-topic multi-round, session-findings registry, command and skill wiring, parity and cost docs) for per-topic orchestration, multi-topic session state, command wiring, parity tests and docs.\n145: \n146: **`lib/council/` (5 modules):**\n147: - `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.\n148: - `round-state-jsonl.cjs` — appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard, repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append, fsyncs writes, and exposes round-state readers for resume.\n149: - `adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs` — scores Round-N -> Round-N+1 adjudicator verdict deltas using ADR-003 weights for option change, confidence delta, material-risk Jaccard delta, axis flip rate, and blocking-disagreement delta.\n150: - `cost-guards.cjs` — normalizes and enforces ADR-004 defaults for `max_rounds_per_topic`, `max_topics_per_session`, `saturation_threshold`, and `seats_per_round`, including upper-bound seat-output calculation.\n151: - `session-state-hierarchy.cjs` — creates and validates the ADR-002 session -> topic -> round state shape, including stable `topic-NNN-slug` and `round-NNN` ids.\n152: \n153: The existing `lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts`, and `lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` remain the sibling runtime contracts. Council modules mirror those durability semantics in a council-scoped CJS surface so downstream `deep-ai-council` phases can consume them without modifying deep-review or deep-research behavior.\n154: \n155: ### Script Entry Points\n156: \n157: Four `.cjs` scripts replace the 4 deleted MCP tools, each honoring the same JSON-in / JSON-out contract documented in ADR-001 (the script interface contract):\n158: \n159: | Script | Replaces | Purpose | Exit codes |\n160: |--------|----------|---------|------------|\n161: | `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes typed CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED decisions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n162: | `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores nodes + edges from iteration `graphEvents` | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n163: | `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims, contradictions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n164: | `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Session-scoped health report | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n165: | `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |\n166: | `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |\n167: | `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |\n168: | `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |\n169: \n170: **Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.\n171: \n172: Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.\n173: \n174: ### Test Organization\n175: \n176: ```text\n177: tests/\n178: ├── unit/         # 13 files — per-module unit tests (atomic-state, executor-config, etc.)\n179: ├── integration/  # 7 files — script invocations + review-depth fixtures\n180: ├── lifecycle/    # 1 file — db-open-close pattern verification\n181: └── _helpers/     # 1 file — spawn-cjs helper for direct-invocation tests\n182: ```\n183: \n184: The system-spec-kit `mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` so a single vitest run discovers both surfaces.\n185: \n186: ---\n187: \n188: ## 4. RULES\n189: \n190: ### ALWAYS\n191: \n192: - **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)\n193: - **ALWAYS** emit JSON-only output to stdout from `scripts/*.cjs` (workflow YAML parses stdout for output bindings)\n194: - **ALWAYS** include `'use strict';` on line 2 of every `.cjs` script (per sk-code OPENCODE JS style)\n195: - **ALWAYS** include a `MODULE: <name>` header comment in every `lib/**/*.ts` file (per sk-code OPENCODE TS-MODULE-HEADER convention)\n196: - **ALWAYS** preserve atomic-state semantics when mutating JSONL state — never partial-write\n197: - **ALWAYS** acquire `loop-lock` before any state-log mutation\n198: \n199: ### NEVER\n200: \n201: - **NEVER** register MCP tools from this skill — the isolation ADR explicitly removed the MCP surface; reintroducing tools defeats the FULL_ISOLATE direction\n202: - **NEVER** open the SQLite DB outside `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` — single owner of the connection\n203: - **NEVER** import from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/` or `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/` — those locations are empty after the runtime moved here; import from this skill instead\n204: - **NEVER** write to `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` without holding the `loop-lock`\n205: - **NEVER** swallow exceptions in script entry points — propagate to the exit-code matrix\n206: \n207: ### ESCALATE IF\n208: \n209: - **ESCALATE** to the user if a runtime call requires a new MCP tool — the architectural direction prohibits MCP additions; the user must decide on policy override\n210: - **ESCALATE** to a new ownership ADR if a new consumer skill (beyond deep-review and deep-research) needs deep-loop runtime — extension requires a new ownership ADR\n211: - **ESCALATE** if the SQLite schema requires a backward-incompatible change — coordinate with all consumer workflows + add migration to `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n212: - **ESCALATE** if a script's stdout JSON contract changes — every workflow YAML output binding depends on the existing shape\n213: \n214: ---\n215: \n216: ## 5. SCOPE\n217: \n218: In scope:\n219: \n220: - runtime libraries under `lib/deep-loop/`\n221: - coverage-graph runtime libraries under `lib/coverage-graph/`\n222: - `.cjs` script entry points under `scripts/`\n223: - runtime-owned SQLite storage under `database/`\n224: - runtime tests under `tests/`\n225: \n226: Out of scope:\n227: \n228: - MCP tool registration\n229: - MCP handler ownership\n230: - DB lifecycle inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`\n231: - direct user command routing\n232: \n233: ---\n234: \n235: ## 6. ARCHITECTURE\n236: \n237: - `lib/deep-loop/` holds shared deep-loop runtime libraries.\n238: - `lib/coverage-graph/` holds coverage-graph schema, query, and signal helpers.\n239: - `scripts/` holds `.cjs` entry points used by workflow YAML and collateral commands.\n240: - `database/` owns relocated runtime SQLite artifacts.\n241: - `tests/` holds runtime-owned tests split from the MCP server surface.\n242: \n243: Shipped layout:\n244: \n245: | Path | Populated By | Purpose |\n246: |------|--------------|---------|\n247: | `lib/deep-loop/` | 10 files | Shared executor config, audit, prompt-pack, validation, state, repair, locking, permissions, scoring, and fallback routing. |\n248: | `lib/coverage-graph/` | 3 files | Coverage-graph schema owner, query helpers, and signal interpretation. |\n249: | `scripts/` | 4 files | Direct `status`, `query`, `upsert`, and `convergence` entry points replacing the removed deep-loop MCP tools. |\n250: | `database/` | SQLite | Runtime-owned `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |\n251: | `tests/` | Unit, integration, lifecycle | Runtime-owned tests discovered by the system-spec-kit MCP server Vitest config. |\n252: \n253: ---\n254: \n255: ## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS\n256: \n257: - **ADR-001 (AI Council SPLIT ruling)**: original AI Council SPLIT ruling. Superseded by user directive.\n258: - **Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP)**: Moves deep-loop runtime, coverage-graph schema/query/signals, script entry points, storage, and tests into this peer skill while removing the deep-loop MCP tool surface.\n259: - **Deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML** call this runtime through script entry points after the runtime cutover.\n260: - **system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts** includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` for cross-package test discovery.\n261: - **deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs** depends on the moved coverage-graph runtime via lib imports.\n262: \n263: ---\n264: \n265: ## 8. SUCCESS CRITERIA\n266: \n267: The runtime is healthy when:\n268: \n269: - Every `scripts/` entry point (`status`, `query`, `upsert`, `convergence`) returns valid JSON on its documented stdout contract\n270: - Consumer workflows resolve their `lib/` imports without path errors\n271: - The runtime-owned SQLite under `database/` is present and its coverage-graph schema matches `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n272: - Runtime tests under `tests/` pass through the system-spec-kit Vitest discovery glob\n273: \n274: ---\n275: \n276: ## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES\n277: \n278: - ADR-001 (script interface contract): documents the script-shim and DB-relocation contract.\n279: - Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP): documents the MCP tool surface removal rationale.\n280: - Decision rationale lives in this skill's changelog and originating decision records.\n281: \n282: Related resources:\n283: \n284: - Consumer skills: the `deep-review` and `deep-research` workflows call this runtime through the script entry points\n285: - `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` discovers this skill's `tests/`\n286: - Per-release detail and rationale: this skill's `changelog/` directory\n\n(End of file - total 286 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"---\nname: deep-loop-runtime\nversion: 1.4.0\ndescription: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\nallowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n---\n\n# Deep Loop Runtime\n\nShared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n\n---\n\n## 1. WHEN TO USE\n\nThis skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n\nUse it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n\n- executor configuration and audit metadata","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md","time":{"start":1780205290639,"end":1780205290662}},"id":"prt_e7c81029a0018WgctXtXLcmFh5","sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","messageID":"msg_e7c80eee8001ZiIO6I4pjCr85X"}}

The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:7:Model an iteration schedule as an ordered sequence of single-executor lineages (bands) inside the existing fan-out machinery, not as new intra-loop executor rotation. Reframe "nine times M2.7 then one time Opus" as two homogeneous bands the system already supports: band A executor `minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` iterations 9; band B executor `claude-opus-4-8` iterations 1, `dependsOn` A. Add a thin `iterationPlan` flag (a.k.a. `model-schedule`) over the existing `executors` schema that desugars one-to-one into ordered lineages plus a minimal sequencing hint, reusing `step_fanout_spawn`, `step_fanout_spawn_cli`, `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, `fanout-merge.cjs`, and `executor-config.ts`. Per-band focus/dimensions become optional fields threaded into existing per-iteration prompt construction; merge precedence (Opus overrides MiniMax on conflict) is an ordered band-priority list consumed by `fanout-merge.cjs`, keeping severity rollup as an invariant above priority. Single-executor and homogeneous fan-out stay byte-identical; the mode is opt-in, absent-by-default. Reuse the deep-research-before-build convention if siblings use it, front-loading research to lock the desugaring contract against the real fan-out APIs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:11:2. T2: front-loaded research — document real interfaces of `executor-config.ts`, `step_fanout_spawn`/`step_fanout_spawn_cli` (both auto YAMLs), `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, `fanout-merge.cjs`; capture exact entry points and the desugaring target fields.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:13:4. T4: specify desugaring and dispatch integration — `step_fanout_spawn`/`step_fanout_spawn_cli` consume desugared lineages through `fanout-pool.cjs`/`fanout-run.cjs` unchanged; per-lineage artifact-dir isolation, session id, per-kind state-dir per band.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:17:8. T8: specify merge precedence — ordered band-priority list in `fanout-merge.cjs` for conflict resolution, preserving the severity-rollup invariant for review and dedup/attribution for research.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:172:Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:192:- **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:184:Four `.cjs` scripts replace the four deleted MCP tools. Each parses argv, opens SQLite inside a `try`, calls the relevant lib function, writes JSON to stdout, closes the DB in a `finally`, exits with the standardized code.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/description.json:3:  "description": "Design (spec only) a first-class contract-compliant per-iteration model schedule for deep-loop fan-out: an operator can request a fixed-count heterogeneous schedule (e.g. nine iterations on minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed then one on claude-opus-4-8) through /deep:start-review-loop, /deep:start-research-loop, and the shared deep-loop-runtime. Models the schedule as ordered single-executor lineages (bands) over the existing fan-out machinery via a thin iterationPlan flag that desugars one-to-one into ordered lineages plus a minimal sequencing hint reusing step_fanout_spawn, fanout-pool.cjs, fanout-run.cjs, fanout-salvage.cjs, fanout-merge.cjs, and executor-config.ts. Produces the schema, routing, audit, merge-precedence, and command-surface design; implementation is this phase's future work.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/description.json:12:    "fanout-merge",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:8:DIMENSION: fanout-merge.cjs consumer merges. Review .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs. Check the research merge (dedup + attribution) and the review merge (severity rollup + strongest-restriction: any active P0 forces merged FAIL). Find rollup bugs, attribution loss, dedup collisions.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:286:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7771:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/005-consumer-merges-synthesis/spec.md:30:- New `deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` (`--loop-type research|review`), reads every `{artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/` registry + iterations + state log.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7775:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:7:Model an iteration schedule as an ordered sequence of single-executor lineages (bands) inside the existing fan-out machinery, not as new intra-loop executor rotation. Reframe "nine times M2.7 then one time Opus" as two homogeneous bands the system already supports: band A executor `minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` iterations 9; band B executor `claude-opus-4-8` iterations 1, `dependsOn` A. Add a thin `iterationPlan` flag (a.k.a. `model-schedule`) over the existing `executors` schema that desugars one-to-one into ordered lineages plus a minimal sequencing hint, reusing `step_fanout_spawn`, `step_fanout_spawn_cli`, `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, `fanout-merge.cjs`, and `executor-config.ts`. Per-band focus/dimensions become optional fields threaded into existing per-iteration prompt construction; merge precedence (Opus overrides MiniMax on conflict) is an ordered band-priority list consumed by `fanout-merge.cjs`, keeping severity rollup as an invariant above priority. Single-executor and homogeneous fan-out stay byte-identical; the mode is opt-in, absent-by-default. Reuse the deep-research-before-build convention if siblings use it, front-loading research to lock the desugaring contract against the real fan-out APIs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7776:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:11:2. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7787:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/description.json:3:  "description": "Design (spec only) a first-class contract-compliant per-iteration model schedule for deep-loop fan-out: an operator can request a fixed-count heterogeneous schedule (e.g. nine iterations on minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed then one on claude-opus-4-8) through /deep:start-review-loop, /deep:start-research-loop, and the shared deep-loop-runtime. Models the schedule as ordered single-executor lineages (bands) over the existing fan-out machinery via a thin iterationPlan flag that desugars one-to-one into ordered lineages plus a minimal sequencing hint reusing step_fanout_spawn, fanout-pool.cjs, fanout-run.cjs, fanout-salvage.cjs, fanout-merge.cjs, and executor-config.ts. Produces the schema, routing, audit, merge-precedence, and command-surface design; implementation is this phase's future work.",

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7810:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/tasks.md:9:- [ ] T2: Front-loaded research (deep-research-before-build) — document real interfaces of executor-config.ts, step_fanout_spawn + step_fanout_spawn_cli (both auto YAMLs), fanout-pool.cjs, fanout-run.cjs, fanout-salvage.cjs, fanout-merge.cjs; capture exact entry points and the desugaring target fields.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7811:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/tasks.md:15:- [ ] T8: Specify merge precedence — ordered band-priority list in fanout-merge.cjs for conflict resolution (Opus band overrides MiniMax band), preserving the severity-rollup invariant for review and dedup/attribution for research.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7812:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:168:| `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7813:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:56:- **`scripts/fanout-merge.cjs`** — Cross-lineage merge script. Reads every `{artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/` sub-packet registry + state log; produces a consolidated registry at the base artifact dir. Research merge: deduplicates `keyFindings` by `id` field, keeping the first-seen entry and appending a `_lineages` attribution array; aggregates total iteration count and averages convergence score; merges `openQuestions` and `ruledOutDirections` similarly. Review merge: applies **strongest-restriction** — iterates active findings across all lineages, escalates to highest severity for duplicate `findingId`, counts active P0/P1/P2, derives `mergedVerdict` (FAIL if activeP0 > 0, CONDITIONAL if activeP1 > 0, PASS otherwise). Both write `{artifact_dir}/fanout-attribution.md` with a per-lineage table (label, kind, model, iterations, convergence score, salvaged count, verdict). Main exits 0=ok, 3=input validation. Exports `mergeResearchRegistries`, `mergeReviewRegistries`, `buildAttributionMd` for unit testing.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7814:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:58:- **`step_fanout_merge` added to top of `phase_synthesis` in all 4 YAMLs** — `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"`. Calls `fanout-merge.cjs` with the base `artifact_dir`. Review variant adds `bind_from_output: {p0_count: "active_p0", p1_count: "active_p1", p2_count: "active_p2"}` so `step_derive_verdict` uses merged counts rather than an empty single-executor state log. Research variant feeds the merged `deep-research-findings-registry.json` into the existing `step_compile_research` unchanged.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7815:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:60:- **`tests/unit/fanout-merge.vitest.ts`** — 10 tests: `mergeResearchRegistries` (3: dedup by id + cross-lineage tracking, total iter aggregation, null registry handled); `mergeReviewRegistries` strongest-restriction (5: clean+P0→FAIL, all clean→PASS, P1-only→CONDITIONAL, duplicate findingId escalates severity, non-active findings ignored); `fanout-merge.cjs` script e2e (2: no lineages dir → ok, 2-lineage review fixture → merged FAIL registry + attribution.md).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7816:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:68:- **`deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md`** — Script table extended with 4 new rows (`fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, `fanout-merge.cjs`). Fan-out isolation invariant documented: per-lineage `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override, per-lineage `session_id` for coverage-graph DB writes (no schema change), per-kind `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` for lockfile isolation.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7853:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:102:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.
