.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11055:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-003.out:7889:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2806:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/review/hs/logs/iter-009.out:43:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780205290663,"sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_function_4vnhn0n98zpe_1","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: ---\n2: name: deep-loop-runtime\n3: version: 1.4.0\n4: description: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\n5: allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n6: ---\n7: \n8: # Deep Loop Runtime\n9: \n10: Shared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n11: \n12: ---\n13: \n14: ## 1. WHEN TO USE\n15: \n16: This skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n17: \n18: Use it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n19: \n20: - executor configuration and audit metadata\n21: - prompt-pack rendering\n22: - post-dispatch validation\n23: - atomic state files and JSONL repair\n24: - loop locking and permissions gates\n25: - Bayesian scoring and fallback routing\n26: - coverage-graph schema, query, and signal interpretation\n27: \n28: ### Activation Triggers\n29: \n30: This skill is activated when:\n31: \n32: - A deep-review or deep-research workflow YAML invokes `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/<name>.cjs`\n33: - A test file imports `from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/...'`\n34: - An operator queries / mutates the coverage graph via the runtime scripts\n35: - A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing\n36: \n37: ### When NOT to Use\n38: \n39: Do not invoke this skill when:\n40: \n41: - A user requests an interactive command (use `/deep:start-review-loop` or `/deep:start-research-loop` instead — those orchestrate this runtime)\n42: - A package outside deep-review / deep-research needs deep-loop semantics (out of supported scope; open a packet to extend ownership)\n43: - An MCP tool surface is needed (this skill explicitly has none — the isolation ADR removed the deep-loop MCP tools)\n44: \n45: ---\n46: \n47: ## 2. SMART ROUTING\n48: \n49: This skill has no direct LLM-facing keyword triggers — it is invoked by workflow YAMLs (via `bash:` script calls) and by test imports. The router below documents which sub-domain to load for a given consumer query.\n50: \n51: ### Primary Detection Signal\n52: \n53: ```bash\n54: request_text=\"$(printf '%s' \"$USER_REQUEST\" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')\"\n55: case \"$request_text\" in\n56:   *\"deep-loop runtime\"*|*\"deep-loop-runtime\"*|*\"executor config\"*|*\"prompt pack\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n57:   *\"coverage graph\"*|*\"deep_loop_graph\"*|*\"bayesian scorer\"*|*\"loop lock\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n58:   *\"fallback router\"*|*\"post-dispatch validate\"*|*\"jsonl repair\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n59:   *) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=0 ;;\n60: esac\n61: ```\n62: \n63: ### Phase Detection\n64: \n65: ```text\n66: TASK CONTEXT\n67:     |\n68:     +- STEP 0: Detect runtime-call intent vs. consumer-orchestration intent\n69:     +- STEP 1: If consumer (workflow YAML), invoke the relevant scripts/*.cjs\n70:     +- STEP 2: If contributor (modifying lib code), load matching lib/ subtree\n71:     +- STEP 3: If test author, load tests/ + lib/ pair\n72: ```\n73: \n74: ### Resource Domains\n75: \n76: ```text\n77: lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)\n78: lib/coverage-graph/*.ts       # 3 schema + query + signals modules\n79: scripts/*.cjs                 # 4 entry points (convergence, upsert, query, status)\n80: database/*.sqlite              # runtime-owned SQLite (deep-loop-graph.sqlite)\n81: tests/{unit,integration,lifecycle}/  # 21+ vitest files split by responsibility\n82: ```\n83: \n84: ### Resource Loading Levels\n85: \n86: | Tier | When loaded | Purpose |\n87: |------|-------------|---------|\n88: | ALWAYS | SKILL.md frontmatter | description, allowed-tools, version |\n89: | CONDITIONAL | When a runtime call dispatches | `lib/` modules required by the called function |\n90: | ON_DEMAND | When a test fails or a contributor extends | `tests/` + matching `lib/` sub-tree |\n91: \n92: ### Smart Router Pseudocode\n93: \n94: ```text\n95: IF request mentions a specific lib name (e.g. \"bayesian-scorer\", \"prompt-pack\"):\n96:   → Load lib/deep-loop/<name>.ts + matching tests/unit/<name>.vitest.ts\n97: ELIF request mentions coverage-graph / convergence / signals:\n98:   → Load lib/coverage-graph/*.ts + tests/integration/review-depth-*.vitest.ts\n99: ELIF request mentions a script (convergence.cjs, status.cjs, etc.):\n100:   → Load scripts/<name>.cjs + tests/integration/<name>-script.vitest.ts + lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts\n101: ELSE:\n102:   → Defer to consumer skill (deep-review / deep-research)\n103: ```\n104: \n105: ---\n106: \n107: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS\n108: \n109: ### Runtime Architecture\n110: \n111: The runtime exposes deep-loop primitives to consumer workflows through two paths: **direct `.cjs` script invocation** (replacing the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools) and **TypeScript library imports** (from workflow scripts and tests).\n112: \n113: ```text\n114: [deep-review/deep-research workflow YAML]\n115:             │\n116:             ├─ bash: node .../scripts/<name>.cjs   (coverage-graph mutations)\n117:             │       └─→ lib/coverage-graph/*.ts → database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite\n118:             │\n119:             └─ ts-import: lib/deep-loop/<lib>.ts   (runtime primitives)\n120:                     └─→ atomic-state, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, etc.\n121: ```\n122: \n123: ### Library Structure\n124: \n125: **`lib/deep-loop/` (10 modules):**\n126: - `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings\n127: - `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)\n128: - `prompt-pack.ts` — renders the iteration prompt template\n129: - `post-dispatch-validate.ts` — validates iteration outputs (markdown + JSONL + delta)\n130: - `atomic-state.ts` — atomic state-log writes\n131: - `jsonl-repair.ts` — recovers corrupt JSONL state lines\n132: - `loop-lock.ts` — single-writer locking\n133: - `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks\n134: - `bayesian-scorer.ts` — convergence scoring\n135: - `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix\n136: \n137: **`lib/coverage-graph/` (3 modules):**\n138: - `coverage-graph-db.ts` — SQLite schema + node-kind allow-list + connection lifecycle\n139: - `coverage-graph-query.ts` — query builders\n140: - `coverage-graph-signals.ts` — convergence signal extraction\n141: \n142: ### Council Primitives\n143: \n144: The Runtime Boundary Decision (ADR-001) extends `deep-loop-runtime/lib/` with council-compatible infrastructure primitives while keeping operator-facing and domain workflow semantics in `deep-ai-council`. These primitives are consumed by downstream deep-ai-council orchestration (per-topic multi-round, session-findings registry, command and skill wiring, parity and cost docs) for per-topic orchestration, multi-topic session state, command wiring, parity tests and docs.\n145: \n146: **`lib/council/` (5 modules):**\n147: - `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.\n148: - `round-state-jsonl.cjs` — appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard, repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append, fsyncs writes, and exposes round-state readers for resume.\n149: - `adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs` — scores Round-N -> Round-N+1 adjudicator verdict deltas using ADR-003 weights for option change, confidence delta, material-risk Jaccard delta, axis flip rate, and blocking-disagreement delta.\n150: - `cost-guards.cjs` — normalizes and enforces ADR-004 defaults for `max_rounds_per_topic`, `max_topics_per_session`, `saturation_threshold`, and `seats_per_round`, including upper-bound seat-output calculation.\n151: - `session-state-hierarchy.cjs` — creates and validates the ADR-002 session -> topic -> round state shape, including stable `topic-NNN-slug` and `round-NNN` ids.\n152: \n153: The existing `lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts`, and `lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` remain the sibling runtime contracts. Council modules mirror those durability semantics in a council-scoped CJS surface so downstream `deep-ai-council` phases can consume them without modifying deep-review or deep-research behavior.\n154: \n155: ### Script Entry Points\n156: \n157: Four `.cjs` scripts replace the 4 deleted MCP tools, each honoring the same JSON-in / JSON-out contract documented in ADR-001 (the script interface contract):\n158: \n159: | Script | Replaces | Purpose | Exit codes |\n160: |--------|----------|---------|------------|\n161: | `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes typed CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED decisions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n162: | `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores nodes + edges from iteration `graphEvents` | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n163: | `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims, contradictions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n164: | `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Session-scoped health report | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n165: | `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |\n166: | `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |\n167: | `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |\n168: | `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |\n169: \n170: **Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.\n171: \n172: Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.\n173: \n174: ### Test Organization\n175: \n176: ```text\n177: tests/\n178: ├── unit/         # 13 files — per-module unit tests (atomic-state, executor-config, etc.)\n179: ├── integration/  # 7 files — script invocations + review-depth fixtures\n180: ├── lifecycle/    # 1 file — db-open-close pattern verification\n181: └── _helpers/     # 1 file — spawn-cjs helper for direct-invocation tests\n182: ```\n183: \n184: The system-spec-kit `mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` so a single vitest run discovers both surfaces.\n185: \n186: ---\n187: \n188: ## 4. RULES\n189: \n190: ### ALWAYS\n191: \n192: - **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)\n193: - **ALWAYS** emit JSON-only output to stdout from `scripts/*.cjs` (workflow YAML parses stdout for output bindings)\n194: - **ALWAYS** include `'use strict';` on line 2 of every `.cjs` script (per sk-code OPENCODE JS style)\n195: - **ALWAYS** include a `MODULE: <name>` header comment in every `lib/**/*.ts` file (per sk-code OPENCODE TS-MODULE-HEADER convention)\n196: - **ALWAYS** preserve atomic-state semantics when mutating JSONL state — never partial-write\n197: - **ALWAYS** acquire `loop-lock` before any state-log mutation\n198: \n199: ### NEVER\n200: \n201: - **NEVER** register MCP tools from this skill — the isolation ADR explicitly removed the MCP surface; reintroducing tools defeats the FULL_ISOLATE direction\n202: - **NEVER** open the SQLite DB outside `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` — single owner of the connection\n203: - **NEVER** import from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/` or `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/` — those locations are empty after the runtime moved here; import from this skill instead\n204: - **NEVER** write to `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` without holding the `loop-lock`\n205: - **NEVER** swallow exceptions in script entry points — propagate to the exit-code matrix\n206: \n207: ### ESCALATE IF\n208: \n209: - **ESCALATE** to the user if a runtime call requires a new MCP tool — the architectural direction prohibits MCP additions; the user must decide on policy override\n210: - **ESCALATE** to a new ownership ADR if a new consumer skill (beyond deep-review and deep-research) needs deep-loop runtime — extension requires a new ownership ADR\n211: - **ESCALATE** if the SQLite schema requires a backward-incompatible change — coordinate with all consumer workflows + add migration to `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n212: - **ESCALATE** if a script's stdout JSON contract changes — every workflow YAML output binding depends on the existing shape\n213: \n214: ---\n215: \n216: ## 5. SCOPE\n217: \n218: In scope:\n219: \n220: - runtime libraries under `lib/deep-loop/`\n221: - coverage-graph runtime libraries under `lib/coverage-graph/`\n222: - `.cjs` script entry points under `scripts/`\n223: - runtime-owned SQLite storage under `database/`\n224: - runtime tests under `tests/`\n225: \n226: Out of scope:\n227: \n228: - MCP tool registration\n229: - MCP handler ownership\n230: - DB lifecycle inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`\n231: - direct user command routing\n232: \n233: ---\n234: \n235: ## 6. ARCHITECTURE\n236: \n237: - `lib/deep-loop/` holds shared deep-loop runtime libraries.\n238: - `lib/coverage-graph/` holds coverage-graph schema, query, and signal helpers.\n239: - `scripts/` holds `.cjs` entry points used by workflow YAML and collateral commands.\n240: - `database/` owns relocated runtime SQLite artifacts.\n241: - `tests/` holds runtime-owned tests split from the MCP server surface.\n242: \n243: Shipped layout:\n244: \n245: | Path | Populated By | Purpose |\n246: |------|--------------|---------|\n247: | `lib/deep-loop/` | 10 files | Shared executor config, audit, prompt-pack, validation, state, repair, locking, permissions, scoring, and fallback routing. |\n248: | `lib/coverage-graph/` | 3 files | Coverage-graph schema owner, query helpers, and signal interpretation. |\n249: | `scripts/` | 4 files | Direct `status`, `query`, `upsert`, and `convergence` entry points replacing the removed deep-loop MCP tools. |\n250: | `database/` | SQLite | Runtime-owned `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |\n251: | `tests/` | Unit, integration, lifecycle | Runtime-owned tests discovered by the system-spec-kit MCP server Vitest config. |\n252: \n253: ---\n254: \n255: ## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS\n256: \n257: - **ADR-001 (AI Council SPLIT ruling)**: original AI Council SPLIT ruling. Superseded by user directive.\n258: - **Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP)**: Moves deep-loop runtime, coverage-graph schema/query/signals, script entry points, storage, and tests into this peer skill while removing the deep-loop MCP tool surface.\n259: - **Deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML** call this runtime through script entry points after the runtime cutover.\n260: - **system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts** includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` for cross-package test discovery.\n261: - **deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs** depends on the moved coverage-graph runtime via lib imports.\n262: \n263: ---\n264: \n265: ## 8. SUCCESS CRITERIA\n266: \n267: The runtime is healthy when:\n268: \n269: - Every `scripts/` entry point (`status`, `query`, `upsert`, `convergence`) returns valid JSON on its documented stdout contract\n270: - Consumer workflows resolve their `lib/` imports without path errors\n271: - The runtime-owned SQLite under `database/` is present and its coverage-graph schema matches `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n272: - Runtime tests under `tests/` pass through the system-spec-kit Vitest discovery glob\n273: \n274: ---\n275: \n276: ## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES\n277: \n278: - ADR-001 (script interface contract): documents the script-shim and DB-relocation contract.\n279: - Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP): documents the MCP tool surface removal rationale.\n280: - Decision rationale lives in this skill's changelog and originating decision records.\n281: \n282: Related resources:\n283: \n284: - Consumer skills: the `deep-review` and `deep-research` workflows call this runtime through the script entry points\n285: - `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` discovers this skill's `tests/`\n286: - Per-release detail and rationale: this skill's `changelog/` directory\n\n(End of file - total 286 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"---\nname: deep-loop-runtime\nversion: 1.4.0\ndescription: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\nallowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n---\n\n# Deep Loop Runtime\n\nShared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n\n---\n\n## 1. WHEN TO USE\n\nThis skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n\nUse it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n\n- executor configuration and audit metadata","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md","time":{"start":1780205290639,"end":1780205290662}},"id":"prt_e7c81029a0018WgctXtXLcmFh5","sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","messageID":"msg_e7c80eee8001ZiIO6I4pjCr85X"}}

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:11066:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-003.out:7976:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:2788:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/gpt55/iterations/iteration-001.md:107:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.

   143	
   144	  step_fanout_spawn:
   145	    description: "Spawn fan-out lineages; skip and proceed to phase_init normally when single-executor"
   146	    skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
   147	    instructions: |
   148	      Fan-out mode is active. Spawn all lineages. Each lineage runs the full loop in its own
   149	      isolated sub-packet under {artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/.
   150	      After all lineages complete, jump directly to phase_synthesis — skip phase_init and
   151	      phase_main_loop entirely (those are the single-executor path). The merge step in
   152	      phase_synthesis will consolidate results across all lineages.
   153	    sub_steps:
   154	      step_fanout_spawn_cli:
   155	        description: "Spawn all CLI lineages in capped worker pool"
   156	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind != 'native'"
   157	        command: |
   158	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs \
   159	            --spec-folder {spec_folder} \
   160	            --loop-type research \
   161	            --fanout-config-json '{config.fanout_json}' \
   162	            --base-artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
   163	        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
   164	        wait_for_completion: true
   165	      step_fanout_spawn_native:
   166	        description: "Dispatch each native lineage as a sequential deep-research sub-agent"
   167	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind == 'native'"
   168	        for_each: "lineage in expanded native entries of config.fanout"
   169	        dispatch:
   170	          agent: deep-research
   171	          model: opus
   172	          context: |
   173	            Execute the deep-research loop with these parameters:
   174	              spec_folder: {spec_folder}
   175	              config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: {artifact_dir}/lineages/{lineage.label}
   176	              session_id: fanout-{lineage.label}-{fanout_run_id}
   177	              executor: native
   178	            Run to convergence. Write all outputs to the override directory.
   179	        wait_for_completion: true
   180	
   181	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   182	  # PHASE 1: INITIALIZATION
   183	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   184	  phase_init:
   185	    purpose: Create all state files for the research session
   945	        set:
   946	          current_iteration: "{current_iteration + 1}"
   947	          stuck_count: "if status == 'thought': unchanged (skip). if status == 'insight': 0 (reset). if newInfoRatio < convergence_threshold: stuck_count + 1, else: 0"
   948	        log: "Iteration {N} complete. newInfoRatio: {newInfoRatio}. Findings: {findings_count}. Stuck count: {stuck_count}."
   949	
   950	      step_loop_decision:
   951	        action: "Return to step_read_state for next iteration"
   952	        goto: step_read_state
   953	
   954	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   955	  # PHASE 3: SYNTHESIS
   956	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   957	  phase_synthesis:

   959	    steps:

   961	      step_fanout_merge:
   962	        description: "Merge N lineage registries into one consolidated registry; skip when single-executor"
   963	        skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
   964	        command: |
   965	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs \
   966	            --loop-type research \
   967	            --artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
   968	        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
   969	        wait_for_completion: true

   973	        read:

   975	          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
   976	          - "{state_paths.registry}"
   977	          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
   978	        extract:

   983	          - ratio_prev: "Extract newInfoRatio from second-latest iteration record, else 'N/A'"
   984	          - ratio_latest: "Extract newInfoRatio from latest iteration record, else 'N/A'"
   985	          - remaining_questions: "Count unchecked items in 'Key Questions'"

   137	      Fan-out mode is active. Spawn all lineages. Each lineage runs the full loop in its own
   138	      isolated sub-packet under {artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/.
   139	      After all lineages complete, jump directly to phase_synthesis — skip phase_init and
   140	      phase_main_loop entirely (those are the single-executor path). The merge step in
   141	      phase_synthesis will consolidate results across all lineages.
   142	    sub_steps:
   143	      step_fanout_spawn_cli:
   144	        description: "Spawn all CLI lineages in capped worker pool"
   145	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind != 'native'"
   146	        command: |
   147	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs \
   148	            --spec-folder {spec_folder} \
   149	            --loop-type review \
   150	            --fanout-config-json '{config.fanout_json}' \
   151	            --base-artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
   152	        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
   153	        wait_for_completion: true
   154	      step_fanout_spawn_native:
   155	        description: "Dispatch each native lineage as a sequential deep-review sub-agent"
   156	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind == 'native'"
   157	        for_each: "lineage in expanded native entries of config.fanout"
   158	        dispatch:
   159	          agent: deep-review
   160	          model: opus
   161	          context: |
   162	            Execute the deep-review loop with these parameters:
   163	              spec_folder: {spec_folder}
   164	              config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: {artifact_dir}/lineages/{lineage.label}
   165	              session_id: fanout-{lineage.label}-{fanout_run_id}
   166	              executor: native
   167	            Run to convergence. Write all outputs to the override directory.
   168	        wait_for_completion: true
   169	
   170	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   171	  # PHASE 1: INITIALIZATION
   172	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   173	  phase_init:
   174	    purpose: Create all state files for the review session and resolve review scope
   175	    steps:
  1158	            if newFindingsRatio <= 0.05: stuck_count + 1
  1159	            else: 0
  1160	        log: "Iteration {N} complete. Dimensions: {dimensions}. newFindingsRatio: {newFindingsRatio}. Findings: P0={severity_counts.P0} P1={severity_counts.P1} P2={severity_counts.P2}. Stuck count: {stuck_count}."
  1161	
  1162	      step_loop_decision:
  1163	        action: "Return to step_read_state for next iteration"
  1164	        goto: step_read_state
  1165	
  1166	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  1167	  # PHASE 3: SYNTHESIS
  1168	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  1169	  phase_synthesis:
  1170	    purpose: Compile all iteration findings into review/review-report.md
  1171	    steps:
  1172	
  1173	      step_fanout_merge:
  1174	        description: "Merge N lineage registries with strongest-restriction; bind p0/p1/p2 counts for step_derive_verdict; skip when single-executor"
  1175	        skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
  1176	        command: |
  1177	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs \
  1178	            --loop-type review \
  1179	            --artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
  1180	        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
  1181	        wait_for_completion: true
  1182	        bind_from_output:
  1183	          p0_count: "active_p0"
  1184	          p1_count: "active_p1"
  1185	          p2_count: "active_p2"
  1186	
  1187	      step_hydrate_summary_metrics:
  1188	        action: "Hydrate summary metrics before synthesis"
  1189	        read:
  1190	          - "{state_paths.config}"
  1191	          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
  1192	          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
  1193	          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
  1194	        extract:
  1195	          - iteration_count: "Count lines where type === 'iteration'"

    31

    39	
    40	# SINGLE CONSOLIDATED SETUP PROMPT
    41	
    42	This workflow gathers all setup inputs in one prompt. Confirm mode still includes multiple approval gates after setup; only the setup round-trip is consolidated here.
    43	
    44	---
    45	
    46	## 0. UNIFIED SETUP PHASE
    47	
    48	**FIRST MESSAGE PROTOCOL**: For `:confirm` or no suffix, the consolidated setup prompt MUST be your FIRST response. No implementation or file-modifying tool calls before asking. Lightweight read-only discovery to suggest a spec folder or load prior context is allowed, then ask ALL questions immediately and wait.
    49	
    50	For `:auto`, do not emit the consolidated prompt by default. Resolve setup with the three-tier branch below, then load the auto YAML only after all required values are bound.
    51	
    52	### `:auto` Setup Resolution
    53	
    54	Setup contract: see `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/workflows/auto_mode_contract.md`.
    55	
    56	Under `execution_mode = AUTONOMOUS` (from the `:auto` suffix), follow the three-tier flow:
    57	
    58	1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist to `{spec_folder}/review/deep-review-config.json` (shape: `reviewTarget`, `reviewTargetType`, `reviewDimensions`, `specFolder`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `resource_map.emit`, `config.executor.*`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.
    59	
    60	2. **Tier 2 — Targeted ask** (contract §1): when 1-2 required fields are genuinely ambiguous AND no default exists, emit ONE narrow question per ambiguous field. Command-specific Tier-2-eligible fields (per the Default Resolution Table below): `review_target_type`, `spec_folder`. **Ordering rule**: if `review_target_type` is ambiguous, ask only for `review_target_type` first — the answer may make `spec_folder` self-evident on the next Tier 1 pass. Missing `review_target` is absence, not ambiguity — go to Tier 3.
    61	
    62	3. **Tier 3 — Fail fast** (contract §4): emit the named-missing-inputs error format with `/deep:start-review-loop:auto` as the command name. Exit non-zero. Do not load YAML.
    63	
    64	`:confirm` path stays unchanged — see the Consolidated Setup Prompt section below.
    65	
    66	### PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS Schema (for `:auto` non-interactive dispatch)
    67	
    68	The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults.
    69	
    70	```yaml
    71	PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:
    72	  review_target: <spec-folder>
    73	  review_target_type: spec-folder  # one of: spec-folder | skill | agent | track | files
    74	  review_dimensions: all  # or comma-separated subset: correctness, security, traceability, maintainability
    75	  spec_folder: existing  # one of: existing | new | update-related | phase-folder, or an explicit specs/.opencode/specs path
    76	  execution_mode: AUTONOMOUS  # from :auto suffix
    77	  maxIterations: 10
    78	  convergenceThreshold: 0.10
    79	  executor: native  # one of: native | cli-codex | cli-gemini | cli-claude-code | cli-opencode | cli-devin
    80	  executor_model: ""  # optional, executor-specific
    81	  executor_reasoning: ""  # optional
    82	  executor_service_tier: ""  # optional
    83	  executor_timeout: 900  # optional
    84	  resource_map_emit: true  # optional
    85	```
    86	
    87	Rules:
    88	
    89	- Any unspecified field falls back to its documented default.
    90	- Marker fields take precedence over `$ARGUMENTS` flags because the caller explicitly bound setup in the prompt body.
    91	- Unknown fields are warnings, not errors.
    92	- Malformed lines, including missing `:`, emit a parse error naming the offending line. Known fields parsed before the error may still be used, and unresolved fields continue to Tier 2 or Tier 3.
    93	- Empty strings count as unresolved for required fields.
    94	- Compact legacy answer strings are only for the consolidated `:confirm` prompt. They are not a `:auto` marker format.
    95	
    96	### Default Resolution Table
    97	
    98	| Field | Required | Resolves Via | Default | Tier-2 Candidate |
    99	|-------|----------|--------------|---------|------------------|
   100	| `review_target` | Y | `$ARGUMENTS` first positional target, or marker `review_target` | none | N |
   101	| `review_target_type` | Y | marker `review_target_type`, or auto-detect from `review_target` | inferred only | Y, when target is present but ambiguous |
   102	| `review_dimensions` | Y | flag `--dims` if supported by caller, marker `review_dimensions`, or default | `"all"` | N |
   103	| `spec_folder` | Y | flag `--spec-folder`, marker `spec_folder`, or target path when target is a spec folder | none | Y, when target is not a spec folder |
   104	| `execution_mode` | Y | attached suffix `:auto` or marker `execution_mode` | `AUTONOMOUS` under `:auto` | N |
   105	| `maxIterations` | Y | flag `--max-iterations`, marker `maxIterations`, or default | `7` | N |
   106	| `convergenceThreshold` | Y | flag `--convergence`, marker `convergenceThreshold`, or default | `0.10` | N |
   107	| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
   108	| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |
   109	| `executor_reasoning` | N | flag `--reasoning-effort`, marker `executor_reasoning`, or executor default | none | N |
   110	| `executor_service_tier` | N | flag `--service-tier`, marker `executor_service_tier`, or executor default | none | N |
   111	| `executor_timeout` | N | flag `--executor-timeout`, marker `executor_timeout`, or default | `900` | N |
   112	| `resource_map_emit` | N | flag `--no-resource-map`, marker `resource_map_emit`, or default | `true` | N |
   113	| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each group accepts `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
   114	| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
   115	
   116	**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Review fan-out uses strongest-restriction: any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-review` sub-agents; CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses in the capped pool.
   117	
   118	### Consolidated Setup Prompt for `:confirm` and No-Suffix Mode
   119	
   120	Use this block only when `execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"` or when no suffix was supplied and Q2 must ask for the execution mode.
   121	
   122	**STATUS: BLOCKED**
   123	
   124	```
   125	EXECUTE THIS SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT:
   126	
   127	1. CHECK for mode suffix:
   128	   |-- ":auto"    -> execution_mode = "AUTONOMOUS"
   129	   |-- ":confirm" -> execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"
   130	   +-- No suffix  -> execution_mode = "ASK" (include Q2)
   131	
   132	2. CHECK $ARGUMENTS for target (review_target):
   133	   |-- Has content (ignoring suffixes and flags):
   134	   |     -> review_target = $ARGUMENTS, omit Q0
   135	   +-- Empty -> include Q0
   136	
   137	3. PARSE optional flags from $ARGUMENTS:
   138	   |-- --max-iterations=N -> maxIterations = N
   139	   |-- --convergence=N -> convergenceThreshold = N
   140	   |-- --spec-folder=PATH -> spec_path = PATH, omit Q1
   141	   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
   142	   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
   143	   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
   144	   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)
   145	   |-- --executor-timeout=<seconds> -> config.executor.timeoutSeconds (positive integer, default `900`)
   146	   |-- --no-resource-map -> config.resource_map.emit = false
   147	   |-- --executor=<type> [--model=X] [--reasoning-effort=Y] [--service-tier=Z] [--executor-timeout=N] [--iters=N] [--label=X] [--count=N]
   148	   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
   149	   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch
   150	   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
   151	   |
   152	   |   Fan-out default policy:
   153	   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)
   154	   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
   155	   |   - Review fan-out verdict: strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL)
   156	   |
   157	   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=7, convergenceThreshold=0.10, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`
   158	
   159	   Executor precedence for setup resolution:
   160	   - CLI flag > config file > schema defaults
   161	   - The generated `deep-review-config.json` stores executor settings under `config.executor.*`
   162	
   163	   Parsing to config mapping:
   164	   - `--executor` -> `config.executor.type`
   165	   - `--model` -> `config.executor.model`
   166	   - `--reasoning-effort` -> `config.executor.reasoningEffort`
   167	   - `--service-tier` -> `config.executor.serviceTier`
   168	   - `--executor-timeout` -> `config.executor.timeoutSeconds`
   169	
   170	   Validation hook:
   171	   - `parseExecutorConfig` from `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` runs at config-write time
   172	   - Invalid combinations fail fast with clear errors, including `cli-codex` without `--model` and reserved-but-unwired executor kinds
   173	
   174	4. Search for related spec folders across alias roots:
   175	   $ find specs .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -type d 2>/dev/null | sort | tail -10
   176	
   177	5. Search for prior work (background):
   178	   - memory_context({ input: review_target OR "deep-review", mode: "focused", includeContent: true })
   179	   - Store: prior_work_found = [yes/no]
   180	
   181	6. ASK with SINGLE prompt (include only applicable questions):
   182	   - Include Q-Exec only when `--executor` is NOT present and the target text does NOT already mention executor hints such as `cli-codex`, `codex`, or `gpt-5.4`
   183	   - If Q-Exec is omitted and no executor is otherwise resolved, default to `native`
   184	
   185	   Q0. Review Target (if not in command): What to review?
   186	     Examples: spec folder path, `skill:sk-name`, `agent:name`, `track:NN--name`, or file paths/globs
   187	
   188	   Q1_type. Review Target Type (auto-detect from Q0, confirm):
   189	     A) spec-folder -- Review spec artifacts + implementation files
   190	     B) skill -- SKILL.md + references/ + assets/ + agents + commands
   191	     C) agent -- Agent family across all runtimes
   192	     D) track -- All child spec folders in a feature track
   193	     E) files -- Arbitrary file paths or globs
   194	
   195	   Q_dims. Which review dimensions? (default: all 4)
   196	     All: correctness, security, traceability, maintainability
   197	     Or specify a subset (e.g., "correctness, security")
   198	
   199	   Q1. Spec Folder (required):
   200	     A) Use existing [suggest if found]
   201	     B) Create new under `specs/[track]/[###]-[slug]/` (accept `.opencode/specs/` alias roots when already in use)
   202	     C) Update related [if match found]
   203	     D) Phase folder (e.g., `specs/NN-track/NNN-name/001-phase/` or matching `.opencode/specs/` alias)
   204	
   205	   Q2. Execution Mode (if no suffix):
   206	     A) Autonomous -- all iterations without approval
   207	     B) Interactive -- pause at each iteration for review
   208	
   209	   Q3. Max Iterations (if not set via flag):
   210	     Default is 7 for review. Change? [Enter number or press enter for default]
   211	
   212	   Q-Exec. Executor (optional, press enter for default):
   213	     A) Native (default) — dispatch via @deep-review agent with Opus.
   214	     B) cli-codex — `codex exec` with --model X -c model_reasoning_effort -c service_tier.
   215	     C) cli-gemini — `gemini "PROMPT" -m gemini-3.1-pro-preview -y -o text`. Single supported model currently. No reasoning-effort or service-tier.
   216	     D) cli-claude-code — `claude -p "PROMPT" --model X --permission-mode acceptEdits` with optional --effort. No service-tier.
   217	     E) cli-opencode — `opencode run --model X --agent general --format json --dangerously-skip-permissions --pure --dir {repo_root} [--variant Y] "PROMPT" </dev/null`. `reasoningEffort` maps to `--variant`. No service-tier.
   218	     F) cli-devin — `devin --print --prompt-file ... --model X --permission-mode auto`. Default model swe-1.6. No reasoning-effort or service-tier.
   219	
   220	   Reply format examples:

End §0.
    61	
    62	2. **Tier 2 — Targeted ask** (contract §1): when 1-2 required fields are genuinely ambiguous AND no default exists, emit ONE narrow question per ambiguous field. Command-specific Tier-2-eligible fields (per the Default Resolution Table below): `spec_folder`. **Ordering rule**: none needed. Missing `research_topic` is absence, not ambiguity — go to Tier 3.
    63	
    64	3. **Tier 3 — Fail fast** (contract §4): emit the named-missing-inputs error format with `/deep:start-research-loop:auto` as the command name. Exit non-zero. Do not load YAML.
    65	
    66	`:confirm` path stays unchanged — see the consolidated setup prompt section below.
    67	
    68	### PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS Schema (for `:auto` non-interactive dispatch)
    69	
    70	The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults. Grammar: see `auto_mode_contract.md` §2.
    71	
    72	```yaml
    73	PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:
    74	  research_topic: WebSocket reconnection strategies  # string
    75	  spec_folder: <spec-folder>  # existing | new | update-related | phase-folder | explicit path
    76	  execution_mode: AUTONOMOUS  # from :auto suffix
    77	  maxIterations: 10  # positive integer
    78	  convergenceThreshold: 0.05  # decimal 0..1
    79	  executor: native  # native | cli-codex | cli-gemini | cli-claude-code | cli-opencode | cli-devin
    80	  executor_model: ""  # optional executor-specific model id
    81	  executor_reasoning: ""  # optional reasoning effort
    82	  executor_service_tier: ""  # optional service tier
    83	  executor_timeout: 900  # optional positive integer seconds
    84	  resource_map_emit: true  # boolean
    85	```
    86	
    87	Rules: see `auto_mode_contract.md` §2 (unspecified fields fall back to default; marker fields take precedence over `$ARGUMENTS` flags; unknown fields warn; malformed lines parse-error).
    88	
    89	### Default Resolution Table
    90	
    91	| Field | Required | Resolves Via | Default | Tier-2 Candidate |
    92	|-------|----------|--------------|---------|------------------|
    93	| `research_topic` | Y | `$ARGUMENTS` positional topic, or marker `research_topic` | none | N |
    94	| `spec_folder` | Y | flag `--spec-folder`, marker `spec_folder`, or targeted choice among suggested existing/new/update-related/phase folder | none | Y, when topic is present but folder choice is ambiguous |
    95	| `execution_mode` | Y | attached suffix `:auto` or marker `execution_mode` | `AUTONOMOUS` under `:auto` | N |
    96	| `maxIterations` | Y | flag `--max-iterations`, marker `maxIterations`, or default | `10` | N |
    97	| `convergenceThreshold` | Y | flag `--convergence`, marker `convergenceThreshold`, or default | `0.05` | N |
    98	| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
    99	| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |
   100	| `executor_reasoning` | N | flag `--reasoning-effort`, marker `executor_reasoning`, or executor default | none | N |
   101	| `executor_service_tier` | N | flag `--service-tier`, marker `executor_service_tier`, or executor default | none | N |
   102	| `executor_timeout` | N | flag `--executor-timeout`, marker `executor_timeout`, or default | `900` | N |
   103	| `resource_map_emit` | N | flag `--no-resource-map`, marker `resource_map_emit`, or default | `true` | N |
   104	| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each `--executor` group accepts `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
   105	| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
   106	
   107	**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-research` agent dispatches with isolated dirs. CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses concurrently in the capped pool.
   108	
   109	**STATUS: BLOCKED**
   110	
   111	```
   112	EXECUTE THIS SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT:
   113	
   114	1. CHECK for mode suffix:
   115	   |-- ":auto"         -> execution_mode = "AUTONOMOUS"
   116	   |-- ":confirm"      -> execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"
   117	   +-- No suffix       -> execution_mode = "ASK"
   118	
   119	2. CHECK $ARGUMENTS for topic:
   120	   |-- Has content (ignoring suffixes and flags):
   121	   |     -> research_topic = $ARGUMENTS, omit Q0
   122	   +-- Empty -> include Q0
   123	
   124	3. PARSE optional flags from $ARGUMENTS:
   125	   |-- --max-iterations=N -> maxIterations = N
   126	   |-- --convergence=N -> convergenceThreshold = N
   127	   |-- --spec-folder=PATH -> spec_path = PATH, omit Q1
   128	   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
   129	   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
   130	   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
   131	   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)
   132	   |-- --executor-timeout=<seconds> -> config.executor.timeoutSeconds (positive integer, default `900`)
   133	   |-- --no-resource-map -> config.resource_map.emit = false
   134	   |-- --executor=<type> [--model=X] [--reasoning-effort=Y] [--service-tier=Z] [--executor-timeout=N] [--iters=N] [--label=X] [--count=N]
   135	   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
   136	   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch for complex configs
   137	   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
   138	   |
   139	   |   Fan-out default policy:
   140	   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)
   141	   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
   142	   |
   143	   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=10, convergenceThreshold=0.05, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`
   144	
   145	   Executor precedence for setup resolution:
   146	   - CLI flag > config file > schema defaults
   147	   - The generated `deep-research-config.json` stores executor settings under `config.executor.*`
   148	
   149	   Parsing to config mapping:
   150	   - `--executor` -> `config.executor.type`
   151	   - `--model` -> `config.executor.model`
   152	   - `--reasoning-effort` -> `config.executor.reasoningEffort`
   153	   - `--service-tier` -> `config.executor.serviceTier`
   154	   - `--executor-timeout` -> `config.executor.timeoutSeconds`
   155	
   156	   Validation hook:
   157	   - `parseExecutorConfig` from `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` runs at config-write time
   158	   - Invalid combinations fail fast with clear errors, including `cli-codex` without `--model` and reserved-but-unwired executor kinds
   159	
   160	4. Search for related spec folders across alias roots:
   161	   $ find specs .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -type d 2>/dev/null | sort | tail -10
   162	
   163	5. Search for prior work (background):
   164	   - memory_context({ input: research_topic OR "deep-research", mode: "focused", includeContent: true })
   165	   - Store: prior_work_found = [yes/no]
   166	
   167	6. ASK with SINGLE prompt (include only applicable questions):
   168	   - Include Q-Exec only when `--executor` is NOT present and the topic text does NOT already mention executor hints such as `cli-codex`, `codex`, or `gpt-5.4`
   169	   - If Q-Exec is omitted and no executor is otherwise resolved, default to `native`
   170	
   171	   Q0. Research Topic (if not in command): What topic to research deeply?
   172	
   173	   Q1. Spec Folder (required):
   174	     A) Use existing [suggest if found]
   175	     B) Create new under `specs/[track]/[###]-[slug]/` (accept `.opencode/specs/` alias roots when already in use)
   176	     C) Update related [if match found]
   177	     D) Phase folder (e.g., `specs/NN-track/NNN-name/001-phase/` or matching `.opencode/specs/` alias)
   178	
   179	   Q2. Execution Mode (if no suffix):
   180	     A) Autonomous -- all iterations without approval
   181	     B) Interactive -- pause at each iteration for review
   182	
   183	   Q3. Max Iterations (if not set via flag):
   184	     Default is 10. Change? [Enter number or press enter for default]
   185	
   186	   Q-Exec. Executor (optional, press enter for default):
   187	     A) Native (default) — dispatch via @deep-research agent with Opus.
   188	     B) cli-codex — `codex exec` with --model X -c model_reasoning_effort -c service_tier.
   189	     C) cli-gemini — `gemini "PROMPT" -m gemini-3.1-pro-preview -y -o text`. Single supported model currently. No reasoning-effort or service-tier.
   190	     D) cli-claude-code — `claude -p "PROMPT" --model X --permission-mode acceptEdits` with optional --effort. No service-tier.
   191	     E) cli-opencode — `opencode run --model X --agent general --format json --dangerously-skip-permissions --pure --dir {repo_root} [--variant Y] "PROMPT" </dev/null`. `reasoningEffort` maps to `--variant`. No service-tier.
   192	     F) cli-devin — `devin --print --prompt-file ... --model X --permission-mode auto`. Default model swe-1.6. No reasoning-effort or service-tier.
   193	
   194	   Reply format examples:
   195	   - `"A, A"`
   196	   - `"WebSocket research, A, B, 15"`
   197	   - `"WebSocket reconnection strategies, B, A, 10, 0.05, B, gpt-5.4, high, fast"`
   198	   - `"agent execution guardrails research, B, A, 15, 0.05, C, gpt-5.4, _, _"`
   199	
   200	7. WAIT for user response (DO NOT PROCEED)
   201	
   202	8. Parse response and store ALL results:
   203	   - research_topic = [from Q0 or $ARGUMENTS]
   204	   - spec_choice = [A/B/C/D from Q1]
   205	   - spec_path = [derived path]
   206	   - execution_mode = [AUTONOMOUS/INTERACTIVE]
   207	   - maxIterations = [from Q3 or flag or default 10]
   208	   - convergenceThreshold = [from flag or default 0.05]
   209	   - executor config = [CLI flags, compact reply, config file, or default `native`; map compact reply fields to `config.executor.type/model/reasoningEffort/serviceTier`, and accept an optional volunteered convergence value before executor fields]
   210	
   211	9. SET STATUS: PASSED
   212	
   213	STOP HERE - Wait for user answers before continuing.
   214	
   215	DO NOT proceed until user explicitly answers
   216	NEVER auto-create spec folders without confirmation
   217	NEVER split questions into multiple prompts
   218	```
   219

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:105:  lock_file: "{artifact_dir}/.deep-research.lock"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:224:      step_acquire_lock:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:225:        action: "Acquire the advisory lock defined by the spec_check_protocol before session classification"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:226:        command: "Open {state_paths.lock_file} with exclusive non-blocking advisory locking semantics; fail closed on contention."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:227:        note: "macOS/BSD locking here is advisory. Stale-lock override is confirm-only or explicit recovery-only."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:229:        # Lock-cleanup contract: the lock file MUST be released on every terminal exit path that follows acquisition.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:230:        # Runtimes honoring step_acquire_lock are required to perform this cleanup before halting or pausing.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:232:          action: "Release {state_paths.lock_file} before any terminal halt branch returns control"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:234:          command: "Remove or close-with-release {state_paths.lock_file}; failures here are logged but do not block the halt"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:236:          action: "Release {state_paths.lock_file} on user cancel or pause-sentinel detection before sentinel processing"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:237:          command: "Remove or close-with-release {state_paths.lock_file}; cancel completes regardless of cleanup result"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:239:          action: "Release {state_paths.lock_file} as the final step of any workflow exit path (success or failure)"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:240:          command: "Remove or close-with-release {state_paths.lock_file}; idempotent — safe to invoke when already released"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:272:        note_deferred_branches: "fork and completed-continue are deferred and no longer runtime-supported in this release. The reducer still reads `lineageMode` from config for dashboards and analysis, but only `new`, `resume`, and `restart` are live. See deep-research/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branch Contract for the one-session contract."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:348:          - "{state_paths.lock_file}"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:353:          conflict-detected: "missing host anchor, duplicate markers, or generated-block ambiguity makes mutation unsafe"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:357:        append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"spec_check_result","folder_state":"{folder_state}","normalized_topic":"{normalized_topic}","specPath":"{spec_folder}/spec.md","lockPath":"{state_paths.lock_file}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:464:          - graph_blockers_json: "Tool blockers array, else []"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:467:        append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"graph_convergence","mode":"research","run":{current_iteration},"decision":{graph_decision_json},"signals":{graph_signals_json},"blockers":{graph_blockers_json},"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:472:          1. if iteration_count >= max_iterations: STOP (maxIterationsReached). This terminal cap bypasses quality-guard overrides; record failed guards/blockers as synthesis evidence instead of continuing.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:476:               step_graph_convergence already recorded graph_decision, graph_signals_json, graph_blockers_json, and graph_convergence_score for this run.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:494:                 reason = "blockedStop"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:495:                 stop_blocked = true
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:496:                 blocked_by_json = JSON array derived from graph_blockers_json
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:497:                 blocked_by_csv = comma-separated blocker names derived from graph_blockers_json
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:500:                 gate_key_question_coverage_pass = no blocker named questionCoverage
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:501:                 gate_evidence_density_pass = no blocker named sourceDiversity or evidenceDepth
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:504:                 recovery_strategy = first blocker reason/message, else "Collect deeper graph evidence before stopping."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:508:                 stop_blocked = false
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:520:          - stop_blocked: "Boolean marker set when graph convergence vetoes an inline STOP candidate"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:521:          - blocked_by_json: "JSON array of graph blocker names when decision == BLOCKED"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:522:          - blocked_by_csv: "Comma-separated graph blocker names when decision == BLOCKED"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:525:          - gate_key_question_coverage_pass: "Boolean pass/fail derived from graph blocker coverage signals"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:526:          - gate_evidence_density_pass: "Boolean pass/fail derived from graph blocker evidence-depth signals"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:528:          - gate_hotspot_saturation_pass: "Research placeholder gate; true unless an explicit blocker sets otherwise"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:531:      step_emit_blocked_stop:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:532:        action: "Persist a first-class blocked_stop event when the legal-stop decision tree returns blocked"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:533:        if_blocked:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:534:          append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"blocked_stop","mode":"research","run":{current_iteration},"blockedBy":{blocked_by_json},"gateResults":{"convergence":{"pass":{gate_convergence_pass},"score":{gate_convergence_score}},"keyQuestionCoverage":{"pass":{gate_key_question_coverage_pass},"answered":{answered_count},"total":{total_questions}},"evidenceDensity":{"pass":{gate_evidence_density_pass},"sources":{gate_evidence_density_sources}},"hotspotSaturation":{"pass":{gate_hotspot_saturation_pass}}},"recoveryStrategy":"{recovery_strategy}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:535:          log: "Graph convergence blocked STOP. Failed blockers: {blocked_by_csv}. Recovery strategy: {recovery_strategy}"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:538:            reason: "blockedStop"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:539:            stop_blocked: false
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:556:        stop_reasons_enum: [converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused]
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:837:              ships with literal "<repo-root>" and "<packet-root>" placeholders. The command block substitutes them
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:851:            - "</dev/null required: family convention for non-interactive dispatch to avoid stdin-blocking edge cases across CLI versions"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:990:          - reason: "Use the latest synthesis/recovery stop reason if present, else the frozen default 'converged'. (Must be a member of the STOP_REASONS enum: converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused.)"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1041:        action: "Write or replace the bounded generated findings block in spec.md before config completion"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1054:          - "Replace exactly one generated findings block under the chosen host anchor."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1055:          - "Fail closed if the host anchor is missing, duplicate markers exist, or human edits are detected inside the generated block."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1056:          - "Do not overwrite a generated block that fails checksum validation or carries an explicit MANUAL-EDIT marker."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1074:        on_fail: "Log warning. Do not block synthesis. The artifact_dir may be outside a git repo (e.g., temp test fixture) which is a valid case."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1097:        on_fail: "Log warning but do not block completion"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1099:      step_release_lock:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1100:        action: "Release the advisory lock after save, skip-save, or cancellation cleanup has resolved"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1101:        command: "Close or unlock {state_paths.lock_file} using advisory semantics only."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1102:        append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"lock_released","path":"{state_paths.lock_file}","releasePhase":"save","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:259:          note: "fork and completed-continue branches are deferred. The reducer still reads `lineageMode` from config for dashboards and analysis, but only `new`, `resume`, and `restart` are runtime-supported in this release. See deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md `Session Lifecycle` for the canonical one-session model."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:356:          releaseReadinessState: "in-progress"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:480:          3. Preserve raw graph signals and blockers for reducer rollup and blocked-stop evidence.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:481:          4. Normalize blockers to both JSON array and CSV forms for downstream stop handling.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:482:          5. Set graph_stop_blocked = true only when graph_decision == "STOP_BLOCKED".
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:485:        append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"graph_convergence","mode":"review","run":{current_iteration},"decision":"{graph_decision}","signals":{graph_signals_json},"blockers":{graph_blockers_json},"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{session_id}","generation":{generation}}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:489:          - graph_blockers_json: "JSON array of graph blockers when present, else []"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:490:          - graph_blockers_csv: "Comma-separated blocker summary when graph blocks STOP, else empty string"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:491:          - graph_stop_blocked: "Boolean true when graph_decision == STOP_BLOCKED"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:500:               stop_blocked = true
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:501:               stopReason = "blockedStop"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:502:               blocked_gates = ["claimAdjudicationGate"]
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:504:               Skip hard stops (1) and composite convergence (4); proceed directly to step 5 legal-stop decision-tree assembly so blocked_by_json, gate outputs, and the blocked_stop event still populate consistently.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:512:               graph_decision, graph_signals_json, and graph_blockers_json are already populated by step_graph_convergence.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:514:               If graph_decision == "STOP_BLOCKED", set stop_blocked = true and route through blocked_stop emission instead of stopping.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:537:               h) candidateCoverageGate: skip entirely when `reviewDepthSchemaVersion` is not 2 on the latest iteration. Otherwise pass when the current iteration's `reviewDepthApplicability.scopeClass` is `trivial` with `enforcement: "skip"` AND `reviewDepthApplicability.evidenceRefs` is non-empty, OR when the reducer registry's `searchDebt` is empty AND `candidateCoverage.covered` is a superset of `searchCoverage.requiredBugClasses` for the lineage. Otherwise pass = false; record `searchDebt` items as blocked-stop evidence; blocker name = "candidateCoverageGate"; recovery_strategy = "Resolve deferred/blocked obligations in next iteration or downgrade scope explicitly to trivial with cited evidence."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:538:               i) graphlessFallbackGate: skip when `reviewDepthSchemaVersion` is not 2 OR the reducer registry's last-iteration `searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode` is `graph`. Applies when graphCoverageMode is `graphless_fallback` or `unavailable_blocked`. Pass when graphCoverageMode is `graphless_fallback` AND at least one `searchLedger` row exists for each entry in `searchCoverage.requiredBugClasses` with `searchActions[].method` in {"direct_read","exact_grep","semantic_search","code_graph_status_check","producer_consumer_trace","negative_test_inspection"} AND non-empty `evidenceRefs`. Fail automatically when graphCoverageMode is `unavailable_blocked`; record the explicit unavailability reason. Otherwise pass = false; blocker name = "graphlessFallbackGate"; recovery_strategy = "Add cited fallback ledger rows (direct read, exact search, producer/consumer trace, negative-test inspection) or set graphCoverageMode to 'graph' once graph data is available."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:541:               stop_blocked = true
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:542:               stopReason = "blockedStop"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:543:               blocked_by_json = JSON array of failing gate names (always include "claimAdjudicationGate" when claim_adjudication_gate_pass == false, "fixCompletenessReplayGate" when fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass == false, "candidateCoverageGate" when candidate_coverage_gate_pass == false, and "graphlessFallbackGate" when graphless_fallback_gate_pass == false)
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:544:               blocked_by_csv = comma-separated list of failing gate names
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:562:               candidate_coverage_search_debt_json = JSON array of reducer-owned searchDebt obligations blocking STOP, else []
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:567:               graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason = explicit reason when graphCoverageMode == "unavailable_blocked", else empty string
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:573:                    stop_blocked = true
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:574:                    stopReason = "blockedStop"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:575:                    # Blocked-stop closure: blockedBy must be string[] per the
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:576:                    # published review blocked-stop contract. Extract the `.type`
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:577:                    # field from each structured graph blocker; preserve the full
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:578:                    # objects in graph_blocker_detail_json for operator inspection.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:579:                    blocked_by_json = JSON.stringify(graph_blockers_json.map(b => typeof b === "string" ? b : (b && b.type) || "graph_blocker"))
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:580:                    blocked_by_csv = graph_blockers_csv
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:581:                    graph_blocker_detail_json = graph_blockers_json
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:582:                    recovery_strategy = "Resolve review coverage-graph blockers before stopping."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:585:                    stop_blocked = false
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:591:          - stop_blocked: "Boolean true when STOP is vetoed by inline legal-stop gates or graph_decision == STOP_BLOCKED"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:592:          - blocked_by_json: "JSON array of failed legal-stop gates when decision == BLOCKED"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:593:          - blocked_by_csv: "Comma-separated list of failed legal-stop gates when decision == BLOCKED"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:611:          - candidate_coverage_search_debt_json: "JSON array of reducer-owned searchDebt obligations used as blocked-stop evidence"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:613:          - graphless_fallback_gate_pass: "Boolean pass/fail for graphlessFallbackGate (false vetoes STOP when graphless fallback evidence is absent or unavailable_blocked)"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:616:          - graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason: "Explicit unavailability reason when graphCoverageMode == unavailable_blocked"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:619:          - graph_blockers_json: "JSON array of graph blockers"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:620:          - graph_blockers_csv: "Comma-separated graph blocker summary"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:623:      step_emit_blocked_stop:

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:625:        if_blocked:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:626:          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"blocked_stop","mode":"review","run":{current_iteration},"blockedBy":{blocked_by_json},"gateResults":{"convergenceGate":{"pass":{convergence_gate_pass},"score":{convergence_gate_score}},"dimensionCoverageGate":{"pass":{dimension_coverage_gate_pass},"covered":{dimension_coverage_gate_covered_json},"missing":{dimension_coverage_gate_missing_json}},"p0ResolutionGate":{"pass":{p0_resolution_gate_pass},"activeP0":{active_p0_count}},"evidenceDensityGate":{"pass":{evidence_density_gate_pass},"avgEvidencePerFinding":{avg_evidence_per_finding}},"hotspotSaturationGate":{"pass":{hotspot_saturation_gate_pass}},"claimAdjudicationGate":{"pass":{claim_adjudication_gate_pass},"activeP0P1":{claim_adjudication_active_count}},"fixCompletenessReplayGate":{"pass":{fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass},"securitySensitive":{security_sensitive_fix_scope},"requiredRows":{fix_completeness_replay_required_rows},"passingRows":{fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows}},"candidateCoverageGate":{"pass":{candidate_coverage_gate_pass},"searchDebt":{candidate_coverage_search_debt_json},"missing":{candidate_coverage_missing_json}},"graphlessFallbackGate":{"pass":{graphless_fallback_gate_pass},"mode":"{graphless_fallback_mode}","missing":{graphless_fallback_missing_json},"unavailabilityReason":"{graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason}"}},"graphBlockerDetail":{graph_blockers_json},"recoveryStrategy":"{recovery_strategy}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{session_id}","generation":{generation}}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:627:          log: "Blocked STOP persisted for review loop. Failed gates: {blocked_by_csv}. Recovery strategy: {recovery_strategy}"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:628:        if_not_blocked:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:636:        if_blocked:

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:529:               h) candidateCoverageGate: skip entirely when `reviewDepthSchemaVersion` is not 2 on the latest iteration. Otherwise pass when the current iteration's `reviewDepthApplicability.scopeClass` is `trivial` with `enforcement: "skip"` AND `reviewDepthApplicability.evidenceRefs` is non-empty, OR when the reducer registry's `searchDebt` is empty AND `candidateCoverage.covered` is a superset of `searchCoverage.requiredBugClasses` for the lineage. Otherwise pass = false; record `searchDebt` items as blocked-stop evidence; blocker name = "candidateCoverageGate"; recovery_strategy = "Resolve deferred/blocked obligations in next iteration or downgrade scope explicitly to trivial with cited evidence."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:530:               i) graphlessFallbackGate: skip when `reviewDepthSchemaVersion` is not 2 OR the reducer registry's last-iteration `searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode` is `graph`. Applies when graphCoverageMode is `graphless_fallback` or `unavailable_blocked`. Pass when graphCoverageMode is `graphless_fallback` AND at least one `searchLedger` row exists for each entry in `searchCoverage.requiredBugClasses` with `searchActions[].method` in {"direct_read","exact_grep","semantic_search","code_graph_status_check","producer_consumer_trace","negative_test_inspection"} AND non-empty `evidenceRefs`. Fail automatically when graphCoverageMode is `unavailable_blocked`; record the explicit unavailability reason. Otherwise pass = false; blocker name = "graphlessFallbackGate"; recovery_strategy = "Add cited fallback ledger rows (direct read, exact search, producer/consumer trace, negative-test inspection) or set graphCoverageMode to 'graph' once graph data is available."
